| 9 | Transactional outbox (microservices.io): a service writes domain state and the corresponding event record to an outbox table inside one ACID transaction; a separate message relay (polling publisher or transaction-log tailing/CDC) publishes outbox rows to the broker — eliminating the dual-write inconsistency between DB and message queue; delivery is at-least-once, so consumers must deduplicate. | M20 notification events and all work-request state-change events are emitted via outbox + relay, never by direct broker publish inside request handlers; consumers are idempotent keyed on event ID, guaranteeing no lost or phantom notifications. | [microservices.io](https://microservices.io/patterns/data/transactional-outbox.html) |
| 10 | AWS retry guidance: naive exponential backoff still synchronizes clients into retry storms; "full jitter" (`sleep = random(0, min(cap, base·2^attempt))`) empirically reduces both completion time and total work, with "decorrelated jitter" as a variant; retries must be capped and applied at a single layer of the stack, since per-layer retries multiply load during outages; messages exhausting retries go to a dead-letter queue for inspection and operator-driven replay. | Shared retry-policy library for all backend services: capped full-jitter backoff, retry budget per dependency, retries owned by one layer only; queue consumers route poison messages to per-service DLQs with replay tooling and DLQ-depth alerts. | [aws.amazon.com](https://aws.amazon.com/builders-library/timeouts-retries-and-backoff-with-jitter/), [architecture blog](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/architecture/exponential-backoff-and-jitter/) |
| 11 | Circuit breaker pattern: Closed (calls flow; failures counted) trips to Open after a failure threshold, where calls fail fast without executing and the dependency gets recovery headroom; after a timeout, Half-Open admits limited trial calls — success closes the circuit, failure re-opens it. Prevents callers hanging on a dead downstream and cascading failure. | Circuit breakers wrap cross-service and third-party calls (robot fleet gateways, webhook/notification deliveries, external storage); breaker state transitions exported as metrics and surfaced in M20 alerting so an open circuit pages before users report errors. | [learn.microsoft.com](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/patterns/circuit-breaker), [martinfowler.com](https://martinfowler.com/bliki/CircuitBreaker.html) |
| 12 | Kubernetes pod termination lifecycle: on delete, the pod is marked Terminating and removed from Service endpoints, the `preStop` hook runs first (if defined), then kubelet sends SIGTERM, and SIGKILL follows after `terminationGracePeriodSeconds` (default 30s); apps must handle SIGTERM by draining — stop accepting work, finish or checkpoint in-flight tasks, close connections — and readiness probes (which run for the whole container lifecycle) should fail during drain to stop traffic before shutdown. | Zero-dropped-work deploys: every backend service implements SIGTERM drain (stop polling queues, ack-or-requeue in-flight messages); pipeline workers get preStop hooks plus a grace period sized to max task checkpoint time; readiness probes gate rollouts and shed traffic during drain. | [kubernetes.io](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle/), [cloud.google.com](https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/containers-kubernetes/kubernetes-best-practices-terminating-with-grace) |
| 13 | OpenTelemetry context propagation: W3C Trace Context is OTel's default propagation format — the `traceparent` header carries `{version}-{trace-id}-{parent-span-id}-{trace-flags}`, with trace-id constant across every hop while parent-span-id changes per service; OTel SDKs automatically inject trace_id/span_id into log records, correlating structured logs with distributed traces. | End-to-end traceability for async work: trace context propagated from API request → outbox event → queue message headers → workflow engine → GPU job pod; all services emit structured JSON logs carrying trace_id/span_id so one work request is queryable across every service and retry. | [opentelemetry.io](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/context-propagation/) |
| 14 | OTel sensitive-data handling: the Collector scrubs telemetry before export using three processors — attributes (delete/hash known PII keys), redaction (attribute allow-list + regex value masking; traces only), and transform (OTTL logic for log bodies and metrics); community benchmarks show ~5–8% CPU overhead at 10k logs/s with 6 regex patterns. Prometheus Alertmanager then handles the alert path: it deduplicates, groups related alerts into single notifications (`group_by`, `group_wait`/`group_interval`/`repeat_interval`), routes via a label-matched routing tree to receivers (PagerDuty, email, webhooks), and supports inhibition (mute dependent alerts when a causal alert fires) and silences. | Centralized collector pipeline masks tenant PII, tokens, and robot serial numbers before logs/traces leave the cluster (deny-by-default attribute allow-list); M20 alerting groups by tenant+service+severity, routes critical→pager / warning→channel, and uses inhibition rules to suppress downstream noise during cluster-level incidents. | [opentelemetry.io](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/security/handling-sensitive-data/), [prometheus.io](https://prometheus.io/docs/alerting/latest/alertmanager/) |

Key architectural references: **Temporal** (durable workflow/activity/signal model for long-running work-request orchestration, with Argo Workflows as the Kubernetes-native pipeline execution layer and Airflow 3's DAG-versioning/backfill semantics as the template-versioning benchmark); **Kueue + Volcano** (two-level LocalQueue/ClusterQueue tenant quota with fair-sharing preemption, gang scheduling for distributed GPU training, OpenCost-spec cost attribution); the **Stripe idempotency-key + transactional-outbox + full-jitter-retry/DLQ** reliability stack for exactly-once-effect async services; and **OpenTelemetry W3C trace context with Alertmanager-style grouped, severity-routed alerting** for cross-service observability and notification hygiene.


---

## 1. Executive Summary & Module Map

### 1.1 Executive Summary

This FDD translates the FRD for the Humanoid Robotics Physical AI Data & Compute Infrastructure Platform into an implementation-ready design. The platform is decomposed into **20 functional modules** (M01–M20) organized in six groups — Platform Management, Physical Data Factory, Simulation & Synthetic Data Factory, ModelOps & Evaluation, Orchestration & Compute Operations, and Cross-Cutting Capabilities — plus **15 independent backend services** (SVC-01…SVC-15, Chapter 3). Every FRD requirement family (A1–A8, B1–B5, C1–C6, D1–D5, X1), every business rule (BR-01–BR-14), every report (RPT-01–RPT-11), and the four mandated platform modules (Login, Tenant Configuration, User Role Configuration, Analytics & Mining) map to exactly one owning module, with cross-references where capabilities span modules. Section 1.7 provides the zero-omission coverage matrix.

### 1.2 Decomposition Methodology (Iterative Convergence Record)

The decomposition executed three extract → validate → reflect → adjust → regenerate rounds:

**Round 1 — Extraction.** Direct extraction from FRD §9 produced 24 candidate modules: one per requirement family (A1–A8, B1–B5, C1–C6, D1–D5, X1) plus 3 mandated platform modules and a reporting module.

**Round 2 — Validation & reflection.** Validation against cohesion/coupling and data-ownership criteria exposed five defects: (1) A2 ingestion and A3 intake validation share one lifecycle (a recording is not usable until validated) — merged into M05; (2) A4 alignment and A5 segmentation both produce/refine the Episode entity — merged into M06; (3) B2/B3/B4 share the Simulation Run → Synthetic Batch → Validation pipeline and one executor — merged into M10; (4) C1/C2 share the Training Plan→Run lifecycle — merged into M11; (5) D1/D2 (templates/runs + work requests/approvals) and D3/D4 (quota + monitoring) form two coherent operational modules — merged into M16 and M17. Reflection on the mandate exposed two boundary blurs: catalog scalar search (A7) vs. mandated hybrid scalar-vector analytics — resolved by DA-15 (M08 owns governed catalog; M18 owns hybrid retrieval/visualization/distribution); and D5 tenancy/roles vs. mandated M02/M03 — resolved by assigning containers/resources to M02, identities/permissions/audit to M03, authentication to M01 (§1.6.1). A standalone "Reporting" module was rejected: each RPT dashboard is owned by the module owning its data (§1.7.3), eliminating a read-everything module with no data ownership.

**Round 3 — Regeneration & convergence check.** The regenerated 20-module map was re-validated: (a) all four mandated modules present and exactly specified (M01, M02, M03, M18); (b) every FR maps to ≥1 module and every module to ≥1 FR or mandate clause (§1.7); (c) no two modules write the same entity (single-writer rule; Table 0.3); (d) every BP-01…BP-06 process route crosses module boundaries only through interfaces in §1.5; (e) B5 (real↔sim loop) deliberately splits: scenario-request conversion lives in M09, failure intake in M15, verifying zero boundary ambiguity. No further adjustments emerged; the decomposition converged.

### 1.3 Module Map

| ID | Module | Group | Scope boundary (owns / excludes) | Primary interacting roles | Upstream dependencies | Downstream consumers |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| M01 | Platform Login & Session Management | Platform Mgmt | Email-code authentication, sessions, login audit hooks. Excludes: user CRUD (M03), permissions (M03). | All roles | M03 (user records), SVC-MAIL | All modules (session context) |
| M02 | Tenant & Resource Management | Platform Mgmt | Tenants, projects, physical/logical compute & storage resource inventory and bindings. Excludes: quota numbers (M17), user identity (M03). | Platform Admin, Tenant Admin, Ops Engineer | M01, M03 | M16, M17, M05 (storage targets), all project-scoped modules |
| M03 | User, Role & Permission Management | Platform Mgmt | User accounts (pre-registration), atomic permission catalog, built-in/custom roles, role bindings, authz decisions, audit log, access reviews. Excludes: login flow (M01), tenant containers (M02). | Platform Admin, Tenant Admin | M01 (session), M02 (scopes) | All modules (authz + audit) |
| M04 | Data Collection Campaign Management | Data Factory | Campaign lifecycle, coverage goals, privacy/consent/restriction tags at campaign level. Excludes: file ingestion (M05). | Data Collection Lead, Robotics Data Engineer | M02/M03 | M05, M18 (coverage), M16 |
| M05 | Data Ingestion & Quality Management | Data Factory | Ingest manifests/sessions (batch + collection streaming), immutable raw recordings, intake validation, quality rules/status, quarantine, remediation tasks. Excludes: alignment (M06). | Robotics Data Engineer, Data Collection Lead | M04, M02 (storage bindings), SVC-INGEST | M06, M08, M15, M18 |
| M06 | Alignment, Episode & Segmentation Management | Data Factory | Alignment tolerance policies, canonical episodes, lineage to raw, segmentation (manual+assisted), scenario tags, taxonomies. Excludes: labels/QA (M07). | Robotics Data Engineer, Annotation QA Lead | M05, SVC-ALIGN | M07, M08, M18, M19 |
| M07 | Annotation & QA Management | Data Factory | Annotation projects/tasks, pre-annotation jobs and review, QA sampling/adjudication/agreement metrics, annotation sets, annotation operations report. Excludes: episode creation (M06), snapshot publishing (M08). | Annotator, Annotation QA Lead | M06, M18 (cohorts), SVC-PREANN | M08, M15, M19 |
| M08 | Data Catalog & Dataset Snapshot Governance | Data Factory | Unified asset catalog, scalar search/browse, dataset snapshots (publish/deprecate), dataset cards, usage-restriction enforcement (usage-check), governance flags, audit-visible dataset inventory. Excludes: vector search & distributions (M18). | Robotics Data Engineer, Governance Approver, all readers | M05/M06/M07/M10 (assets) | M11, M13, M16 (usage checks), M18 |
| M09 | Scenario Management | Sim Factory | Scenario/scenario-collection catalog, versions, validation status, links to episodes/failures/gaps, scenario requests, scenario coverage report. Excludes: run execution (M10). | Simulation Engineer, Evaluation Lead | M15 (failures), M18 (gaps) | M10, M13 |
| M10 | Simulation Run & Synthetic Data Management | Sim Factory | Simulation runs, generation requests, synthetic batches, synthetic validation & composition policies, output classification, impact tracking, reruns. Excludes: scenario definitions (M09), snapshot publication (M08). | Simulation Engineer | M09, M16, SVC-SIM | M08, M13, M15 |
| M11 | Training Management | ModelOps | Training plans (dataset binding, restriction validation, approvals) and training runs (status, metrics, artifacts, comparison). Excludes: candidate registration (M12). | Model Engineer | M08 (snapshots, usage checks), M13 (suites), M16/M17, SVC-TRAIN | M12, M18 |
| M12 | Model Registry | ModelOps | Model candidates, versions, lifecycle states, model cards, lineage, baseline/challenger links, release-candidate eligibility gate. Excludes: evaluation execution (M13), release packaging (M14). | Model Engineer, Evaluation Lead, Governance Approver | M11 | M13, M14, M19 |
| M13 | Evaluation Management | ModelOps | Evaluation suites, evaluation runs (offline + simulation), immutable evaluation reports, breakdowns, baselines comparison, trial-evidence records, regression sets, model comparison report. Excludes: failure triage (M15). | Evaluation Lead | M12, M08, M09/M10, SVC-EVAL | M14, M15, M19 |
| M14 | Release Candidate Governance | ModelOps | Release candidates, evidence assembly/freeze, approval decisions, packaging metadata, change re-review, post-release log evidence attachment, release status report. Excludes: OTA/fleet operations (out of scope). | Model Engineer, Governance Approver | M12, M13 | M15, M03 (audit) |
| M15 | Failure Analysis & Closed-Loop Management | ModelOps | Failure records (all sources), triage, clustering (via M18), conversion to remediation work items, verification links, closed-loop report. Excludes: executing remediation (owning modules). | Evaluation Lead, all engineers | M07/M10/M13/M14 (sources) | M04/M05/M07/M09/M11 via M16, M13 (regression sets) |
| M16 | Workflow Orchestration & Work Request Management | Orchestration | Workflow templates (versioned), workflow runs (start/pause/cancel/rerun), task events, output registration, work requests, approvals, queue visibility, service classes. Excludes: quota math (M17), domain logic (owning modules). | All roles, Platform Operator | M17 (admission), M02/M03, SVC-SCHED | All modules (executions), M17 |

Excludes: governed catalog of record (M08), episode playback (M19). | R&D Engineer personas, Program Manager | M05–M08, M10, M13, M15 (sources), SVC-INDEX, SVC-ANALYTICS | M04 (coverage), M07 (cohorts→tasks), M09 (gaps), M15 (clusters), M19 |
| M19 | Integrated 4D Workbench | Cross-Cutting | Synchronized 4D review sessions (video, point cloud, robot state, actions, annotations, predictions, eval outcomes), status overlays, comments, review decisions routed to owning modules, failure tagging, side-by-side comparison, saved views. Excludes: owning any reviewed asset. | All engineering personas | M05/M06/M07/M10/M12/M13 (content), M18 (visual aggregates) | M07, M13, M15 (decisions) |
| M20 | Notification & Alerting | Cross-Cutting | In-app notification center, user preferences, alert fan-out from alert rules (M17) and module events, email channel. Excludes: alert rule definitions (M17). | All roles | All modules (events), SVC-NOTIFY | Users |

### 1.4 Global Design Conventions (Normative Contract Pack)

All module interface, data, permission, and logging designs in Chapter 2 MUST follow these conventions. They are defined once here and referenced everywhere ("per §1.4").

#### 1.4.1 API style

- Protocol: HTTPS REST + JSON. Public base path `/api/v1`; service-to-service base path `/internal/v1` (mTLS, service identity, never exposed at the edge gateway).
- Resource naming: plural kebab-case path segments; resource IDs are type-prefixed ULIDs (`cmp-…`, `rec-…`, per Table 0.3).
- Custom (non-CRUD) actions use the `:verb` suffix form: `POST /api/v1/dataset-snapshots/{snapshot_id}:publish`.
- Scoping pattern: platform-scope `/api/v1/admin/...`; tenant-scope `/api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/...`; project-scope `/api/v1/projects/{project_id}/...`; instance routes `/api/v1/{resource}/{id}` (tenant/project derived from the instance, authorization re-checked).
- JSON fields snake_case; timestamps RFC 3339 UTC (`2026-06-11T08:00:00Z`); enums lower_snake strings; all list responses `{"items": [...], "next_page_token": string|null, "total_size": int|null}`.
- Pagination: `page_size` (default 50, max 500) + `page_token` query params. Filtering: documented per endpoint; common params `q`, `status`, `created_after`, `created_before`, `owner_id`, `sort` (`field` or `-field`).

#### 1.4.2 Authentication & authorization (binding to M01/M03)

- Every `/api/v1` request carries `Authorization: Bearer {session_token}` (opaque token from M01). The gateway calls M01 introspection (`POST /internal/v1/auth/introspect`), then injects trusted headers `X-User-Id`, `X-Session-Id`, `X-Active-Tenant-Id` to backend modules. Modules MUST NOT trust client-supplied copies of these headers.
- Every handler performs `POST /internal/v1/authz/check` (M03) with `{user_id, permission_key, scope: {type, id}, resource_ref?}` before business logic; asset-level restriction checks (`E_RESTRICTED_DATA`) are additional deny-wins gates per §0.5.3.
- Service-to-service calls authenticate by service identity (mTLS SAN); service identities hold service-scoped permission keys (M03 §2.3.5).

#### 1.4.3 Error envelope & shared error codes

Error responses: appropriate HTTP status + body `{"error": {"code": "E_...", "message": "human readable", "details": [...], "trace_id": "..."}}`.

Shared codes (modules add module-specific codes prefixed `E_{MODULE}_`): `E_UNAUTHENTICATED` (401), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (403), `E_TENANT_SUSPENDED` (403), `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` (403), `E_NOT_FOUND` (404), `E_CONFLICT` (409), `E_STATE_INVALID` (409, illegal status transition), `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED` (412, ETag mismatch), `E_VALIDATION` (422, field errors in `details[]`), `E_RATE_LIMITED` (429), `E_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` (429), `E_IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT` (409, same key different payload), `E_INTERNAL` (500), `E_UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE` (503).

#### 1.4.4 Idempotency, concurrency, transactions

- All state-creating `POST` endpoints accept an `Idempotency-Key` header (UUID, retained 24 h). Replay with identical payload returns the original response with `Idempotency-Replayed: true`; replay with a different payload returns `E_IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT`.
- All mutable resources carry a `version` integer and `ETag`; `PATCH/PUT/DELETE` require `If-Match` (else 428); mismatch returns `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED` (optimistic locking).
- Status transitions are validated against the owning module's state machine; illegal transitions return `E_STATE_INVALID` with `details.allowed_transitions`.
- Cross-module side effects use the transactional outbox pattern (write business row + outbox event in one local transaction; SVC relays deliver at-least-once; consumers are idempotent by event id). No distributed two-phase commits anywhere in the platform.

#### 1.4.5 Eventing & async operations

- Long-running operations return `202` with a `workflow_run_ref` (M16) or job resource; progress is polled on that resource. No bare fire-and-forget endpoints.
- Domain events are JSON envelopes `{event_id (ulid), event_type ("module.entity.verb"), occurred_at, tenant_id, project_id?, actor: {type: user|service, id}, subject: {type, id}, payload, trace_id}` on the platform event bus, fed by outbox relays. Event types referenced in module designs use this exact shape.

#### 1.4.6 Audit & logging binding (per M03 + §2.x.6 of every module)

- Every governance-relevant action (catalog in M03 §2.3.6) MUST emit an Audit Event via `POST /internal/v1/audit-events` (batched) with `{action, subject, actor, scope, before_summary?, after_summary?, decision?, reason?}`.
- Application logs are structured JSON per the shared schema: `{ts, level, service, module, trace_id, span_id, user_id?, tenant_id?, action, message, payload_summary?, result, latency_ms?, error_code?}`. Levels: DEBUG (developer diagnostics, off in production by default), INFO (state transitions, successful requests), WARN (recoverable anomalies, retries, quota warnings), ERROR (failed requests, task failures), FATAL (service cannot continue). Sensitive-data masking per §1.4.7 applies to all fields.
- Trace context: W3C `traceparent` propagated through gateway → modules → backend services → workflow tasks.

#### 1.4.7 Sensitive-data handling in logs and responses

Never log: session tokens, login codes (even hashed), credential secrets of logical resource bindings, signed URLs. Mask emails to `s***@domain` in non-audit logs; asset payloads logged only as `{asset_id, byte_size, checksum}` summaries; PII-bearing free-text (instructions, comments) logged by reference id only. Audit events store actor/subject ids, never secrets.

#### 1.4.8 Permission model binding

Permission keys follow `{domain}.{resource}:{action}` with actions drawn from `create|read|update|delete|execute|approve|admin` (e.g., `data.snapshot:approve`, `ops.quota:update`). The complete atomic catalog is owned by M03 (§2.3.5); each module's §2.x.5 lists only its own keys and the default grants for the four built-in roles: **PA** = Platform Administrator (implicit `*:*` platform-wide), **TA** = Tenant Administrator (implicit `*:*` within tenant, except platform-admin domain), **OE** = Operations Engineer (infrastructure domains platform-wide, no business domains), **RD** = R&D Engineer (use-level infra permissions + business permissions within assigned projects).

### 1.5 Interface Contract Index (Agent A Contract)

Authoritative endpoint registry. Chapter 2 module designs elaborate request/response schemas and processing logic for exactly these endpoints — names and paths MUST NOT drift. (i) marks internal service-to-service interfaces.

**M01 Platform Login & Session Management**

| # | Method & Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| M01-1 | `POST /api/v1/auth/login-codes` | Request one-time login code for a pre-registered email |
| M01-2 | `POST /api/v1/auth/sessions` | Verify email+code, create session |
| M01-3 | `GET /api/v1/auth/sessions/current` | Current session: user, tenants, roles, effective permission summary |
| M01-4 | `PUT /api/v1/auth/sessions/current/active-tenant` | Switch active tenant context |
| M01-5 | `DELETE /api/v1/auth/sessions/current` | Logout (revoke session) |
| M01-6 | `DELETE /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/sessions` | Admin: revoke all sessions of a user |
| M01-7 (i) | `POST /internal/v1/auth/introspect` | Gateway session introspection |

**M02 Tenant & Resource Management**

| # | Method & Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| M02-1 | `POST /api/v1/admin/tenants` · `GET /api/v1/admin/tenants` | Create / list tenants |
| M02-2 | `GET /api/v1/admin/tenants/{tenant_id}` · `PATCH` · `POST ...:suspend` · `POST ...:reactivate` | Tenant detail / update / lifecycle |
| M02-3 | `POST /api/v1/admin/resources` · `GET /api/v1/admin/resources` | Register / list platform resource inventory (physical machine, logical compute service, physical volume, logical storage service) |
| M02-4 | `GET /api/v1/admin/resources/{resource_id}` · `PATCH` · `POST ...:decommission` | Resource detail / update / decommission |
| M02-5 | `POST /api/v1/admin/tenants/{tenant_id}/resource-bindings` · `GET` (same path) | Bind resource to tenant / list bindings (admin view) |
| M02-6 | `DELETE /api/v1/admin/tenants/{tenant_id}/resource-bindings/{binding_id}` | Unbind resource |
| M02-7 | `GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}` · `GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/resource-bindings` | Tenant self view + its bindings (TA/RD read) |
| M02-8 | `POST /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/projects` · `GET` (same path) | Create / list projects |
| M02-9 | `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}` · `PATCH` · `POST ...:archive` | Project detail / update / archive |
| M02-10 | `PUT /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/members/{user_id}` · `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/members` · `DELETE .../members/{user_id}` | Manage project membership (persona template assignment) |

|---|---|---|

| M03-2 | `GET /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}` · `PATCH` · `POST ...:deactivate` · `POST ...:reactivate` | User detail / update / lifecycle |

| M03-5 | `GET /api/v1/roles/{role_id}` · `PATCH` · `DELETE` | Role detail / recompose / delete (custom only) |

| M03-7 | `GET /api/v1/users/{user_id}/effective-permissions?scope_type&scope_id` | Effective permission resolution (debug/UI) |
| M03-8 | `GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/audit-events` · `GET /api/v1/admin/audit-events` | Audit query (tenant / platform) |
| M03-9 | `POST /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/access-reviews` · `GET` (same) · `GET /api/v1/access-reviews/{review_id}` | Access review reports for restricted datasets & release artifacts (RPT-11) |
| M03-10 (i) | `POST /internal/v1/authz/check` · `POST /internal/v1/authz/batch-check` | Permission decision point |
| M03-11 (i) | `POST /internal/v1/audit-events` | Append-only audit ingestion (batch) |

### 1.6 Boundary Resolutions (Zero-Ambiguity Record)

1. **Login vs. Tenancy vs. RBAC (mandated blur).** M01 only proves identity and issues sessions. M02 only defines containers (tenant, project) and infrastructure bindings. M03 only maps identities to atomic permissions within containers and records audit. Runtime order per request: introspect (M01) → scope resolution (M02 data) → permission check (M03) → asset restriction check (M08 for data assets). No module duplicates another's check.
2. **M08 vs. M18 (DA-15).** M08 is the *system of record and governance gate* (what exists, who may use it); M18 is the *computation layer* (how to find/understand it). M08-1 scalar search answers exact-filter queries from the catalog DB; any query with a vector/similarity component is delegated to M18-1 and results re-checked against M08 governance flags before return.
3. **M16 vs. M17.** M16 decides *what runs and in which order* (DAG, control verbs, approvals); M17 decides *whether and where it may run* (admission, quota, priority policy) and *what it consumed*. The single touchpoint is M17-9 admission check + M17-10 metering.
4. **M19 vs. owning modules.** Workbench writes nothing to reviewed assets directly; every decision flows through M19-5/M19-6 into M07/M13/M15 official interfaces, preserving single-writer ownership and audit (FR-X1.8).
5. **M15 vs. sources.** Failure *detection* stays in source modules (M07 QA, M10 sim diagnostics, M13 eval, M14 post-release); failure *management* (record, triage, cluster, convert, verify) is exclusively M15.
6. **M12 vs. M14.** M12 governs the model candidate lifecycle up to `release_candidate` eligibility; M14 owns the release candidate object, evidence freeze, and approval workflow. The M14-1 gate enforces FR-C3.7 by querying M12 lineage completeness.

### 1.7 Zero-Omission Coverage Matrices

#### 1.7.1 FRD requirement family → owning module

| FRD family | Module(s) | | FRD family | Module(s) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1.1–A1.5 | M04 | | C1.1–C1.6 | M11 |
| A2.1–A2.7 | M05 | | C2.1–C2.6 | M11 |
| A3.1–A3.6 | M05 | | C3.1–C3.7 | M12 |
| A4.1–A4.7 | M06 | | C4.1–C4.9 | M13 (C4.6 with M19) |
| A5.1–A5.6 | M06 | | C5.1–C5.7 | M14 |
| A6.1–A6.9 | M07 | | C6.1–C6.5 | M15 (C6.5 with M13) |
| A7.1–A7.6 | M08 (A7.4/A7.6 with M18) | | D1.1–D1.7 | M16 |
| A8.1–A8.8 | M08 | | D2.1–D2.6 | M16 |
| B1.1–B1.5 | M09 | | D3.1–D3.6 | M17 |
| B2.1–B2.6 | M10 | | D4.1–D4.6 | M17 (alerts delivery M20) |
| B3.1–B3.6 | M10 | | D5.1–D5.7 | M02/M03 (D5.7 retention with SVC-15) |
| B4.1–B4.5 | M10 | | X1.1–X1.8 | M19 |
| B5.1–B5.4 | M09/M15 (B5.4 report M09-7) | | Mandate: Login / Tenant / Roles / Analytics | M01 / M02 / M03 / M18 |

#### 1.7.2 Business rule → enforcing module(s)

BR-01 M05 (immutability) · BR-02 M06/M08 (lineage) · BR-03 M10/M08 (synthetic marking) · BR-04 M08 (snapshot immutability) · BR-05 M08-9 usage gate (called by M11/M13/M16) · BR-06 M07 (pre-annotation policy) · BR-07 M14 (RC completeness) · BR-08 M13 (report immutability) · BR-09 M12/M14 (promotion gate) · BR-10 M15 (triage→work items) · BR-11 M10 (synthetic deprecation) · BR-12 M16/M17 (quota & approval) · BR-13 M03 (least privilege) · BR-14 M03 (audit retention).

#### 1.7.3 Report (FRD §12) → owning module & endpoint

RPT-01 M05-11 · RPT-02 M08-10 · RPT-03 M18-9 · RPT-04 M07-13 · RPT-05 M09-7 · RPT-06 M11-8 · RPT-07 M13-7 · RPT-08 M14-9 · RPT-09 M15-7 · RPT-10 M17-8 · RPT-11 M03-9.

#### 1.7.4 Acceptance criteria (FRD §13) → module route

AC-01 M04+M05 · AC-02 M06+M19 · AC-03 M07 · AC-04 M08 · AC-05 M08-1/M18-1 · AC-06 M09+M10 · AC-07 M10+M08 · AC-08 M11 · AC-09 M12 · AC-10 M13 · AC-11 M19+M15 · AC-12 M14 · AC-13 M16-7/M17-3/M17-5 · AC-14 M03-8/M03-9.

---

## 2. Module Designs

### 2.1 M01 — Platform Login & Session Management

#### 2.1.1 Module Overview

**Module ID & name:** M01 — Platform Login & Session Management.

**Scope boundary.**
- *Owns:* the email one-time-code (OTP) authentication flow, login-code issuance/verification lifecycle, session records and lifecycle (creation, sliding idle expiry, absolute expiry, revocation), active-tenant context on the session, gateway session introspection, and login/session audit hooks.
- *Excludes:* user account CRUD and lifecycle (M03 §2.3.3 (M03-1/M03-2)), permission evaluation (M03 §2.3.3 (M03-10)), tenant/project containers (M02), email transport itself (SVC-14 OTP Mailer), and any password, self-registration, or third-party IdP mechanism (out of scope per DA-03).

**User roles & personas.** All authenticated users of every built-in role (PA/TA/OE/RD) and every persona pass through M01. M01-6 (admin session revocation) is used by PA only, typically from the M03 user-detail screen. No persona-specific behavior exists at login; persona effects begin after authentication.

**Upstream/downstream dependencies.**
- Upstream: M03 §2.3.3 (M03-1/M03-2 data) — user records (email, status) read by issuance/verification; M03 §2.3.3 (M03-10) — used indirectly when assembling the M01-3 permission summary; SVC-14 OTP Mailer — delivers code emails from the outbox.
- Downstream: every module — receives trusted session context (`X-User-Id`, `X-Session-Id`, `X-Active-Tenant-Id`) injected by the gateway after M01-7 introspection per §1.4.2; M03 §2.3.3 (M03-11) — receives M01 audit events via SVC-13 Audit Event Shipper; M03 consumes the `auth.user.first_login` event to transition users invited→active.

**FRD/mandate coverage:** Mandated login module (email one-time code, pre-registered email prerequisite — §0.2.3 item 1, BP-M1); DA-03 (no passwords/SSO in v1), DA-17 (corporate SMTP relay); FR-D5.3/FR-D5.4 partial (session access control + authentication audit trail); research findings r1 §0.7.1 #1–#4 (NIST SP 800-63B OTP and session parameters, AWS hash-at-rest OTP storage).

**Documented limitation (r1 #1, binding design note).** Per NIST SP 800-63B §5.1.3.1, email delivery does not prove possession of a specific device; email OTP is therefore treated as a single-factor, AAL1-class login acceptable for this internal/B2B platform. Compensating controls in this design: short session lifetimes (≤1 h idle, ≤24 h absolute), strict throttling, single-use ≥20-bit codes, and full authentication audit. The documented step-up path (enterprise SSO/OIDC or TOTP second factor for privileged operations such as tenant resource administration) is explicitly deferred beyond v1 per DA-03; no v1 feature implements it.

#### 2.1.2 Functional & UI/UX Design

**Primary functions.**
- Request a one-time login code for a pre-registered corporate email (mandate; BP-M1; DA-03). Unregistered emails receive an indistinguishable response (anti-enumeration, mandate).
- Verify email + code and establish a session with an active-tenant context (mandate; FR-D5.1 context binding).
- Inspect current session: user identity, tenant memberships, role bindings, effective permission summary (supports BR-13 least-privilege transparency).
- Switch active tenant within an existing session (FR-D5.1).
- Logout (self) and administrative revocation of all sessions of a user (FR-D5.3; used by M03-2 deactivation).
- Gateway introspection for every API call (§1.4.2).
- Emit authentication audit events (FR-D5.4, BR-14) via M03 §2.3.3 (M03-11).

**Login-code policy (mandated, normative).** Code format: **8-digit numeric** (00000000–99999999), chosen over the 6-char alphanumeric alternative because it is unambiguous to read from email and types faster on all keyboards; entropy log2(10^8) ≈ 26.6 bits ≥ the mandated 20-bit minimum (r1 #2). Generated by CSPRNG. Validity **10 minutes** (NIST ceiling). **Single-use**: consumed on first successful verification. **Max 5 verification attempts** per code, then the code transitions to `expired`. Requesting a new code supersedes (expires) all prior outstanding codes for that email — at most one live code per email. Stored **only as a salted keyed hash** (`HMAC-SHA-256(pepper, salt || code)`, 16-byte random salt per code, pepper held in the platform KMS/secret manager, never in the database) per r1 #3; a database leak exposes no usable codes. Codes are never logged (§1.4.7).

**Throttling policy (normative summary).** All limits are sliding-window, enforced server-side; client UI mirrors them with countdowns.

| # | Control | Scope | Limit | On breach | Enumeration-safe because |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| T1 | Code-issue abuse limit | source IP/device | 10 requests / hour | 429 `E_RATE_LIMITED` (with `Retry-After`) | applied before user lookup, identical for registered and unregistered emails |
| T2 | Code-issue per email | email (silent) | 3 / 15 min and 10 / 24 h | 202 returned, no mail sent | response body and status identical to the success path |
| T3 | Verify attempts per code | login code | 5 attempts | code → `expired (attempts_exhausted)`; uniform 401 | error indistinguishable from wrong/expired/unknown |
| T4 | Verify backoff per email | email | after 5 consecutive failures: 30 s × 2^n, cap 15 min | 429 `E_RATE_LIMITED` | keyed on submitted email regardless of existence |
| T5 | NIST ceiling guard | account | hard stop ≪ 100 consecutive failures (T3×T4 compose to ~25/h max) | — | per r1 #2 (§5.2.2) |

**Session policy (normative).** Session token: opaque 256-bit CSPRNG value, returned once at creation; server stores only its SHA-256 hash. Idle (inactivity) timeout **60 minutes**, sliding — refreshed on introspection, persisted at most once per 60 s to bound write amplification. Absolute lifetime **24 hours**; after either expiry the user re-authenticates via OTP (r1 #4, AAL2-style lifetimes adopted deliberately for an infrastructure-control platform; both values deployment-configurable downward only). Logout and admin revocation revoke server-side immediately; revocation latency to the gateway is bounded by the 60 s introspection-cache TTL plus the explicit cache purge (effective immediately in the common path). Multiple concurrent sessions per user are permitted; M01-6 revokes all.

**End-to-end flow (BP-M1 binding).**
1. PA pre-registers `jane.doe@company.com` via M03 §2.3.3 (M03-1) and binds roles (M03-6) — without this step every M01-1 call for that address is silently absorbed (anti-enumeration 202).
2. User opens `/login`, submits email → M01-1 → 202; SVC-14 OTP Mailer delivers the 8-digit code.
3. User submits code → M01-2 → session created; `invited→active` first-login transition propagates to M03 via the `auth.user.first_login` outbox event.
4. Every subsequent API call: gateway → M01-7 introspection → trusted headers per §1.4.2 → module authz via M03 §2.3.3 (M03-10).
5. Tenant switch (M01-4) re-anchors `X-Active-Tenant-Id`; logout (M01-5) or admin revoke (M01-6) terminates.

**State machines (exact).**
- Login code: `issued → consumed | expired` (expired by: TTL lapse, 5th failed attempt, supersession by newer code, user deactivation).
- Session: `active → expired | revoked` (expired by idle or absolute timeout; revoked by M01-5, M01-6, or M03-2 deactivation cascade).

##### UI page 1 — Sign-in (email step), route `/login`

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation rules |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| email | text (email) | RFC 5322 addr-spec, ≤254 chars | empty (or last-used email from localStorage) | name@company.com | Required; real-time format check on blur; lowercased before submit; no domain hint shown (anti-enumeration) |

- **Validation logic:** real-time — `email` format on blur with inline message "Enter a valid email address."; on submit — re-validate, trim, lowercase. No check of registration status client-side (impossible by design). Input sanitized (trim, NFC normalize); no HTML ever rendered from input.
- **API binding:** "Send code" button → `POST /api/v1/auth/login-codes` (M01-1).
- **Output rendering:** success (always 202) → advance to the code step with neutral text "If this email is registered, a login code has been sent. It expires in 10 minutes."; loading — button spinner + disabled form (no skeleton; single field); the message and timing are identical whether or not the email is registered.
- **Exception handling:** network failure → toast "Network error. Check your connection and retry." with Retry; `E_RATE_LIMITED` (429) → button disabled with countdown from `Retry-After`, message "Too many requests. Try again in {n}s."; `E_VALIDATION` (422) → field error. `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` cannot occur (anonymous endpoint).

##### UI page 2 — Code verification, route `/login/verify`

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation rules |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| email | text (read-only) | as entered in step 1 | carried from step 1 | — | Immutable on this screen; "Change" link returns to step 1 |
| code | one-time-code input (8 boxes) | exactly 8 digits `^[0-9]{8}$` | empty | 8 separated digit boxes | Required; digits only (paste of 8-digit string supported, whitespace stripped); auto-submit when 8th digit entered |

- **Validation logic:** real-time — non-digits rejected at keystroke; on submit — length check, error "Enter the 8-digit code from your email." Failed verification renders one uniform message regardless of cause (wrong code / expired / consumed / attempts exhausted / unknown email): **"This code is invalid or has expired. Request a new code."** — deliberate anti-enumeration uniformity. Remaining-attempt counts are never displayed.
- **API binding:** auto-submit / "Verify" → `POST /api/v1/auth/sessions` (M01-2); "Resend code" → `POST /api/v1/auth/login-codes` (M01-1), disabled for 60 s after each send with visible countdown; a 10-minute expiry countdown is shown.
- **Output rendering:** success (201) → store session token (httpOnly secure cookie set by gateway), toast "Signed in", redirect to the workspace home of the active tenant; if the user belongs to multiple tenants and no last-used tenant exists, open the tenant-switcher dialog (page 3) first. Loading — code boxes locked + inline spinner.
- **Exception handling:** `E_AUTH_CODE_INVALID` (401) → uniform message above, code boxes cleared, focus reset; `E_RATE_LIMITED` (429) → "Too many attempts. Try again in {n}s." with disabled input and countdown; network failure → inline retry banner preserving entered state; `E_VALIDATION` → field error.

##### UI page 3 — Session & tenant context (header component + `/account/session`)

The persistent app header shows the active tenant name and a switcher; the session page shows session details and a sign-out button.

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation rules |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| active_tenant | single-select | tenant list from M01-3 (`tnt-` ids, display names, status badges) | current `active_tenant_id` | Select tenant | Must be one of the user's assigned, non-archived tenants; suspended tenants listed but disabled with badge "suspended" |

- **Validation logic:** on-submit only; client prevents selecting suspended/archived tenants (server re-validates).
- **API binding:** header mount → `GET /api/v1/auth/sessions/current` (M01-3); tenant selection → `PUT /api/v1/auth/sessions/current/active-tenant` (M01-4) then full data reload; "Sign out" → `DELETE /api/v1/auth/sessions/current` (M01-5) then redirect to `/login`.
- **Output rendering:** M01-3 renders user chip (name, masked email `s***@domain` style is NOT used in UI — full own email shown to self), tenant list with role badges per tenant, expiry hint ("Session expires at {absolute} or after 60 min inactivity"); loading skeleton — header shows shimmer chip; empty state — user with zero tenant assignments sees "No tenant assigned yet. Contact your platform administrator." with all tenant-scoped navigation hidden.
- **Exception handling:** `E_UNAUTHENTICATED` anywhere → silent redirect to `/login` with toast "Your session has expired. Sign in again."; `E_AUTH_TENANT_NOT_ASSIGNED` (403) on switch → toast "You no longer have access to this tenant." and list refresh; `E_TENANT_SUSPENDED` → toast "This tenant is suspended."; network failure → keep prior context, banner "Reconnecting…".

##### UI element 4 — Admin "Revoke all sessions" (embedded in M03 user-detail page)

Button "Revoke all sessions" with confirm dialog ("This signs {display_name} out of every device. Continue?"). API binding: `DELETE /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/sessions` (M01-6). Success toast "{n} session(s) revoked." Errors: `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → toast "You do not have permission to revoke sessions."; `E_NOT_FOUND` → "User not found."

#### 2.1.3 Interface Design

##### M01-1 Request login code
- **Method/path:** `POST /api/v1/auth/login-codes` (anonymous; gateway-exempt from session auth).
- **Request body:**
```json
{"email": "string (RFC5322, <=254, lowercased)"}
```
- **Processing logic:**
  1. Validate format → else 422 `E_VALIDATION`.
  2. Enforce per-IP/device abuse throttle (10 requests / IP / hour, sliding). Exceeded → 429 `E_RATE_LIMITED`. This limit is independent of registration status, so it leaks nothing.
  3. Look up user by lowercased email in the M01-local replica of `iam.users` (read-only view). **All subsequent branches return the identical 202 body** (mandate: M01-1 always returns 202).
  4. If user not found, or status `deactivated`: perform a dummy HMAC computation (timing uniformity), log WARN with masked email, emit no mail. Return 202.
  5. If found (`invited` or `active`): enforce silent per-email issue throttle (≤3 codes/15 min, ≤10 codes/24 h). If exceeded: return 202 without sending (identical body — per-email limits never surface as errors, preventing an enumeration oracle via 429 differences).
  6. Transaction: expire all `issued` codes for this user (`reason=superseded`); generate 8-digit CSPRNG code; insert `auth.login_codes` row (salt, `code_hash`, `expires_at = now()+10min`, `attempt_count=0`, status `issued`); write outbox event `auth.otp_mail.requested` (payload: user_id, mail template ref, code passed to SVC-14 via the outbox payload encrypted with the mailer's KMS key — never logged). Commit.
  7. SVC-14 OTP Mailer consumes outbox, sends mail via corporate SMTP/ESP relay (DA-17), records send status.
  8. Emit audit `auth.login_code.requested` (actor = anonymous, subject = usr- id; only for registered users).
- **Response:** always `202` →
```json
{"status": "accepted", "message": "If the email is registered, a login code has been sent.", "resend_available_in_s": 60}
```
- **Errors:** `E_VALIDATION` (422), `E_RATE_LIMITED` (429, IP/device scope only). Module codes: none beyond shared.
- **Idempotency & concurrency:** no `Idempotency-Key` (anonymous); natural idempotency via supersession — repeated calls leave exactly one live code. Supersede+insert in one transaction; unique partial index forbids two `issued` codes per user.
- **Permission key:** none (anonymous).

##### M01-2 Verify code, create session
- **Method/path:** `POST /api/v1/auth/sessions` (anonymous).
- **Request body:**
```json
{"email": "string", "code": "string (^[0-9]{8}$)", "client": {"user_agent": "string?", "device_label": "string?"}}
```
- **Processing logic:**
  1. Validate shape → 422 `E_VALIDATION`.
  2. Per-email verification backoff check (after 5 consecutive failed verifications across codes: exponential backoff 30 s × 2^n, capped 15 min — well below the NIST 100-attempt account ceiling, r1 #2) → 429 `E_RATE_LIMITED` if in backoff.
  3. Transaction: `SELECT … FOR UPDATE` the single `issued` code row for the user (resolved via email). If no user, no row, status ≠ `issued`, or `expires_at < now()` → release, increment failure counters, return uniform 401 `E_AUTH_CODE_INVALID` (constant-time hash comparison performed even when no row exists).
  4. Compute `HMAC-SHA-256(pepper, salt || submitted_code)`; constant-time compare. Mismatch → `attempt_count += 1`; if `attempt_count >= 5` set status `expired` (`reason=attempts_exhausted`). Commit; return 401 `E_AUTH_CODE_INVALID`.
  5. Match → set code status `consumed` (`consumed_at`); create `auth.sessions` row: `ses-{ulid}`, `token_hash`, `idle_expires_at = now()+60min`, `absolute_expires_at = now()+24h`, `active_tenant_id` = last-used tenant if still assigned, else the single assigned tenant, else `null`; write outbox events `auth.login.succeeded` and, if user status is `invited`, `auth.user.first_login` (M03 consumes → invited→active). Commit (single transaction boundary for steps 3–5).
  6. Reset the email's failure backoff counter.
- **Response:** `201` →
```json
{"session_token": "opaque-256bit (returned once)", "session_id": "ses-01J…", "idle_expires_at": "RFC3339", "absolute_expires_at": "RFC3339",
 "user": {"user_id": "usr-01J…", "display_name": "string", "email": "string", "status": "active"},
 "active_tenant_id": "tnt-01J…|null", "tenant_selection_required": false}
```
- **Errors:** `E_AUTH_CODE_INVALID` (401, deliberately covers unknown email / wrong / expired / consumed / attempts-exhausted / deactivated user — indistinguishable), `E_RATE_LIMITED` (429), `E_VALIDATION` (422).
- **Idempotency & concurrency:** single-use codes make replays fail closed (second submit of the same code → `E_AUTH_CODE_INVALID`); row lock serializes concurrent verifications of one code; no `Idempotency-Key`.
- **Permission key:** none (anonymous; the code itself is the credential).

##### M01-3 Current session
- **Method/path:** `GET /api/v1/auth/sessions/current`.
- **Processing:** resolve session from bearer token; assemble user record (M03 replica), tenant memberships with non-archived tenants (M02 data via internal read), role bindings per tenant (M03), and an effective-permission summary for the active tenant via M03 §2.3.3 (M03-10 batch-check of the UI's capability probe set). Cached 30 s per session.
- **Response:** `200` →
```json
{"session": {"session_id": "ses-…", "created_at": "RFC3339", "idle_expires_at": "RFC3339", "absolute_expires_at": "RFC3339"},
 "user": {"user_id": "usr-…", "email": "string", "display_name": "string", "status": "active"},
 "active_tenant_id": "tnt-…|null",
 "tenants": [{"tenant_id": "tnt-…", "name": "string", "status": "active|suspended", "roles": ["tenant_administrator"], "projects": [{"project_id": "prj-…", "name": "string", "persona_templates": ["persona.model_engineer"]}]}],
 "permission_summary": {"scope": {"type": "tenant", "id": "tnt-…"}, "allowed_keys": ["data.snapshot:read", "…"]}}
```
- **Errors:** `E_UNAUTHENTICATED` (401). **Idempotency:** read-only. **Permission key:** authenticated session only.

##### M01-4 Switch active tenant
- **Method/path:** `PUT /api/v1/auth/sessions/current/active-tenant`.
- **Request body:** ```json
{"tenant_id": "tnt-01J…"}
```
- **Processing:** 1. Validate tenant exists and status `active` (M02 replica) → 404 `E_NOT_FOUND` / 403 `E_TENANT_SUSPENDED`. 2. Verify user holds ≥1 role binding at that tenant or a project within it (M03 read) → else 403 `E_AUTH_TENANT_NOT_ASSIGNED`. 3. Update `auth.sessions.active_tenant_id` + `last_used_tenant_id` on the user profile; invalidate the introspection cache entry for this token. 4. Audit `auth.session.tenant_switched`.
- **Response:** `200` `{"active_tenant_id": "tnt-…"}`. **Errors:** `E_AUTH_TENANT_NOT_ASSIGNED` (403, module code), shared codes. **Idempotency:** naturally idempotent (last-write-wins on one session row). **Permission key:** authenticated session only.

##### M01-5 Logout
- **Method/path:** `DELETE /api/v1/auth/sessions/current`.
- **Processing:** set session status `revoked` (`revoked_reason=logout`), purge introspection cache, audit `auth.session.revoked`. Already revoked/expired → still 204 (idempotent).
- **Response:** `204`. **Permission key:** authenticated session only.

##### M01-6 Admin: revoke all sessions of a user
- **Method/path:** `DELETE /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/sessions` (path param: `user_id`, `usr-` ULID).
- **Processing:** 1. Authz check `iam.user_session:delete` at platform scope (M03-10). 2. Update all `active` sessions of the user to `revoked` (`revoked_reason=admin_revoke`), single statement. 3. Cache purge (by user_id). 4. Audit `auth.session.revoked_all` with count. Also invoked service-to-service by M03 on deactivation (service identity).
- **Response:** `200` `{"revoked_count": 3}`. **Errors:** `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND`. **Idempotency:** idempotent (re-run revokes 0). **Permission key:** `iam.user_session:delete`.

##### M01-7 (i) Session introspection
- **Method/path:** `POST /internal/v1/auth/introspect` (mTLS; gateway + service identities only).
- **Request body:** ```json
{"session_token": "opaque"}
```
- **Processing:** 1. SHA-256 the token; look up in Redis read-through cache (TTL 60 s) → PostgreSQL on miss. 2. Evaluate status + both expiries; expired → mark `expired` lazily. 3. On success, slide `idle_expires_at = now()+60min` (persisted at most once per 60 s). 4. Return identity context. p99 target ≤ 10 ms.
- **Response:** `200` →
```json
{"active": true, "user_id": "usr-…", "session_id": "ses-…", "active_tenant_id": "tnt-…|null", "user_status": "active", "idle_expires_at": "RFC3339", "absolute_expires_at": "RFC3339"}
```
  or `{"active": false, "reason": "expired|revoked|unknown"}` (still HTTP 200; the gateway converts to 401 `E_UNAUTHENTICATED`).
- **Permission key:** service identity `iam.session_introspect:execute` (mTLS SAN allowlist).

**Module error codes:** `E_AUTH_CODE_INVALID` (401), `E_AUTH_TENANT_NOT_ASSIGNED` (403); plus shared §1.4.3 codes.

#### 2.1.4 Data Design

**Storage choice:** PostgreSQL (platform default for relational control-plane data), schema `auth`; Redis as bounded cache for introspection (TTL 60 s, explicit delete on revoke/switch) and for sliding-window throttle counters (native TTL). Rationale: sessions and codes are low-volume, strongly consistent control-plane rows; revocation must be immediate and transactional — cache is acceleration only, never authority.

**Entities (ER-style).**

`auth.login_codes` — Login Code (state: issued→consumed|expired)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| code_id | text PK | `lgc-{ulid}` (module-local id; never shown to users) |
| user_id | text FK→iam.users | NOT NULL, indexed |
| code_hash | bytea | NOT NULL; HMAC-SHA-256(pepper, salt‖code); plaintext never stored |
| salt | bytea(16) | NOT NULL, CSPRNG |
| status | enum(issued, consumed, expired) | NOT NULL default issued |
| expire_reason | enum(ttl, superseded, attempts_exhausted, user_deactivated) | NULL unless expired |
| attempt_count | smallint | NOT NULL default 0, CHECK ≤ 5 |
| expires_at / consumed_at / created_at | timestamptz | expires_at NOT NULL (=created_at+10 min) |
| request_ip_hash | bytea | salted hash for abuse forensics; raw IP not stored |
Unique partial index: `(user_id) WHERE status='issued'` (max one live code per user).

`auth.sessions` — Session (state: active→expired|revoked)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| session_id | text PK | `ses-{ulid}` |
| user_id | text FK→iam.users | NOT NULL, indexed |
| token_hash | bytea UNIQUE | SHA-256 of opaque token; token itself never stored |
| status | enum(active, expired, revoked) | NOT NULL default active |
| revoked_reason | enum(logout, admin_revoke, user_deactivated) | NULL unless revoked |
| active_tenant_id | text FK→tenancy.tenants | NULL allowed (no tenant assigned) |
| client_user_agent / client_device_label | text | optional, ≤256 |
| created_at / idle_expires_at / absolute_expires_at / last_seen_at / revoked_at | timestamptz | idle/absolute NOT NULL |

`auth.login_throttle` — per-email verification backoff (email_hash PK, consecutive_failures int, backoff_until timestamptz) — mirrored in Redis; PostgreSQL row is the durable fallback.

Relationships: User 1—N Login Code; User 1—N Session; Session N—1 Tenant (active context). M01 holds read-only replicas/views of `iam.users` (user_id, email, status, last_used_tenant_id) and tenant/membership lookups — single-writer rule preserved (M03/M02 write them).

**Partitioning/sharding:** none required (control-plane volume: ≤10^5 users, ≤10^6 sessions/yr). `auth.login_codes` pruned aggressively instead.

**Retention (per DA-11):** login codes hard-deleted 24 h after terminal state (hashes only anyway); sessions in terminal state retained 90 days for security review then purged (authentication history persists in the audit store per BR-14, which is the long-term record); throttle rows expire with their windows.

**Processing logic.**
- CRUD rules: codes are insert+status-transition only (no updates to hash/salt); sessions never reuse tokens; all transitions validated against the state machines (illegal → `E_STATE_INVALID`); writes to `auth.login_codes`/`auth.sessions` happen only inside the M01-1/M01-2 transactions and the sweepers — no ad-hoc mutation paths.
- Transaction boundaries: M01-1 (supersede prior codes + insert code + outbox mail event) and M01-2 (lock code + consume + create session + outbox audit/first-login events) are each one local transaction; cross-module effects ride the outbox (§1.4.4), never distributed transactions.
- Batch jobs: `code-sweeper` (every minute: TTL-expire `issued` codes past `expires_at`; purge terminal >24 h); `session-sweeper` (every 5 min: mark idle/absolute-expired sessions `expired`; purge terminal >90 d); `outbox-relay` health is SVC-13/SVC-14 concern, monitored via WARN logs on lag.
- Cache + invalidation: introspection cache keyed by token_hash, TTL 60 s (bounded staleness backstop), explicit DELETE on M01-4/5/6 and on the M03 deactivation cascade; throttle counters in Redis with window TTLs; Redis loss degrades to direct PostgreSQL reads (correctness preserved, latency degraded — WARN).
- On `iam.user.deactivated` event (from M03): revoke all active sessions (`revoked_reason=user_deactivated`), expire issued codes (`reason=user_deactivated`), purge cache entries — idempotent consumer keyed by event id.

#### 2.1.5 Permission Design

| Permission key | PA | TA | OE | RD | Persona template notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `iam.user_session:delete` (M01-6) | ✓ (platform) | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | not in any persona template |
| `iam.session_introspect:execute` (M01-7) | service identities only | — | — | — | gateway + backend services via mTLS |
| (M01-1/2 anonymous; M01-3/4/5 any authenticated session) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | all personas |

**Granularity statement:** M01 permissions are platform-level only; sessions are user-scoped objects (a user manages only `current`); tenant scoping enters via `active_tenant_id` but authorization inside tenants is wholly M03's concern (§0.5.3 — "login never grants scope").

**API key & credential scoping:** M01-7 is mTLS-only on `/internal/v1`; the gateway's service identity is the sole caller permitted to introspect arbitrary tokens. The OTP pepper and SVC-14 SMTP credentials live in the platform secret manager; M01 holds read access to the pepper only. Session tokens are bearer credentials — transported only via TLS, stored client-side in an httpOnly secure cookie, never accepted in query strings.

#### 2.1.6 Logging Design

**Levels & module triggers:** DEBUG — code-format validation failures, cache hit/miss ratios (off in production); INFO — code issued (no code value), session created/revoked, tenant switched, introspection sliding-update; WARN — login-code request for unknown/deactivated email (masked), verification failure, throttle activation, mail-send retry (SVC-14 feedback); ERROR — outbox write failure, M03 replica unavailable, introspection store failure; FATAL — pepper/KMS unavailable at startup.

**Structured log schema:** per §1.4.6 base schema plus module fields: `auth_flow` (`code_request|verify|introspect|switch|revoke`), `code_id` (lgc- id, never the code), `session_id`, `throttle_scope` (`ip|email|none`), `attempt_no`.

**Sensitive-data masking (binding):** login codes NEVER appear in any log, event, audit record, or error message — not even hashed (§1.4.7); session tokens never logged (only `session_id`); emails masked to `s***@domain` in all non-audit logs; IPs stored only as salted hashes.

**Shipping:** stdout JSON → node collector → central log store (trace-correlated via W3C `traceparent`); audit events written to the M01 outbox and shipped by SVC-13 Audit Event Shipper to M03 §2.3.3 (M03-11), at-least-once with event-id dedupe.

**Audit events emitted:** `auth.login_code.requested` (registered users only), `auth.login.succeeded`, `auth.login.failed` (uniform reason class `code_invalid`, includes attempt ordinal), `auth.user.first_login`, `auth.session.tenant_switched`, `auth.session.revoked`, `auth.session.revoked_all`.

---

### 2.2 M02 — Tenant & Resource Management

#### 2.2.1 Module Overview

**Module ID & name:** M02 — Tenant & Resource Management.

**Scope boundary.**
- *Owns:* tenant containers and lifecycle; platform resource inventory (the four resource kinds: physical compute, logical compute, physical storage, logical storage); tenant↔resource bindings (the unit that makes a resource usable by a tenant); projects under tenants; project membership records (persona-template assignment); vault references for logical-binding credentials.
- *Excludes:* quota numbers and admission (M17), utilization metering (M17), user identity and role bindings (M03 — M02-10 membership *triggers* M03 materialization but M03 writes its own rows), workflow placement decisions (M16), hardware/service provisioning itself (DA-20 — the platform **brokers and accounts**; it does not provision hardware).

**User roles & personas.** Platform Administrator (tenant CRUD, lifecycle, bindings), Operations Engineer (resource inventory + bindings, platform-wide), Tenant Administrator (own-tenant view, projects, members), R&D Engineer (read own tenant/projects). Personas: Platform Operator works the inventory/binding screens; all project-scoped personas are *assigned* via M02-10.

**Upstream/downstream dependencies.** Upstream: M01 (session context), M03 §2.3.3 (M03-10 authz checks; M03-3 persona-template catalog for M02-10 validation). Downstream: M16 §2.16.3 / M17 §2.17.3 (consume `tenant.resource_binding.*` events to learn where workflows may run and meter), M05 (storage targets for ingest sessions resolve through storage bindings), all project-scoped modules (project existence/status), M03 (consumes `tenant.project.member_*` events to materialize project-scoped role bindings; consumes tenant/project status for scope checks).

**FRD/mandate coverage:** Mandated tenant-configuration module — tenants isolate compute/storage AND business resources; dual-type (physical/logical) bindings for both compute and storage; projects for finer-grained business-resource management (§0.2.3 items 2–3, BP-M2); FR-D5.1 (P0, work organized by tenant/project; DA-02 fixes hierarchy Tenant→Project), FR-D5.5 (P1, project-level ownership/stewardship); DA-20 (broker-and-account binding semantics); research r1 §0.7.1 #5–#9 (namespace-per-tenant, taint/label physical binding, RayCluster-per-tenant, S3 prefix/bucket isolation, token-vended scoped credentials); supports BR-12 and AC-13 (resource scoping basis consumed by M16/M17).

**Isolation model (normative).** A tenant is the hard isolation unit for *both* planes: (a) **infrastructure** — compute and storage reachable by a tenant's workloads are exactly those with an `active` resource binding to that tenant; (b) **business** — every business asset belongs to exactly one project, every project to exactly one tenant (Table 0.3 #21–22); no API ever returns cross-tenant business data. Control-plane mapping per r1 #5/#8: each tenant maps to a dedicated namespace (`namespace_ref` recorded on the tenant; vCluster is the documented escalation path, not v1). Physical compute binding compiles to a tenant-scoped node taint + label (`tenant={tnt-id}:NoSchedule`), with the matching toleration + nodeSelector injected only into that tenant's workloads (r1 #6) — exclusivity is scheduler-enforced, not conventional. Logical compute services (e.g., Ray clusters) are always tenant-dedicated instances; never shared across tenants (r1 #7). Logical storage default is a shared object store with an enforced tenant prefix (`{bucket}/tenants/{tenant_id}/…`); `dedicated_bucket` is the premium isolation tier; all data-plane access uses short-lived credentials scoped to the tenant prefix via token vending (r1 #9). M02 records these descriptors and brokers credentials; executing infra automation is outside the FDD per DA-20.

#### 2.2.2 Functional & UI/UX Design

**Primary functions.**
- Create/list/inspect/update tenants; lifecycle suspend/reactivate/archive (mandate; FR-D5.1).
- Register/list/update/decommission inventory resources of the four kinds (mandate; DA-20).
- Bind/unbind resources to tenants — two compute types (physical GPU machine; logical compute service such as a Ray cluster instance) and the same dual-type model for storage (physical volume/filesystem; logical object-storage service) (mandate).
- Tenant self-view for TA/RD: tenant profile + sanitized binding list (mandate; BP-M2).
- Create/list/update/archive projects under a tenant; project ownership/stewardship (mandate; FR-D5.5; DA-02).
- Manage project membership with persona-template assignment (mandate §0.2.4; DA-01).

**State machines (exact):** Tenant: `active → suspended → archived` (suspend reversible via reactivate; archived terminal, metadata retained). Project: `active → archived` (terminal). Resource: `active → decommissioned` (terminal; blocked while bindings exist). Binding: `active → released` (row retained for accounting per DA-20).

##### UI page 1 — Tenant administration list, route `/admin/tenants` (PA)

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation rules |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| q | search text | ≤120 chars | empty | Search tenants… | debounced 300 ms |
| status | multi-select | active/suspended/archived | active+suspended | — | — |
| (create dialog) name | text | slug `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{2,38}$` | empty | acme-robotics | required; uniqueness checked on submit (409 surfaced inline) |
| display_name | text | 1–80 chars | empty | Acme Robotics | required |
| description | textarea | ≤2000 chars | empty | Purpose of this tenant | optional |
| storage_isolation | radio | prefix_per_tenant / dedicated_bucket | prefix_per_tenant | — | required; help text explains tiers (r1 #9) |

- **Validation logic:** real-time slug format with message "Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens; 3–39 chars."; on-submit uniqueness (`E_CONFLICT` → "A tenant with this name already exists."). All text sanitized (trim, control-chars stripped).
- **API binding:** table load → `GET /api/v1/admin/tenants` (M02-1); create → `POST /api/v1/admin/tenants` (M02-1); row click → tenant detail (page 2).
- **Output rendering:** paginated table (name, display name, status badge, projects count, bindings count, created_at); loading skeleton 8 shimmer rows; empty state "No tenants yet. Create the first tenant." with CTA; success toast "Tenant {display_name} created."
- **Exception handling:** `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → full-page 403 "Platform administrator access required."; `E_RATE_LIMITED` → toast with retry countdown; network failure → table retry banner; `E_VALIDATION` → field-level messages.

##### UI page 2 — Tenant detail & lifecycle, route `/admin/tenants/{tenant_id}` (PA; tabs: Profile · Resource bindings · Projects)

Profile tab fields mirror the create dialog (name immutable). Lifecycle buttons: "Suspend" (active only; confirm dialog warns "All API access for this tenant will be blocked and running work paused via operations policy."), "Reactivate" (suspended only), "Archive" (suspended only; type-name-to-confirm; irreversible).
- **API binding:** `GET`/`PATCH /api/v1/admin/tenants/{tenant_id}`, `POST …:suspend`, `POST …:reactivate` (M02-2); archive uses guarded `PATCH` status transition (see M02-2 processing).
- **Output:** status timeline (created/suspended/reactivated/archived with actor); optimistic-lock conflicts (`E_PRECONDITION_FAILED`) → banner "This tenant changed since you loaded it. Reload."
- **Exceptions:** `E_STATE_INVALID` → toast "Action not allowed from status {status}." (shows `allowed_transitions`).

##### UI page 3 — Resource inventory, route `/admin/resources` (PA, OE)

Register dialog is a 2-step wizard: kind selection (2×2 matrix: compute/storage × physical/logical), then kind-specific descriptor form.

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation rules |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| name | text | 1–80 chars, unique | empty | gpu-node-a17 | required |
| resource_class | radio | compute / storage | — | — | required, fixed after creation |
| form | radio | physical / logical | — | — | required, fixed after creation |
| descriptor.hostname (compute/physical) | text | RFC1123 hostname | empty | node-a17.dc1.local | required for this kind |
| descriptor.gpu_model / gpu_count | select / int | e.g. H100; 1–16 | — / 8 | — | required; count ≥1 |
| descriptor.cpu_cores / memory_gib | int | ≥1 | — | — | required |
| descriptor.service_type (logical kinds) | select | ray_cluster / object_storage / other_registered | — | — | required; compute-logical defaults ray_cluster, storage-logical object_storage |
| descriptor.endpoint_url (logical kinds) | text (URL) | https:// or s3:// URI | empty | https://ray-head.acme.svc:8265 | required; scheme allowlist |
| descriptor.capacity (logical compute) | group (gpu_count, cpu_cores, memory_gib, max_workers) | ints ≥0 | — | — | at least one field >0 |
| descriptor.volume_ref / filesystem / capacity_gib / export_path (storage/physical) | text/select/int/text | — | — | /exports/robodata1 | required for this kind |
| descriptor.bucket / region (storage/logical) | text | bucket naming rules | — | platform-data | bucket required for object_storage |
| credential (logical kinds) | secret group (access_key_id, secret, or token) | write-only | — | — | required for logical kinds; never echoed back; stored in vault |
| labels | key-value list | ≤20 pairs | empty | zone=dc1 | keys snake_case |

- **Validation logic:** per-kind conditional requirements enforced real-time (fields appear only for the chosen kind); endpoint URL syntax on blur; on submit the server performs a reachability probe for logical kinds — failure surfaces `E_TNT_CREDENTIAL_INVALID` → inline "Endpoint or credential rejected. The platform brokers existing services; verify the service is provisioned and reachable (it is not created by the platform)." (DA-20 phrasing).
- **API binding:** list → `GET /api/v1/admin/resources` (M02-3, filters resource_class/form/status/q); register → `POST /api/v1/admin/resources` (M02-3); detail/edit → `GET`/`PATCH /api/v1/admin/resources/{resource_id}` (M02-4); decommission → `POST /api/v1/admin/resources/{resource_id}:decommission` (M02-4).
- **Output:** table grouped by kind with capacity summary chips and binding count; detail drawer shows bindings and health (last probe at/result); decommission success toast; loading skeleton cards per kind.
- **Exceptions:** `E_TNT_RESOURCE_IN_USE` on decommission → dialog listing active bindings with links "Release these bindings first."; `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_RATE_LIMITED`, network → standard patterns as page 1.

##### UI page 4 — Tenant resource bindings, route `/admin/tenants/{tenant_id}` → "Resource bindings" tab (PA, OE)

Bind wizard: pick kind quadrant → pick an `active` inventory resource (compatible, not exclusively taken) → binding settings.

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation rules |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| resource_id | searchable select | `res-` id | — | Select resource… | required; list excludes incompatible/decommissioned; exclusively-bound resources shown disabled with owner tenant |
| binding_mode | radio | exclusive / shared | exclusive (physical), fixed dedicated (logical compute), shared (logical storage prefix tier) | — | physical kinds: both allowed; logical compute: forced exclusive (r1 #7); logical storage: shared unless tenant tier is dedicated_bucket |
| purpose | text | ≤200 chars | empty | e.g. training pool | optional |
| storage_prefix (storage/logical, shared) | read-only computed | `{bucket}/tenants/{tenant_id}/` | computed | — | display-only; enforced server-side |

- **API binding:** list → `GET /api/v1/admin/tenants/{tenant_id}/resource-bindings` (M02-5); bind → `POST` same path (M02-5); unbind → `DELETE /api/v1/admin/tenants/{tenant_id}/resource-bindings/{binding_id}` (M02-6, confirm dialog).
- **Output:** four-quadrant summary (counts + capacity) above the binding table (resource, kind, mode, status, bound_at, bound_by); toast "Resource bound. Scheduling targets update within seconds." (eventing to M16/M17).
- **Exceptions:** `E_TNT_BINDING_CONFLICT` → "This resource is exclusively bound to another tenant."; `E_TNT_RESOURCE_IN_USE` on unbind → dialog "Active workloads or stored data still reference this binding. Drain before release."; standard shared-code handling.

##### UI page 5 — Tenant self view, route `/tenant` (TA, RD read)

Read-only tenant profile + sanitized bindings (kind, name, capacity, status, health; **no endpoints' credentials, no vault paths**). API: `GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}`, `GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/resource-bindings` (M02-7). Empty state: "No resources bound yet. Contact your platform administrator." `E_TENANT_SUSPENDED` → full-page notice "This tenant is suspended. Contact your platform administrator."

##### UI page 6 — Projects & members, routes `/tenant/projects`, `/projects/{project_id}` (TA manage; RD read)

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation rules |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| name | text | slug, 3–39 chars, unique per tenant | empty | grasping-v2 | required; real-time format |
| display_name | text | 1–80 | empty | Grasping v2 | required |
| description | textarea | ≤2000 | empty | — | optional |
| owner_user_id | user select | `usr-` of a tenant member | current user | Select owner… | required (FR-D5.5 stewardship) |
| default_storage_binding_id | select | storage bindings of this tenant | tenant's sole storage binding if exactly one | Select storage… | required if ≥1 storage binding exists; else blocked with hint "Bind storage to the tenant first." |
| (members tab) user_id | user select | tenant-visible users | — | Add member… | required; user must be invited/active |
| persona_templates | multi-select | template keys from M03-3 catalog | persona.rd_default? no — empty | Select personas… | ≥1 required; only templates compatible with the user's built-in role (validated server-side) |

- **API binding:** create/list → `POST`/`GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/projects` (M02-8); detail/update/archive → `GET`/`PATCH /api/v1/projects/{project_id}`, `POST …:archive` (M02-9); members → `PUT`/`DELETE /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/members/{user_id}`, `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/members` (M02-10).
- **Output:** project cards with owner, member count, status; members table (user, persona templates, added_by, added_at); upsert toast "Member saved. Permissions take effect within a few seconds." (outbox propagation honesty); archive shows banner on all project pages "This project is archived (read-only)."
- **Exceptions:** archive with running workflows → `E_TNT_PROJECT_ACTIVE_WORK` dialog listing blocking runs; `E_VALIDATION` on unknown template key; `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` for RD attempting writes; standard handling otherwise.

#### 2.2.3 Interface Design

##### M02-1 Create / list tenants
- **Method/path:** `POST /api/v1/admin/tenants` · `GET /api/v1/admin/tenants` (query: `q`, `status`, `page_size`, `page_token`, `sort`).
- **Request body (POST):**
```json
{"name": "acme-robotics", "display_name": "Acme Robotics", "description": "string?",
 "settings": {"storage_isolation": "prefix_per_tenant|dedicated_bucket"}}
```
- **Processing (POST):** 1. Authz `tenancy.tenant:create` @ platform. 2. Validate slug/uniqueness → 409 `E_CONFLICT`. 3. Transaction: insert `tenancy.tenants` (status `active`, `namespace_ref = "tnt-" + name`, version 1) + outbox `tenant.created`. 4. Audit `tenant.created`. (Namespace/quota provisioning is downstream automation per DA-20/r1 #5 — M02 records the descriptor only.)
- **Response:** `201` tenant object →
```json
{"tenant_id": "tnt-01J…", "name": "acme-robotics", "display_name": "Acme Robotics", "status": "active",
 "settings": {"storage_isolation": "prefix_per_tenant"}, "namespace_ref": "tnt-acme-robotics",
 "version": 1, "created_at": "RFC3339", "created_by": "usr-…"}
```
  GET → `200` list envelope per §1.4.1.
- **Errors:** shared codes. **Idempotency:** `Idempotency-Key` honored 24 h. **Concurrency:** unique index on `name`. **Permission:** `tenancy.tenant:create` / `tenancy.tenant:read` (platform).

##### M02-2 Tenant detail / update / lifecycle
- **Method/path:** `GET`/`PATCH /api/v1/admin/tenants/{tenant_id}` · `POST …:suspend` · `POST …:reactivate`.
- **PATCH body (any subset):** `{"display_name": "…", "description": "…", "settings": {…}, "status": "archived"}` — `status` is accepted **only** for the guarded transition `suspended→archived`; all other lifecycle changes MUST use the `:suspend`/`:reactivate` verbs (any other status value → 409 `E_STATE_INVALID` with `allowed_transitions`). `If-Match` required (else 428).
- **`:suspend` body:** `{"reason": "string (10–500)"}`. **Processing:** 1. Authz `tenancy.tenant:admin` @ platform. 2. Validate transition per state machine `active→suspended`. 3. Transaction: update status + outbox `tenant.suspended`. 4. Effects (event consumers): gateway/M03 decision cache invalidation (all checks in the tenant now deny with `E_TENANT_SUSPENDED`); M17 pauses admission for the tenant; M01 sessions keep working only for tenant-switching away. 5. Audit. `:reactivate` mirrors (`suspended→active`).
- **Archive (guarded PATCH):** requires status `suspended`, zero non-archived projects → else 409 `E_TNT_TENANT_NOT_EMPTY`; bindings must all be `released` → else 409 `E_TNT_RESOURCE_IN_USE`. Terminal; metadata retained (DA-11 `purged-metadata-retained` applies only to business assets, not the tenant row).
- **Response:** `200` tenant object (as M02-1). **Errors:** `E_STATE_INVALID`, `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED`, `E_TNT_TENANT_NOT_EMPTY`, `E_TNT_RESOURCE_IN_USE`. **Idempotency:** lifecycle verbs are idempotent when already in target state → `200` with `Idempotency-Replayed` semantics (no error). **Permission:** `tenancy.tenant:read` / `tenancy.tenant:update` / `tenancy.tenant:admin`.

##### M02-3 Register / list inventory resources
- **Method/path:** `POST /api/v1/admin/resources` · `GET /api/v1/admin/resources` (query: `resource_class`, `form`, `status`, `q`, pagination).
- **Request body (POST):**
```json
{"name": "gpu-node-a17", "resource_class": "compute|storage", "form": "physical|logical",
 "descriptor": {
   "compute_physical": {"hostname": "node-a17.dc1.local", "gpu_model": "H100", "gpu_count": 8, "cpu_cores": 96, "memory_gib": 1024, "node_labels": {"zone": "dc1"}},
   "compute_logical":  {"service_type": "ray_cluster", "endpoint_url": "https://…", "version": "2.x", "capacity": {"gpu_count": 16, "cpu_cores": 256, "memory_gib": 2048, "max_workers": 32}},
   "storage_physical": {"volume_ref": "san-vol-009", "filesystem": "xfs", "capacity_gib": 65536, "export_path": "/exports/robodata1"},
   "storage_logical":  {"service_type": "object_storage", "endpoint_url": "https://s3.dc1.local", "bucket": "platform-data", "region": "dc1", "capacity_gib": null}
 },
 "credential": {"access_key_id": "write-only", "secret": "write-only"},
 "labels": {"zone": "dc1"}}
```
  (exactly one descriptor branch present, matching class×form; `credential` required for logical kinds, write-only.)
- **Processing:** 1. Authz `infra.resource:create` @ platform. 2. Validate kind-conditional schema → 422 `E_VALIDATION` with per-field details. 3. Logical kinds: store secret in the platform vault → keep `credential_ref` (vault path) only; run reachability/auth probe; failure → 422 `E_TNT_CREDENTIAL_INVALID` (vault entry rolled back). 4. Insert `tenancy.resources` (status `active`, `health = reachable|unprobed`) + outbox `infra.resource.registered`. 5. Audit. The record is an inventory descriptor of externally provisioned equipment/services — registration provisions nothing (DA-20).
- **Response:** `201` resource object (descriptor echoed **without** any credential material; `credential_ref` shown as opaque marker `"vault://…"` to PA/OE only). GET → list envelope.
- **Errors:** `E_TNT_CREDENTIAL_INVALID` (422), shared. **Idempotency:** `Idempotency-Key`; unique `name`. **Permission:** `infra.resource:create` / `infra.resource:read`.

##### M02-4 Resource detail / update / decommission
- **Method/path:** `GET`/`PATCH /api/v1/admin/resources/{resource_id}` · `POST …:decommission`.
- **PATCH:** mutable: `name`? No — `name` immutable; mutable: `descriptor` capacity/labels/endpoint fields, `credential` (rotation: new vault version written, old kept 24 h for in-flight workloads, then disabled), `labels`. `If-Match` required. Class/form immutable → 422 `E_VALIDATION`.
- **`:decommission` processing:** 1. Authz `infra.resource:admin`. 2. Guard: zero `active` bindings → else 409 `E_TNT_RESOURCE_IN_USE` (details list binding ids). 3. Status `active→decommissioned` (terminal), outbox `infra.resource.decommissioned`, audit.
- **Response:** `200` resource object. **Idempotency:** decommission idempotent. **Permission:** `infra.resource:read` / `infra.resource:update` / `infra.resource:admin`.

##### M02-5 Bind resource to tenant / list bindings (admin view)
- **Method/path:** `POST /api/v1/admin/tenants/{tenant_id}/resource-bindings` · `GET` (same path; query: `resource_class`, `form`, `status`, pagination).
- **Request body:**
```json
{"resource_id": "res-01J…", "binding_mode": "exclusive|shared", "purpose": "string?"}
```
- **Processing:** 1. Authz `tenancy.resource_binding:create` @ platform. 2. Tenant must be `active` (suspended → 403 `E_TENANT_SUSPENDED`; archived → 409 `E_STATE_INVALID`); resource must be `active`. 3. Mode rules: compute/logical forced `exclusive` (tenant-dedicated service instance, r1 #7 — request with `shared` → 422 `E_VALIDATION`); physical kinds: `exclusive` or `shared`; storage/logical: `shared` (prefix tier) or `exclusive` (dedicated-bucket tier; requires tenant `settings.storage_isolation = dedicated_bucket`). 4.
