Structured JSON per §1.4.6 with module fields: `campaign_id`, `status_from`, `status_to`, `goal_count`, `dimension`, `attainment_pct`, `result`, `latency_ms`. **Masking (§1.4.7):** purpose/notes free text logged by reference + length only; emails masked `s***@domain`; governance tag *values* are loggable (they are enums, not PII), consent free-text never.

**Shipping:** stdout JSON → platform collector (per §1.4.6); audit via M03-11 batched; `traceparent` propagated on M04→M03/M05/M18 calls.

**Audit events (M03-11):** `data.campaign.created`, `data.campaign.updated` (before/after summary), `data.campaign.status_changed` (from/to, reason — covers suspend/archive for AC-14), `data.campaign.goals_changed` (diff summary), `data.campaign.governance_changed` (tag tightening/loosening with actor — FR-A1.3 traceability).

---

### 2.5 M05 — Data Ingestion & Quality Management

#### 2.5.1 Module Overview

M05 is the intake gateway and quality authority of the Physical Data Factory: it receives raw humanoid robot recordings through **resumable, checksummed, manifest-driven upload sessions** (batch import and collection/streamed capture — FR-A2.2, DA-12), preserves them as **immutable source assets** (FR-A2.1/A2.5, BR-01) in write-once object storage, and gates everything downstream through a **write-audit-publish quarantine pipeline** (per §0.7.2): recordings land `pending_validation`, SVC-01 evaluates them against campaign-scoped quality rules, and only `accepted`/`accepted_with_warnings` recordings become visible to M06 alignment and the M08 catalog. MCAP/ROS 2 bag is the primary ingest container; legacy `.db3`/ROS 1 bags are accepted and normalized to indexed MCAP as a recorded derivation, never destructively (per §0.7.2, FR-A2.1).

**Owns:** Ingest Manifest, Ingest Session (+ parts), Raw Recording (`rec-{ulid}`, Table 0.3 #2) + modality streams, Quality Rule, Quality Report (+ findings), recording quality status, Remediation Task, ingestion status report (RPT-01), signed content URLs for raw streams.
**Excludes:** campaign definition (M04); alignment/episodes/lineage beyond the recording root (M06); catalog browse/governance flags (M08); failure-record lifecycle (M15 — M05 emits convertible quality failures); storage-binding inventory (M02); workflow scheduling (M16).

**Roles & personas:** Robotics Data Engineer (primary — persona template `ingest:*`, `quality:*` per §0.2.4: opens sessions, drives validation, manages quality status and rules), Data Collection Lead (`ingest:initiate`: opens collection sessions, raises recollection remediation), Annotation QA Lead (consumes findings), Tenant Administrator (quality-rule governance, privacy-review remediation), Operations Engineer (none — business domain; storage health lives in M02/M17).

**Upstream:** M04 (campaign context + status fence per §2.4.3), M02 (tenant logical storage bindings = upload targets), M01/M03 (session, authz/audit), M16 (validation workflow runs), SVC-01 Ingestion & Intake Validation Worker (executor).
**Downstream:** M06 (accepted recordings — knowledge-base edge M05→M06 per §0.5.2), M08 (recording catalog sync), M15 (quality failures convertible to failure records), M18 (quality findings as searchable metadata, FR-A3.5), M20 (quality-block alerts), M04 (ingest summaries, coverage actuals).

**FRD coverage:** FR-A2.1–A2.7, FR-A3.1–A3.6, BR-01 (immutability), RPT-01 (M05-11), DA-12 (collection sessions), BP-01 steps 2–3, AC-01 (with M04).

#### 2.5.2 Functional & UI/UX Design

**Primary functions**

| # | Function | Description | FRD |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Non-destructive ingest | Original bytes stored verbatim, write-once; normalization to indexed MCAP recorded as derivation (per §0.7.2) | FR-A2.1, BR-01 |
| F2 | Dual ingestion modes | `batch_import` and `collection_stream` sessions (chunked, resumable; DA-12) under one session lifecycle | FR-A2.2 |
| F3 | Modality registry | Exact registry: `video, depth, point_cloud, imu, tactile_force, joint_state, motor_command, action_token, language_instruction, robot_status, environment_context`; per-stream rows with topic, frequency, clock domain, timestamp source (per §0.7.2) | FR-A2.3, FR-A2.4 |
| F4 | Provenance preservation | Source timestamps, source/robot/sensor identifiers, capture-session metadata kept on recording + streams | FR-A2.4 |
| F5 | Immutable raw asset record | One `rec-` row per accepted recording; payload and provenance fields frozen (BR-01); only quality/governance metadata mutable | FR-A2.5 |
| F6 | Ingest manifests | Expected files/streams/modalities/campaign association; reconciliation detects missing/unexpected early | FR-A2.6 |
| F7 | Quarantine | Manual + rule-driven quarantine of incomplete/restricted/corrupted/awaiting-review recordings; quarantined assets invisible to downstream selection | FR-A2.7, FR-A3.3 |
| F8 | Intake validation report | Per recording or batch: missing modalities, unreadable files, duplicates, incomplete metadata, timestamp gaps (mcap doctor-equivalent structural checks + recover salvage with delta logged, per §0.7.2) | FR-A3.1, FR-A3.2 |
| F9 | Controlled quality states | Exactly: `pending_validation → accepted \| accepted_with_warnings \| quarantined \| rejected \| requires_manual_review` | FR-A3.3 |
| F10 | Scoped quality rules | Rule sets selected by campaign / embodiment / sensor suite / dataset purpose; versioned | FR-A3.4 |
| F11 | Searchable findings | Findings stored as structured metadata, indexed for mining via M18 | FR-A3.5 |
| F12 | Remediation tasks | Kinds exactly: missing_metadata, corruption_review, privacy_review, recollection_request | FR-A3.6 |
| F13 | RPT-01 | Ingestion status by campaign, project, modality, quality status | RPT-01 |

**UI pages**

**P1 — Ingest Console** (project-scoped). Two panels: active sessions (state chips per the session machine, per-session progress = bytes/parts/files complete, resumable indicator) and recent recordings by quality status (5-state color chips). CTA "New ingest session" wizard. Bindings: `M05-2`, `M05-3`, `M05-6`. Empty: "No sessions — open one against a collecting campaign." Loading: dual skeleton panels.

**P2 — New Session Wizard** (binding `M05-2 POST`; steps: campaign → mode → manifest → review):

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| campaign_id | searchable picker | `cmp-` ulid; only campaigns in `collecting`/`ingesting` | — | "kitchen-manipulation…" | required; other statuses disabled with tooltip (pre-empts `E_ING_CAMPAIGN_STATE`) |
| mode | radio | `batch_import \| collection_stream` (DA-12) | batch_import | — | required |
| manifest_id | select existing / "create new" → P3 | `man-` ulid | none | — | optional for batch_import; **required** for collection_stream (early detection, FR-A2.6) |
| recording_grouping | select | `per_file \| per_directory \| manifest_defined` | manifest_defined | — | required when manifest present |
| declared_total_bytes | integer | bytes | — | "120000000000" | optional; >0; drives progress UI |
| part_size_hint | select | 16/32/64 MiB (multipart practice per §0.7.2) | 64 MiB | — | — |
| notes | textarea | ≤2000 | — | — | optional |

On submit → session created `open`, wizard shows the **upload descriptor** (protocol, presigned-part endpoint pattern, session-scoped upload token TTL, per-part checksum algorithm) for CLI/edge-agent handoff; copy-as-command button.

**P3 — Manifest Editor** (binding `M05-1 POST/GET`). Header: campaign, name, description. Repeating expected-file rows:

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| path | text | relative path, no `..`, ≤512 chars | — | "run_014/robot.mcap" | required; unique in manifest; sanitized |
| container | select | `mcap \| ros1_bag \| ros2_db3 \| video \| csv \| json \| binary_other` | mcap | — | required |
| expected_streams[] | repeating (topic, modality, expected_hz) | modality from FR-A2.3 enum | — | "/imu/data, imu, 200" | modality enum → `E_VALIDATION` |
| expected_bytes | integer | >0 | — | — | optional |
| expected_sha256 | text | 64 hex | — | — | optional; format check |
| required | bool | — | true | — | — |
| recording_unit | text | group key (files sharing a key form one recording) | path | "run_014" | — |

Real-time row validation; on-submit whole-manifest check (≥1 required file). Toast with manifest id; immutable once any session references it (edits fork a new manifest — banner explains).

**P4 — Session Detail / Upload Monitor** (bindings `M05-3 GET` poll 5 s, `M05-4` rows, `M05-5`, `M05-3 :abort`). Per-file part matrix (uploaded/verified/missing), per-part checksum status icons, bytes & throughput, manifest reconciliation preview (missing required files red, unexpected files amber — FR-A2.6). Buttons: "Complete session" (disabled until every required file has a complete part set; tooltip lists gaps), "Abort" (confirm dialog, reason). Completion → 202 toast "Assembling… validation workflow wfr-… started" and live state chip `assembling`; on `completed`, links to created recordings; on `failed`, error panel with per-file causes and "reopen as new session" CTA. Network failure during polling → stale banner + auto-retry; expired session token → `E_ING_SESSION_EXPIRED` dialog ("re-issue upload credentials" action, permission-gated).

**P5 — Recordings Browser + Detail** (bindings `M05-6`, `M05-7`, `M05-12`). Faceted list: quality status (5 chips), campaign, modality, robot/sensor ids, date, restriction badge (inherited from campaign), duplicate-group indicator. Detail tabs: Overview (provenance: source ids, robot id, sensor suite, capture session, clock domains per stream — FR-A2.4); Streams (modality rows: topic, message_count, hz actual vs expected, gap count); Quality Report (P6 embed); Content (stream list with "Get signed URL" buttons → `M05-12`, TTL countdown, audit notice); Lineage root (links to M06 episodes). Immutability is explicit UI: no edit controls on provenance/payload fields, header badge "Immutable raw asset (BR-01)".

**P6 — Quality Report Viewer** (binding `M05-7`). Report header (rule-set version, SVC-01 run ref, started/finished), findings table: severity (info/warn/error), check code (`missing_modality`, `unreadable_file`, `duplicate_asset`, `incomplete_metadata`, `timestamp_gap`, `frequency_low`, `checksum_mismatch`, `structure_invalid`, `salvaged_truncation` — FR-A3.2 set + structural checks per §0.7.2), affected stream/file, measured vs threshold, evidence pointer. Actions row (permission-gated): quality-status change dialog (P7), "create remediation task" (prefilled P8), "convert to failure record" (M15-1 deep-link).

**P7 — Quality Status Change dialog** (binding `M05-8 PUT`):

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| target_status | select | only legal targets for current state (machine §2.5.3) | — | — | required; illegal → button disabled |
| reason | textarea | 10–2000 chars | — | "Privacy review cleared by…" | required for every manual transition |
| waive_finding_ids[] | multi-select | open error findings | — | — | required when accepting over `error` findings |

**P8 — Remediation Board** (bindings `M05-10`). Kanban `open → in_progress → resolved | cancelled`; card: kind badge (4 kinds, FR-A3.6), linked recording/finding, assignee, due date, age. Create form fields: kind (enum), recording_id/finding_id pickers, summary (required ≤200), description, assignee, due_date (≥today), priority. Recollection cards show campaign link and "notify Collection Lead" (M20).

**P9 — RPT-01 Ingestion Status Dashboard** (binding `M05-11`). Filter bar (project, campaign, date range); widgets: recordings by quality status (stacked bars per campaign), bytes & counts by modality, validation backlog (pending/requires_manual_review aging), session success/failure trend, quarantine list. Export CSV. Skeleton cards while loading; per-widget error isolation (one failed widget never blanks the dashboard).

**Validation & exception handling (module-wide):** real-time field validation on blur; checksums and part bookkeeping validated server-side authoritatively; all paths/free text sanitized (no `..`, control chars stripped). Network failure mid-upload → parts resume by offset, UI shows "resumable — nothing re-sent" (per §0.7.2 tus-style); `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → read-only + tooltip; `E_RATE_LIMITED` → backoff with `Retry-After` countdown on part registration; `E_VALIDATION` → field/row-anchored; `E_STATE_INVALID` on session/quality transitions → refresh dialog with `allowed_transitions`.

#### 2.5.3 Interface Design

All endpoints follow §1.4. Module error namespace `E_ING_*`. Authz M03-10; audit M03-11. Long operations return **202 + workflow_run_ref** (M16).

**Normative state machines (exact):**

```text
IngestSession.status:
  open ──first part registered──▶ uploading ──:complete──▶ assembling ──SVC-01──▶ completed | failed
  open|uploading ──:abort──▶ aborted        assembling is system-owned (no user verbs except status read)

Recording.quality_status (FR-A3.3, exact states):
  pending_validation ──SVC-01──▶ accepted | accepted_with_warnings | quarantined | rejected | requires_manual_review
  requires_manual_review ──M05-8──▶ accepted | accepted_with_warnings | quarantined | rejected
  quarantined ──M05-8──▶ accepted | accepted_with_warnings | rejected | requires_manual_review
  accepted | accepted_with_warnings ──M05-8──▶ quarantined            (late concern, e.g. privacy — FR-A2.7)
  rejected: terminal (payload retained per BR-01/DA-11; metadata never deleted)
```

Payload bytes and provenance fields are immutable in **all** states (BR-01); transitions move metadata only. Demotion of an `accepted*` recording to `quarantined` fans out `data.quality_status.changed` so M06/M08 flag dependent episodes/snapshots (visibility, not retro-mutation).

**Module error codes (`E_ING_*`)**

| Code | HTTP | Raised when |
|---|---|---|
| `E_ING_CAMPAIGN_STATE` | 409 | Manifest/session creation against a campaign not in `collecting`/`ingesting` (status fence from M04 events) |
| `E_ING_MANIFEST_IMMUTABLE` | 409 | PATCH/replace of a manifest already referenced by a session |
| `E_ING_SESSION_STATE` | 409 | Part registration or `:complete` on a session not `open/uploading` |
| `E_ING_SESSION_EXPIRED` | 410 | Upload token / presigned-part window expired (re-issue via M05-3 GET with `refresh_credentials=true`) |
| `E_ING_PART_CHECKSUM` | 422 | Registered part checksum mismatches object-store computed checksum |
| `E_ING_PART_INCOMPLETE` | 409 | `:complete` while required files have missing parts (details: per-file gap list) |
| `E_ING_DUPLICATE_RECORDING` | 409 | Full-object SHA-256 already accepted in tenant (duplicate detection FR-A3.2); details carry existing `rec-` id |
| `E_ING_IMMUTABLE` | 409 | Any attempt to modify payload/provenance of a recording (BR-01) |
| `E_ING_RULE_IN_USE` | 409 | DELETE of a quality-rule version referenced by reports (deactivate instead) |
| `E_ING_STORAGE_UNBOUND` | 409 | No active tenant logical storage binding for upload target (M02) |
| Shared | — | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_STATE_INVALID`, `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED`, `E_CONFLICT`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_RATE_LIMITED`, `E_IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT` per §1.4.3 |

**M05-1 `POST /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/ingest-manifests` · `GET .../ingest-manifests` · `GET /api/v1/ingest-manifests/{manifest_id}`** — declare expected files/streams/modalities (FR-A2.6).
Permission: `data.ingest:initiate` (POST) / `data.recording:read` (GET).
POST body (compact):

```json
{ "name": "2026-07-04-run-batch", "description": "...",
  "expected_files": [ { "path": "run_014/robot.mcap", "container": "mcap", "required": true,
    "recording_unit": "run_014", "expected_bytes": 5368709120, "expected_sha256": "ab12…",
    "expected_streams": [ { "topic": "/cam_head/image_raw", "modality": "video", "expected_hz": 30 },
                          { "topic": "/joint_states", "modality": "joint_state", "expected_hz": 100 } ] } ] }
```

Processing (POST): 1. authz at campaign's project; campaign status fence → `E_ING_CAMPAIGN_STATE`; 2. validate paths (relative, unique), modality enum (FR-A2.3), checksum format → `E_VALIDATION`; 3. **Tx:** insert `data.ingest_manifest` + file rows, status `declared`, outbox `data.manifest.created` — commit; 4. audit; **201** `{manifest_id}`. Manifests referenced by a session are frozen (`E_ING_MANIFEST_IMMUTABLE`); GET detail includes reconciliation results per session once completed (`reconciled | discrepant` + finding refs).

**M05-2 `POST /api/v1/campaigns/{campaign_id}/ingest-sessions`** — open batch or collection session (FR-A2.2, DA-12).
Permission: `data.ingest:initiate`.
Body: `{ "mode": "batch_import"|"collection_stream", "manifest_id": "man-…"?, "recording_grouping": "manifest_defined", "declared_total_bytes": 120000000000?, "part_size_hint_mib": 64, "notes": "..." }`
Processing:

1. Authz; campaign fence (`E_ING_CAMPAIGN_STATE`); `collection_stream` requires `manifest_id` (`E_VALIDATION`).
2. Resolve tenant logical storage binding (M02-7, cached) → `E_ING_STORAGE_UNBOUND`; derive write-once target prefix (layout §2.5.4).
3. **Tx:** insert `data.ingest_session` (status `open`, expiry = now + 72 h batch / + campaign date_end for stream), mint **session-scoped upload token** (hashed at rest; scope: this session's prefix only, PUT-part + register rights, TTL 12 h renewable), outbox `data.ingest_session.opened` — commit.
4. Audit; **201**: `{ "session_id": "ing-…", "status": "open", "upload": { "protocol": "multipart_presigned", "resume": "offset_query_supported", "part_checksum_algo": "crc32c", "full_object_checksum_algo": "sha256", "part_url_pattern": "PUT {signed}", "token_expires_at": "…" } }` (multipart + per-part checksums + resumability per §0.7.2; first part registration flips status to `uploading`).

**M05-3 `GET /api/v1/ingest-sessions/{session_id}` · `POST ...:abort`** — status / abort.
Permission: `data.recording:read` / `data.ingest:initiate` (abort: opener, campaign owner, or TA).
GET returns session, per-file part bitmap, bytes done, reconciliation preview, `refresh_credentials=true` param re-issues the upload token (authz re-checked, old token revoked, audited). `:abort` body `{reason}`: allowed from `open/uploading` (else `E_ING_SESSION_STATE`); **Tx:** status `aborted` + outbox `data.ingest_session.aborted` — commit; SVC-01 asynchronously aborts the object-store multipart uploads and GCs orphan parts (per §0.7.2 incomplete-upload hygiene); 200.

**M05-4 `POST /api/v1/ingest-sessions/{session_id}/parts`** — register/commit uploaded part (resumable, checksummed).
Permission: session upload token (service-validated) or `data.ingest:initiate`.
Body: `{ "file_path": "run_014/robot.mcap", "part_number": 17, "byte_size": 67108864, "checksum_crc32c": "…", "storage_etag": "…" }`
Processing:

1. Token/authz + session state ∈ {open, uploading} → `E_ING_SESSION_STATE`; expiry → `E_ING_SESSION_EXPIRED`.
2. Validate `file_path` against manifest (when bound): unknown path ⇒ recorded as `unexpected_file` part (WARN, kept for reconciliation — FR-A2.6 early detection), not an error; `part_number` ∈ [1, 10000] (per §0.7.2 multipart limits).
3. Verify `checksum_crc32c` against the object store's part checksum (`storage_etag` lookup; lazy batch verify ≤2 s) → `E_ING_PART_CHECKSUM` (client re-uploads that part only).
4. **Tx:** upsert `data.ingest_part` keyed `(session_id, file_path, part_number)` — re-registration while `uploading` replaces (resume/retry semantics, attempt counter incremented); flip session `open→uploading` on first part — commit. **200** `{received_parts, missing_parts_hint}`. Rate limit: 50 req/s/session → `E_RATE_LIMITED`.

**M05-5 `POST /api/v1/ingest-sessions/{session_id}:complete`** — close session → create recordings, trigger validation.
Permission: `data.ingest:initiate`. Idempotency-Key honored; re-complete replays the original 202.
Processing (mandated pattern):

1. Authz; session state ∈ {open, uploading} → `E_ING_SESSION_STATE`.
2. **Validate parts:** every manifest-required file has a gapless part set [1..n] with verified checksums → `E_ING_PART_INCOMPLETE` (details: per-file missing part lists); compose full-object SHA-256 per file from part digests; tenant-wide dedup check on composed digests → `E_ING_DUPLICATE_RECORDING` (override flag `allow_duplicate=true` requires `data.quality:update` and is audited).
3. **One transaction:** session → `assembling`; group files by `recording_unit` and insert one `data.recording` row per unit (`rec-{ulid}`, quality_status `pending_validation`, provenance snapshot: campaign ref, source identifiers from manifest/headers, governance tags inherited from campaign per FR-A1.3); insert `data.recording_file` rows (object keys, sizes, sha256); insert manifest reconciliation findings (missing/unexpected — FR-A2.6); write outbox event `data.ingest_session.completed` carrying recording ids — **commit**.
4. Outbox relay starts the M16 validation workflow (template `intake_validation`, executor SVC-01); response **202** `{ "session_id", "status": "assembling", "recording_ids": ["rec-…"], "workflow_run_ref": "wfr-…" }`.
5. SVC-01 finalizes multipart objects, runs structural validation (mcap doctor-equivalent; `recover` salvage for truncated files with salvage delta logged as `salvaged_truncation` finding, per §0.7.2), normalizes legacy containers to indexed MCAP under `derived/` (recorded as transformation), extracts modality streams (FR-A2.3/A2.4), evaluates the resolved quality-rule set, writes the Quality Report, and sets the quality status (machine above); session → `completed` (or `failed` with per-file causes). Each SVC-01 state write is its own tx + outbox event (`data.recording.accepted`, `data.recording.quarantined`, …).

**M05-6 `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/recordings` · `GET /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}`** — list/detail immutable recordings.
Permission: `data.recording:read` (+ deny-wins `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` when campaign restrictions apply, §0.5.3).
List filters: `campaign_id, quality_status, modality, robot_id, sensor_id, captured_after/before, has_finding_code, duplicate_group, q, sort`. Item: `{recording_id, campaign_id, quality_status, modalities[], duration_s, byte_size, robot_id, captured_at, restriction_summary, finding_counts{error,warn}}`. Detail adds streams[], files[] (`{path, byte_size, sha256, container, storage_class}` — payload summaries only per §1.4.7), provenance, quality history, lineage root refs (M06 episodes). PATCH/PUT/DELETE on payload or provenance → **405/`E_ING_IMMUTABLE`** (BR-01).

**M05-7 `GET /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/quality-report`** — intake validation report (FR-A3.1/A3.2).
Permission: `data.recording:read`. Params `version?` (default latest). Response: `{report_id, recording_id, rule_set_ref: {rule_ids[], resolved_by: "campaign|embodiment|sensor_suite|purpose"}, workflow_run_ref, started_at, finished_at, summary: {error_count, warn_count, info_count, recommendation}, findings: [{finding_id, code, severity, stream_ref?, file_ref?, measured, threshold, message, evidence_ref?}]}`. 404 with `details.status="pending_validation"` while the first report is being produced (UI shows in-progress state).

**M05-8 `PUT /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/quality-status`** — controlled manual status change (FR-A3.3, FR-A2.7).
Permission: `data.quality:update`.
Body: `{ "target_status": "accepted", "reason": "…", "waive_finding_ids": ["fnd-…"], "expected_current_status": "requires_manual_review" }`
Processing:

1. Authz; row lock; `expected_current_status` (or If-Match) mismatch → `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED`.
2. Transition legality per the exact machine (manual edges only; `pending_validation` is SVC-01-owned; `rejected` terminal) → `E_STATE_INVALID` + `allowed_transitions`.
3. Accepting over open `error` findings requires each waived in `waive_finding_ids` → `E_VALIDATION`; waivers stored on findings with actor.
4. **Tx:** update status + append `data.quality_status_history{from,to,actor,reason,waivers}` + outbox `data.quality_status.changed` — commit. Downstream: M06/M08 visibility recompute; M20 alert on demotions.
5. Audit `data.quality_status.changed` (reason mandatory); **200** `{recording_id, quality_status, history_tail}`. Idempotent: setting current status replays 200 without history row.

**M05-9 `POST /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/quality-rules` · `GET` (same) · `GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/quality-rules/{rule_id}`** — scoped rule sets (FR-A3.4).
Permission: `data.quality_rule:create/read/update/delete` (TA or RD with grant).
POST body: `{ "name": "manip-campaign-default", "scope_selector": { "campaign_ids": ["cmp-…"]?, "embodiment": "humanoid-g1"?, "sensor_suite": "suite-v2"?, "dataset_purpose": "training"? }, "checks": [ { "code": "required_modalities", "params": {"modalities": ["video","joint_state"]}, "severity_on_fail": "error" }, { "code": "topic_frequency", "params": {"topic": "/joint_states", "min_hz": 95}, "severity_on_fail": "warn" }, { "code": "timestamp_gap", "params": {"max_gap_ms": 500} , "severity_on_fail": "error"}, { "code": "metadata_completeness", "params": {"required_fields": ["robot_id","operator_role"]}, "severity_on_fail": "warn" } ], "on_error_status": "quarantined", "on_warn_status": "accepted_with_warnings", "active": true }`
Processing: 1. authz; validate check codes against the registered check catalog (structural `file_integrity`, `duplicate_check` always implicit) and `on_*_status` against the 5-state enum → `E_VALIDATION`; 2. **Tx:** insert rule version (rules are versioned; PATCH creates version n+1, prior versions immutable — reports reference exact versions); 3. audit. DELETE deactivates; hard delete only for never-referenced versions → `E_ING_RULE_IN_USE`. **Resolution order at validation time:** campaign > sensor_suite > embodiment > dataset_purpose > tenant default; the resolved set is recorded on the report (FR-A3.4 auditability).

**M05-10 `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/remediation-tasks` · `GET` (same) · `GET/PATCH /api/v1/remediation-tasks/{task_id}`** — remediation work (FR-A3.6).
Permission: `data.remediation:create/read/update`.
POST body: `{ "kind": "missing_metadata|corruption_review|privacy_review|recollection_request", "recording_id": "rec-…"?, "finding_id": "fnd-…"?, "campaign_id": "cmp-…"?, "summary": "…", "description": "…", "assignee_id": "usr-…"?, "due_date": "2026-07-15"?, "priority": "p1" }`
Processing: 1. authz; kind enum exact (FR-A3.6) → `E_VALIDATION`; referenced recording/finding must exist in-project; `recollection_request` requires `campaign_id`; 2. **Tx:** insert task status `open` + outbox `data.remediation.created` (M20 notifies assignee; recollection notifies campaign owner) — commit; 3. audit; **201**. PATCH: triage fields + transitions `open→in_progress→resolved|cancelled` (resolution_note required on `resolved`; cancel reason required) → `E_STATE_INVALID` otherwise. Privacy-review tasks auto-link the recording's quarantine: resolving with `outcome=cleared` proposes (not auto-applies) an M05-8 transition, keeping human-in-the-loop.

**M05-11 `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/reports/ingestion-status`** — RPT-01.
Permission: `data.recording:read`. Params: `campaign_id?, group_by=campaign|modality|quality_status|day, from?, to?, format=json|csv`.
Response: `{generated_at, rows: [{group_key, recordings: {pending_validation: n, accepted: n, accepted_with_warnings: n, quarantined: n, rejected: n, requires_manual_review: n}, bytes_total, sessions: {open,uploading,assembling,completed,failed,aborted}, validation_backlog_age_p95_s}], totals}`. Served from `data.ingestion_status_mv` (event-driven + 10-min refresh); `generated_at` is the staleness marker; per-user cache TTL 5 min.

**M05-12 `GET /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/content-urls`** — time-limited signed read URLs for raw streams.
Permission: `data.recording:read` **+** asset-level restriction re-check (campaign usage_restrictions ⇒ `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` deny-wins, §0.5.3).
Params: `streams=topic1,topic2?`, `files=path?`, `ttl_s` (default 600, max 3600).
Processing: 1. authz + restriction gate; recording must not be `rejected` (`E_STATE_INVALID`); 2. resolve object keys; serve from the **signed-URL cache** keyed `(recording_id, file, user_id)` when remaining TTL > 50%, else mint fresh presigned GETs against the tenant's storage binding; 3. audit `data.recording.content_accessed` (file list summary — access trail for RPT-11/M03-9); 4. **200** `{urls: [{path, topic?, url, expires_at}], watermark: {user_id, issued_at}}`. URLs are never logged (§1.4.7).

**Concurrency & idempotency summary:** part registration is upsert-idempotent per `(session, file, part_number)`; `:complete` idempotent by Idempotency-Key (replays 202 with same `workflow_run_ref`); quality transitions serialize per recording via row lock + `expected_current_status`; rule versions immutable; session expiry sweep (SVC-01) aborts stale `open/uploading` sessions older than expiry with audit trail.

#### 2.5.4 Data Design

**Control plane:** PostgreSQL schema **`data.*`** (single-writer: M05 owns the tables below). **Payload plane:** tenant-isolated object storage via the tenant's logical storage binding (M02) with a **write-once layout** (object lock / versioning + deny-overwrite bucket policy enforcing BR-01):

```text
{tenant_binding_bucket}/                       ── hard tenant isolation: one bucket/endpoint per tenant binding (per §0.2.3)
  raw/{project_id}/{campaign_id}/{recording_id}/
    source/{original files verbatim}           ── immutable originals (BR-01; FR-A2.1)
    derived/mcap/{normalized indexed .mcap}    ── non-destructive normalization, recorded as transformation (per §0.7.2)
    derived/quality/{report evidence blobs}
  uploads/{session_id}/…                       ── multipart staging; GC'd on abort/expiry (per §0.7.2)
```

| Table | Key columns (abridged) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `data.ingest_manifest` | `manifest_id pk (man-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `project_id`, `campaign_id fk`, `name`, `description`, `status` (`declared\|reconciling\|reconciled\|discrepant`), `created_by`, timestamps | Frozen once referenced by a session |
| `data.ingest_manifest_file` | `(manifest_id, path) pk`, `container`, `required bool`, `recording_unit`, `expected_bytes?`, `expected_sha256?`, `expected_streams jsonb` | Modality values constrained to FR-A2.3 enum |
| `data.ingest_session` | `session_id pk (ing-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `project_id`, `campaign_id fk`, `manifest_id fk?`, `mode` (`batch_import\|collection_stream`), `status` (6-state machine), `storage_prefix`, `upload_token_hash`, `token_expires_at`, `declared_total_bytes?`, `opened_by`, `expires_at`, `failure_causes jsonb?`, `etag_version`, timestamps | Idx `(campaign_id, status)`, `(tenant_id, status)`; token stored hashed only (§1.4.7) |
| `data.ingest_part` | `(session_id, file_path, part_number) pk`, `byte_size`, `checksum_crc32c`, `storage_etag`, `verified bool`, `attempt_count`, `received_at` | Upsert-idempotent; bitmap queries for gap detection |
| `data.recording` | `recording_id pk (rec-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `project_id`, `campaign_id fk`, `quality_status` (5+1 enum), `captured_at`, `duration_s`, `byte_size`, `robot_id`, `sensor_suite`, `operator_role`, `source_identifiers jsonb` (FR-A2.4), `capture_session_meta jsonb`, `governance jsonb` (inherited FR-A1.3 snapshot), `full_sha256`, `duplicate_group_id?`, `session_id fk`, `storage_prefix`, `etag_version` (metadata only), `created_at` | **Partitioned by `(tenant_id, month(created_at))`**; provenance/payload columns write-once (DB trigger rejects UPDATE on frozen columns — BR-01 defense in depth); idx `(project_id, quality_status)`, `(campaign_id)`, `(full_sha256)` unique per tenant |
| `data.recording_file` | `file_id pk`, `recording_id fk`, `path`, `container`, `byte_size`, `sha256`, `object_key`, `kind` (`source\|derived_mcap\|evidence`) | Write-once rows |
| `data.modality_stream` | `stream_id pk`, `recording_id fk`, `topic`, `modality` (FR-A2.3 enum), `message_count`, `actual_hz`, `start_ts`, `end_ts`, `clock_domain` (`ptp\|ntp\|driver`), `timestamp_source` (`hardware\|driver_receive\|arrival`), `sensor_id` | Per §0.7.2 clock-provenance finding; idx `(recording_id)`, `(modality)` |
| `data.quality_rule` / `data.quality_rule_version` | `rule_id pk (qrl-ulid)` / `(rule_id, version) pk`, `tenant_id`, `name`, `scope_selector jsonb`, `checks jsonb`, `on_error_status`, `on_warn_status`, `active bool`, `created_by` | Versions immutable; reports pin exact versions (FR-A3.4) |
| `data.quality_report` | `report_id pk (qrp-ulid)`, `recording_id fk`, `version int`, `rule_set_ref jsonb`, `workflow_run_ref`, `summary jsonb`, `started_at`, `finished_at` | One row per validation pass; latest = max(version) |
| `data.quality_finding` | `finding_id pk (fnd-ulid)`, `report_id fk`, `recording_id fk`, `code`, `severity`, `stream_ref?`, `file_ref?`, `measured jsonb`, `threshold jsonb`, `message`, `waived_by?`, `waived_reason?`, `evidence_object_key?` | **Searchable metadata (FR-A3.5):** indexed `(code, severity)`, `(recording_id)`; replicated to M18 index via outbox |
| `data.quality_status_history` | `id pk`, `recording_id fk`, `from_status`, `to_status`, `actor` (`svc-01\|usr-…`), `reason`, `waiver_refs text[]`, `at` | Append-only |
| `data.remediation_task` | `task_id pk (rmd-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `project_id`, `kind` (4-kind enum FR-A3.6), `recording_id?`, `finding_id?`, `campaign_id?`, `summary`, `description`, `status` (`open\|in_progress\|resolved\|cancelled`), `assignee_id?`, `due_date?`, `priority`, `resolution_note?`, `outcome?`, `etag_version`, timestamps | Idx `(project_id, status, kind)`, `(assignee_id, status)` |
| `data.ingestion_status_mv` | keyed `(project_id, campaign_id, group_key)` | Read model backing M05-11/RPT-01 and M04 ingest summaries |
| `data.signed_url_cache` | `(recording_id, object_key, user_id) pk`, `url_encrypted`, `expires_at` | TTL cache; encrypted at rest, never logged |
| `data.outbox` | per §1.4.4 | Shared; M05 partition by aggregate `rec-`/`ing-` |

**ER:** `campaign (M04) 1—n ingest_manifest 1—n manifest_file`; `campaign 1—n ingest_session 1—n ingest_part`; `ingest_session 1—n recording 1—n recording_file / modality_stream / quality_report 1—n quality_finding`; `recording 1—n quality_status_history`; `remediation_task n—1 recording/finding/campaign`; `recording 1—n M06 lineage edges` (M06-owned). Lineage **root** is the recording row (FR-A2.4); M05 writes no lineage edges itself.

**Volumetrics & partitioning:** recordings 10⁴–10⁶ rows/tenant/year, findings ×10 — `data.recording`, `data.quality_report`, `data.quality_finding`, `data.quality_status_history` are range-partitioned by tenant + month; `data.ingest_part` is high-churn (10⁷+) — partitioned by session-creation month and dropped 30 days after terminal session state (parts are bookkeeping, not provenance; full-object checksums persist on `recording_file`).

**Retention (DA-11):** raw recordings default **indefinite retention** (BR-01) regardless of quality status; `rejected` recordings keep payload + metadata unless tenant policy moves them to `archived` (cold storage class) after the configured window — never `purged-metadata-retained` for recordings referenced by episode/snapshot lineage. SVC-15 executes archival (storage-class transition only; object lock stays), records `data.recording.archived` audit events, and GCs `uploads/` staging + expired `signed_url_cache` rows. Quality reports/findings retained with their recording; remediation tasks archived 24 months after terminal state.

**Processing logic:** (1) CRUD + transactional outbox per §2.5.3; (2) **SVC-01 batch validation pipeline** (WAP per §0.7.2): finalize multipart → structural validation (doctor/recover) → normalize to indexed MCAP → stream/modality extraction → rule evaluation → report + status in per-recording transactions; quarantined data never enters M06/M08 visibility (audit-branch semantics); (3) **dedup index** maintenance on `full_sha256`; (4) **MV refresh** event-driven + 10-min schedule; (5) **session hygiene sweep** (expiry aborts, staging GC); (6) **findings replication** to M18 index (FR-A3.5) via outbox relay.

#### 2.5.5 Permission Design

| Permission key | PA | TA | OE | RD (default) | Persona templates granting it |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `data.ingest:initiate` (manifests, sessions, parts, complete, abort) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (member projects) | Robotics Data Engineer (`ingest:*`), Data Collection Lead (`ingest:initiate`) |
| `data.recording:read` (lists, detail, reports, RPT-01, content URLs¹) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Robotics Data Engineer, Data Collection Lead, Annotation QA Lead, Program Manager (report slice) |
| `data.quality:update` (M05-8 status changes, duplicate override) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (grant) | Robotics Data Engineer (`quality:*`) |
| `data.quality_rule:create/update/delete` | ✓ | ✓ | — | optional grant | Robotics Data Engineer; tenant-default rules TA-only |
| `data.quality_rule:read` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | all data personas |
| `data.remediation:create/read/update` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Robotics Data Engineer, Data Collection Lead (recollection), Annotation QA Lead (privacy_review read) |

¹ M05-12 additionally enforces the asset-level restriction gate: campaign `usage_restrictions`/privacy tags trigger the deny-wins `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` re-check (§0.5.3) — role grant alone is insufficient for restricted content.

**Granularity:** project scope primary; quality rules are tenant-scoped (M05-9 path) with campaign-level selectors; asset-level overrides restrict content access (signed URLs) and governance-block visibility on restricted campaigns' recordings. OE: no business-domain grants — storage *infrastructure* health is M02/M17 territory (§0.5.3).

**Credential scoping:** upload tokens are **session-scoped** (single session prefix, PUT+register only, TTL 12 h renewable via M05-3, hashed at rest, revoked on abort/complete/expiry); signed read URLs are **time-limited** (default 600 s, max 3600 s), user-bound, minted per request from the tenant storage binding's credentials and never persisted in plaintext or logs (§1.4.7). SVC-01 holds a service identity with `data.ingest:initiate` + `data.quality:update` at service scope (M03 §2.3.5) — it is the only writer of `pending_validation→*` transitions. SVC-15 holds `data.recording:read` + archival service keys.

#### 2.5.6 Logging Design

| Level | Trigger examples |
|---|---|
| DEBUG | per-part checksum verify detail, rule-evaluation traces, MV refresh timing (off in prod) |
| INFO | session opened/completed/aborted; recording created; every quality-status transition; report produced; remediation transitions; signed URLs issued (count only) |
| WARN | unexpected manifest file; part checksum retry; salvage (`recover`) applied with delta; duplicate detected; validation backlog above threshold; token re-issue |
| ERROR | session failed (per-file causes); SVC-01 pipeline step failure after retries; outbox relay failure; storage binding unreachable |
| FATAL | object storage credentials invalid at startup; `data.*` schema unreachable |

Structured JSON per §1.4.6 with module fields: `session_id`, `recording_id`, `manifest_id`, `campaign_id`, `file_path?`, `part_number?`, `bytes`, `checksum_status` (`verified|mismatch|pending`), `quality_status_from/to`, `rule_set_ref`, `finding_code?`, `workflow_run_ref`, `result`, `latency_ms`. **Masking (§1.4.7):** payload contents never logged — assets appear only as `{recording_id, byte_size, checksum}` summaries; signed URLs and upload tokens never logged (issuance logged by count + TTL); operator notes/free text by reference id + length; emails masked.

**Shipping:** stdout JSON → platform log collector (per §1.4.6); SVC-01 worker logs carry the same `trace_id` as the originating `:complete` request via `traceparent` through M16 task context; audit via M03-11 (batched).

**Audit events (M03-11):** `data.manifest.created`, `data.ingest_session.opened`, `data.ingest_session.completed` (recording id list), `data.ingest_session.aborted` (reason), `data.recording.created`, `data.recording.accepted`, `data.recording.quarantined`, `data.recording.rejected`, `data.quality_status.changed` (from/to, reason, waivers — every manual M05-8 action), `data.quality_rule.changed` (version diff), `data.remediation.created/resolved`, `data.recording.content_accessed` (file summary — feeds RPT-11 access review via M03-9), `data.recording.archived` (SVC-15). All carry actor, scope `{tenant, project}`, before/after summaries per §1.4.6 — supporting AC-01/AC-14.

---

### 2.6 M06 — Alignment, Episode & Segmentation Management

#### 2.6.1 Module Overview

M06 converts accepted raw recordings into the platform's **canonical Episode/Trajectory** business object (`epi-{ulid}`, Table 0.3 #3): multi-modal streams are aligned under governed, per-modality-pair **Alignment Tolerance Policies** (FR-A4.2, DA-05), normalized into an **RLDS-style step structure** (observation/action/outcome + boundary flags) persisted as **LeRobot-style chunked Parquet + encoded video at rest** (FR-A4.5/A4.6, DA-04, per §0.7.2), with source timestamps, alignment assumptions, calibration/coordinate-frame references preserved on every derived record (FR-A4.1/A4.4) and full raw→derived lineage (FR-A4.7, BR-02). M06 also owns **segmentation** of long recordings into episodes/events/phases/task attempts (manual + assisted via SVC-03 — FR-A5.1/A5.2), **hierarchical action labels** (task→phase→atomic_action, no universal taxonomy lock-in — FR-A5.5), and **versioned scenario tags governed by tenant taxonomies** (FR-A5.4/A5.6, DA-06). Episodes failing tolerance are flagged `alignment_failed` and excluded from snapshot eligibility (FR-A4.3).

**Owns:** Alignment Tolerance Policy, Episode (+ versions, step store), Segment, Scenario Tag assignments, Taxonomy (+ versions, DA-06), Lineage Edge (raw→derived), calibration-set references, embodiment feature-schema registry (per §0.7.2), synchronized stream manifests (M06-5).
**Excludes:** recording intake/quality (M05); human labels and QA (M07 — segments and scenario tags are *structural/engineering* metadata, annotation sets are M07's); snapshot membership/eligibility enforcement (M08 reads the `alignment_failed`/quality flags); embedding/search (M18); 4D playback UI (M19 — consumes M06-5 manifests); workflow execution (M16; executors SVC-02 alignment, SVC-03 suggestions).

**Roles & personas:** Robotics Data Engineer (primary — persona template `alignment:*` per §0.2.4: policies, alignment jobs, episode curation, segmentation), Annotation QA Lead (taxonomy proposals — `taxonomy:propose`; segment review), Tenant Administrator (taxonomy approval per DA-06, policy governance), Model/Evaluation engineers (read consumers).

**Upstream:** M05 (accepted recordings + modality streams — edge M05→M06 per §0.5.2), M04 (campaign default policy hints), M16 (workflow runs), SVC-02 Alignment & Episode Builder, SVC-03 Pre-annotation & Segmentation Suggestion Runner, M01/M02/M03 (session/scope/authz-audit).
**Downstream:** M07 (episodes available for annotation — M16 §2.16.3 (M16-9) output registration applies to workflow-produced episodes), M08 (catalog sync + snapshot eligibility flags), M18 (episodes/segments/tags for indexing and distributions), M19 (timeline manifests), M15 (alignment failures convertible), M20 (notifications).

**FRD coverage:** FR-A4.1–A4.7, FR-A5.1–A5.6, BR-02 (lineage), DA-04 (canonical schema), DA-05 (tolerance policies), DA-06 (taxonomy governance), BP-01 steps 4–5, AC-02 (with M19).

#### 2.6.2 Functional & UI/UX Design

**Primary functions**

| # | Function | Description | FRD |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Tolerance policy governance | Tenant-scoped policies, selectable per campaign/embodiment/modality; per-modality-pair `max_skew_ms` entries; default tolerance = half the slowest stream's period (per §0.7.2) | FR-A4.2, DA-05 |
| F2 | Alignment & normalization jobs | Approximate-time matching over accepted recordings → canonical episodes; 202 + workflow_run_ref (SVC-02 via M16) | FR-A4.5, FR-A2.2→A4 route |
| F3 | Timing & assumption preservation | Source timestamps, per-stream clock domain/timestamp source, slop used, reference clock, resample strategy stored per episode | FR-A4.1 |
| F4 | Alignment QA flagging | Frames exceeding pair tolerance flagged; episodes above policy `max_flagged_frame_ratio` → `alignment_failed`, excluded from snapshot eligibility | FR-A4.3 |
| F5 | Calibration & frames | Episodes reference the calibration set (camera intrinsics + extrinsic tf tree + URDF revision) active at capture (per §0.7.2) | FR-A4.4 |
| F6 | Canonical episode | RLDS-style steps (observation, action, outcome labels, is_first/is_last/is_terminal; reward/discount optional) + episode metadata; supports observations, robot state, actions/commands, language instructions, environment context, annotations refs, outcomes | FR-A4.5, FR-A4.6, DA-04 |
| F7 | Lineage | Append-only raw→derived edges with transformation descriptors for every episode/version | FR-A4.7, BR-02 |
| F8 | Segmentation | Manual segments (episodes/events/phases/task attempts) + assisted suggestions (SVC-03) with accept/edit/reject review | FR-A5.1, FR-A5.2 |
| F9 | Source referencing | Every segment keeps source recording ref + source-timestamp range | FR-A5.3 |
| F10 | Scenario tagging | Tag dimensions: task, environment, object, actor, robot_posture, instruction_type, outcome, failure_type; assignments pin taxonomy versions | FR-A5.4, FR-A5.6 |
| F11 | Hierarchical action labels | task→phase→atomic_action label paths on segments without enforcing a universal taxonomy (per-tenant taxonomies, DA-06) | FR-A5.5 |
| F12 | Taxonomy governance | QA Leads propose versions, TA approves; historical tags keep their taxonomy version | FR-A5.6, DA-06 |

**UI pages**

**P1 — Alignment Policy Admin** (tenant-scoped; bindings `M06-1`). List + editor. Editor fields:

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| name | text | 3–120, unique per tenant | — | "manip-default-v1" | required; 409 `E_CONFLICT` |
| scope_selector | structured (campaign_ids[], embodiment?, modalities[]?) | refs resolvable | — | — | ≥1 selector or `tenant_default=true` |
| reference_clock | select | `hardware \| driver_receive \| arrival` (prefer hardware per §0.7.2) | hardware | — | required |
| pair_tolerances[] | repeating (modality_a, modality_b, max_skew_ms) | modalities from FR-A2.3 enum | — | "video × joint_state × 20" | pair unique; max_skew_ms > 0; else `E_VALIDATION` |
| default_tolerance_rule | select | `half_slowest_period \| fixed_ms` (+ value) | half_slowest_period | — | fixed requires value > 0 |
| max_flagged_frame_ratio | number | 0–1 | 0.02 | "0.02" | required (FR-A4.3 gate) |
| resample_strategy | select | `nearest \| zero_order_hold \| interpolate_numeric` | nearest | — | required |
| require_calibration_set | bool | — | true | — | — |

Real-time pair-duplication checks; on-submit cross-field rules. Deleting a policy referenced by episodes → blocked dialog (`E_EPI_POLICY_IN_USE`, offers deactivate).

**P2 — Alignment Job Launcher** (on recording detail; binding `M06-2 POST`). Fields: policy (resolved default shown: campaign > embodiment > tenant_default; overridable), boundary source (`whole_recording | manual_ranges[] | suggestion_set ref`), calibration_set (auto-resolved from capture metadata; manual override permission-gated), target embodiment schema (auto from registry). Submit → 202 toast with `workflow_run_ref` + progress chip on the recording (polls M16-3). Disabled with tooltip when recording quality status ∉ {accepted, accepted_with_warnings} (pre-empts `E_EPI_RECORDING_NOT_ACCEPTED`).

**P3 — Episode Browser + Detail** (bindings `M06-3`, `M06-4`, `M06-5`). Faceted list: status (`building/ready/alignment_failed` chips), campaign, task tag, embodiment, duration, flagged-frame ratio, tag filters, snapshot-eligibility badge (grey when `alignment_failed` — FR-A4.3). Detail tabs: **Overview** (metadata, alignment summary: policy ref, slop per pair, flagged ratio, reference clock, calibration set link); **Steps** (schema preview: observation/action/outcome field tree from the embodiment schema + step count, sample rows); **Segments** (P4 embed); **Tags** (P5 embed); **Lineage** (graph from M06-4: recording → transforms → episode versions, BR-02); **Open in Workbench** (M19 deep-link, AC-02). `alignment_failed` episodes render a red diagnosis panel (top offending pairs, gap histogram refs) with actions "re-run with policy…" (P2 prefilled) and "convert to remediation/failure" (M05-10 / M15-1).

**P4 — Segment Editor** (bindings `M06-6`, suggestions via `M06-7`). Timeline strip per modality with draggable range handles. Segment table fields:

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| level | select | `episode_boundary \| event \| phase \| atomic_action \| task_attempt` | event | — | required (FR-A5.1) |
| start_ts / end_ts | timestamp pair | source-clock ns | selection | — | required; start < end; within episode range → `E_EPI_RANGE_INVALID` |
| action_label_path | hierarchical picker | `task/phase/atomic_action` path from action taxonomy version | — | "pick_place/reach/grasp" | optional; path depth ≤3 (FR-A5.5); term must exist in pinned version → `E_EPI_TAXONOMY_VERSION` |
| parent_segment_id | select | existing higher-level segment | auto | — | child range ⊆ parent range |
| outcome | select | `success \| failure \| partial \| unknown` | unknown | — | — |
| note | text | ≤500 | — | — | sanitized |

"Suggest segments" button → `M06-7 POST` (202; suggestion chips render on completion with confidence; accept/edit/reject per chip — accepted chips become M06-6 creates with `suggestion_ref`, FR-A5.2). Lock banner when episode is referenced by a published snapshot: "Segments frozen (referenced by dss-…); create a new episode version to re-segment" (`E_EPI_SEGMENT_LOCKED`).

**P5 — Tag Panel** (binding `M06-8 PUT`). Dimension-grouped tag chips (8 dimensions, FR-A5.4); add-tag popover: dimension select, taxonomy version indicator ("scenario-tax v4 (active)"), term path autocomplete, optional value. Revisions show history drawer (who/when/version pinned — FR-A5.6). Stale-version warning chip when an assignment pins a superseded taxonomy version, with one-click "re-tag under v5" (creates new assignment, history preserved).

**P6 — Taxonomy Governance** (tenant-scoped; binding `M06-9`). Taxonomy list (kind: scenario/action/failure; current active version; pending proposals badge). Version composer: term tree editor (add/rename/deprecate terms, max depth 3 for action kind), diff vs active version, submit-for-approval. Approval queue for TA: side-by-side diff, approve/reject with reason (DA-06). Create-taxonomy form: name (unique per tenant), kind enum, description.

**Validation & exception handling (module-wide):** real-time field checks; segment geometry validated client-side then authoritatively on submit; all free text sanitized. Network failure → drafts preserved (segment edits queue locally, replay on reconnect with If-Match guard); `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → read-only with key tooltip; `E_RATE_LIMITED` on suggestion jobs → cooldown chip with `Retry-After`; `E_VALIDATION`/`E_EPI_*` → field/row-anchored messages; `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED` on concurrent segment edits → merge dialog showing the other editor's change.

#### 2.6.3 Interface Design

All endpoints follow §1.4. Module error namespace `E_EPI_*`. Authz M03-10; audit M03-11. Long operations return **202 + workflow_run_ref** (M16).

**Normative state machines (exact):**

```text
Episode.status:           building ──SVC-02──▶ ready | alignment_failed
  · re-derivation (new alignment run over same recording/boundaries) creates version n+1 (Table 0.3 #3); versions immutable once terminal
  · alignment_failed: readable for diagnosis; excluded from snapshot eligibility (FR-A4.3; M08 enforces via eligibility flag)
Segment mutability:       mutable while owning episode version is NOT referenced by a published snapshot (M08); afterwards frozen (E_EPI_SEGMENT_LOCKED)
TaxonomyVersion.status:   draft ──submit──▶ proposed ──approve──▶ active ──(next approval)──▶ superseded ; proposed ──reject──▶ draft (DA-06)
```

**Module error codes (`E_EPI_*`)**

| Code | HTTP | Raised when |
|---|---|---|
| `E_EPI_RECORDING_NOT_ACCEPTED` | 409 | Alignment/suggestion job on a recording whose quality status ∉ {accepted, accepted_with_warnings} (WAP gate, per §0.7.2) |
| `E_EPI_POLICY_IN_USE` | 409 | DELETE of a policy referenced by episodes (deactivate instead) |
| `E_EPI_CALIBRATION_MISSING` | 422 | Policy requires a calibration set and none is resolvable for the recording (FR-A4.4) |
| `E_EPI_NOT_READY` | 409 | Content-urls/manifest requested on a `building` episode |
| `E_EPI_RANGE_INVALID` | 422 | Segment range outside episode bounds, start ≥ end, or child range ⊄ parent |
| `E_EPI_SEGMENT_LOCKED` | 409 | Segment create/update/delete on an episode referenced by a published snapshot |
| `E_EPI_TAXONOMY_VERSION` | 422 | Tag/label references a term absent from the pinned taxonomy version, or pins a non-existent version |
| `E_EPI_TAXONOMY_STATE` | 409 | Taxonomy version action illegal for its status (e.g. approve a draft, edit an active version) |
| Shared | — | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_STATE_INVALID`, `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED`, `E_CONFLICT`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_RESTRICTED_DATA`, `E_RATE_LIMITED` per §1.4.3 |

**M06-1 `POST /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/alignment-policies` · `GET` (same) · `GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/alignment-policies/{policy_id}`** — tolerance policies (DA-05).
Permission: `data.alignment_policy:create/read/update/delete` (create/update/delete: TA or RD with grant).
POST body (compact):

```json
{ "name": "manip-default-v1", "tenant_default": false,
  "scope_selector": { "campaign_ids": ["cmp-…"], "embodiment": "humanoid-g1", "modalities": null },
  "reference_clock": "hardware", "resample_strategy": "nearest", "require_calibration_set": true,
  "pair_tolerances": [ { "modality_a": "video", "modality_b": "joint_state", "max_skew_ms": 20 },
                       { "modality_a": "video", "modality_b": "tactile_force", "max_skew_ms": 10 } ],
  "default_tolerance_rule": { "kind": "half_slowest_period" }, "max_flagged_frame_ratio": 0.02 }
```

Processing (POST): 1. authz at tenant scope; 2. validate modalities against the FR-A2.3 enum, pair uniqueness, ratio ∈ (0,1], positive skews → `E_VALIDATION`; 3. **Tx:** insert `data.alignment_policy` version 1 (policies are versioned; PATCH creates version n+1, prior versions immutable — episodes pin exact versions for auditability, FR-A4.2) + outbox `data.alignment_policy.changed` — commit; 4. audit; **201**. DELETE deactivates; hard delete only when never referenced → `E_EPI_POLICY_IN_USE`. GET list filters: `active, embodiment, campaign_id, tenant_default`.

**M06-2 `POST /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/alignment-jobs`** — launch alignment + normalization (202 → workflow_run_ref).
Permission: `data.alignment:execute` (project scope of the recording).
Body: `{ "policy_id": "alp-…"?, "policy_version": 3?, "boundaries": { "source": "whole_recording" | "manual_ranges" | "suggestion_set", "ranges": [{"start_ts": "…", "end_ts": "…"}]?, "suggestion_set_id": "sgs-…"? }, "calibration_set_id": "cal-…"?, "rederive_of_episode_id": "epi-…"? }`
Processing:

1. Authz; recording quality gate → `E_EPI_RECORDING_NOT_ACCEPTED` (only `accepted`/`accepted_with_warnings` recordings are alignable — edge M05→M06 per §0.5.2).
2. Resolve policy: explicit (must be active version) else selector resolution campaign > embodiment > tenant_default → `E_VALIDATION` when none resolves; resolve calibration set from capture metadata or explicit id; `require_calibration_set` unmet → `E_EPI_CALIBRATION_MISSING` (FR-A4.4).
3. Validate boundaries (ranges within recording span, non-overlapping) → `E_EPI_RANGE_INVALID`; `rederive_of_episode_id` must reference an episode of this recording (version bump semantics).
4. **Tx:** insert placeholder `data.episode` rows (one per boundary range; status `building`, version = prior+1 on re-derivation else 1, pinned `policy_id@version`, `calibration_set_id`, embodiment schema ref) + outbox `data.alignment.requested` — **commit**.
5. Outbox relay launches M16 workflow (template `alignment_normalization`, executor SVC-02); **202** `{ "workflow_run_ref": "wfr-…", "episode_ids": ["epi-…"], "policy_ref": "alp-…@3" }`. Idempotency-Key replays the original 202.
6. SVC-02 per episode: approximate-time matching with per-pair slop (default half-slowest-period, per §0.7.2) on the preferred clock; builds RLDS-style steps; writes chunked Parquet + AV1 (H.264 fallback) video with per-frame decode timestamps in Parquet (DA-04, per §0.7.2); computes flagged-frame ratio → status `ready` or `alignment_failed` (FR-A4.3); persists alignment summary (slop used, pairs, flagged ratio, reference clock, resample strategy — FR-A4.1) and **lineage edges** (recording → transform `align@svc-02:vX` → episode version, BR-02); each terminal write is one tx + outbox `data.episode.created` / `data.episode.alignment_failed`. Output registration to consumers flows via M16-9.

**M06-3 `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/episodes` · `GET /api/v1/episodes/{episode_id}`** — list / detail.
Permission: `data.episode:read` (+ deny-wins `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` for restricted-campaign descendants, §0.5.3).
List filters: `status (building|ready|alignment_failed), campaign_id, recording_id, tag (dimension:term), action_label, embodiment, min/max_duration_s, snapshot_eligible, created_after/before, q, sort`. Item: `{episode_id, version, status, recording_id, campaign_id, time_range, duration_s, step_count, flagged_frame_ratio, policy_ref, tags_summary, segment_counts, snapshot_eligible}`. Detail (`?version=n`, default latest) adds alignment summary, calibration ref, embodiment schema ref, storage refs (chunk/file indices — summaries only per §1.4.7), versions[], outcome labels. `building` episodes return live `workflow_run_ref`.

**M06-4 `GET /api/v1/episodes/{episode_id}/lineage`** — raw→derived graph (FR-A4.7, BR-02).
Permission: `data.episode:read`. Params: `direction=upstream|both` (default upstream), `depth` (default full).
Response: `{ "nodes": [ {"ref": "rec-…", "type": "raw_recording"}, {"ref": "epi-…@2", "type": "episode"} ], "edges": [ { "from": "rec-…", "to": "epi-…@2", "transform": { "kind": "align_normalize", "executor": "svc-02@1.4", "policy_ref": "alp-…@3", "workflow_run_ref": "wfr-…", "at": "…" } } ] }`. Edges are append-only; downstream expansion (snapshots, training runs) is delegated to M08/M12 lineage endpoints and merged client-side — M06 serves only edges it owns (single-writer rule §1.2).

**M06-5 `GET /api/v1/episodes/{episode_id}/content-urls`** — signed URLs + synchronized stream manifest.
Permission: `data.episode:read` + restriction re-check (`E_RESTRICTED_DATA`).
Params: `streams?`, `ttl_s` (default 600, max 3600). Episode must be terminal → `E_EPI_NOT_READY`.
Processing: 1. authz + gate; 2. resolve the episode's chunk/file slices (episodes are logical slices of chunked files, per §0.7.2) and mint signed GETs (signed-URL cache as M05-12); 3. audit `data.episode.content_accessed`; 4. **200** `{ "manifest": { "time_base": "source_ns", "episode_range": {…}, "streams": [ { "key": "video/cam_head", "modality": "video", "container": "mp4_av1", "url": "…", "byte_range": "…", "frame_index_ref": "parquet:decode_ts", "expires_at": "…" }, { "key": "steps", "container": "parquet", "url": "…", "row_range": [120, 870] } ], "calibration_set_url": "…" } }` — the same manifest M19 consumes for synchronized 4D review (AC-02).

**M06-6 `POST /api/v1/episodes/{episode_id}/segments` · `GET` (same) · `PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/segments/{segment_id}`** — manual segmentation (FR-A5.1–A5.3).
Permission: `data.segment:update` (create/patch/delete), `data.episode:read` (GET).
POST body: `{ "level": "phase", "start_ts": "…", "end_ts": "…", "parent_segment_id": "seg-…"?, "action_label": { "taxonomy_id": "tax-…", "taxonomy_version": 4, "path": "pick_place/reach/grasp" }?, "outcome": "success"?, "note": "…"?, "suggestion_ref": "sgs-…#3"? }`
Processing:

1. Authz; **snapshot lock check**: owning episode version referenced by a published M08 snapshot ⇒ `E_EPI_SEGMENT_LOCKED` (mutable-until-published rule).
2. Validate level enum (FR-A5.1), range within episode bounds and parent containment → `E_EPI_RANGE_INVALID`; segment inherits and stores `recording_id` + source-timestamp range (FR-A5.3).
3. Validate `action_label` path against the pinned taxonomy version (≤3 levels task/phase/atomic_action, FR-A5.5) → `E_EPI_TAXONOMY_VERSION`.
4. **Tx:** insert `data.segment` + outbox `data.segment.changed` (M18 re-index; suggestion acceptance recorded on the suggestion set when `suggestion_ref` present — FR-A5.2 review trail) — commit; audit; **201**.
PATCH/DELETE: If-Match required; same gates (lock, range, label); DELETE is soft (tombstone retained for suggestion/audit reconstruction). GET filters: `level, action_label_prefix, outcome, parent_segment_id`.

**M06-7 `POST /api/v1/recordings/{recording_id}/segmentation-suggestions`** — assisted segmentation job (202, FR-A5.2).
Permission: `data.alignment:execute`.
Body: `{ "generator": "action-segmenter@2.1"?, "target_levels": ["episode_boundary","atomic_action"], "episode_id": "epi-…"? (intra-episode mode), "params": {…}? }`
Processing: 1. authz; recording quality gate (`E_EPI_RECORDING_NOT_ACCEPTED`); generator must be a registered suggestion generator (DA-13 registry, served by SVC-03) → `E_VALIDATION`; rate limit per project (`E_RATE_LIMITED`); 2. **Tx:** insert `data.suggestion_set` (status `running`, generator identity/version pinned) + outbox → M16 workflow (executor SVC-03) — commit; **202** `{suggestion_set_id, workflow_run_ref}`. SVC-03 writes suggestion items `{level, start_ts, end_ts, proposed_label_path?, confidence}` and flips status `completed|failed`. Suggestions are **never auto-applied**: acceptance flows through M06-6 (`suggestion_ref`) or M06-2 boundaries (`suggestion_set` source) — mirroring BR-06's human-review stance for machine outputs. GET of the set rides on M06-3 episode detail / recording detail embeds.

**M06-8 `PUT /api/v1/episodes/{episode_id}/tags`** — apply/revise scenario tags (FR-A5.4/A5.6).
Permission: `data.tag:update`.
Body: `{ "assignments": [ { "dimension": "task", "taxonomy_id": "tax-…", "taxonomy_version": 4, "term_path": "kitchen/drawer_open", "value": null }, { "dimension": "outcome", "taxonomy_id": "tax-…", "taxonomy_version": 4, "term_path": "outcome/success" } ] }`
Processing:

1. Authz; If-Match on episode tag-set etag.
2. Validate each: dimension ∈ exact FR-A5.4 enum {task, environment, object, actor, robot_posture, instruction_type, outcome, failure_type} → `E_VALIDATION`; taxonomy version exists and term path present in it → `E_EPI_TAXONOMY_VERSION`; version need not be `active` for **re-assertion** of historical tags, but new terms must come from `active` versions (DA-06).
3. **Tx:** declarative diff vs stored assignments; superseded assignments are **closed, not deleted** (validity interval + actor recorded — FR-A5.6 reproducibility); insert new rows pinning `taxonomy_version`; outbox `data.episode.tags_changed` (M18 re-index, M04 coverage cache) — commit.
4. Audit with diff summary; **200** effective assignment set + history cursor.

**M06-9 `POST /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/taxonomies` · `GET` (same) · `POST /api/v1/taxonomies/{taxonomy_id}/versions` · `GET /api/v1/taxonomies/{taxonomy_id}`** — taxonomy governance (DA-06).
Permission: create taxonomy `data.taxonomy:propose` (QA Lead persona) — activation governance below; read `data.taxonomy:read`.
Create body: `{ "name": "scenario-tax", "kind": "scenario|action|failure", "description": "…" }` → **201** taxonomy with no active version.
**Versions endpoint** (single path per §1.5 contract — operation discriminator in body, no path drift):

- `{ "action": "propose", "base_version": 4?, "terms": { "tree": [ { "term": "kitchen", "children": [ { "term": "drawer_open" } ] } ] }, "change_note": "…" }` — permission `data.taxonomy:propose`. Validates tree (unique sibling terms, depth ≤3 for `action` kind — FR-A5.5; deprecations marked, never removed); **Tx:** insert version n+1 status `proposed` + outbox; **201**.
- `{ "action": "approve", "version": 5, "reason": "…" }` — permission `data.taxonomy:approve` (TA per DA-06). Version must be `proposed` → `E_EPI_TAXONOMY_STATE`; **Tx:** version → `active`, prior active → `superseded`, outbox `data.taxonomy.approved` — commit. Existing assignments keep their pinned versions (FR-A5.6); M20 notifies tag owners of superseded-version usage.
- `{ "action": "reject", "version": 5, "reason": "…" }` — permission `data.taxonomy:approve`; `proposed → draft` with reason; **200**.

GET taxonomy returns head + versions[] (status, term counts, change notes) + `?version=n` full term tree. Concurrency: version creation serializes per taxonomy via head-row lock.

**Concurrency & idempotency summary:** POSTs accept `Idempotency-Key`; alignment/suggestion job replay returns the original 202 refs; segment and tag writes are If-Match-guarded; episode versions and policy/taxonomy versions are immutable once terminal/approved; snapshot-lock checks consult the M08 reference read model (event-fed, deny-on-stale ⇒ recheck synchronously before reject).

#### 2.6.4 Data Design

**Control plane:** PostgreSQL schema **`data.*`** (M06-owned tables below). **Payload plane:** canonical episode store under the tenant's storage binding, LeRobot-v3-style chunked layout (per §0.7.2) — episodes are logical slices, not files (DA-04):

```text
{tenant_binding_bucket}/canonical/{project_id}/
  data/chunk-{0000}/file-{000000}.parquet        ── frame-aligned RLDS-style steps, many episodes per file
  videos/{camera_key}/chunk-{0000}/file-{000000}.mp4   ── AV1 (H.264 fallback), random-access keyframes (per §0.7.2)
  meta/episodes.parquet · tasks.parquet · stats.parquet ── unified Parquet metadata incl. episode→(chunk,file,row/frame-range) index
  calibration/{calibration_set_id}/…             ── intrinsics + extrinsics tf tree + URDF revision (per §0.7.2)
```

Step columns (DA-04, RLDS-style per §0.7.2): `observation.*` (per embodiment schema: camera decode_ts refs, depth/point_cloud refs, imu, tactile_force, joint_state, robot_status, environment_context), `action.*` (motor_command / action_token spaces), `language_instruction?`, `outcome?`, `reward?/discount?` (optional for teleop), `is_first/is_last/is_terminal`, `source_ts_ns`, `aligned_ts_ns`, `flagged bool` (tolerance breach, FR-A4.3). Cross-embodiment normalization happens only at training-export time, never at rest (per §0.7.2).

| Table | Key columns (abridged) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `data.alignment_policy` | `policy_id pk (alp-ulid)` + `version`, `tenant_id`, `name`, `tenant_default bool`, `scope_selector jsonb`, `reference_clock`, `resample_strategy`, `require_calibration_set`, `pair_tolerances jsonb`, `default_tolerance_rule jsonb`, `max_flagged_frame_ratio`, `active bool`, `created_by` | Versions immutable (DA-05); unique `(tenant_id, name, version)` |
| `data.episode` | `episode_id pk (epi-ulid)` + `version int`, `tenant_id`, `project_id`, `campaign_id`, `recording_id fk`, `status` (`building\|ready\|alignment_failed`), `time_range_src tstzrange`, `duration_s`, `step_count`, `policy_ref` (`alp-…@v`), `calibration_set_id fk`, `embodiment_schema_ref`, `alignment_summary jsonb` (slop per pair, flagged_ratio, reference_clock — FR-A4.1), `storage_slice jsonb` (chunk/file/row+frame ranges), `snapshot_eligible bool` (derived: status=ready ∧ recording accepted*), `outcome_labels jsonb?`, `workflow_run_ref`, `tag_etag`, `etag_version`, timestamps | **Partitioned by `(tenant_id, month(created_at))`**; idx `(project_id, status)`, `(recording_id)`, `(campaign_id)`, partial idx on `snapshot_eligible` |
| `data.segment` | `segment_id pk (seg-ulid)`, `episode_id+version fk`, `recording_id`, `level` (5-level enum FR-A5.1), `start_ts_ns`, `end_ts_ns`, `parent_segment_id?`, `action_label jsonb` (taxonomy_id, version, path — FR-A5.5), `outcome`, `note`, `suggestion_ref?`, `deleted_at?` (tombstone), `created_by`, `etag_version` | Idx `(episode_id, level)`, `(recording_id)`, GIN on `action_label`; FR-A5.3 source refs denormalized |
| `data.suggestion_set` | `set_id pk (sgs-ulid)`, `recording_id`, `episode_id?`, `generator_identity`, `generator_version`, `status` (`running\|completed\|failed`), `params jsonb`, `workflow_run_ref`, `created_by` | DA-13 generator pinning |
| `data.suggestion_item` | `(set_id, ord) pk`, `level`, `start_ts_ns`, `end_ts_ns`, `proposed_label jsonb?`, `confidence`, `review_status` (`pending\|accepted\|edited\|rejected`), `applied_segment_id?` | FR-A5.2 review trail |
| `data.tag_assignment` | `assignment_id pk (tga-ulid)`, `episode_id`, `dimension` (8-dim enum FR-A5.4), `taxonomy_id`, `taxonomy_version int` (pinned — FR-A5.6), `term_path`, `value?`, `valid_from`, `valid_to?` (closed, never deleted), `actor_id` | Idx `(episode_id, dimension)` where `valid_to is null`, `(taxonomy_id, taxonomy_version)`, GIN on `term_path` |
| `data.taxonomy` / `data.taxonomy_version` | `taxonomy_id pk (tax-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `name`, `kind` (`scenario\|action\|failure`), `active_version?` / `(taxonomy_id, version) pk`, `status` (`draft\|proposed\|active\|superseded`), `terms jsonb` (tree, deprecations marked), `change_note`, `proposed_by`, `approved_by?`, `decided_at?` | DA-06; unique `(tenant_id, name)`; versions immutable after approval |
| `data.lineage_edge` | `edge_id pk (lin-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `from_ref` (`rec-…`), `to_ref` (`epi-…@v`), `transform jsonb` (kind, executor+version, policy_ref, workflow_run_ref, at) | **Append-only** (BR-02, FR-A4.7); no UPDATE/DELETE grants; idx `(from_ref)`, `(to_ref)` |
| `data.calibration_set` | `calibration_set_id pk (cal-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `robot_id`, `intrinsics_ref`, `extrinsics_ref`, `urdf_revision`, `valid_from`, `valid_to?`, `source_attachment_ref?` (MCAP attachment, per §0.7.2) | Versioned first-class (per §0.7.2); referenced by episodes (FR-A4.4); **rows created by SVC-01/SVC-02 extracting MCAP calibration attachments at intake** — no public CRUD endpoint in v1 |
| `data.embodiment_schema` | `schema_ref pk`, `tenant_id`, `embodiment`, `version`, `observation_spec jsonb`, `action_spec jsonb`, `camera_set jsonb` | Per-embodiment feature schemas (per §0.7.2, OXE lesson); **seeded per tenant at provisioning (BP-M2), updated via platform migration in v1** |
| `data.snapshot_ref_cache` | `(episode_id, version) pk`, `published_snapshot_refs text[]`, `updated_at` | Event-fed from M08; backs the segment-lock gate |
| `data.outbox` | per §1.4.4 | M06 partition by aggregate `epi-`/`tax-` |

**ER:** `recording (M05) 1—n episode (versions) 1—n segment / tag_assignment`; `episode n—1 alignment_policy@version, calibration_set, embodiment_schema`; `suggestion_set 1—n suggestion_item n—0..1 segment`; `taxonomy 1—n taxonomy_version 1—n tag_assignment (pinned)`; `lineage_edge: recording → episode@version` (append-only, BR-02).

**Volumetrics & partitioning:** episodes 10⁵–10⁷ rows/tenant/year, segments ×5–20, tag assignments ×10 — `data.episode`, `data.segment`, `data.tag_assignment`, `data.lineage_edge` partitioned by tenant + month; Parquet/video payloads are size-dominant and live wholly in object storage (control plane stores slice indices only).

**Retention (DA-11):** episodes referenced by published snapshots / training lineage are `active` indefinitely (BR-02 chain); unreferenced `alignment_failed` episodes older than the tenant window → `archived` (Parquet slices compacted; control rows retained), then `purged-metadata-retained` only by explicit tenant policy with lineage edges and alignment summaries always retained. Suggestion sets archived 12 months after review completion. Taxonomy versions: never purged (tag reproducibility, FR-A5.6). SVC-15 executes transitions and **chunk compaction** (re-packing Parquet/video chunks when episode deletions fragment files; lineage edges record compaction as a non-semantic transform).

**Processing logic:** (1) CRUD + outbox per §2.6.3; (2) **SVC-02 build pipeline** (per-episode tx: steps → Parquet/video → summary → status → lineage edge → event); (3) **snapshot-ref cache** consumer (M08 publish events ⇒ segment-lock + `snapshot_eligible` recompute); (4) **signed-URL cache** shared pattern with M05; (5) **taxonomy supersede sweep** notifying owners of stale-pinned assignments; (6) **M18 index feed** (episode/segment/tag upserts via outbox relay to M18-11).

#### 2.6.5 Permission Design

| Permission key | PA | TA | OE | RD (default) | Persona templates granting it |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `data.alignment_policy:read` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | all data personas |
| `data.alignment_policy:create/update/delete` | ✓ | ✓ | — | grant | Robotics Data Engineer (`alignment:*`); tenant defaults TA-only |
| `data.alignment:execute` (M06-2, M06-7) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (member projects) | Robotics Data Engineer |
| `data.episode:read` (M06-3/4/5) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Robotics Data Engineer, Annotator (via M07 tasks), QA Lead, Model/Evaluation Engineer, Program Manager (lists) |
| `data.segment:update` (M06-6 CUD) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (grant) | Robotics Data Engineer, Annotation QA Lead |
| `data.tag:update` (M06-8) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (grant) | Robotics Data Engineer, Annotation QA Lead |
| `data.taxonomy:read` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | all personas |
| `data.taxonomy:propose` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (grant) | Annotation QA Lead (`taxonomy:propose`, §0.2.4) |
| `data.taxonomy:approve` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | Tenant Administrator only (DA-06); Governance Approver template at tenant scope |

**Granularity:** project scope primary for episodes/segments/tags; policies, taxonomies, calibration sets, embodiment schemas are tenant-scoped. Asset-level restriction overrides: episodes derived from restricted-campaign recordings inherit the restriction posture — M06-5 content URLs and step-store access re-check deny-wins `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` (§0.5.3) regardless of role grants; `data.episode:read` alone yields metadata-only visibility for such assets.

**Credential scoping:** signed content URLs time-limited (600 s default / 3600 s max), user-bound, never logged; no upload tokens (M06 ingests nothing from users — payloads are produced by SVC-02 under its own write-scoped storage credentials confined to `canonical/{project}/…`). Service identities: SVC-02 (`data.alignment:execute` + episode write at service scope — sole writer of `building→ready|alignment_failed`), SVC-03 (suggestion write only; cannot touch episodes/segments), SVC-15 (archival/compaction), M18 indexer (read-only episode/segment/tag metadata).

#### 2.6.6 Logging Design

| Level | Trigger examples |
|---|---|
| DEBUG | per-pair matching stats, Parquet chunk write timings, suggestion-generator I/O traces (off in prod) |
| INFO | policy created/versioned; alignment job requested; episode ready (step_count, flagged_ratio); segments/tags changed; taxonomy proposed/approved; manifest/content URLs served (count) |
| WARN | flagged-frame ratio within 80–100% of policy max; calibration fallback used; stale snapshot-ref cache forcing synchronous recheck; suggestion generator degraded; stale-taxonomy tag re-assertion |
| ERROR | alignment job failed (cause class); episode `alignment_failed` (policy_ref, ratio); Parquet/video write failure; outbox relay failure; taxonomy tx conflict after retries |
| FATAL | canonical store credentials invalid at startup; embodiment schema registry unreachable |

Structured JSON per §1.4.6 with module fields: `recording_id`, `episode_id`, `episode_version`, `policy_ref`, `calibration_set_id`, `flagged_frame_ratio`, `step_count`, `bytes`, `checksum_status`, `segment_id?`, `suggestion_set_id?`, `taxonomy_id?`, `taxonomy_version?`, `workflow_run_ref`, `result`, `latency_ms`. **Masking (§1.4.7):** no step payloads, frames, or instruction text in logs — language instructions and notes by reference id + length; assets as `{episode_id, byte_size, checksum}` summaries; signed URLs never logged.

**Shipping:** stdout JSON → platform collector; SVC-02/SVC-03 logs join the originating request's `trace_id` via M16 task context (`traceparent` per §1.4.6); audit via M03-11 (batched).

**Audit events (M03-11):** `data.alignment_policy.changed` (version diff), `data.alignment.requested`, `data.episode.created` (status ready; policy/calibration refs), `data.episode.alignment_failed` (ratio vs policy — FR-A4.3 evidence), `data.episode.version_created` (re-derivation), `data.segment.changed` (create/update/delete diff), `data.suggestion.reviewed` (accept/edit/reject per item — FR-A5.2), `data.episode.tags_changed` (diff with pinned versions — FR-A5.6), `data.taxonomy.created`, `data.taxonomy.version_proposed`, `data.taxonomy.approved` (DA-06 decision + reason), `data.taxonomy.version_rejected`, `data.episode.content_accessed` (manifest issuance — feeds RPT-11). All carry actor, scope, before/after summaries per §1.4.6 — supporting AC-02/AC-14.





### 2.7 M07 — Annotation & QA Management

#### 2.7.1 Module Overview

M07 operationalizes BP-01 steps 6–8: machine-assisted **pre-annotation**, human **labeling**, and **QA governance** (sampling, overlap/consensus review, adjudication, agreement measurement) that turn M06 episodes into approved, versioned **Annotation Sets** consumable by M08 snapshots. Its design center is the human-in-the-loop contract of BR-06: foundation-model outputs are *suggestions with provenance*, never human-approved labels, unless an explicit, authorized low-risk acceptance policy says otherwise (FR-A6.2). M07 follows the Label-Studio-style predictions/ML-backend pre-annotation pattern with SageMaker-Ground-Truth-style pluggable consolidation, and CVAT's gold-task/honeypot QA model with agreement-ordered review queues gated on Krippendorff's alpha (per §0.7.3).

**Owns:** Annotation Project (config, instructions, label schema refs, review/acceptance policy — FR-A6.5), Label Schema versions, Annotation Task lifecycle (+ assignments, claims, escalation), Pre-Annotation records and review dispositions, Annotation records (human labels + provenance), QA Samples/Reviews, Adjudication Decisions, Agreement Metrics, versioned Annotation Sets with reviewer history (FR-A6.7), annotation operations report (RPT-04).
**Excludes:** episode/segment creation and taxonomy governance (M06 — M07 consumes `epi-`/segment refs and taxonomy versions read-only); dataset snapshot assembly/publishing (M08 — approved `ans-` versions are *inputs* to M08-4, M07 never publishes snapshots); cohort computation (M18-8 — M07 consumes `coh-` refs); failure-record lifecycle (M15 — M07 emits QA failure events only); 4D rendering/playback (M19 — see embed relationship §2.7.2 P3); pre-annotation model hosting (SVC-03 executes registered generators per DA-13; M07 stores results and never bundles models).

**Roles & personas:** **Annotator** (claims tasks, reviews pre-annotations, submits labels — `label.task:*`, `label.preannotation:review`); **Annotation QA Lead** (project setup, QA policy, sampling, adjudication, set approval — `label.project:*`, `label.qa:*`, `label.set:approve`); expert reviewers (escalation targets, FR-A6.8) drawn from the project expert pool; Robotics Data Engineer (downstream consumer of approved sets); Governance Approver (reads agreement/quality evidence during snapshot/release reviews). Built-in role mapping per §0.2.4 (both annotation personas are RD-role templates).
