| `composition` | generated, frozen | M08-6 step 4 stats (FR-A8.3) |
| `known_gaps`* | author | coverage findings (M18-9 link-outs) |
| `quality_limitations`* | author, auto-drafted | M05 quality rollups + M07 agreement summary |
| `approval_status` | generated | publish/approval records |
| `license_privacy` | generated | frozen usage policy (FR-A8.4) |
| `maintenance` / `contact` | living | owner-editable post-publish |
| `known_issues` / `errata` / `recommended_replacements` | living | owner-editable post-publish, audited |

YAML front-matter mirrors machine-readable fields (license, tags, composition, restrictions) for programmatic consumers (per §0.7.3).

**M08-9 (i) `POST /internal/v1/data-governance/usage-checks`** — usage-policy gate (FR-A8.5, BR-05).
Transport: `/internal/v1`, mTLS; caller service identity must hold `data.usage_check:execute`. Registered callers: **M11-2/M11-3** (training plan precheck + launch), **M13-2** (evaluation runs), **M16 scheduler** (workflow admission, BP-06 step 2), **M10-1/M10-4** (simulation source + generation source datasets). Body / response:

```json
{ "caller": {"service": "m16-scheduler"}, "acting_user_id": "usr-...",
  "context": { "workflow_kind": "training", "project_id": "prj-...", "purpose": "tpl-... launch", "workflow_ref": "wfr-..." },
  "assets": [ {"ref": "dss-...", "requested_use": "training_input"}, {"ref": "syn-...", "requested_use": "training_input"} ] }
```

`requested_use` is a **closed enum**: `training_input | evaluation_input | simulation_source | generation_source | export`; per-asset decisions are `allow | deny | needs_approval`. This is the canonical vocabulary for every caller — M11-2/M11-3, M13-2, M10-1/M10-4, M16-2 — none may rename fields or values.
```json

{ "decisions": [ { "ref": "dss-...", "decision": "deny", "reasons": [
      {"code": "USAGE_RESTRICTION", "restriction": "no_training", "policy_ref": "pol-..."} ],
    "escalation": null },
  { "ref": "syn-...", "decision": "needs_approval", "reasons": [{"code": "DEPRECATED_REPLACEMENT_AVAILABLE", "replacement": "dss-..."}],
    "escalation": {"approval_kind": "restricted_data", "route": "M16-6"} } ],
  "decision_id": "ucd-...", "evaluated_at": "..." }
```

Processing: (1) mTLS identity + `data.usage_check:execute` (M03-10 service scope); (2) per asset, load policy snapshot + governance flags (policy cache TTL 60 s, **invalidated on M08-3/M08-6/M08-7/M08-11 events** — deny-wins re-evaluation at execution admission, never only at plan time); (3) rule evaluation, deny-wins order: asset not found / not `published` (drafts are "unapproved", FR-A8.5) → deny; `restricted` policy forbids `workflow_kind` or requires absent grant for `acting_user_id` → deny; retention `purged-metadata-retained` → deny; deprecated → deny with `needs_approval` escalation (reproduction-style runs may be approved via M16-6); composition/license/privacy tag rules (e.g. `no_export` × `workflow_kind=export`) → deny; else allow; (4) **Tx:** insert `catalog.usage_check_decision` rows (request-hash keyed for replay dedupe) — commit; (5) audit: every **deny** emits `data.usage_check.denied` (asset, caller, workflow_kind, reasons — the BR-05 enforcement evidence); allows logged INFO only; (6) response is always **200 with per-asset decisions** (transport errors only for authn/validation) — consumers translate `deny` to their own `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` surfaces. Batch limit 200 assets (`E_VALIDATION`). P99 ≤ 150 ms from cache.

Default restriction vocabulary (tenant-extensible Usage Policy rules; FR-A8.4/A8.5):

| Restriction tag | Denied `workflow_kind`s (default rule) | Escalation |
|---|---|---|
| `no_training` | training, synthetic_generation (as source) | none (hard deny) |
| `no_release_evaluation` | evaluation when run is release-critical (M13 flag) | M16-6 `restricted_data` |
| `internal_only` | export | none |
| `consent_research_only` | training/evaluation outside declared research purpose | M16-6 |
| `no_export` | export | none |
| `pii_review_pending` | all except annotation (privacy remediation path, M05-10) | M16-6 |
| *(state-derived)* draft/unapproved snapshot | all (FR-A8.5 "unapproved") | publish it first |
| *(state-derived)* deprecated | all new selections | M16-6 reproduction-run approval |
| *(state-derived)* retention `purged-metadata-retained` | all | none |

**M08-10 `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/reports/dataset-inventory`** — RPT-02.
Permission: `data.catalog:read`. Params: `status?, purpose?, owner_id?, restriction?, composition=real|synthetic|mixed?, format=json|csv`.
Response rows: `{snapshot_id, name, semver, owner_id, purpose, status, composition: {real_fraction, synthetic_fraction, augmented_fraction, mixed}, restrictions[], privacy_tags[], license_tags[], retention_class, approval_state, published_at, where_used_counts, governance_flags, replacement_snapshot_id?}` + `generated_at`. Served from the inventory MV (§2.8.4); restricted snapshots appear as redacted stubs to ungranted callers (existence-listed policy).

**M08-11 `PUT /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/usage-policies` · `GET` (same)** — usage-policy vocabulary management (BR-05 rule source).
Permission: PUT `data.usage_policy:update` (TA/PA), GET `data.usage_policy:read`. PUT body (full replace, If-Match on vocabulary version): `{"policies": [{"code": "no_external_release", "description": "…", "forbidden_workflow_kinds": ["export"], "requires_governance_approval": true, "active": true}]}`.
Processing: (1) authz; codes snake_case ≤ 64 chars, unique (`E_VALIDATION`); deactivating a code still referenced by published snapshots is allowed but reported (`still_referenced[]` — policy snapshots frozen on published snapshots are immutable, BR-04); (2) **Tx:** versioned upsert of `catalog.usage_policy` rows (prior versions retained) + outbox `data.usage_policy.updated` — commit; (3) M08-9 policy cache invalidated (≤ 60 s propagation); (4) audit `data.usage_policy.updated` (vocabulary diff). Defaults (`restricted`, `no_export`, `privacy_sensitive`, `license_limited`, `internal_only`, `consent_research_only`, `pii_review_pending`) are seeded at tenant provisioning (BP-M2).

**Cross-module consumption map (M08 as provider/consumer):** M11-3 / M13-2 / M16 scheduler / M10-4 — call M08-9 usage checks at launch/admission (BR-05; deny → their `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` surfaces); M11/M13 — resolve published snapshots by `dss-@manifest_hash` for reproducible training/evaluation inputs (BP-03/BP-04; manifests verified by content address, per §0.7.3); M18-1 — receives delegated hybrid queries and returns ranked ids for M08 governance re-check (DA-15, §1.6.2); SVC-04 — consumes M08 outbox (publish/deprecate/flag events) to maintain M18 indexes via M18-11 and coverage recomputation (M18-9); M10-8 — composition policies read at M08-4 preflight and enforced at M08-6 step 4; M16-6 — approval routing target for restricted publications and `needs_approval` usage escalations; M14 — release-candidate evidence freeze references snapshot lineage (`/lineage`, `/usage`); M03-9 — access reviews join `data.asset.accessed` + `data.usage_check.denied` audit streams (RPT-11); M20 — deprecation/restriction fan-out to where-used owners; SVC-15 — executes DA-11 retention transitions under M08 lineage-anchor guards.

**Concurrency & idempotency summary:** publish serializes per snapshot via row lock in step 7 and is idempotent by Idempotency-Key; **published snapshot rows are never updated — supersede only** (new semver), with deprecation as the sole governance-column exception; draft re-assembly (M08-4 with `snapshot_id`) is declarative replace guarded by If-Match; flag PUTs are declarative with If-Match; usage-check is a pure decision endpoint with request-hash dedupe for its decision records; manifests are write-once content-addressed objects (identical membership ⇒ identical hash ⇒ natural dedupe across snapshots per §0.7.3).

#### 2.8.4 Data Design

PostgreSQL, schema **`catalog.*`** (M08 single writer); snapshot member manifests in object storage (content-addressed, write-once). The asset index is a **denormalized read-model** — source truth stays in owning modules (§1.6.2).

| Table | Key columns (abridged) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `catalog.asset_index` | `asset_ref pk`, `asset_type` (F1 enum), `tenant_id`, `project_id`, `name`, `owner_id`, `campaign_id?`, `task?`, `robot_embodiment?`, `environment?`, `object_classes text[]`, `modalities text[]`, `scenario_tags text[]`, `action_labels text[]`, `failure_categories text[]`, `evaluation_outcomes jsonb`, `quality_status`, `annotation_status`, `validation_status?`, `dataset_memberships text[]`, `governance_summary jsonb`, `search_tsv tsvector`, `source_version`, `source_updated_at`, `indexed_at` | **List-partitioned by `asset_type`**; GIN on `search_tsv` + array facets; btree on scalar facets; `indexed_at` = staleness marker (DA-15: vector search lives in M18, never here) |
| `catalog.lineage_edge` | `(src_ref, dst_ref, relation) pk`, `relation` (`derived_from\|member_of\|annotates\|generated_from\|replaces`), `source_module`, `occurred_at` | Projected from owning-module events (OpenLineage-style per §0.7.3); BR-02 graph; idx both directions |
| `catalog.dataset_snapshot` | `snapshot_id pk (dss-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `project_id`, `name`, `semver`, `purpose`, `description`, `owner_id`, `status` (`draft\|published\|deprecated`), `member_count`, `composition jsonb` (frozen at publish), `quality_rollup jsonb`, `annotation_rollup jsonb`, `manifest_hash?`, `manifest_uri?`, `previous_snapshot_id?`, `replacement_snapshot_id?`, `publication_card_version?`, `published_at/by`, `deprecated_at/by`, `deprecation jsonb?`, `etag_version (draft only)`, timestamps | Unique `(project_id, name, semver)`; **no UPDATE path post-publish except deprecation columns** (BR-04, enforced by trigger guard + service layer) |
| `catalog.snapshot_member_draft` | `(snapshot_id, ord) pk`, `member_type`, `member_ref`, `member_version?`, `eligibility jsonb`, `inherited_tags jsonb` | Draft-only; deleted after publish (frozen manifest authoritative) |
| `catalog.dataset_card` | `(snapshot_id, card_version) pk`, `sections jsonb`, `generated jsonb`, `yaml_frontmatter jsonb`, `rendered_md_uri`, `updated_by/at` | Append-only versions; publication version pinned on snapshot |
| `catalog.governance_flag` | `asset_ref pk`, `manual jsonb` (`deprecated/superseded_by/restricted`), `computed jsonb` (`overused/underused` + evidence), `etag_version`, `updated_at` | + `catalog.governance_flag_history` append-only (FR-A7.5, FR-A8.8) |
| `catalog.usage_policy` | `policy_id pk (pol-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `name`, `rules jsonb` (restriction → denied workflow kinds, grant requirements, approval routing), `requires_governance_approval bool`, `version`, `status` | Tenant vocabulary; versioned |
| `catalog.snapshot_usage_policy` | `(snapshot_id) pk`, `restrictions text[]`, `privacy_tags text[]`, `license_tags text[]`, `retention_class`, `policy_refs text[]`, `frozen_at` | Frozen at publish (FR-A8.4) |
| `catalog.usage_check_decision` | `decision_id pk (ucd-ulid)`, `request_hash`, `caller`, `acting_user_id?`, `workflow_kind`, `asset_ref`, `decision`, `reasons jsonb`, `evaluated_at` | **Monthly range partitions**; idx `(asset_ref, evaluated_at)`, `(decision, evaluated_at)`; BR-05 evidence |
| `catalog.inventory_mv` / `catalog.usage_stats_mv` | rollups keyed `(project_id, snapshot_id)` / `(asset_ref, window)` | Back M08-10 / where-used + computed flags |
| `catalog.outbox` | per §1.4.4 | Publish/deprecate/flag/card events |

**Facet provenance (FR-A7.2/A7.3 → source of truth → index column):**

| Facet | Source module & event | `asset_index` column |
|---|---|---|
| campaign | M04/M05 (`ingest.recording.accepted` carries campaign ref) | `campaign_id` |
| task | M06 episode metadata / M04 campaign task | `task` |
| robot (embodiment) | M05 recording metadata → M06 episode | `robot_embodiment` |
| environment | M06 episode/segment scenario context | `environment` |
| object | M06 tags + M07 object labels (`label.set.approved` rollup) | `object_classes[]` |
| modality | M05 manifest modalities | `modalities[]` |
| annotation status | M07 (`label.set.*`, task rollups) | `annotation_status` |
| quality status | M05 quality-status changes (M05-8) | `quality_status` |
| ownership | owning-module owner fields | `owner_id` |
| dataset membership | M08 itself (`data.snapshot.published` manifests) | `dataset_memberships[]` |
| scenario tags | M06-8 tag events (taxonomy-versioned, DA-06) | `scenario_tags[]` |
| failure categories | M15 failure-record links + M07 QA failure categories | `failure_categories[]` |
| action labels | M06 segments + M07 action labels | `action_labels[]` |
| evaluation outcomes | M13 (`eval.report.approved` per-asset outcomes) | `evaluation_outcomes jsonb` |

**Object storage:** `curated/manifests/{sha256}.jsonl` — canonical sorted JSONL member manifests, write-once, retention-locked, **exempt from lifecycle/VACUUM-style policies while any referencing snapshot exists** (pinned-tag rule per §0.7.3); identical membership across snapshots dedupes to one manifest object (zero-copy). Rendered cards at `curated/cards/{snapshot_id}/v{n}.md`.

**ER relationships:** `dataset_snapshot 1—1 snapshot_usage_policy`, `1—n dataset_card versions`, `1—0..1 previous/replacement` self-links (family chain); `dataset_snapshot —manifest_hash→ manifest object 1—n member refs` (polymorphic: `epi-@v`, `ans-@v`, `syn-`); `asset_index 1—0..1 governance_flag`; `lineage_edge` polymorphic over all governed refs; `usage_check_decision n—1 asset`. Source-of-truth FKs are soft (refs validated against read-model + owning modules; single-writer rule §1.2).

**Volumetrics & partitioning:** `asset_index` 10⁶–10⁸ rows/tenant (episodes dominate) — list partitions by `asset_type` keep episode scans off snapshot queries; `usage_check_decision` ~10⁵–10⁶/month — monthly partitions, 13-month hot; snapshots/cards/policies low-volume (10³–10⁴). Search SLO: scalar P95 ≤ 800 ms at 10⁷ episodes via partial indexes per `(tenant_id, asset_type)`.

**Retention (DA-11):** published snapshots + manifests are **lineage anchors — never purged while referenced** by any training run, evaluation report, or release candidate (BR-02/BR-04; reproducibility pinning per §0.7.3); deprecated snapshots unreferenced for the tenant window → `archived` (metadata + manifest retained, signed-URL issuance disabled); drafts deleted after 90 days idle; `asset_index` rows follow their source asset (source purge ⇒ row kept with `retention_status='purged-metadata-retained'` so lineage/audit stay resolvable); `usage_check_decision` + governance histories retained per audit class (BR-14, default 7 years).

**Processing logic (module jobs):** (1) **catalog projector** — consumes owning-module outbox events (`ingest.recording.accepted`, `episode.created/tagged`, `label.set.approved`, `sim.batch.validated/deprecated`, `eval.report.approved`, M08's own publish/deprecate) → upserts `asset_index` + `lineage_edge`; idempotent by event id; freshness SLO P95 ≤ 30 s, exposed as `indexed_at`; (2) **tsvector maintenance** — trigger-based on index upsert; (3) **computed-flag job** (nightly) — where-used joins → `overused/underused` with evidence payload (FR-A7.5); (4) **card rendering** — regenerates YAML+markdown artifact on card/composition change; (5) **cache invalidation on flag changes** — M08-3/6/7 events purge the usage-check policy cache and search caches and push metadata sync to SVC-04/M18 (≤ 60 s contract, §2.8.3); (6) **manifest GC** — deletes orphaned manifest objects only when zero snapshot rows reference the hash (publish-failure cleanup); (7) **inventory/usage MV refresh** — 15-min incremental.

#### 2.8.5 Permission Design

Defaults per §1.4.8: **PA** implicit `*:*`; **TA** implicit within tenant; **OE** — none (business-data domain, §0.5.3); **RD** — via project membership + persona templates. Asset-level restriction checks (`E_RESTRICTED_DATA`) are deny-wins **on top of** every grant below (§0.5.3).

| Permission key | PA | TA | OE | RD (default) | Persona templates granting it |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `data.catalog:read` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (member projects) | all engineering personas, Program Manager (read-mostly template) |
| `data.snapshot:create` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Robotics Data Engineer |
| `data.snapshot:publish` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (grant-listed) | Robotics Data Engineer (`snapshot:publish` template per §0.2.4); restricted publications additionally require an M16-6 Governance Approver decision |
| `data.snapshot:approve` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✗ | Governance Approver persona — decides `restricted_data_use` approval subjects in the M16-6 inbox (§2.16.2 gate matrix); not part of the standard RD template |
| `data.usage_policy:update` / `data.usage_policy:read` | ✓ | ✓ (own tenant) | — | read only | Usage-policy vocabulary management via M08-11; defaults seeded at tenant provisioning (BP-M2) |
| `data.snapshot:deprecate` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (owner) | Robotics Data Engineer (own snapshots), Governance Approver |
| `data.card:update` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (owner/maintainer) | Robotics Data Engineer |
| `data.governance_flag:update` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | Governance Approver (TA-backed template per §0.2.4) |
| `data.usage_check:execute` (service) | — | — | — | — | Service identities only: M11, M13, M16 scheduler, M10 (mTLS, §1.4.2); never granted to users |
| `data.report:read` (RPT-02; folded into `data.catalog:read`) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Program Manager, Governance Approver |

**Granularity:** catalog reads and snapshot verbs evaluate at **project scope** (instance routes derive project per §1.4.1); governance-flag updates evaluate at **tenant scope** (flags cross project visibility); usage-check executes at **service scope** with the `acting_user_id` re-checked for asset grants (the gate enforces the *user's* entitlement, not the service's). Restricted-asset visibility is policy-controlled per asset (`listed` stub vs. `hidden`), enforced in every list/search serializer.

**Credential scoping:** snapshot `manifest_uri` is issued as a **signed URL** (15-min TTL, read-only, bound to the caller's session) and the manifest is independently verifiable by `manifest_hash` — consumers (M11 training executors via SVC-07) verify content-address before use (**signed + content-addressed manifests**; tamper evidence per §0.7.3). M08 never issues raw bucket credentials; member content access goes through owning-module signed URLs (M05-12/M06-5). M08-9 is callable only by the four registered service identities; gateway blocks `/internal/v1` at the edge (§1.4.1).

#### 2.8.6 Logging Design

| Level | M08 triggers |
|---|---|
| DEBUG | search query plans, projector event application detail, manifest serialization timing (off in prod) |
| INFO | searches served (mode: scalar/hybrid), snapshot created/published/deprecated, card updated, flags updated, usage-check allow decisions, report served |
| WARN | projector lag > 60 s (staleness SLO), M18 delegation fallback to scalar, composition warn-mode acknowledgements, usage-check `needs_approval`, eligibility-degraded drafts (member became ineligible after assembly) |
| ERROR | publish transaction failure (with manifest GC scheduling), projector poison event (dead-lettered), usage-check policy-cache rebuild failure, outbox relay failure |
| FATAL | catalog DB or policy store unavailable at startup |

Structured JSON per §1.4.6 with module fields: `snapshot_id`, `asset_ref`, `asset_type`, `manifest_hash`, `member_count`, `composition_summary`, `decision`, `decision_id`, `workflow_kind`, `policy_ref`, `index_freshness_ms`, `query_mode`. **Masking (§1.4.7):** card free text and descriptions logged by id + length only; member payloads never logged (`{asset_id, byte_size, checksum}` summaries); signed manifest URLs never logged; search query strings logged truncated (128 chars) with PII-bearing free text hashed. **Shipping:** stdout JSON → platform pipeline, 30-day hot / 13-month cold; `traceparent` propagated M08 → M03/M18/M16 and to usage-check callers (decision ids join caller traces).

**Audit events (M03-11):** `data.snapshot.created`, `data.snapshot.published` (manifest hash, composition, policy summary, approval ref — BR-04 evidence), `data.snapshot.deprecated` (reason, replacement link — FR-A8.7), `data.card.updated` (section diff), `data.governance_flag.updated` (before/after), `data.usage_policy.updated` (vocabulary diff — M08-11), `data.usage_check.denied` (asset, caller service, acting user, workflow_kind, reasons — BR-05/FR-A8.5 enforcement record), `data.usage_check.escalated` (needs_approval routing), `data.asset.accessed` (restricted-asset detail reads — FR-A8.8, feeds M03-9 access reviews / RPT-11). Together these reconstruct creation → approval → modification → deprecation → access per FR-A8.8 and AC-04/AC-14.

### 2.9 M09 — Scenario Management

#### 2.9.1 Module Overview

M09 is the system of record for the **simulation scenario catalog** of Subsystem B. A Scenario is a **declarative, versioned configuration document** — not code — following the Isaac Lab manager-based pattern (per §0.7.5): it composes task, environment, actors, objects, robot embodiment, starting conditions, constraints, expected outcome, and a variation plan into a single registry entry from which any simulation or generation job is reproducible (FR-B1.1). M09 also owns Scenario Collections (benchmark/evaluation suites of pinned scenario versions, FR-B1.5) and Scenario Requests, the governed intake that converts failures and coverage gaps into scenario work (FR-B5.1, BP-02 steps 1–3).

**Owns:** Scenario (+ versions, validation status, links), Scenario Collection (+ versions), Scenario Request, scenario coverage report (RPT-05, FR-B5.4).
**Excludes:** simulation run execution and synthetic generation (M10); failure-record lifecycle and remediation conversion (M15 — M09 only receives scenario requests produced by M15-4); coverage-gap computation (M18-10 — M09 consumes gap refs); dataset snapshot publication (M08); taxonomy governance (M06-9).

**Roles:** Simulation Engineer (primary author/owner of scenarios and variation plans), Evaluation Lead (collection curation for release evaluation plans, scenario-request origination from evaluation findings), Model Engineer (consumer; raises scenario requests for training-data needs). Validation approval may be reserved to Tenant Administrator by tenant rubric (DA-07).

**Upstream:** M15 (failure → scenario request via M15-4, knowledge-base edge M15→M09 per §0.5.2), M18 (coverage gaps via M18-10 feeding M09-6), M06 (episodes referenced by links), M13 (evaluation requirements motivating requests), M01/M02/M03 (session, scope, authz/audit).
**Downstream:** M10 (only approved/validated scenarios are runnable or generation sources — FR-B2.1, FR-B3.1), M13 (collections pinned into evaluation suites), M18 (scenario tags/coverage joins), M20 (notifications).

**FRD coverage:** FR-B1.1–B1.5 (catalog, purpose, governance metadata, links, collections), FR-B5.1 (failure→scenario request), FR-B5.4 + RPT-05 (coverage report), AC-06 (with M10). Per §1.7.1, B5.1/B5.4 ownership is split M09/M15 with the report at M09-7.

#### 2.9.2 Functional & UI/UX Design

**Primary functions**

| # | Function | Description | FRD |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Declarative scenario authoring | Create/edit scenario config documents (task, environment, actors, objects, robot embodiment, starting conditions, constraints, expected outcome, variation plan) validated against a registered `scenario_config_schema_version` (per §0.7.5) | FR-B1.1 |
| F2 | Purpose classification | Mandatory purpose enum, exactly: `training_data_generation \| robustness_testing \| failure_reproduction \| evaluation \| demonstration` | FR-B1.2 |
| F3 | Governance metadata & versioning | Mandatory owner, version, validation_status, source_inspiration, known_limitations on every version; validated versions are frozen; edits fork a new version | FR-B1.3 |
| F4 | Validation lifecycle | `draft → validated → invalidated`; re-validation creates a new version; validation evidence references M10 diagnostic runs | FR-B1.3, DA-07 |
| F5 | Linking | Bidirectional links to episodes, failure records, coverage gaps (and scenario requests) with typed relations | FR-B1.4 |
| F6 | Collections | Versioned scenario collections per robot capability / task family / release evaluation plan; member versions pinned at collection-version freeze | FR-B1.5 |
| F7 | Scenario requests | Triage board converting failures/gaps/eval requirements into scenario work; fulfillment links produced scenarios back to origin | FR-B5.1, BP-02 |
| F8 | Coverage reporting | Scenario coverage by task, environment, object, failure category, and model release plan, with validation-status breakdown | FR-B5.4, RPT-05 |

**UI pages**

**P1 — Scenario Catalog** (project-scoped list). Faceted browse with **purpose facets** (the five FR-B1.2 values), plus facets validation_status, embodiment family, task, environment class, owner, tags, linked-failure presence. Card shows `scn-` id, name, purpose badge, current version + validation_status chip (draft amber / validated green / invalidated red), owner, link counts, last run summary (read from M10). Bindings: `M09-1 GET`, facet counts from the coverage read model (§2.9.4). Row actions: open editor, new version, add to collection, launch run (deep-link to M10 wizard; disabled with tooltip "scenario not validated" when current version is not `validated` — pre-empting `E_SCN_NOT_VALIDATED`).

**P2 — Scenario Editor** (create / version-edit). Two-pane: left a structured **declarative config form**, right a live YAML/JSON view of the same document (read-only mirror, copy/export). Sections mirror the config schema:

| Field | Control | Req | Validation / binding |
|---|---|---|---|
| name / title | text | yes | unique per project (409 `E_CONFLICT`); kebab-case suggested |
| purpose | select (5 enum values) | yes | exact FR-B1.2 enum; `E_VALIDATION` otherwise |
| task | goal picker + success criteria builder + time limit | yes | goal from M06-9 task taxonomy; ≥1 success criterion |
| environment | scene asset ref + site class + terrain | yes | scene URI syntactically valid; site class from taxonomy |
| actors[] | repeating group (type, count, motion profile) | no | motion profile from registered profiles |
| objects[] | repeating group (asset ref, class, spawn spec) | yes ≥0 | object class from taxonomy |
| robot_embodiment | family + morphology version + sensor suite | yes | must match a registered embodiment descriptor |
| starting_conditions | robot pose + object states + scene state | yes | pose schema check |
| constraints[] | rule list (safety/feasibility) | no | known constraint types only |
| expected_outcome | kind (success/failure/measure) + metric expr | yes | metric expression parses |
| variation_plan | **variation plan builder** (below) | yes (may be empty axes) | category enum check |
| owner | user picker (default: creator) | yes | project member (FR-B1.3) |
| source_inspiration | typed ref (failure_record / coverage_gap / episode / free_text) | yes | ref resolvable when typed (FR-B1.3) |
| known_limitations | textarea | yes | non-empty (FR-B1.3) |
| tags[] | tag input | no | taxonomy-versioned tags (M06-9) |

The **variation plan builder** adds axes rows: category (exactly the FR-B3.4 enum: `lighting, object_placement, scene_layout, sensor_condition, actor_motion, physical_disturbance, task_initial_state`), parameter path, sampling spec (distribution, min/max or value set), per-axis seed policy. The builder previews the sampled-combination count and warns above a tenant-configurable explosion threshold (WARN only). Save → `M09-1 POST` (create) or `M09-2 PATCH` (draft version edit); editing a validated version prompts "Create new version?" → `M09-2 POST :new-version` (else `E_SCN_VERSION_FROZEN` surfaced inline). A **Validation panel** shows status history, linked diagnostic runs (from M10), and a "Request validation" / "Mark validated" / "Invalidate" action → `M09-3 PUT`; insufficient evidence renders the `E_SCN_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED` message with a shortcut to submit a diagnostics run in M10. A **Links tab** manages typed links → `M09-4 PUT`.

**P3 — Collection Builder.** Left: searchable scenario picker (validated-only toggle, default on); right: ordered member list with pinned version selector (`v3` or `latest_validated@freeze`), weight, required flag. Header fields: name, purpose_scope (`robot_capability | task_family | release_evaluation`), description. Freeze action creates an immutable collection version → `M09-5 POST :new-version`. For `release_evaluation` scope, freezing with any non-validated member is blocked (`E_SCN_NOT_VALIDATED` listing offending members); other scopes get a confirm-with-warning. Bindings: `M09-5`.

**P4 — Scenario-Request Triage Board.** Kanban over the exact state machine `open → accepted → fulfilled | rejected`. Card: origin badge (failure_record / coverage_gap / evaluation_requirement / manual), requested purpose, priority, age, assignee. Drag open→accepted (assign owner required); reject requires reason dialog (PATCH); "Fulfill" opens scenario picker / "Create scenario from request" (prefills P2 with origin as source_inspiration) → `M09-6 POST :fulfill`. Filters: origin kind, priority, assignee, age. Bindings: `M09-6`. Illegal drags are bounced with the `E_STATE_INVALID.allowed_transitions` payload.

**P5 — Scenario Coverage Report (RPT-05).** Dimension switcher (task / environment / object / failure_category / release_plan), matrix of scenario counts × validation_status × purpose, gap overlay from M18-10, release-plan rows joining M13 suites that pin M09 collections. Export CSV. Binding: `M09-7`.

**Exception handling (UI-wide):** 403 `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → read-only rendering with action tooltips; 409 `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED` → reload-merge dialog (optimistic locking per §1.4.4); 422 `E_VALIDATION` → field-anchored errors; deep links to invalidated scenario versions render a red banner with the superseding version, mirroring BR-11 semantics upstream.

#### 2.9.3 Interface Design

All endpoints follow §1.4 (envelope, pagination, `Idempotency-Key` on POST, `If-Match` on PATCH/PUT, ULID ids). Module error namespace `E_SCN_*`. Authz via M03-10; audit via M03-11.

**Normative state machines (M09)**

```text
Scenario.validation_status (per version, exact):
  draft ──validate──▶ validated ──invalidate──▶ invalidated   (terminal)
  · invalidated → * forbidden; re-validation = M09-2 :new-version (new version starts at draft)
  · validated version documents are frozen (definition/variation_plan immutable)

ScenarioRequest.status (exact states):
  open ──accept──▶ accepted ──:fulfill──▶ fulfilled            (terminal)
  open ──reject──▶ rejected (terminal)    accepted ──reject──▶ rejected (terminal)
```

**Module error codes (`E_SCN_*`)**

| Code | HTTP | Raised when |
|---|---|---|
| `E_SCN_NOT_VALIDATED` | 409 | An operation requires a `validated` scenario version (M09-5 release freeze; M10-1/M10-4 launch) |
| `E_SCN_VERSION_FROZEN` | 409 | PATCH attempts to edit document fields of a non-draft version |
| `E_SCN_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED` | 422 | Validation requested without rubric-required diagnostic-run evidence |
| `E_SCN_LINK_TARGET_NOT_FOUND` | 422 | Link target (episode/failure/gap/request) unresolvable within tenant |
| Shared | — | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_STATE_INVALID`, `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED`, `E_CONFLICT`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` per §1.4.3 |

**M09-1 `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/scenarios` · `GET` (same)** — create / list scenarios.
Permission: `sim.scenario:create` / `sim.scenario:read` (project scope).
POST body (compact):

```json
{ "name": "drawer_open_low_light", "title": "...", "purpose": "robustness_testing",
  "definition": { "schema_version": "1.0", "task": {...}, "environment": {...}, "actors": [...],
    "objects": [...], "robot_embodiment": {...}, "starting_conditions": {...},
    "constraints": [...], "expected_outcome": {...} },
  "variation_plan": { "axes": [ { "category": "lighting", "parameter": "light.intensity_lux",
    "sampling": { "distribution": "uniform", "min": 40, "max": 600 } } ], "seed_policy": "per_episode_random" },
  "owner_id": "usr-...", "source_inspiration": { "kind": "failure_record", "ref": "fal-...", "note": "..." },
  "known_limitations": "...", "tags": ["kitchen"], "links": [ { "target_type": "failure_record", "target_ref": "fal-...", "relation": "reproduces" } ] }
```

Processing (POST):

1. Authz `sim.scenario:create` at `{project_id}` (M03-10); tenant-suspension gate per §1.4.2.
2. Validate `purpose` against the exact FR-B1.2 enum; any other value → `E_VALIDATION`.
3. Validate `definition` against the registered `scenario_config_schema_version` (task, environment, robot_embodiment, starting_conditions, expected_outcome required); validate every `variation_plan.axes[].category` against the exact FR-B3.4 enum → `E_VALIDATION` with field paths in `details[]`.
4. Enforce FR-B1.3 mandatory governance metadata: `owner_id` (defaults to caller; must be a project member), `source_inspiration`, `known_limitations` non-empty → `E_VALIDATION`.
5. Resolve typed link targets within the caller's tenant (episode via M06, failure via M15, gap via M18) → `E_SCN_LINK_TARGET_NOT_FOUND` (422).
6. **Tx begin** — insert `sim.scenario` head + `sim.scenario_version` v1 (`validation_status='draft'`) + `sim.scenario_link` rows + outbox `sim.scenario.created` — **commit**.
7. Audit `sim.scenario.created`; return **201** `{scenario_id, version: 1, validation_status: "draft", etag}`.

GET filters: `q, purpose, validation_status, embodiment_family, task, environment_class, owner_id, tag, linked_target_ref, created_after/before, sort`. List item shape:

```json
{ "items": [ { "scenario_id": "scn-...", "name": "...", "title": "...", "purpose": "robustness_testing",
  "current_version": 4, "latest_validated_version": 3, "validation_status": "draft",
  "owner_id": "usr-...", "tags": ["kitchen"], "link_counts": { "failure_record": 2, "episode": 5, "coverage_gap": 1 },
  "updated_at": "..." } ], "next_page_token": null, "total_size": 124 }
```

**M09-2 `GET /api/v1/scenarios/{scenario_id}` · `PATCH` · `POST /api/v1/scenarios/{scenario_id}:new-version`**
Permission: `sim.scenario:read` / `sim.scenario:update` / `sim.scenario:update`.
GET: `?version=n` (default current); returns head + requested version document + `versions[]` summary + links + validation history.
PATCH processing:

1. Authz `sim.scenario:update`; If-Match ETag check → `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED` on mismatch.
2. Catalog fields (title, tags, owner_id, description) are always editable on the head row.
3. Document fields (`definition`, `variation_plan`, `purpose`, `known_limitations`) are editable **only while the addressed version is `draft`** — otherwise `E_SCN_VERSION_FROZEN` (409) with `details.suggest=":new-version"`. Schema/enum validation re-runs as in M09-1 steps 2–4.
4. Status transitions via PATCH are rejected (`E_VALIDATION`) — only M09-3 mutates `validation_status`.
5. Tx: update rows + outbox `sim.scenario.updated`; audit with before/after summary.

`:new-version` body `{ "base_version": 3, "reason": "widen lighting range" }`:

1. Authz `sim.scenario:update`; `Idempotency-Key` honored.
2. Lock head row (`SELECT ... FOR UPDATE`, serializes version creation per scenario); verify `base_version` exists (`E_NOT_FOUND`).
3. **Tx:** copy base document into v(n+1) with `validation_status='draft'`, `derived_from_version=base`, `reason`; head `current_version` advances for editing, but consumers (M10 launch, collection pins) resolve `latest_validated_version`; outbox `sim.scenario.version_created` — commit.
4. Audit; **201** `{scenario_id, version: n+1, validation_status: "draft"}`.

**M09-3 `PUT /api/v1/scenarios/{scenario_id}/validation-status`** — validation lifecycle.
Permission: `sim.scenario:approve` (tenant rubric may restrict to TA scope, DA-07).
Body: `{ "version": 4, "target_status": "validated", "evidence_run_ids": ["sim-..."], "rubric_id": "rub-...", "limitations_update": "...", "reason": "..." }`
Processing:

1. Authz `sim.scenario:approve`; when the tenant rubric sets `require_tenant_admin_validation=true` (DA-07), the M03 check is issued at **tenant** scope (effectively TA/PA only).
2. State machine on the addressed version — allowed exactly: `draft→validated`, `validated→invalidated`. Any transition out of `invalidated`, or `validated→draft`, → `E_STATE_INVALID` with `details.allowed_transitions`; re-validation requires M09-2 `:new-version` (per the exact machine).
3. For `target_status=validated`: rubric evidence check — every `evidence_run_ids[]` entry must be an M10 run on **this scenario version**, terminal `succeeded`, with outputs classified `scenario_diagnostics` (checked against the local evidence cache, §2.9.4, M10 fallback read) → `E_SCN_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED` (422).
4. For `target_status=invalidated`: `reason` mandatory (`E_VALIDATION`); `limitations_update` appended to the version's limitations history.
5. **Tx:** update version row (+ head `latest_validated_version` maintenance), append `sim.scenario_validation_history`, outbox `sim.scenario.validation_changed` — commit.
6. Audit `sim.scenario.approved` or `sim.scenario.invalidated`; invalidation fans out M20 notifications to owners of collections, M13 suites, and open M10 requests referencing the version.
7. Idempotency: setting the already-current status replays **200** unchanged.

**M09-4 `PUT /api/v1/scenarios/{scenario_id}/links`** — declarative replace of the link set (FR-B1.4).
Permission: `sim.scenario:update`. Body: `{ "links": [ { "target_type": "episode|failure_record|coverage_gap|scenario_request", "target_ref": "epi-...", "relation": "reproduces|inspired_by|covers_gap|derived_from|evidence_for", "note": "..." } ] }`
Processing:

1. Authz `sim.scenario:update`; If-Match on scenario head required.
2. Validate every target exists in-tenant (episode via M06, failure record via M15, coverage gap via M18 report item ref, scenario request locally) → `E_SCN_LINK_TARGET_NOT_FOUND`; linking a restricted episode additionally requires read access to it (deny ⇒ `E_RESTRICTED_DATA`, §0.5.3 deny-wins).
3. Validate `relation` against the closed relation enum (`E_VALIDATION`).
4. **Tx:** compute diff against the stored set; insert/delete `sim.scenario_link` rows; outbox `sim.scenario.links_changed` carrying adds/removes (M15 consumes failure-link adds to surface "scenario exists" on the failure record — FR-B1.4 closed-loop edge per §0.5.2) — commit.
5. Audit with diff summary; **200** returns the full effective link set.

**M09-5 `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/scenario-collections` · `GET` (same) · `GET/PATCH /api/v1/scenario-collections/{collection_id}` · `POST /api/v1/scenario-collections/{collection_id}:new-version`**
Permission: `sim.collection:create/read/update` (`:new-version` ⇒ `sim.collection:update`).
Create body: `{ "name": "...", "purpose_scope": "release_evaluation", "description": "...", "members": [ { "scenario_id": "scn-...", "pin": { "mode": "fixed", "version": 3 } | { "mode": "latest_validated" }, "weight": 1.0, "required": true } ] }`
Processing (create): authz; members must exist and be unique (`E_VALIDATION`); tx insert head + draft member list; **201**. PATCH edits draft member list/metadata only (If-Match; frozen versions untouched).
`:new-version` (freeze) processing:

1. Authz `sim.collection:update`; lock collection head (serializes freezes).
2. Resolve every `latest_validated` pin to a concrete validated scenario version **at freeze time**; a pin on a scenario with no validated version → `E_SCN_NOT_VALIDATED`.
3. `purpose_scope=release_evaluation` → every resolved member must be `validated`, else `E_SCN_NOT_VALIDATED` (409, offending members in `details[]`); other scopes proceed with `warnings[]`.
4. **Tx:** write immutable `sim.scenario_collection_version` + member rows (pins frozen), bump head `current_version`, outbox `sim.collection.version_created` — commit.
5. Audit; **201** `{collection_id, version}`.

M13 suites reference `scc-{id}@v{n}`; frozen versions are never mutated, so model-candidate comparison runs face identical member sets and seeds (supports FR-B2.4).

**M09-6 `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/scenario-requests` · `GET` (same) · `GET/PATCH /api/v1/scenario-requests/{request_id}` · `POST /api/v1/scenario-requests/{request_id}:fulfill`**
Permission: `sim.request:create/read/update`; `:fulfill` ⇒ `sim.request:update` + `sim.scenario:read` on linked scenarios.
Create body: `{ "title": "...", "origin": { "kind": "failure_record|coverage_gap|evaluation_requirement|manual", "ref": "fal-...|gap ref|evs-...", "summary": "..." }, "requested_purpose": "failure_reproduction", "priority": "p1", "due_date": "2026-07-01", "description": "..." }`
Created with `status='open'`. Callers include users, M15-4 conversion (failure → scenario request, FR-B5.1, acting-user context preserved; knowledge-base edge M15→M09 per §0.5.2), and M18-10 gap-analysis handoff.
PATCH: triage fields (assignee_id, priority, due_date) and **state transitions** `{"status": "accepted"|"rejected", "reason"?}` — allowed: `open→accepted` (assignee required), `open→rejected`, `accepted→rejected` (reason mandatory on reject, `E_VALIDATION`); all else `E_STATE_INVALID`. Terminal states: `fulfilled | rejected`.
`:fulfill` body `{ "scenario_ids": ["scn-..."], "note": "..." }` — processing:

1. Authz `sim.request:update` + `sim.scenario:read` on each referenced scenario.
2. Request must be `accepted` → `E_STATE_INVALID` otherwise (no fulfillment straight from `open`).
3. Every `scenario_ids[]` entry must exist in the project; each scenario must carry a link (`derived_from` this request, or to the origin failure/gap) — auto-created when absent, keeping FR-B1.4/FR-B5.2 traceability bidirectional.
4. **Tx:** status→`fulfilled`, store fulfillment refs + note, outbox `sim.request.fulfilled` (M15 consumes it to progress the originating failure record's remediation tracking; closes BP-02 steps 1–2) — commit.
5. Audit `sim.request.fulfilled`; **200** with the final record.

GET list filters: `status, origin_kind, priority, assignee_id, requested_purpose, overdue`.

**M09-7 `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/reports/scenario-coverage`** — RPT-05 / FR-B5.4.
Permission: `sim.scenario:read`. Params: `dimension=task|environment|object|failure_category|release_plan`, `purpose?`, `validation_status?`, `as_of?`, `format=json|csv`.
Response (compact): `{ "dimension": "task", "rows": [ { "key": "open_drawer", "scenario_count": 14, "by_validation_status": {"draft":4,"validated":9,"invalidated":1}, "by_purpose": {"robustness_testing":6, ...}, "open_request_count": 2, "linked_gap_refs": ["..."], "collections": ["scc-...@v2"] } ], "release_plans": [ { "evaluation_suite_ref": "evs-...@v3", "collection_ref": "scc-...@v2", "member_count": 40, "validated_fraction": 0.93 } ], "generated_at": "..." }`
Processing:

1. Authz `sim.scenario:read`; validate `dimension` enum (`E_VALIDATION`).
2. Serve rows from `sim.scenario_coverage_mv` (§2.9.4), refreshed on outbox events + 15-min schedule; response carries `generated_at` as the staleness marker.
3. `failure_category` dimension joins the M15 failure-record read replica through `sim.scenario_link(target_type='failure_record')`; gap overlays join M18-10 results by origin ref.
4. `release_plan` dimension joins M13 suite→collection references (`scc-…@v{n}`) and computes `validated_fraction` per plan (release-readiness signal, RPT-05).
5. `format=csv` streams the same rows. Read-only; per-user response cache TTL 5 min.

**Cross-module consumption map (M09 as provider):** M10-1/M10-4 — scenario/collection resolution + validation gate (cache-fed); M13-1 — collection version pinning into suites; M15 — reverse link lookup (`scenario_link` by `fal-` ref) and request-fulfillment events; M18 — scenario tags/links for coverage joins; SVC-06 — frozen scenario document resolution at task start (read-only service identity, §2.9.5).

**Concurrency & idempotency summary:** all POSTs accept `Idempotency-Key` (24 h, §1.4.4); PATCH/PUT require `If-Match`; scenario version creation serializes per scenario via head-row `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE`; collection freeze serializes per collection likewise.

#### 2.9.4 Data Design

PostgreSQL, schema **`sim.*`** (shared with M10; single writer per table — M09 writes only the tables below). Scenario documents are JSONB (small declarative configs); heavyweight scene/object assets are referenced by URI and owned by external asset storage, not by M09.

| Table | Key columns (abridged) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `sim.scenario` | `scenario_id pk (scn-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `project_id`, `name`, `title`, `purpose` (enum, FR-B1.2 exact), `owner_id`, `tags text[]`, `current_version int`, `latest_validated_version int?`, `etag_version`, timestamps | Head row; unique `(project_id, name)`; idx `(project_id, purpose)`, GIN on `tags` |
| `sim.scenario_version` | `scenario_id fk`, `version int`, pk `(scenario_id, version)`, `definition jsonb`, `variation_plan jsonb`, `schema_version`, `validation_status` (`draft\|validated\|invalidated`), `source_inspiration jsonb`, `known_limitations text`, `derived_from_version int?`, `created_by`, `validated_at?`, `invalidated_at?` | Document frozen once `validated`; re-validation = new row (state machine §2.9.3) |
| `sim.scenario_validation_history` | `id pk`, `(scenario_id, version) fk`, `from_status`, `to_status`, `actor_id`, `rubric_id?`, `evidence_run_ids text[]`, `reason`, `at` | Append-only |
| `sim.scenario_link` | `link_id pk`, `scenario_id fk`, `target_type` (`episode\|failure_record\|coverage_gap\|scenario_request`), `target_ref`, `relation`, `note`, `created_by`, `created_at` | Unique `(scenario_id, target_type, target_ref, relation)`; idx `(target_type, target_ref)` for reverse lookup (M15/M18 joins) |
| `sim.scenario_collection` | `collection_id pk (scc-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `project_id`, `name`, `purpose_scope`, `description`, `owner_id`, `current_version int`, `draft_members jsonb`, `etag_version` | Draft member list lives on head |
| `sim.scenario_collection_version` | `(collection_id, version) pk`, `frozen_at`, `frozen_by`, `member_count` | Immutable |
| `sim.scenario_collection_member` | `(collection_id, version, ord) pk`, `scenario_id`, `scenario_version int` (resolved), `weight`, `required bool` | Pinned versions only |
| `sim.scenario_request` | `request_id pk (scr-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `project_id`, `title`, `origin_kind`, `origin_ref`, `origin_summary`, `requested_purpose`, `priority`, `due_date`, `status` (`open\|accepted\|fulfilled\|rejected`), `assignee_id?`, `reject_reason?`, `fulfilled_scenario_ids text[]`, `etag_version`, timestamps | Idx `(project_id, status, priority)`, `(origin_kind, origin_ref)` |
| `sim.scenario_coverage_mv` | materialized read model keyed `(project_id, dimension, key)` | Backs M09-7 / P5 facets |
| `sim.outbox` | per §1.4.4 | Shared sim-schema outbox (M09 + M10 partitions by aggregate) |

**ER relationships:** `scenario 1—n scenario_version`; `scenario 1—n scenario_link` (polymorphic targets: episode `epi-`, failure record `fal-`, coverage-gap ref, request `scr-`); `scenario_collection 1—n collection_version 1—n collection_member n—1 scenario_version` (pinned); `scenario_request n—m scenario` via `fulfilled_scenario_ids` + reciprocal links; M10 references `(scenario_id, version)` from `sim.simulation_run` / `sim.generation_request` with read-only FK semantics enforced at the service layer (single-writer rule, §1.2).

**Volumetrics & partitioning:** scenario catalogs are low-volume (10³–10⁴ rows/tenant) — no partitioning; B-tree + GIN indexes suffice. `scenario_link` may reach 10⁵ — plain indexes. (High-volume partitioning applies to M10 run tables, §2.10.4.)

**Retention (DA-11):** scenarios, versions, links, collections — `active` indefinitely while referenced by any run, batch, suite, or release evidence (lineage anchor); archival to `archived` (cold rows, excluded from default lists) via SVC-15 only when unreferenced for the tenant-configured window; never `purged-metadata-retained` for versions referenced by released models. Scenario requests: archived 24 months after terminal state.

**Processing logic:** (1) **Validation-status publication** — every `sim.scenario.validation_changed` outbox event updates the validation cache consumed by M10 admission (§2.10.4) and invalidates the M09-7 read model rows; (2) **coverage aggregation** — incremental MV refresh on scenario/collection/request events + 15-min full refresh joining M15 failure categories and M18 gap refs; (3) **request SLA sweep** — daily job flags overdue `open/accepted` requests → M20 notification to assignee/owner.

#### 2.9.5 Permission Design

Keys (atomic catalog in M03 §2.3.5; actions per §1.4.8). Defaults: **PA** implicit `*:*`; **TA** implicit within tenant; **OE** — none (business domain, §0.5.3); **RD** — per project membership via persona templates.

| Permission key | PA | TA | OE | RD (default) | Persona templates granting it |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sim.scenario:read` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (member projects) | Simulation Engineer, Evaluation Lead, Model Engineer, Program Manager |
| `sim.scenario:create` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Simulation Engineer |
| `sim.scenario:update` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (owner or grant) | Simulation Engineer |
| `sim.scenario:approve` (validation status) | ✓ | ✓ | — | optional | Simulation Engineer (`validation:submit`); rubric may require tenant scope ⇒ TA-only (DA-07) |
| `sim.collection:create/update` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Simulation Engineer, Evaluation Lead |
| `sim.collection:read` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | all engineering personas |
| `sim.request:create` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Evaluation Lead, Model Engineer, Simulation Engineer (also exercised by M15-4 conversion in user context) |
| `sim.request:read/update` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (update: assignee/owner) | Simulation Engineer (triage/fulfill) |

**Granularity:** project scope is primary for all keys (scenarios belong to exactly one project, §0.2.3). Validation approval (`sim.scenario:approve`) is evaluated at project scope by default; when the tenant rubric sets `require_tenant_admin_validation=true` (DA-07), M03 check is issued at tenant scope, effectively TA/PA-only. Asset-level deny-wins applies to links: linking a restricted episode requires read access to it (`E_RESTRICTED_DATA` otherwise).

**Credential scoping:** no payload uploads occur in M09. SVC-06 (Simulation & Synthetic Generation Bridge) holds a service identity with read-only `sim.scenario:read` (service scope) to resolve frozen scenario documents at run launch; it can never mutate scenarios. UI tokens are user sessions per §1.4.2; no signed URLs are issued by M09 (scene asset URIs resolve through their owning stores).

#### 2.9.6 Logging Design

| Level | Trigger examples |
|---|---|
| DEBUG | config-schema validation detail, MV refresh timings (off in prod) |
| INFO | scenario/version/collection/request created; validation transitions; links changed; fulfillments; report served |
| WARN | variation-combination count above tenant threshold; link target slow-resolve/retry; freeze attempted with non-validated members (non-release scopes); overdue request sweep hits |
| ERROR | outbox relay failure; cross-module resolution failure after retries; MV refresh failure |
| FATAL | schema registry unavailable at startup |

Structured JSON per §1.4.6, with module fields: `scenario_id`, `scenario_version`, `collection_id`, `collection_version`, `request_id`, `purpose`, `validation_status_from/to`, `link_target_type/ref`, `rubric_id`. **Masking (§1.4.7):** free-text (`known_limitations`, descriptions, notes) logged by length + reference only; emails masked; no scenario document bodies in logs (`{scenario_id, version, byte_size, checksum}` summary).

**Shipping:** stdout JSON → platform log pipeline (per §1.4.6), 30-day hot / 13-month cold; trace propagation `traceparent` across M09→M03/M15/M18 calls.

**Audit events (M03-11):** `sim.scenario.created`, `sim.scenario.version_created`, `sim.scenario.approved` (draft→validated, with rubric + evidence refs), `sim.scenario.invalidated`, `sim.scenario.links_changed` (diff summary), `sim.collection.created`, `sim.collection.version_created` (member pin list hash), `sim.request.created`, `sim.request.accepted`, `sim.request.rejected` (reason), `sim.request.fulfilled` (scenario refs). All carry actor, scope `{tenant, project}`, before/after summaries per §1.4.6 — supporting AC-14.

---

### 2.10 M10 — Simulation Run & Synthetic Data Management

#### 2.10.1 Module Overview

M10 operationalizes the Simulation & Synthetic Data Factory's execution half: **simulation runs** (submission, monitoring, rerun, output classification — FR-B2.1–B2.6), **governed synthetic generation** (generation requests, approval, execution — FR-B3.1–B3.6), **synthetic batch governance** (validation reports/status, composition policies, impact tracking, deprecation — FR-B4.1–B4.5, BR-03, BR-11, DA-07), and the sim-side of the closed loop (FR-B5.2 origin traceability, FR-B5.3 conversion of sim findings into collection/mining work via M15-4/M16). Execution itself is delegated: M10 submits workflow runs to M16 (template families `simulation_run`, `synthetic_generation`) executed by **SVC-06 Simulation & Synthetic Generation Bridge**, which adapts procedural-sim and world-model generator backends behind one job contract (per §0.7.5).

**Owns:** Simulation Run (+ context document, events, output items), Generation Request, Synthetic Data Batch (+ validation reports), Composition Policy, Impact Record.
**Excludes:** scenario definition and validation (M09); **snapshot publication — validated synthetic batches become dataset snapshots only via M08-4/M08-6** with composition metadata (BP-02 step 7); workflow scheduling/quota (M16/M17); failure-record management (M15 — M10 emits, never triages); evaluation reporting (M13 — M10 supplies `evaluation_evidence` per DA-18).

**Roles:** Simulation Engineer (primary: submits runs, drafts generation requests, validates batches), Evaluation Lead (model-comparison runs FR-B2.4, consumes evaluation evidence, validation review), Model Engineer (consumes validated batches via M08 snapshots; reads impact). Generation approval and batch validation may be reserved to TA by tenant rubric (DA-07).

**Upstream:** M09 (validated scenarios/collections), M12 (model candidates for comparison runs), M08 (source datasets + usage checks M08-9), M16/M17 (orchestration/admission), SVC-06 (executor callbacks M10-10).
**Downstream:** M08 (batch catalog sync; snapshot assembly), M13 (evaluation evidence; impact joins), M15 (sim failure events; FR-B5.3 conversions), M18 (batch distributions), M20 (alerts).

**FRD coverage:** FR-B2.1–B2.6, FR-B3.1–B3.6, FR-B4.1–B4.5, FR-B5.2/B5.3, BR-03 (synthetic always labeled), BR-11 (deprecation), DA-07 (rubrics), DA-18 (sim evaluation route), AC-06/AC-07 (with M09/M08).

#### 2.10.2 Functional & UI/UX Design

**Primary functions**

| # | Function | Description | FRD |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Run submission & governance | Runs only from approved (validated) scenarios/collections; full context recorded: scenario version, embodiment, task goal, variation plan, owner, intended output type | FR-B2.1, FR-B2.2 |
| F2 | Output classification | Exact enum `synthetic_data \| evaluation_evidence \| scenario_diagnostics \| discarded`; per-item or default-class | FR-B2.3 |
| F3 | Model-candidate comparison runs | `model_candidate_ids[]` against one scenario collection version under identical seeds | FR-B2.4 |
| F4 | Sim-defect capture | Simulation failure / artifact / invalid-run reasons recorded **separately from model failures** (model failures route to M15) | FR-B2.5 |
| F5 | Rerun from record | `:rerun` copies frozen context (scenario version, embodiment, variation plan, seeds, candidates) with explicit overrides | FR-B2.6 |
| F6 | Governed generation | Generation only from approved scenario / approved generation request / approved source dataset; request states target gap or eval objective | FR-B3.1, FR-B3.2 |
| F7 | Synthetic marking & lineage | Every batch `synthetic=true` + generator, source scenario, source data, settings, validation status — catalog-authoritative + portable sidecar manifest (per §0.7.5) | FR-B3.3, BR-03 |
| F8 | Variation & randomization logging | Variation categories exactly per FR-B3.4; domain-randomization dimensions + sampling ranges + seeds logged per generation job (per §0.7.5) | FR-B3.4 |
| F9 | Generated ground truth | Simulator/generator-provided labels ingested as batch label manifests | FR-B3.5 |
| F10 | World-model intake (constrained) | Generative scene-reconstruction / world-model outputs accepted **only** as governed candidate synthetic assets: `generator_class=world_model_generative` ⇒ `candidate_asset=true`, tenant flag `world_model_generation_enabled` default **off** (P2, non-default) | FR-B3.6 |
| F11 | Validation before use | Batch validation status gates training/release-eval selection; reports state intended use, limitations, failure modes, suitability `training\|evaluation\|exploration_only` per tenant rubric | FR-B4.1, FR-B4.2, DA-07 |
| F12 | Composition policies | Dataset-owner-defined synthetic-to-real caps for mixed datasets; enforced at M08 snapshot assembly and M11 plan validation | FR-B4.3 |
| F13 | Impact tracking | `improve\|degrade\|unknown` vs target evaluations, computed by joining M13 reports through snapshot/training lineage | FR-B4.4 |
| F14 | Deprecation with lineage | Deprecation flag on batches (unrealistic/mislabeled/harmful); historical lineage preserved | FR-B4.5, BR-11 |
| F15 | Loop traceability & conversion | Runs/batches carry origin refs (failure, gap, eval requirement) (FR-B5.2); sim findings convert to collection/mining tasks via M15-4/M16 | FR-B5.2, FR-B5.3 |

**UI pages**

**P1 — Run Submission Wizard** (4 steps; binding `M10-1 POST`).

| Step / field | Control | Req | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. Source: scenario_id+version or collection_id+version | searchable picker (validated versions highlighted) | yes (XOR) | non-validated selectable only when step 2 output type = `scenario_diagnostics`; else inline `E_SCN_NOT_VALIDATED` |
| 2. purpose | select = M09 purpose enum (FR-B1.2 values) | yes | enum |
| 2. intended_output_type | select = FR-B2.3 enum | yes | cross-check vs purpose (e.g. `evaluation` ⇒ `evaluation_evidence`) |
| 2. task_goal | text (prefilled from scenario) | yes | non-empty (FR-B2.2) |
| 3. embodiment | family + morphology version | yes | must match scenario embodiment or declared compatible |
| 3. variation_overrides | axes editor (subset of plan) + seed + episode_count | no | categories ⊆ scenario plan axes; count ≤ tenant cap |
| 3. model_candidate_ids[] | multi-picker from M12 | no (req. for comparison) | artifacts available; ≤ tenant max per run |
| 3. source_dataset_ids[] | snapshot picker | no | M08-9 usage check preview; restricted ⇒ `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` warning pre-submit |
| 4. service_class, executor_profile, notes, origin_ref | selects/text | class yes | quota preview via M17 read |

Submit returns 202 + `workflow_run_ref`; wizard deep-links to P2.

**P2 — Run Monitor (live status).** List + detail. List: status chips for the exact machine `submitted → queued → running → succeeded | failed | cancelled`, purpose, scenario@version, owner, progress %, ETA. Detail tabs: **Timeline** (M10-10 event stream: state changes, progress, artifact warnings rendered amber), **Context** (frozen FR-B2.2 record: scenario version, embodiment, task goal, effective variation plan, seeds, candidates, origin ref — FR-B5.2), **Outputs** (manifest items with classification editor → `M10-3`), **Diagnostics** (sim failure/artifact/invalid reasons FR-B2.5, with "Send to M15" for model-behavior failures and "Convert to collection/mining task" which opens the M15-4 conversion flow per FR-B5.3), **Compare** (for `model_candidate_ids[]` runs: per-candidate success/metric table, link to M13). Actions: cancel (`M10-2 :cancel`, enabled in non-terminal states), rerun (`M10-2 :rerun`, prefilled context diff view). Live updates via 5 s poll of `M10-2 GET`.

**P3 — Generation Request Form (with gap linkage).** Fields: title; **objective** — required typed block `{kind: coverage_gap (picker from M18-10 results) | evaluation_objective (free text + optional evs- ref)}` (FR-B3.2); source (XOR, FR-B3.1): approved scenario@version / approved source dataset (M08 picker) / parent generation request; generator (class `procedural_sim | world_model_generative`, name, version) — world-model option hidden unless tenant flag enabled, and when chosen shows the persistent "candidate asset — exploration-grade until validated" banner (FR-B3.6); variation_spec axes (FR-B3.4 enum) + **randomization log preview** (dimensions, ranges, seeds — per §0.7.5); output_spec (modalities, episode/frame counts, label outputs FR-B3.5); composition_target (optional, validated against `M10-8` policies with live cap meter). Save = draft (`M10-4 POST`); Submit (`:submit`) runs source-approval checks and routes to the M16-6 approval inbox; status banner tracks `draft → submitted → approved | rejected → executing → completed | failed`.

**P4 — Batch Detail with Validation Panel.** Header: `syn-` id, **permanent `SYNTHETIC` badge (BR-03, non-dismissable)**, generator (+class; `candidate_asset` badge for world-model outputs), source scenario@version, source datasets, generation request, randomization log, label manifest summary, deprecation banner (red, with reason + evidence) when flagged. **Validation panel:** status chip (`pending → validated_training | validated_evaluation | exploration_only | rejected`), rubric selector, report composer (intended_use, methodology, sample size, findings, known_limitations, failure_modes, suitability) → `M10-6 POST`; status change → `M10-6 PUT` (gated: requires ≥1 report, may require TA per DA-07). Sample viewer uses short-lived signed URLs. Actions: deprecate (`M10-7`, reason enum + evidence refs), view impact (P6), "where used" (M08-5 usage deep-link).

**P5 — Composition Policy Editor.** Project-scoped table of policies: scope (task family / model family / dataset purpose), `max_synthetic_fraction` slider, per-generator-class caps, `require_validated_status` toggle, enforcement `block|warn`. Save = declarative `M10-8 PUT` with If-Match; shows downstream enforcement points (M08-4 assembly, M11 plan validation) read-only.

**P6 — Impact Dashboard.** Per-batch and per-generator rollups of FR-B4.4 direction (`improve|degrade|unknown`) joined with M13 reports: evaluation pairs table (candidate-with-batch vs baseline-without, suite@version, metric deltas, confidence), trend sparklines, "degrade" rows flagged with one-click deprecation proposal (BR-11). Binding: `M10-9` + nightly recomputation status.

**Exception handling (UI-wide):** `E_SCN_NOT_VALIDATED` → blocking dialog with scenario validation deep-link (M09 P2); `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` → restriction summary + request-access path (M16-6); `E_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` → quota panel + escalation request (M16-5); `E_SIM_WORLD_MODEL_DISABLED` → tenant-flag explanation (TA contact); optimistic-lock and validation errors per §1.4.

#### 2.10.3 Interface Design

Module error namespace `E_SIM_*` (plus cross-module `E_SCN_NOT_VALIDATED`, shared `E_RESTRICTED_DATA`). Conventions per §1.4.

**Normative state machines (M10)**

```text
SimulationRun.status (exact):
  submitted ─▶ queued ─▶ running ─▶ succeeded | failed        (terminal)
  submitted | queued | running ──:cancel──▶ cancelled         (terminal)
  · transitions beyond `submitted` are driven exclusively by M16 acceptance + M10-10 executor events

GenerationRequest.status (exact):
  draft ──:submit──▶ submitted ──approval──▶ approved | rejected   (rejected terminal)
  approved ──workflow launch──▶ executing ─▶ completed | failed    (terminal)

SyntheticBatch.validation_status (exact):
  pending ─▶ validated_training | validated_evaluation | exploration_only | rejected   (terminal)
  · `deprecated` is an orthogonal boolean flag (BR-11): set once, validation history and lineage preserved
```

**Module error codes (`E_SIM_*`)**

| Code | HTTP | Raised when |
|---|---|---|
| `E_SIM_CANDIDATE_UNAVAILABLE` | 422 | `model_candidate_ids[]` unresolved or artifact missing (M10-1/2) |
| `E_SIM_RUN_NOT_TERMINAL` | 409 | Output classification attempted before terminal state (M10-3) |
| `E_SIM_OUTPUT_LOCKED` | 409 | Re-classification after downstream reference (M10-3) |
| `E_SIM_RERUN_CONTEXT_MISSING` | 409 | `:rerun` on a non-terminal run or absent context document (M10-2) |
| `E_SIM_SOURCE_UNAPPROVED` | 409 | Generation source fails FR-B3.1 approval rules (M10-4) |
| `E_SIM_WORLD_MODEL_DISABLED` | 403 | `world_model_generative` used while tenant flag is off (FR-B3.6) |
| `E_SIM_VALIDATION_REPORT_REQUIRED` | 422 | Batch status change without a consistent validation report (M10-6) |
| `E_SIM_BATCH_DEPRECATED` | 409 | Validation attempted on a deprecated batch (M10-6) |
| `E_SIM_COMPOSITION_VIOLATION` | 409 | Synthetic-to-real cap exceeded at enforcement points (M08-4 / M11, FR-B4.3) |
| `E_SIM_EVENT_SEQUENCE_CONFLICT` | 409 | Executor event replays a sequence with a different payload (M10-10) |
| Cross/shared | — | `E_SCN_NOT_VALIDATED` (409), `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` (403), `E_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` (429), `E_STATE_INVALID`, `E_VALIDATION` per §1.4.3 |

**M10-1 `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/simulation-runs` · `GET` (same)** — submit / list runs (FR-B2.1/B2.2/B2.4).
Permission: `sim.run:execute` / `sim.run:read`.
POST body (compact):

```json
{ "scenario_ref": { "scenario_id": "scn-...", "version": 4 } , "collection_ref": null,
  "purpose": "evaluation", "intended_output_type": "evaluation_evidence", "task_goal": "open_drawer",
  "embodiment": { "family": "humanoid_g1", "morphology_version": "1.4", "sensor_suite": "ss-std-02" },
  "variation_overrides": { "axes": [...], "seed": 1234, "episode_count": 500 },
  "model_candidate_ids": ["mdl-aaa", "mdl-bbb"], "source_dataset_ids": ["dss-..."],
  "origin_ref": { "kind": "failure_record|coverage_gap|evaluation_run|generation_request|manual", "ref": "fal-..." },
  "service_class": "standard", "executor_profile": "isaaclab_4.2", "notes": "..." }
```

Processing (POST — submission pipeline, tx boundaries marked):

1. Authz `sim.run:execute` at `{project_id}` (M03-10); tenant-suspension gate.
2. Resolve `scenario_ref` XOR `collection_ref` (`E_VALIDATION` if both or neither); pin concrete versions (collection versions are already frozen pins, M09-5).
3. **Scenario validation-status check** against `sim.scenario_validation_cache` (synchronous M09 fallback read on miss): every referenced scenario version must be `validated`; exception — `intended_output_type=scenario_diagnostics` may target `draft` versions (that is how draft scenarios earn validation evidence); `invalidated` versions are never runnable → `E_SCN_NOT_VALIDATED` (409, offending refs in `details[]`).
4. If `source_dataset_ids[]` present → **M08-9 usage check** per snapshot with `requested_use=simulation_source`; any deny ⇒ `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` (403; BR-05 deny-wins, §0.5.3).
5. Resolve `model_candidate_ids[]` via M12 (candidate exists, artifact retrievable) → `E_SIM_CANDIDATE_UNAVAILABLE` (422). Comparison runs (≥2 candidates) require `collection_ref` so all candidates face identical pinned scenarios and seeds (FR-B2.4).
6. **Admission — M17-9 via M16:** create the M16-2 workflow run (template `simulation_run@vN`, service class, business params = frozen context); M16 performs the M17-9 quota/priority admission; deny propagates `E_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` (429) or the approval-pending envelope per BR-12.
7. **Tx:** insert `sim.simulation_run` (`status='submitted'`; `context_doc` freezing the resolved scenario document, embodiment, task goal, effective variation plan, seeds, candidates, source datasets, origin_ref — the complete FR-B2.2 record) + outbox `sim.run.submitted` — commit.
8. Audit; **202** `{ "run_id": "sim-...", "status": "submitted", "workflow_run_ref": "wfr-..." }` (202+workflow_run_ref pattern, §1.4.5).

`queued` is set on the M16 acceptance event; all later transitions arrive only via M10-10 (state machine block above).
GET filters: `status, purpose, intended_output_type, scenario_id, collection_id, model_candidate_id, owner_id, origin_kind, created_after/before, has_artifact_warnings`. List item shape:

```json
{ "items": [ { "run_id": "sim-...", "status": "running", "purpose": "evaluation",
  "intended_output_type": "evaluation_evidence", "scenario_ref": "scn-...@v4", "collection_ref": null,
  "embodiment_family": "humanoid_g1", "model_candidate_ids": ["mdl-aaa","mdl-bbb"], "owner_id": "usr-...",
  "progress_percent": 64.0, "artifact_warning_count": 1, "submitted_at": "...", "workflow_run_ref": "wfr-..." } ],
  "next_page_token": null, "total_size": 311 }
```

**M10-2 `GET /api/v1/simulation-runs/{run_id}` · `POST ...:cancel` · `POST ...:rerun`**
Permissions: `sim.run:read` / `sim.run:cancel` / `sim.run:execute`.
GET: full record — context doc, status + timeline (from events), output manifest + classifications, diagnostics (FR-B2.5), comparison summary, `origin_ref`/links (FR-B5.2), `rerun_of`/`rerun_ids`.
`:cancel` processing:

1. Authz `sim.run:cancel` (run owner; otherwise explicit grant or TA).
2. Allowed only from `submitted | queued | running` → `E_STATE_INVALID` otherwise.
3. Call M16-4 `:cancel` on `workflow_run_ref`; set interim `cancel_requested` flag (not a state).
4. Status→`cancelled` lands via the M16/SVC-06 confirmation event (M10-10); audit `sim.run.cancelled`.
5. Idempotency: repeat cancel of a terminal run → `E_STATE_INVALID`; repeat while cancellation pending → **202** replay.

`:rerun` (FR-B2.6) — body `{ "overrides": { "variation_overrides"?: {...}, "model_candidate_ids"?: [...], "service_class"?: "...", "notes"?: "..." }, "reuse_seed": true }` — processing:

1. Authz `sim.run:execute`.
2. Source run must be terminal with a persisted `context_doc` → `E_SIM_RERUN_CONTEXT_MISSING` (409).
3. Copy the frozen context — scenario version pin, embodiment, task goal, effective variation plan, seeds (when `reuse_seed=true`), candidates, source datasets, origin_ref — then apply explicit overrides only; everything not overridden is bit-identical (FR-B2.6: reproduce or refine "without rebuilding context manually").
4. Re-execute M10-1 steps 3–8: validation, usage, and admission are **re-checked at rerun time** — a since-invalidated scenario fails `E_SCN_NOT_VALIDATED`; a since-restricted dataset fails `E_RESTRICTED_DATA`.
5. New run persists `rerun_of=sim-{source}` (source gains `rerun_ids[]`); **202** with new `run_id` + `workflow_run_ref`.

**M10-3 `PUT /api/v1/simulation-runs/{run_id}/output-classification`** — FR-B2.3/B2.5.
Permission: `sim.run:update`. If-Match required.
Body: `{ "default_class": "scenario_diagnostics", "items": [ { "item_id": "out-...", "class": "synthetic_data|evaluation_evidence|scenario_diagnostics|discarded", "reason"? } ], "invalid_run": { "is_invalid": false, "reasons": [ { "kind": "simulator_failure|physics_artifact|rendering_artifact|scenario_config_error|infrastructure_failure|other", "detail": "..." } ] } }`
Processing:

1. Authz `sim.run:update`; If-Match ETag check.
2. Run must be terminal → `E_SIM_RUN_NOT_TERMINAL` (409).
3. Validate classes against the exact FR-B2.3 enum (`synthetic_data | evaluation_evidence | scenario_diagnostics | discarded`); item ids must exist in the output manifest (`E_VALIDATION`).
4. Validate invalid-run reason kinds: these record **simulation defects only** — model-behavior failures are raised to M15 via M15-1 and are never stored as run-invalid reasons (FR-B2.5 separation of sim defects from model failures).
5. **Tx:** upsert per-item classification + `invalid_run` block + outbox events — commit.
6. Side effects by class: `synthetic_data` ⇒ materialize/refresh a `syn-` batch (status `pending`, `synthetic=true`, lineage = run + scenario@version + generation settings, BR-03) and emit `sim.batch.created`; `evaluation_evidence` ⇒ outbox event consumed by M13 (DA-18); `scenario_diagnostics` ⇒ citable evidence for M09-3; `discarded` ⇒ payload eligible for early purge (§2.10.4).
7. Audit `sim.run.classified` (per-class counts + invalid-run reasons). Re-classification stays open until the derived batch/evidence is referenced by a published snapshot or approved report → thereafter `E_SIM_OUTPUT_LOCKED` (409).

**M10-4 `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/generation-requests` · `GET` (same) · `GET/PATCH /api/v1/generation-requests/{request_id}` · `POST ...:submit`** — FR-B3.1/B3.2/B3.4/B3.6.
Permissions: create `sim.generation:create`; read `sim.generation:read`; PATCH `sim.generation:update` (draft only; triage notes while submitted); `:submit` `sim.generation:execute`.
Create body (compact):

```json
{ "title": "...", "objective": { "kind": "coverage_gap", "gap_ref": "dst-.../row-17", "eval_suite_ref": null, "statement": "increase low-light drawer episodes" },
  "source": { "scenario_ref": {"scenario_id": "scn-...", "version": 4}, "source_dataset_id": null, "parent_request_id": null },
  "generator": { "class": "procedural_sim", "name": "replicator", "version": "2.1" },
  "variation_spec": { "axes": [ { "category": "lighting", "parameter": "light.intensity_lux", "sampling": {"distribution": "loguniform", "min": 20, "max": 900}, "seed": 77 } ] },
  "output_spec": { "modalities": ["rgb","depth","robot_state"], "episode_count": 2000, "label_outputs": ["segmentation","object_pose","action_segments"] },
  "expected_volume_gb": 350, "composition_target": { "policy_id": "cpp-...", "synthetic_fraction": 0.3 } }
```

State machine (exact): `draft → submitted → approved | rejected → executing → completed | failed` (transitions: draft→submitted via `:submit`; submitted→approved|rejected via M16-6 approval decision; approved→executing on workflow launch; executing→completed|failed via M10-10).
`:submit` processing:

1. Authz `sim.generation:execute`; request must be `draft` (`E_STATE_INVALID`).
2. Completeness — `objective` mandatory and typed (target coverage gap or evaluation objective, FR-B3.2); exactly one `source` (FR-B3.1) → `E_VALIDATION`.
3. **Source approval checks (FR-B3.1):** scenario source must be a `validated` version (`E_SCN_NOT_VALIDATED`); dataset source must be a **published** snapshot passing M08-9 with `requested_use=generation_source` (`E_RESTRICTED_DATA` on deny); parent generation request must be `completed` (`E_SIM_SOURCE_UNAPPROVED`, 409). No other source kind is representable — generation from nothing is structurally impossible.
4. `generator.class=world_model_generative` requires the tenant flag `world_model_generation_enabled=true`, else `E_SIM_WORLD_MODEL_DISABLED` (403); when enabled, resulting batches are forced `candidate_asset=true` — accepted only as governed candidate synthetic assets, P2, non-default (FR-B3.6).
5. Validate `variation_spec.axes[].category` against the exact FR-B3.4 enum; persist the complete randomization log — dimensions, parameters, sampling ranges, seeds — on the request (per §0.7.5); `output_spec.label_outputs[]` recorded for FR-B3.5 generated ground-truth ingestion.
6. **Tx:** status→`submitted` + create M16-6 approval item (`approval_type=synthetic_generation`, approver permission `sim.generation:approve`) + outbox — commit; **202** `{request_id, status: "submitted", approval_ref}`.
7. On the approval decision event: `submitted→approved` (audit `sim.generation.approved`) → M10 launches the M16-2 workflow (template `synthetic_generation@vN`; M16 performs M17-9 admission; a quota deny surfaces as `blocked_reason` while state stays `approved` until admitted) → `approved→executing` (audit `sim.generation.launched`). Rejection records approver + reason → terminal `rejected` (audit `sim.generation.rejected`).
PATCH: draft-only field edits (If-Match); submitted+ edits → `E_STATE_INVALID`.
GET list filters: `status, objective_kind, generator_class, source_scenario_id, owner_id`.

**M10-5 `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/synthetic-batches` · `GET /api/v1/synthetic-batches/{batch_id}`** — FR-B3.3, BR-03.
Permission: `sim.batch:read`.
Detail response (compact):

```json
{ "batch_id": "syn-...", "synthetic": true, "candidate_asset": false,
  "generator": { "class": "procedural_sim", "name": "replicator", "version": "2.1" },
  "source": { "scenario_ref": "scn-...@v4", "generation_request_id": "gnr-...", "simulation_run_ids": ["sim-..."], "source_dataset_ids": [] },
  "generation_settings_ref": "obj://.../settings.json", "randomization_log": { "axes": [...], "seeds": [...] },
  "content": { "episode_count": 2000, "modalities": [...], "byte_size": 3.5e11, "manifest_uri": "obj://...", "label_manifest": { "types": ["segmentation","object_pose"], "coverage": 1.0 } },
  "validation_status": "pending", "suitability": null, "validation_report_ids": [],
  "deprecated": false, "deprecation": null, "impact_summary": { "direction": "unknown" },
  "origin_ref": { "kind": "coverage_gap", "ref": "dst-.../row-17" }, "sidecar_manifest_uri": "obj://.../c2pa.json" }
```

`synthetic=true` is a server-set constant — never absent, never client-mutable (BR-03); the sidecar manifest carries the same provenance portably (per §0.7.5). List filters: `validation_status, suitability, generator_class, candidate_asset, deprecated, scenario_id, generation_request_id, created_after/before`. Restricted source data propagates restriction flags → reads re-checked (deny ⇒ `E_RESTRICTED_DATA`).

**M10-6 `POST /api/v1/synthetic-batches/{batch_id}/validation-reports` · `PUT /api/v1/synthetic-batches/{batch_id}/validation-status`** — FR-B4.1/B4.2, DA-07.
Permission: `sim.batch:validate` (rubric may require tenant scope, DA-07).
POST report body: `{ "rubric_id": "rub-...", "intended_use": "...", "methodology": "...", "sample_size": 200, "findings": { "fidelity_metrics": {...}, "label_quality": {...}, "artifact_rate": 0.012 }, "known_limitations": "...", "failure_modes": ["..."], "suitability": "training|evaluation|exploration_only", "attachments": ["obj://..."] }` → 201, immutable once referenced by a status change.
PUT status body: `{ "target_status": "validated_training|validated_evaluation|exploration_only|rejected", "validation_report_id": "vld-...", "reason": "..." }`
PUT status processing:

1. Authz `sim.batch:validate`; when the tenant Validation Rubric (DA-07) sets `require_tenant_admin_validation=true`, the M03 check is issued at **tenant** scope.
2. Batch must not be deprecated → `E_SIM_BATCH_DEPRECATED` (409).
3. State machine (exact): only `pending → validated_training | validated_evaluation | exploration_only | rejected`; any status change on a non-pending batch → `E_STATE_INVALID` (the correction path is deprecate + regenerate — never history rewrite, lineage preserved).
4. `validation_report_id` mandatory; the report must belong to this batch and its `suitability` must be consistent with the target status → `E_SIM_VALIDATION_REPORT_REQUIRED` (422). The referenced report becomes immutable.
5. **Tx:** update status + append `sim.batch_status_history` + outbox `sim.batch.validated` — commit. The event drives M08 catalog sync, which gates snapshot membership for training/release-evaluation selection (FR-B4.1): `validated_training` permits training + evaluation use; `validated_evaluation` evaluation only; `exploration_only` mining/analysis only; `rejected` no governed use.
6. Audit `sim.batch.validated` (target status, report id, rubric id).

**M10-7 `POST /api/v1/synthetic-batches/{batch_id}:deprecate`** — FR-B4.5, BR-11.
Permission: `sim.batch:deprecate`.
