Post-release log payloads: cold-storage archival after 12 months, checksummed metadata retained indefinitely while the model is not purged; manifest objects under object-lock (governance mode) — no deletion path while status `active`.

**Processing logic:** (1) **completeness recheck job** — recomputes the BR-07 checklist for `draft` RCs nightly and on consumed events (`mlops.report.approved/superseded`, `mlops.model.card_updated`, `mlops.model.transitioned`, `data.snapshot.deprecated`); refreshes RPT-08 `missing_evidence`. (2) **change detector** — for `submitted|approved|conditionally_approved|remediation_required` RCs, the same events (when they touch frozen items: artifact, lineage snapshot, card limitations, cited report, target class) insert `change_event` + flip status → `re_review_required` in one tx + WARN log + M20 notification (FR-C5.6). (3) **manifest integrity sweep** — weekly re-hash of manifest objects vs. `content_hash`; mismatch → ERROR + FATAL-path tamper alarm (mirror of M13 freeze sweep §2.13.4). (4) **post-release saga** — outbox-driven M15-1 fan-out with write-back (M14-8). (5) **MV refresh** — outbox-incremental + 15 min. (6) **archival** — SVC-15 retention executor per DA-11.

#### 2.14.5 Permission Design

| Permission key | PA | TA | OE | RD (default) | Persona templates granting it |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `release.rc:read` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (member projects) | Model Engineer, Evaluation Lead, Governance Approver, Program Manager |
| `release.rc:create` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Model Engineer (`release:assemble` template §0.2.4) |
| `release.rc:update` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (owner) | Model Engineer |
| `release.rc:submit` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (owner) | Model Engineer |
| `release.rc:approve` (decide + acknowledge-change) | ✓ | ✓ | — | **grant-only** | **Governance / Release Approver** (`approval:decide` template; default built-in role TA per §0.2.4) |
| `release.packaging:update` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Model Engineer; **SVC-09 service identity** (workflow write-back only) |
| `release.post_release_log:create` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Model Engineer, Evaluation Lead |

**Endpoint → permission map (per §1.4.2, one check per handler):** M14-1 POST `release.rc:create` / GET `release.rc:read` · M14-2 GET `release.rc:read` / PATCH `release.rc:update` (withdraw: owner-or-TA rule on top) · M14-3 `release.rc:read` · M14-4 `release.rc:submit` · M14-5 `release.rc:approve` · M14-6 `release.rc:approve` · M14-7 `release.packaging:update` (user or SVC-09 identity) · M14-8 POST `release.post_release_log:create` / GET `release.rc:read` · M14-9 `release.rc:read`.

**Granularity:** project scope primary; instance routes re-check at the RC's project (§1.4.1). Separation of duties is enforced in M14-5 **regardless of role** (matrix default): decider ≠ submitter/owner → `E_RC_SELF_APPROVAL`; PA/TA implicit grants do not bypass it (deny-wins, BR-13). **OE: ✗ on all M14 business resources** (release domain is business, not infrastructure — §0.5.3); OE observes only packaging-workflow infrastructure health via M17. Restricted-lineage rendering: snapshot nodes in evidence views mask labels for readers without M08 access (deny-wins per §0.5.3). Custom roles may receive any subset of these atomic keys (M03-4); `release.rc:approve` is flagged `sensitive` in the M03 catalog, so M03-9 access reviews list its holders per tenant (RPT-11).

**Credential scoping:** evidence manifests and post-release payloads live in tenant evidence buckets with **write-once prefixes**; only the M14 service identity writes manifests; uploads (M14-8) use single-use pre-signed PUT URLs scoped to `…/prl-{id}/` (15-min TTL); downloads are 15-min signed GETs, issuance audited, URLs never logged (§1.4.7). SVC-09 holds exactly `release.packaging:update` + read on the source artifact + write scoped to `…/rls-{id}/packages/` (M16-minted STS, run-scoped). M14's own service identity holds `mlops.model:read` + `mlops.eval:read` for M12-4/M13-3 gate reads. No M14 credential can trigger any deployment system — none is integrated (FR-C5.4).

#### 2.14.6 Logging Design

| Level | Trigger examples |
|---|---|
| DEBUG | checklist evaluation detail, matrix rule resolution, MV refresh (off in prod) |
| INFO | RC created/submitted (manifest seq+hash) / decided (decision enum + quorum) / withdrawn; change acknowledged; packaging descriptor set updated; post-release evidence attached (id+byte_size+checksum) |
| WARN | **approval blocker detected** (quorum pending > SLA, unacknowledged change event, superseded evidence on submitted RC); completeness recheck found new gaps on a draft; self-approval attempt blocked; stale `manifest_seq` decision attempt |
| ERROR | **evidence hash mismatch** (manifest or item re-hash failure — integrity alarm); post-release saga exhaustion; decision tx failure after matrix pass |
| FATAL | confirmed manifest tamper (sweep mismatch reproducible); outbox relay down |

**JSON fields (per §1.4.6 +):** `rc_id`, `model_id`, `manifest_seq`, `content_hash`, `decision`, `quorum_state`, `change_event_id`, `descriptor_id`, `log_id`, `action`, `result`, `error_code`. **Masking:** decision **reason text is stored in the audit event only** — application logs carry `{decision, reason_len}`; limitations/conditions free text logged by id reference; signed URLs and STS tokens never logged; payloads summarized as `{id, byte_size, checksum}` (§1.4.7).

**Shipping:** stdout JSON → platform pipeline (per §1.4.6); `traceparent` spans M14 → M12-4/M13-3 gate reads → M16/SVC-09 packaging → M15 saga, one trace per RC lifecycle action.

**Audit events (M03-11):** `release.rc.created`, `release.rc.submitted` `{manifest_seq, content_hash}`, `release.rc.decided` `{decision, reason, conditions?, manifest_seq, matrix_rule_ref}`, `release.rc.change_detected` `{kind, source_event_id}`, `release.rc.change_acknowledged` `{change_event_ids, new_manifest_seq}`, `release.rc.packaging_updated`, `release.post_release_log.attached`, `release.rc.withdrawn` — the complete AC-12/AC-14 release decision trail consumed by M03-9 access reviews (RPT-11).

### 2.15 M15 — Failure Analysis & Closed-Loop Management

#### 2.15.1 Module Overview

M15 is the **closed-loop station of the platform** (station 7, §0.2.1; BP-05 end to end): the single system of record for **Failure Records** (`fal-`) from every detection source, their triage, their clustering into systematic gaps, their conversion into remediation work items, and the verification links that prove the loop closed. M15 implements the failure flywheel (per §0.7.5): failures are ingested as structured events, clustered by shared attributes or embedding similarity, triaged to candidate root cause, then dispatched as targeted work — new scenarios, new collection, corrections, suite updates, retraining — and tracked until the next candidate is evaluated against the relevant failure cases (BP-05 step 5; ASAM scenario-library regression pattern per §0.7.5).

**Owns:** Failure Record (mutable triage fields, **immutable source evidence**, Table 0.3 #17); Failure Cluster + memberships (computed via M18-7); Conversion Links (failure → work item refs, BR-10); Verification Links (failure → evaluation report refs); closed-loop report (RPT-09).
**Excludes — failure *detection* stays in the source modules** (boundary §1.6.5): M13 evaluation failure cases (converted in via M13-9 §2.13.3), M10 simulation diagnostics, M07 annotation-QA rejections, M13-8 trial evidence, M14-8 post-release logs, M19-6 workbench failure tags — M15 never re-detects, it registers. **Remediation *execution* belongs to the owning modules**: M15 opens work items (M16-5 work requests; M09-6 scenario requests; M05-10 remediation tasks; M18-8 cohorts) and tracks status — it never executes collection, annotation, simulation, training, or evaluation itself. **Regression sets are owned by M13** (M13-10 §2.13.3): M15 links failures into them and consumes their evaluation events; it does not define or version them (FR-C6.5 boundary).

**Roles:** Evaluation Lead (primary triage owner — `failure:triage` persona template §0.2.4); **all engineering personas** create and read failure records from their own surfaces (Robotics Data Engineer, Annotation QA Lead, Simulation Engineer, Model Engineer); Program Manager reads RPT-09; Governance Approver reads safety-relevant failure trails.

**Upstream (sources, FR-C6.1):** M13 §2.13.3 (M13-9 conversion, `mlops.regression.member_evaluated` events), M10 §2.10.3 (simulation diagnostics/invalid-run intakes), M07 §2.7.3 (QA rejection intakes), M14 §2.14.3 (M14-8 post-release saga), M19 §2.19.3 (M19-6 failure tags), M05 (quality failures per §0.5.2 edge M05→M15); M18 §2.18.3 (M18-7 clustering engine).
**Downstream:** M16 §2.16.3 (M16-5 work requests — the uniform remediation spine, BR-10), M09 §2.9.3 (M09-6 scenario requests, FR-B5.1), M05 §2.5.3 (M05-10 remediation tasks, FR-B5.3), M07 (correction tasks via work requests), M11 (retraining requests), M13 (regression-set membership input to M13-10), M18-8 (data-mining cohorts), M09-7/RPT-05 (failure-linked scenario coverage), M20 (severity alerts).

**FRD coverage:** FR-C6.1–C6.4 (C6.5 with M13 per §1.7.1); FR-B5.1/B5.3 conversion touchpoints; BR-10; BP-04 step 5 → BP-05 steps 1–5; RPT-09; AC-11; research basis §0.7.5 (failure flywheel, scenario-library regression).

#### 2.15.2 Functional & UI/UX Design

**Primary functions**

| # | Function | Description | FRD |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Failure intake | Records from exactly five sources: `evaluation \| simulation \| annotation_qa \| real_world_trial \| post_release_log`; deduplicated by source ref; source evidence immutable | FR-C6.1, BP-05 step 1 |
| F2 | Structured record | source, affected model/dataset, task, environment, failure category, severity, owner, status | FR-C6.2 |
| F3 | Triage | Category (8-value BP-05 enum), severity, candidate root causes, recommended action, owner | BP-05 steps 2–3, BR-10 |
| F4 | Clustering | Group by shared attributes (task, object, action phase, scenario, model output) or embedding similarity via M18-7 | FR-C6.3 |
| F5 | Conversion | Six work-item kinds → owning-module objects + M16-5 work requests; links recorded | FR-C6.4, BR-10, FR-B5.1/B5.3, BP-05 step 4 |
| F6 | Verification | Links to evaluation evidence on the **next candidate** covering the relevant failure cases; status flips only on approved immutable reports | FR-C6.4, BP-05 step 5, BR-08 |
| F7 | Closed-loop report | Detection→triage→conversion→remediation→verification funnel | RPT-09 |

**UI pages**

**P1 — Failure Inbox** (project-scoped). Left facet rail with live counts: **source** (5 values), **failure category** (8 values), **severity** (s1–s4), status (7 states), affected model family, task, has_conversion, in_cluster. List rows: severity badge (s1 red pulsing for `safety_relevant_behavior`), title, source chip + deep link to origin (`evr-…#fc-…`, `sim-…`, QA review, trial, `prl-…`), category, age, owner avatar, status. Bulk select → bulk triage prefill / add to cluster scope / convert with cluster members. Default sort: severity then age. Bindings: M15-1 GET (`include_facets=true`).

**P2 — Failure Detail & Triage Panel.** Header: `fal-` id, status stepper (`new → triaged → converted → in_remediation → verified/wont_fix → closed`), severity/category badges. Left: **immutable source evidence block** (observed vs expected, episode/artifact refs with M19 "open in workbench" launcher, origin link) — visually locked. Right: **triage panel**:

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| failure_category | select | exact 8-enum (BP-05) | provisional from source | Select category… | required; enum-only (real-time) |
| severity | select | s1\|s2\|s3\|s4 | provisional | — | required; `safety_relevant_behavior` below s2 demands justification note (warn + audit) |
| root_cause_hypotheses[] | repeater | {hypothesis ≤500, kind enum, confidence} | — | e.g. insufficient transparent-object data | ≥1 required at triage; kind ∈ data_gap\|model_capability\|simulation_artifact\|labeling_error\|sensor_issue |
| recommended_action | select | 6 conversion kinds + wont_fix | — | — | required (BP-05 step 3) |
| owner_id | user picker | `usr-` | suggester by category routing | — | required (FR-C6.2) |
| notes | textarea | ≤2000 | — | Triage context… | optional; sanitized |

Below: **conversion links** (work-item cards with live status mirrors), **verification tracker** strip (linked work items → "awaiting next candidate evaluation" → evidence links with passed/failed chips → Verify button), cluster memberships, timeline. Bindings: M15-2/M15-3/M15-4/M15-6.

**P3 — Conversion Dialog** (from P1/P2/P4). **Work-item type picker**: six cards with descriptions and target module — `data_mining` (M18 cohort), `new_collection` (M04/M05 remediation — FR-B5.3), `annotation_correction` (M07), `simulation_expansion` (M09 scenario request — FR-B5.1), `evaluation_update` (M13 suite/regression update), `model_retraining` (M11 plan). Selecting a card reveals its kind-specific form (e.g., simulation_expansion: title, gap description, variation goals chips; data_mining: cohort spec builder seeded with the failure's shared attributes; annotation_correction: source annotation project pre-resolved from the failure's episode refs). Submit → M15-4; result panel lists created refs (`wrq-…`, scenario request, cohort) with deep links.

Conversion dialog common input fields (kind-specific params per the M15-4 table, §2.15.3):

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| target_kind | card picker | exact 6-enum | recommended_action from triage | — | required; cards outside `details.allowed_kinds[]` disabled with reason tooltip |
| params.* | per-kind form | see M15-4 table | seeded from failure attributes | — | kind-specific required fields enforced real-time; unresolvable refs flagged on blur |
| work_request.priority | select | low\|normal\|high\|critical | normal (s1→critical) | — | required; critical requires s1/s2 severity or justification |
| work_request.due_date | date | RFC 3339 date | — | 2026-07-01 | optional; ≥ today |
| work_request.note | textarea | ≤1000 | triage notes excerpt | Context for the executing team… | optional; sanitized |
| include_cluster_members | checkbox | bool | false | "applies to 17 cluster members" | only enabled when record is in a current cluster; >50 members → async notice (202) |

**P4 — Cluster Explorer.** Cluster-set selector (version, computed_at, method, **stale** chip when recompute pending); cluster grid sized by member count, colored by max severity; detail drawer: label, **shared attributes table** (task, object class, action phase, scenario, model output pattern — FR-C6.3), representative failure, member list, **conversion coverage bar** (% members converted/verified), actions: bulk triage prefill, cluster-level convert (P3 with members), create regression-set proposal (deep link to M13-10 with `source_failure_ids` prefilled). Bindings: M15-5.

Compute panel input fields:

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| scope.source[] / scope.failure_category[] / scope.status[] | multi-select | enums (5 / 8 / 7) | open statuses | — | optional; live preview count ("matches 412 failures"); 0–1 matches blocks submit |
| scope.model_family | select | family list (M12-7) | — | acme-vla | optional |
| scope.created_after | date | RFC 3339 | −90 d | — | optional |
| method | radio | attribute_grouping \| embedding | attribute_grouping | — | required; embedding reveals embedding block |
| group_by[] | multi-select | task_ref, object_class, action_phase, scenario_ref, model_output_pattern | task_ref+object_class | — | required iff attribute_grouping (≥1) — FR-C6.3 attribute set |
| embedding.space_id | select | registered spaces (M18-3) | project default | — | required iff method=embedding |
| embedding.algorithm / min_cluster_size | select / number | hdbscan\|kmeans / 2–100 | hdbscan / 5 | — | required iff embedding; int bounds real-time |

**P5 — Verification Tracker** (list mode). Rows = failures in `converted`/`in_remediation` with work-item status mirrors; "ready for verification" filter (all linked work items done, no passed evidence yet); each row shows which regression set / suite covers it (from M13-10 membership) and the latest candidate evaluated. Binding: M15-1 GET (`status` filter) + M15-6.

Manual verification-link input fields (P2/P5 panel):

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| report_id | picker | approved `evr-` only | latest approved covering this failure | Select evaluation report… | required; draft/superseded excluded from list (server re-checks, BR-08) |
| candidate_id | picker | `mdl-` | report's candidate | — | required; must be later than affected model (next-candidate rule); override requires reason field |
| case_refs[] | multi-select | regression member / failure-case refs | auto-matched by `source_failure_ids` | — | ≥1 required; must cover this failure |
| outcome | radio | passed\|failed | from report verdicts | — | required; prefilled from M13 data, user confirms |
| verdict | checkbox | "mark verified" | off | — | enabled only when status=in_remediation ∧ ≥1 passed link |

**P6 — Closed-Loop Funnel Dashboard (RPT-09).** Funnel detected→triaged→converted→in_remediation→verified→closed with stage conversion rates; median stage ages; breakdown tabs by source/category/severity/model family; aging table of open s1/s2; wont_fix ledger; cluster coverage (% of open failures inside a cluster with ≥1 converted member); verification backlog. Binding: M15-7.

**Validation logic.** Real-time: enum membership (source 5, category 8, severity 4, conversion kinds 6), id prefix regex (`fal-`, `evr-`, `mdl-`, `dss-`, `scn-`, `wrq-`), repeater minimums, kind-specific required params in P3 (card cannot submit with missing params — inline red), cluster scope preview count (blocks empty scope). On-submit (server-authoritative): state-machine legality, source-ref resolution, dedup detection (returns existing record — UI navigates to it with toast "Already registered as fal-… (deduplicated)"), approved-report check on verification. Sanitization: free text HTML-stripped, escaped markdown subset; environment JSON schema-checked; no raw URIs rendered as links unless platform-internal.

**Output rendering.** Loading: skeleton list + facet shimmer; cluster compute shows job progress (202 → poll). Success toasts: "Failure triaged — category: manipulation, severity s2", "Created scenario request scr-… + work request wrq-…", "Verification evidence linked — passed (evr-…)". Empty states: inbox "No failures match — that is either good news or a detection gap" with source-coverage hint; P4 "No cluster set computed yet" + compute CTA.

**Exception handling.** Network: retry banner, optimistic UI only for facet toggles. `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`: triage/convert controls hidden without keys; read-only banner on race. `E_RATE_LIMITED`: backoff toast with Retry-After. `E_VALIDATION`: inline field errors from `details[]`. **`E_FAL_INVALID_CONVERSION` (422): dialog highlights the failing element — invalid kind for record state, missing kind-specific params, unresolvable target refs — and lists `details.allowed_kinds[]`.** `E_FAL_NOT_TRIAGED` (409): convert blocked with "Triage before conversion (BR-10)" + jump to triage panel. `E_FAL_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED`: verify button disabled with tooltip "Requires an approved evaluation report covering this failure's cases". 412: reload-merge per §1.4.4.

#### 2.15.3 Interface Design

Per §1.4 conventions; module error namespace `E_FAL_*`; authz M03-10; audit M03-11.

**Normative state machine (FailureRecord.status, exact)**

```text
new ──triage (M15-3)──▶ triaged ──convert (M15-4)──▶ converted ──work item active (M16 events)──▶
in_remediation ──verification (M15-6, requires linked eval evidence)──▶ verified ──▶ closed
                                                   └──▶ wont_fix ──▶ closed
  · verified REQUIRES ≥1 passed verification link to an approved immutable evaluation report (BR-08)
  · wont_fix: reasoned decision, reachable from triaged or in_remediation (annotated shortcut; reason mandatory)
  · closed: from verified or wont_fix only (M15-2 controlled status write); re-detection after close
    creates a NEW record linked by `recurrence_of`
  · additional conversions allowed from converted/in_remediation (a failure may need several work items)
  · all other transitions are system-driven; source evidence immutable in every state (Table 0.3 #17)
```

**Module error codes**

| Code | HTTP | Raised when |
|---|---|---|
| `E_FAL_SOURCE_INVALID` | 422 | `source` outside the 5-value enum or `source_ref` shape mismatched to the source kind |
| `E_FAL_INVALID_CONVERSION` | 422 | `target_kind` outside the 6-value enum, kind-specific params missing/unresolvable, or conversion illegal for record state; `details.allowed_kinds[]` |
| `E_FAL_NOT_TRIAGED` | 409 | `:convert` on a `new` record (BR-10 ordering: triage before remediation) |
| `E_FAL_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED` | 422 | Verification verdict without a passed link to an approved, non-draft evaluation report |
| `E_FAL_CLUSTER_SCOPE_EMPTY` | 422 | `:compute` scope resolves to <2 records |
| `E_FAL_CLUSTER_JOB_RUNNING` | 409 | Concurrent compute on an overlapping project scope (advisory lock) |
| `E_FAL_EVIDENCE_IMMUTABLE` | 422 | PATCH attempts to alter the source evidence block |
| Shared | — | `E_CONFLICT` (dedup payload mismatch), `E_STATE_INVALID`, `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED`, `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_RATE_LIMITED` per §1.4.3 |

**M15-1 `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/failure-records` · `GET` (same)** — create / list (FR-C6.1/C6.2).
Permission: `failure.record:create` / `failure.record:read`. Callers include users (any engineering persona) and source-module flows in user context (M13-9, M19-6) or service identity (M14-8 saga; recorded as system actor).
POST body:

```json
{ "source": "evaluation",
  "source_ref": { "report_id": "evr-...", "failure_case_id": "fc-..." },
  "title": "drops transparent bottle on regrasp", "description": "...",
  "affected_model_id": "mdl-...", "affected_dataset_ref": { "snapshot_id": "dss-...", "semver": "2.1.0" },
  "task_ref": "tsk-pick-bottle", "environment": { "site": "kitchen-sim", "lighting": "low" },
  "failure_category": "manipulation", "severity": "s2",
  "evidence": { "episode_refs": ["sim-...#ep-17"], "artifact_uris": ["obj://..."],
                "observed": "...", "expected": "..." },
  "owner_id": "usr-..." }
```

Processing:

1. Authz `failure.record:create` at `{project_id}`.
2. Validate `source` against the exact 5-value enum (FR-C6.1) and `source_ref` shape per kind — `evaluation:{report_id, failure_case_id}`, `simulation:{sim_run_id, episode_ref?}`, `annotation_qa:{qa_review_id | task_id}`, `real_world_trial:{trial_evidence_id, item?}`, `post_release_log:{post_release_log_id, item_index?}` → **422 `E_FAL_SOURCE_INVALID`**; category (if provided) against the 8-value enum, severity s1–s4 → `E_VALIDATION`.
3. Resolve source objects in-project via owning-module reads (M13/M10/M07/M14) → `E_NOT_FOUND`/`E_VALIDATION`; restricted-asset refs pass deny-wins checks for the *creator*; evidence URIs must be platform object-store refs.
4. **Dedup:** unique canonical source key `(tenant_id, source, canonical(source_ref))` — existing match with identical payload → **200** existing record + `"deduplicated": true` (idempotent; M13-9 relies on this); differing payload → `E_CONFLICT` (409).
5. **Tx:** insert `failure.failure_record` (`status='new'`; category/severity stored as *provisional* until triage; evidence block marked immutable) + outbox `failure.record.created` — commit. Audit; s1/safety intakes additionally fan to M20 alerting (FR-D4.4). **201** `{failure_id, status:"new"}`.

GET filters: `source, failure_category, severity, status, affected_model_id, affected_model_family, affected_snapshot_id, task_ref, owner_id, cluster_id, has_conversion, has_verification, recurrence_of, created_after/before, q, sort`; `include_facets=true` adds `{facets: {source:{evaluation:41,...}, failure_category:{...}, severity:{...}, status:{...}}}` for P1.

**M15-2 `GET /api/v1/failure-records/{failure_id}` · `PATCH`** — detail / controlled update.
Permission: `failure.record:read` / `failure.record:update`. GET returns record + immutable evidence block + triage block + conversion links (with mirrored work-item statuses) + verification links + cluster memberships + timeline + `allowed_actions[]`. PATCH (If-Match): mutable metadata only — `title, description, owner_id, tags`; **controlled status writes:** `{"status":"wont_fix","reason":"..."}` (from `triaged|in_remediation`, reason required) and `{"status":"closed"}` (from `verified|wont_fix`); any other status value → `E_STATE_INVALID` (system-driven transitions only); any mutation under `evidence` or `source_ref` → **422 `E_FAL_EVIDENCE_IMMUTABLE`** (Table 0.3 #17). Status writes tx with outbox `failure.record.status_changed`; audit.

**M15-3 `PUT /api/v1/failure-records/{failure_id}/triage`** — triage (BP-05 steps 2–3).
Permission: `failure.record:triage`. Body: `{ "failure_category": "manipulation", "severity": "s2", "root_cause_hypotheses": [ { "hypothesis": "...", "kind": "data_gap", "confidence": "high" } ], "recommended_action": "simulation_expansion", "owner_id": "usr-...", "notes"?: "..." }`
Processing:

1. Authz `failure.record:triage`; load `FOR UPDATE`; status ∈ `{new, triaged}` (re-triage allowed until conversion; after `converted`, category/severity changes append a triage revision with WARN + audit rather than overwrite history) → else `E_STATE_INVALID`.
2. `failure_category` against the **exact 8-value enum (BP-05 step 2): `perception | temporal_reasoning | action_prediction | manipulation | locomotion | safety_relevant_behavior | data_quality | simulation_artifact`**; severity required; ≥1 root-cause hypothesis; `recommended_action` ∈ 6 conversion kinds + `wont_fix` → `E_VALIDATION`. `safety_relevant_behavior` with severity < s2 requires `justification` (warn-level audit).
3. **Tx:** update triage fields + status `new→triaged` + append triage revision row + outbox `failure.record.triaged` — commit.
4. Audit `failure.record.triaged`; M20 routes s1 to the project's safety channel. **200** updated record. Idempotent: identical re-PUT replays.

**M15-4 `POST /api/v1/failure-records/{failure_id}:convert`** — create remediation work item (FR-C6.4, BR-10, BP-05 step 4).
Permission: `failure.record:convert` (conversion runs in **user context**: the caller must also hold the target-module create permission — checked transitively, mirror of M13-9 §2.13.3).
Body: `{ "target_kind": "simulation_expansion", "params": { "scenario_request": { "title": "transparent-object regrasp variants", "gap_description": "...", "variation_goals": ["lighting","object_transparency","grasp_pose"] } }, "work_request": { "priority": "high", "due_date"?: "2026-07-01", "note"?: "..." }, "include_cluster_members"?: false }`
Kind-specific `params` (exact kinds → targets, per BP-05 step 4 / FR-C6.4):

| target_kind | Owning-module object created | Work request (M16-5) type |
|---|---|---|
| `data_mining` | M18-8 cohort (spec seeded from failure attributes) | `data_processing` |
| `new_collection` | M05-10 remediation task (`kind=recollection_request`) when recording-bound; else campaign brief for M04 | `data_processing` (FR-B5.3: simulation-source failures → real collection) |
| `annotation_correction` | **M07-3 `tasks:generate`** on the source annotation project (correction scope: the failure's episode/segment refs; generated tasks run the standard claim→submit→QA machine, §2.7.3) | `annotation` |
| `simulation_expansion` | M09-6 scenario request (FR-B5.1; `source_failure_ids` linked) | `simulation` |
| `evaluation_update` | proposal payload for M13 (suite new-version / regression-set member addition via M13-10 — M13 owns the set) | `evaluation` |
| `model_retraining` | **M11-1 draft training plan** prefilled from the failure context (affected model family, suggested snapshots incl. regression members); the Model Engineer completes and submits it through the normal M11-2 flow | `training` |

Processing:

1. Authz `failure.record:convert`; load `FOR UPDATE`; status ∈ `{triaged, converted, in_remediation}` → `new` ⇒ **409 `E_FAL_NOT_TRIAGED`**; terminal states ⇒ `E_STATE_INVALID`.
2. **Validate target type:** `target_kind` in the exact 6-value enum + kind-specific params present and resolvable (annotation project exists, scenario request title non-empty, cohort spec valid, model family known) → **422 `E_FAL_INVALID_CONVERSION`** with `details.allowed_kinds[]` and field paths.
3. **Create target objects (before local tx; each call idempotent by propagated Idempotency-Key, user context):** (a) owning-module object per the table above — M09-6 / M18-8 / M05-10 / M07 / M13-10-proposal / M11-brief; (b) **always** an M16-5 work request (the uniform, BR-10-mandated remediation spine) carrying `{origin: {kind:"failure_record", failure_id}, target_refs}`. Remote failure → no local write; retry-safe by key.
4. **Tx (link in tx):** insert `failure.conversion_link` `{failure_id, target_kind, work_request_ref, target_refs, created_by}` + status `triaged→converted` (unchanged if already ≥ converted) + outbox `failure.record.converted` — **commit**.
5. Audit `failure.record.converted`; **201** `{conversion_link_id, work_request_ref, target_refs}`. `include_cluster_members=true`: fan-out applies the same conversion to every member of the record's current cluster (each idempotent; >50 members executes as an M16 batch workflow → **202 + `workflow_run_ref`** per §1.4.5).
6. Duplicate guard: unique `(failure_id, target_kind, primary_target_ref)` — replays return the existing link (200).

**M15-5 `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/failure-clusters:compute` · `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/failure-clusters` · `GET /api/v1/failure-clusters/{cluster_id}`** — clustering (FR-C6.3).
Permission: `failure.cluster:compute` (POST) / `failure.record:read` (GETs).
POST body: `{ "scope": { "source"?: ["evaluation","simulation"], "failure_category"?: ["manipulation"], "model_family"?: "acme-vla", "status"?: ["new","triaged"], "created_after"?: "..." }, "method": "attribute_grouping|embedding", "group_by"?: ["task_ref","object_class","action_phase","scenario_ref","model_output_pattern"], "embedding"?: { "space_id": "esp-...", "algorithm": "hdbscan", "min_cluster_size": 5 }, "label_policy": "top_shared_attributes" }`
Processing:

1. Authz; resolve scope → <2 records ⇒ **422 `E_FAL_CLUSTER_SCOPE_EMPTY`**; per-project advisory lock ⇒ concurrent compute **409 `E_FAL_CLUSTER_JOB_RUNNING`**.
2. `attribute_grouping` (FR-C6.3 shared attributes: task, object, action phase, scenario, model output): SQL grouping over record attributes + linked episode tags; synchronous for ≤2,000 records → **201** with the new cluster set. `embedding` method or larger scopes: call **M18-7 `POST /internal/v1/analytics/cluster`** (§2.18.3) with feature refs (failure text embeddings + linked episode/model-output embeddings, per §0.7.5 flywheel finding) wrapped in an M16 analytics workflow → **202 + `workflow_run_ref`**; results land via the M18-7 completion callback.
3. **Tx (on completion):** insert `failure.failure_cluster` rows (label = top shared attributes; representative = highest-severity medoid) + `failure.cluster_membership` rows + new `cluster_set_version`; previous set marked `superseded` (history retained); outbox `failure.clusters.computed` — commit; audit.
4. Clusters are **advisory views**: they never mutate member triage fields; bulk actions always re-route through M15-3/M15-4 per record.

GET list params: `cluster_set_version?` (default current), `min_size, failure_category, q, sort=-size|-max_severity`. GET detail: cluster + shared_attributes + members (paged) + conversion coverage `{members, converted, verified}` + representative record.

**M15-6 `PUT /api/v1/failure-records/{failure_id}/verification`** — verification links (FR-C6.4, BP-05 step 5).
Permission: `failure.record:update` (manual); M15 service identity for the automated path (M13 event consumer; recorded as system actor).
Body: `{ "links": [ { "evidence_kind": "evaluation_report|regression_member_result", "report_id": "evr-...", "candidate_id": "mdl-...", "case_refs": ["rgs-...@v1#3"], "outcome": "passed|failed" } ], "verdict"?: "verified" }`
Processing:

1. Authz; load `FOR UPDATE`; links may accumulate from `converted` onward; `verdict:"verified"` requires status `in_remediation` → else `E_STATE_INVALID`.
2. Evidence gates: every `report_id` must be an **approved, immutable** M13 report (BR-08; `content_hash` present), non-superseded — draft/superseded ⇒ **422 `E_FAL_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED`**; `candidate_id` must be a **later candidate** than `affected_model_id` (next-candidate rule, BP-05 step 5: same family later version or the family targeted by the retraining work item; override requires `override_reason`, audited); `case_refs` must cover this failure (regression-set members with this `fal-` in `source_failure_ids` — M13-10 §2.13.3 — or matching failure-case refs).
3. **Tx:** upsert links (unique `(failure_id, report_id, case_ref)`) + on `verdict:"verified"` with ≥1 `passed` link: status `in_remediation→verified`; `failed` outcomes record the attempt, keep `in_remediation`, and after 2 failed attempts WARN + suggest re-triage; outbox `failure.verification.linked` — commit.
4. Audit `failure.verification.linked` `{report_id, outcome, verdict?}`; **200** updated verification block. **Automated path:** the verification status updater (§2.15.4) consumes `mlops.regression.member_evaluated` (M13-10) and calls this endpoint service-side; tenant policy `auto_verify=false` (default) leaves the final `verified` verdict to the owner.

**M15-7 `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/reports/closed-loop`** — RPT-09.
Permission: `failure.record:read`. Params: `window=90d|180d|365d`, `group_by=source|failure_category|severity|model_family`, `format=json|csv`.
Response: `{ "funnel": { "detected": 412, "triaged": 391, "converted": 300, "in_remediation": 240, "verified": 171, "closed": 158, "wont_fix": 31 }, "stage_conversion": { "triage_rate": 0.95, "conversion_rate": 0.77, "verification_rate": 0.71 }, "median_age_days": { "new_to_triaged": 2.1, "triaged_to_converted": 4.0, "converted_to_verified": 19.5 }, "by_group": [ ... ], "open_critical": [ { "failure_id": "fal-...", "severity": "s1", "age_days": 12 } ], "cluster_coverage": { "clustered_pct": 0.64, "clusters_with_conversion_pct": 0.81 }, "verification_backlog": 23 }`. Backed by `failure.closed_loop_mv`; the BP-05 funnel "ensures failures lead to concrete improvement" (RPT-09 purpose); rows deep-link to P1 filters.

**Events published (outbox, envelope per §1.4.5)**

| Event type | Emitted by | Primary consumers |
|---|---|---|
| `failure.record.created` `{source, deduplicated}` | M15-1 | M18 (gap analysis M18-10), M20 (s1 alerts), RPT-09 MV |
| `failure.record.triaged` `{category, severity}` | M15-3 | M20 (safety routing), M09-7/RPT-05 (failure-category coverage), RPT-09 MV |
| `failure.record.converted` `{target_kind, work_request_ref}` | M15-4 | M16 (origin backlink), owning modules (context), RPT-09 MV |
| `failure.record.status_changed` `{from, to}` | mirror job / M15-2 | RPT-09 MV, M20 (verification-ready notices) |
| `failure.verification.linked` `{report_id, outcome}` | M15-6 | M13 (regression coverage acknowledgement), RPT-09 MV |
| `failure.clusters.computed` `{cluster_set_version}` | M15-5 | M18 (coverage), M09 (scenario gap candidates), P4 refresh |

**Events consumed**

| Event type (producer) | M15 reaction |
|---|---|
| `mlops.regression.member_evaluated` / `mlops.report.approved` (M13 §2.13.4) | verification status updater: join covered members' `source_failure_ids` → create verification links (M15-6 service path); `auto_verify` policy applied |
| `orch.work_request.approved/rejected/scheduled/completed/cancelled` (M16) | remediation status mirror: `conversion_link.status_cache`; first active item flips `converted→in_remediation`; `rejected` marks the link `remediation_rejected` and re-opens the conversion prompt; all-done → verification-ready prompt |
| `sim.run.classified` filtered to invalid/discarded outputs (M10), `label.qa.failure_raised` (M07) | optional tenant-enabled auto-intake → M15-1 service path (dedup by source key) — detection stays upstream, registration here (§1.6.5) |
| `release.post_release_log.attached` (M14-8 saga) | failure intakes with `source=post_release_log` write-back links |
| `mlops.model.transitioned` (M12) | refreshes "latest candidate evaluated" hints on P5 verification tracker |

**Cross-module consumption map (M15 as provider):** M09 reads `source_failure_ids` on scenario requests (FR-B5.2 backlink); M13-10 validates `source_failure_ids` and emits member-evaluated events back; M16 carries conversion work requests; M18 clusters and reads failure facts for coverage gaps (M18-10); M19 deep-links evidence into review sessions; M14/RPT-08 reads post-release failure links; M09-7/RPT-05 joins failure-linked scenario coverage.

**Concurrency & idempotency summary:** intake deduped by canonical source key; triage/convert/verify under record row lock + If-Match; conversion remote-creates idempotent by propagated key, local link tx after; cluster compute under project advisory lock, sets versioned append-only; verification links unique by `(failure_id, report_id, case_ref)`.

#### 2.15.4 Data Design

PostgreSQL, schema **`failure.*`** (single writer M15). Evidence artifacts (frames, clips, logs) stay in source-module object storage — M15 stores **refs + checksums only**; failure free text and triage data live in Postgres.

| Table | Key columns (abridged) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `failure.failure_record` | `failure_id pk (fal-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `project_id`, `source` (5-enum, FR-C6.1), `source_ref jsonb` + `source_key` (canonical hash, unique per tenant), `title`, `description`, `affected_model_id?`, `affected_model_family?`, `affected_dataset_ref jsonb?`, `task_ref?`, `environment jsonb?`, `failure_category` (8-enum), `category_provisional bool`, `severity` (s1–s4), `status` (7-state enum §2.15.3), `root_cause_hypotheses jsonb`, `recommended_action?`, `owner_id?`, `evidence jsonb` (**immutable block**: episode_refs, artifact_uris+checksums, observed, expected), `triaged_by/at?`, `wont_fix_reason?`, `recurrence_of?`, `created_by`, `etag_version`, timestamps | **Partitioned by `(tenant_id, created_month)`** (LIST tenant → RANGE month, per mandate); unique `(tenant_id, source_key)` (dedup); idx `(project_id, status, severity)`, `(failure_category)`, `(affected_model_family)`, GIN `(source_ref)`; trigger guard blocks `evidence`/`source_ref` UPDATE (Table 0.3 #17) |
| `failure.triage_revision` | `(failure_id, revision) pk`, `category`, `severity`, `hypotheses jsonb`, `recommended_action`, `owner_id`, `notes?`, `actor`, `at` | Append-only triage history (post-conversion re-triage trail) |
| `failure.failure_cluster` | `cluster_id pk (fcl-ulid)`, `tenant_id`, `project_id`, `cluster_set_version int`, `label`, `shared_attributes jsonb` (FR-C6.3 attribute keys), `method` (`attribute_grouping\|embedding`), `params jsonb`, `size int`, `max_severity`, `representative_failure_id`, `status` (`current\|superseded`), `computed_by`, `computed_at`, `workflow_run_ref?` | Sets versioned per project; previous sets retained `superseded` |
| `failure.cluster_membership` | `(cluster_id, failure_id) pk`, `score?`, `assigned_at` | Bulk-loaded per compute; idx `(failure_id)` (membership reverse lookup) |
| `failure.conversion_link` | `link_id pk (cvl-ulid)`, `failure_id fk`, `target_kind` (6-enum), `work_request_ref (wrq-)`, `target_refs jsonb` (scenario_request_id / cohort_id / remediation_task_id / proposal refs), `status_cache` (mirrored work-item status), `created_by`, `created_at` | Unique `(failure_id, target_kind, primary_target_ref)`; BR-10/FR-C6.4 evidence |
| `failure.verification_link` | `vlink_id pk (vrl-ulid)`, `failure_id fk`, `evidence_kind`, `report_id (evr-)`, `candidate_id (mdl-)`, `case_refs jsonb`, `outcome` (`passed\|failed`), `override_reason?`, `linked_by` (user\|service), `linked_at` | Unique `(failure_id, report_id, case_ref)`; FR-C6.4 verification evidence |
| `failure.intake_dlq` | `(source, source_key)`, payload, error, at | Failed service intakes for operator replay (90 d) |
| `failure.closed_loop_mv` | keyed `(project_id, group_key)` | Backs M15-7 / P6 |

**ER relationships:** `failure_record 1—n triage_revision`, `1—n conversion_link`, `1—n verification_link`, `n—m failure_cluster` via `cluster_membership`; `failure_record n—1 model_candidate` / `dataset_snapshot` (cross-schema refs by id into M12/M08); `conversion_link n—1 work_request` (M16) + polymorphic `target_refs` into M09/M18/M05/M07/M11 (service-layer integrity, single-writer respected — M15 stores refs, never foreign rows); `verification_link n—1 evaluation_report` (M13; report immutability guaranteed upstream by BR-08); regression-set membership lives in M13's `regression_set_member.source_failure_ids` (M13-10 §2.13.4 GIN) — M15 joins by id, never writes it; `recurrence_of` self-reference chains re-detections.

**Volumetrics & partitioning:** failures are the high-volume governance entity of Subsystem C (10⁴–10⁶/tenant/yr at fleet scale) — hence the mandated **tenant + month partitioning**; partition pruning serves the inbox (filters always carry tenant + recency); facet counts from a per-partition rollup. Cluster memberships bulk-load per compute (COPY); MV refresh outbox-incremental + hourly.

**Retention (DA-11):** failure records referenced by regression sets (M13-10), release decisions (M14 remediation refs), or open conversion links — **retained `active`** regardless of age (BR-14 closed-loop auditability). `closed`/`wont_fix` records: archive after tenant window (default 24 months) via SVC-15 → `purged-metadata-retained` keeps identity, source_key, category/severity, status history, conversion/verification link rows; drops free text (description, hypotheses, notes) and evidence ref payload details. Superseded cluster sets: 12 months then purge (recomputable). DLQ 90 d.

**Processing logic:** (1) **intake consumers** — idempotent service intakes from the M14-8 saga and optional tenant-enabled auto-intake of `sim.run.classified` (invalid/discarded outputs) / `label.qa.failure_raised` events (per §0.5.2 edges); all dedup through `source_key`; failures → DLQ. (2) **remediation status mirror** — consumes M16 work-request lifecycle events (`orch.work_request.approved/rejected/scheduled/completed/cancelled`; `rejected` → conversion link `remediation_rejected` + owner re-route notification): updates `conversion_link.status_cache`; first active work item flips `converted→in_remediation` (tx + outbox `failure.record.status_changed`); completed-all prompts the verification tracker (P5). (3) **verification status updater** — consumes `mlops.regression.member_evaluated` + `mlops.report.approved` (M13 §2.13.4): joins report-covered regression members' `source_failure_ids` to open failures → creates verification links via M15-6 service path (joining M13 reports, per mandate); applies `auto_verify` tenant policy. (4) **cluster recompute scheduler** — marks the current set `stale` when new matching failures exceed threshold (default 25 records or 20% growth) and notifies the Evaluation Lead; optional weekly auto-recompute per tenant policy. (5) **MV refresh + facet rollups.** (6) **archival** — SVC-15 per DA-11.

#### 2.15.5 Permission Design

| Permission key | PA | TA | OE | RD (default) | Persona templates granting it |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `failure.record:create` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (member projects) | **all engineering personas** (Robotics Data Engineer, Annotation QA Lead, Simulation Engineer, Model Engineer, Evaluation Lead); service identities: M13 (conversion in user context), M14 saga, M19 routing |
| `failure.record:read` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (member projects) | all engineering personas + Program Manager (RPT-09); covers cluster reads (M15-5 GETs) |
| `failure.record:update` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (owner or triage lead) | Evaluation Lead, record owner; verification links (M15-6) ride this key |
| `failure.record:triage` | ✓ | ✓ | — | grant-only | **Evaluation Lead** (`failure:triage` template §0.2.4 — the template key maps to this atomic unit) |
| `failure.record:convert` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Evaluation Lead, Model Engineer, Simulation Engineer — **plus transitive target-module create permission in user context** (M09-6/M18-8/M05-10/M16-5; deny in target ⇒ conversion denied, BR-13) |
| `failure.cluster:compute` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | Evaluation Lead |

**Endpoint → permission map (per §1.4.2):** M15-1 POST `failure.record:create` / GET `failure.record:read` · M15-2 GET `failure.record:read` / PATCH `failure.record:update` · M15-3 `failure.record:triage` · M15-4 `failure.record:convert` (+ transitive target-module create) · M15-5 POST `failure.cluster:compute` / GETs `failure.record:read` · M15-6 `failure.record:update` (service path: M15 service identity) · M15-7 `failure.record:read`.

**Granularity:** project scope primary; instance routes re-check at the record's project. **OE: ✗ on all M15 business resources** (failure management is business domain — §0.5.3); OE sees only infrastructure-side workflow health in M17. Asset deny-wins: evidence refs to restricted episodes/snapshots mask payload URIs and labels for readers without the underlying M08/M06 read rights — the failure row itself stays visible (governance visibility) with `evidence.redacted=true`. Safety-relevant records (s1/`safety_relevant_behavior`) are tenant-visible to Governance Approvers via TA implicit read. Custom roles compose from these atomic keys (M03-4); `failure.record:triage` and `failure.record:convert` are the two work-routing keys access reviews watch (RPT-11 input: who can open remediation work).

**Credential scoping:** M15 mints **no** upload credentials — evidence stays in source-module storage; M15-2 evidence access issues 15-min signed GETs by delegating to the owning module's content-URL endpoint (M06-5/M05-12/M13 evidence reads), issuance audited, URLs never logged (§1.4.7). M15 service identity holds `failure.record:create/update` (intake + verification updater) + read-only `mlops.eval:read` (report checks) + M18-7 invoke; M18-7 calls carry only failure ids + feature refs, never free text (clustering features resolved by M18 from its own indexes).

#### 2.15.6 Logging Design

| Level | Trigger examples |
|---|---|
| DEBUG | dedup key computation, cluster scope resolution counts, MV/facet rollup timings (off in prod) |
| INFO | record created (source + dedup result); triaged (category/severity); converted (`target_kind`, work_request_ref); status mirrored (`in_remediation`); verification link added (outcome); cluster set computed (method, size, duration); record closed/wont_fix |
| WARN | s1/safety record untriaged > SLA (24 h default); verification `failed` outcome (≥2 ⇒ re-triage suggestion); cluster set stale; conversion target denied by transitive permission; severity below floor on safety category (justified) |
| ERROR | conversion remote-create succeeded but link tx failed after retries (saga alarm); intake DLQ entry; M18-7 clustering job failure; verification updater join failure |
| FATAL | outbox relay down; partition provisioning failure (month rollover) |

**JSON fields (per §1.4.6 +):** `failure_id`, `source`, `failure_category`, `severity`, `status`, `cluster_id`, `cluster_set_version`, `conversion_target` (target_kind), `work_request_ref`, `report_id` (verification), `outcome`, `action`, `result`, `error_code`. **Masking:** failure `description`, root-cause hypotheses, and triage notes are PII-bearing free text — logged **by id reference only**; triage/wont_fix reason text stored **in the audit event only**, logs carry `{reason_len}`; evidence logged as `{ref_count, byte_size, checksum}` summaries; signed URLs never logged (§1.4.7).

**Shipping:** stdout JSON → platform pipeline; `traceparent` spans source-module intake → M15 → M16/M09/M18 conversion calls → M13 verification events (one trace per loop traversal — the BP-05 funnel is traceable end to end).

**Audit events (M03-11):** `failure.record.created` `{source, source_key, deduplicated}`, `failure.record.triaged` `{category, severity, recommended_action, owner}`, `failure.record.converted` `{target_kind, work_request_ref, target_refs}`, `failure.record.status_changed` `{from, to, reason?}`, `failure.verification.linked` `{report_id, candidate_id, outcome, verdict?, override_reason?}`, `failure.clusters.computed` `{cluster_set_version, method, scope_summary, size}`, `failure.record.closed` — jointly the AC-11/BR-10 closed-loop audit trail (detection → remediation → verification, BR-14).

### 2.16 M16 — Workflow Orchestration & Work Request Management

#### 2.16.1 Module Overview

**Module ID & name:** M16 — Workflow Orchestration & Work Request Management.

**Scope boundary.**
- *Owns:* versioned workflow templates and their immutable published versions (FR-D1.1, FR-D1.7); the task-DAG model with dependencies and expected outputs (FR-D1.2); workflow runs and their control verbs start/pause/resume/cancel/rerun/reprioritize (FR-D1.3, FR-D3.5 verb side); task executions, attempts, and retry history (FR-D1.6); registration fan-out of completed outputs to owning modules (FR-D1.4, M16-9); work requests and triage (FR-D2.1/D2.2); the **unified approval inbox** and approval subjects/decisions (FR-D2.3, FR-D2.6, BR-12); queue visibility (FR-D2.4); service classes (FR-D2.5).
- *Excludes:* quota math, admission policy, priority/escalation policy ownership, utilization and consumption accounting (M17, boundary §1.6.3 — single touchpoints M17-9 admission and M17-10 metering); compute/storage resource inventory and bindings (M02); all domain logic of the assets a workflow produces — M16 never writes a recording, episode, snapshot, model, or report itself; owning modules register their own assets from M16-9 fan-out events (single-writer rule §1.2); alert rules and incidents (M17); notification delivery (M20).

**User roles & personas.** Primary: **Platform Operator** (OE persona — template governance, run control, work-request triage, queue management per §0.2.4) and **Ops Engineer**. All other roles consume: RD personas launch runs and file work requests within their projects; Governance Approvers decide approval subjects in M16-6; TA oversees tenant-scoped templates and approvals; Program Managers read queues and run status.

**Upstream/downstream.** Upstream: M01/M03 (session, authz, audit per §1.4.2/§1.4.6); M02 (project/tenant containers, resource bindings constraining where runs execute); M17-9 (admission verdicts) and M17 priority policy; M08-9 (usage checks on dataset-snapshot parameters, BR-05); M11/M14/M17 (approval-subject intake events for the unified inbox); SVC-10 Workflow Scheduler & Task Dispatcher. Downstream: M04–M14, M18 (output registration via M16-9 fan-out, FR-D1.4); M17 (run state events feeding the job board projection and reservation release; consumption context); M20 (workflow failure / approval-blocker events); M03-11 (audit); M15→M16 (remediation work items arrive as work requests via M15-4, BP-05).

**FRD coverage:** FR-D1.1–D1.7, FR-D2.1–D2.6, BR-12 (with M17), BR-05 enforcement point at launch, BP-06 steps 1–5, AC-13 (queue part via M16-7), §11 objects Workflow Run and Work Request. Research basis per §0.7.6: Airflow 3 DAG-versioning (#1) and backfill-as-first-class-run (#2), Argo retryStrategy + exit handlers (#3), Temporal signals for control verbs (#4), Stripe idempotency (#8), transactional outbox (#9), full-jitter retry/DLQ (#10).

#### 2.16.2 Functional & UI/UX Design

**Primary functions.**
1. Maintain a tenant-scoped registry of workflow templates in exactly eleven categories: `ingestion, validation, alignment, annotation, dataset_publishing, simulation, synthetic_generation, training, evaluation, packaging, reporting` (FR-D1.1). Each template version is an immutable DAG definition + parameter schema; runs pin `(template_id, version)` at launch and never pick up "latest" mid-run (FR-D1.7, Airflow 3 DAG-versioning pattern per §0.7.6).
2. Represent each template as a DAG of typed tasks with `depends_on` edges, expected outputs (asset type + target module), per-task retry strategy and resource profile (FR-D1.2; Argo retryStrategy pattern per §0.7.6).
3. Launch runs with parameterized business inputs — `campaign`, `dataset_snapshot`, `scenario_collection`, `model_candidate`, `evaluation_suite`, `priority`, plus template-declared typed params (FR-D1.5) — validated against the pinned version's param schema.
4. Permission-gated control verbs on runs: pause, resume, cancel, rerun, reprioritize (FR-D1.3; FR-D3.5 — eligibility policy owned by M17 and consulted via M17-9 `action=reprioritize`). Rerun always creates a **new** run linked via `rerun_of` under the originally pinned template version (per §0.7.6 #2).
5. Retain for every run: status, owner, timeline, inputs, outputs, failure reason, and full retry history per task attempt (FR-D1.6).
6. On task/run completion, register declared outputs into owning modules via M16-9 fan-out so completed jobs create governed platform records, never loose files (FR-D1.4, BP-06 step 5).
7. Manage work requests of types `data_processing, annotation, simulation, training, evaluation, packaging` (FR-D2.1) carrying requester, project, objective, inputs, priority, expected output, approval status (FR-D2.2); operator triage routes them to templates and service classes.
8. Enforce approval requirements before execution for **restricted data use, high-cost compute, release-critical evaluation, and release packaging** (FR-D2.3, BR-12) through Approval Subjects; provide the unified approval inbox (M16-6) which also hosts subjects created by M11 (training-plan approval), M14 (release review where routed), and M17 (quota escalation, FR-D3.4). Decisions and reviewer comments are retained immutably (FR-D2.6).
9. Queue visibility by project, workflow type, priority/service class, and status (FR-D2.4) from a materialized queue view.
10. Service classes — exactly `exploratory | standard | release_critical | incident_response` (FR-D2.5) — stored on runs and work requests; scheduling weight and class eligibility are M17 policy.

**Seeded reference templates (one per FR-D1.1 category; tenants clone and version them).**

| Category | Reference template (typical task chain) | Registered outputs → module | Default gates |
|---|---|---|---|
| ingestion | `ingest-batch-standard`: stage_manifest → transfer_parts → checksum_verify → commit_recordings | raw_recording → M05 | — |
| validation | `intake-validate-standard`: load_rules → run_checks → write_report → set_quality_status | quality_report → M05 | — |
| alignment | `align-episodes-standard`: load_policy → align_streams → derive_episodes → lineage_write | episode → M06 | — |
| annotation | `preannotate-vlm-caption`: select_cohort → run_generator → attach_preannotations | preannotation set → M07 | — |
| dataset_publishing | `snapshot-publish-standard`: resolve_members → composition_check → freeze_manifest → publish | dataset_snapshot → M08 | restricted_data_use (if members restricted) |
| simulation | `simrun-batch-standard`: resolve_scenarios → provision_sim → execute → classify_outputs | simulation_run → M10 | — |
| synthetic_generation | `synthgen-standard`: validate_request → generate → package_batch → mark_synthetic | synthetic_batch → M10 (BR-03) | restricted_data_use (source data) |
| training | `training-sft-standard`: prepare_data → train (gang-scheduled multi-GPU) → checkpoint_export | training_run → M11 | high_cost_compute |
| evaluation | `eval-suite-standard`: resolve_suite → run_offline_eval / dispatch_sim_eval → compile_report_draft | evaluation_run/report draft → M13 | release_critical_evaluation (when class=release_critical) |
| packaging | `release-package-standard`: collect_evidence → build_artifact_descriptors → attach_packaging_metadata | packaging metadata → M14 | release_packaging (always) |
| reporting | `report-refresh-standard`: compute_aggregates → publish_report_payload | report payload → owning module | — |

**BP-06 binding (FRD §8.6 step → M16/M17 touchpoint).** (1) submit/launch → M16-5/M16-2; (2) project–access–quota–priority–cost-center–restriction checks → M16-2 steps 1–6 (M03 authz, M08-9, M17-9); (3) scheduled & executed as dependent tasks → SVC-10 + M16-8; (4) progress/failures/retries/consumption monitoring → M16-3/M16-7 + M17-5 (consumption M17-4); (5) outputs registered as governed assets → M16-9 fan-out; (6) operational history retained → §2.16.4 retention + M03 audit.

**Approval gate matrix (evaluated at M16-2 launch and M16-5 triage; BR-12).**

| Gate trigger | Subject type | Default decider permission |
|---|---|---|
| Any dataset param with restriction flags → M08-9 verdict `needs_approval` | `restricted_data_use` | `data.snapshot:approve` |
| Estimated cost ≥ the M17 quota-policy `high_cost_threshold_gpu_hours` (template resource profile × est. duration) | `high_cost_compute` | `ops.quota:update` |
| `service_class=release_critical` ∧ category `evaluation` | `release_critical_evaluation` | `mlops.report:approve` |
| Category `packaging` | `release_packaging` | `release.rc:approve` |
| M17-9 verdict `escalate` (FR-D3.4) | `quota_escalation` | `ops.quota:update` |
| Intake events from M11/M14 outboxes | `training_plan`, `release_candidate_review` | per subject payload |

**State machines (exact).**
- WorkflowTemplate version: `draft → published → deprecated`.
- WorkflowRun: `pending → admitted → running → paused → running → succeeded | failed | cancelled` (cancel allowed from pending/admitted/running/paused; rerun creates a NEW run linked via `rerun_of`).
- TaskExecution: `pending → running → succeeded | failed | retrying | skipped` (`retrying → running` on next attempt; `skipped` for tasks never dispatched due to upstream failure or cancel).
- WorkRequest: `draft → submitted → triaged → approved | rejected → scheduled → completed | cancelled` (cancel allowed from any non-terminal state).
- ApprovalSubject: `open → decided(approved | rejected)`.

##### UI page 1 — Template Registry, route `/orchestration/templates`

Table of templates (name, category, latest published version, status, run count 30 d, owner) with category/status filters and a version-history drawer per template (per-version state badges, change note, "view DAG" read-only graph). Actions: New template, New version, Publish draft, Deprecate.

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| name | text | `^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{2,63}$` | empty | training-sft-standard | Required; unique per tenant (server check → `E_WFT_NAME_TAKEN`) |
| category | single-select | 11-value enum (FR-D1.1) | — | Select category | Required |
| description | textarea | ≤2,000 chars | empty | What this workflow does | Optional |
| definition | code editor (YAML/JSON) | task-graph document | category starter skeleton | — | Required; client lint + server DAG validation (acyclic, deps resolve, ≥1 task) |
| param_schema | code editor (JSON Schema subset) | object schema | starter with business-param refs | — | Required; must type business params from the FR-D1.5 set it uses |
| publish | toggle | bool | false (save as draft) | — | Publishing runs full validation |

- **Validation logic:** client lints YAML/JSON and renders a live DAG preview; server is authoritative (`E_WFT_GRAPH_INVALID` details list cycle paths / unknown `depends_on` / untyped outputs). Drafts are re-saveable; published versions are immutable — edit always means "new version".
- **API bindings:** list `GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/workflow-templates`; create `POST` same; version ops `POST /api/v1/workflow-templates/{template_id}:new-version`, `POST ...:deprecate`; detail `GET /api/v1/workflow-templates/{template_id}`.
- **Output rendering:** static list, refreshed on action; no polling. Version drawer shows per-version `published_at`, publisher, run count, and immutability badge.
- **Exception handling:** `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → actions hidden for non-grantees, toast on direct attempt; `E_WFT_NAME_TAKEN` → inline field error; `E_STATE_INVALID` (e.g., deprecate an already-deprecated version) → toast with allowed transitions; `E_WFT_GRAPH_INVALID` → error panel anchored to offending task keys.

##### UI page 2 — Run Launcher, route `/orchestration/templates/{template_id}/launch`

Dynamic form generated from the pinned published version's `param_schema`; business params render as typed asset pickers (campaign, dataset snapshot, scenario collection, model candidate, evaluation suite) scoped to the active project.

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| template_version | single-select | published versions only | latest published | — | Required; draft/deprecated not listed |
| project_id | single-select | member projects | active project | Select project | Required |
| params.* | generated controls | per param schema | schema defaults | per schema | Required/optional per schema; asset pickers validate existence + project scope |
| service_class | single-select | `exploratory\|standard\|release_critical\|incident_response` | standard | — | Required; classes the user may not use are disabled with reason (M17 policy preview) |
| work_request_id | reference picker | `wrq-` ULID | empty (or carried from WR board) | Link a work request | Optional; must be `approved` |
| run_label | text | ≤120 chars | empty | Optional label | Optional |

- **Validation logic:** on submit the form runs a **preflight panel**: param-schema validation, M08-9 usage-check preview for dataset params (restriction verdicts shown per snapshot), and a quota preview (M17-9 dry-run flag) showing remaining quota and predicted verdict. Launch button disabled while preflight shows a hard deny.
- **API binding:** `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/workflow-runs` (M16-2) with `Idempotency-Key`.
- **Output rendering:** 202 → redirect to the DAG Run Monitor of the new `wfr-` id; if held for approval, banner "Run is pending approval — view subject" linking the `apr-` record.
- **Exception handling:** `E_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` (429) → blocking dialog showing quota snapshot from `details` with **escalation CTA**: "Request escalation" (re-submits with `request_escalation: true`, creating a quota_escalation approval subject when policy allows) or "File work request"; `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` → per-field error on the offending snapshot picker with policy reason; `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → toast "You cannot launch workflows in this project."; `E_WFR_PARAM_INVALID` → field-level errors from `details[]`; `E_WFT_VERSION_NOT_PUBLISHED` → version selector error.

##### UI page 3 — DAG Run Monitor, route `/orchestration/runs/{run_id}`

Three synchronized panes: (a) **DAG graph** — nodes colored by task status, badge for attempt count, critical path highlighted; (b) **task table** — task_key, status, attempt n/limit, started/ended, duration, failure stage+category, recommended next action (FR-D4.2 fields), logs link; (c) **retry history drawer** per task — every attempt with timestamps, exit code/category, backoff applied (FR-D1.6). Header: run status chip, owner, service class, pinned template version (FR-D1.7 audit cue), params summary (ids only), timeline bar, outputs tab (registered assets with deep links), linked work request and approval subjects. Control buttons: Pause / Resume / Cancel / Rerun / Reprioritize — each rendered only with the corresponding permission.

- **API bindings:** `GET /api/v1/workflow-runs/{run_id}` + `GET .../tasks` + `GET .../outputs` (M16-3); verbs `POST /api/v1/workflow-runs/{run_id}:pause|:resume|:cancel|:rerun|:reprioritize` (M16-4).
- **Output rendering & polling:** while the run is non-terminal the page polls M16-3 run+tasks every **5 s**; polling stops at terminal status; outputs tab refreshes once on terminal transition. Empty states: "No outputs registered yet"; cancelled runs show cancel actor + reason.
- **Exception handling:** `E_STATE_INVALID` on an illegal verb (e.g., pause a succeeded run) → toast with `details.allowed_transitions`, buttons re-rendered from fresh state; `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → disabled button tooltip "Requires orch.run:control"; reprioritize denial from M17 policy → dialog "Service class incident_response requires Platform Operator" (`E_QTA_CLASS_NOT_PERMITTED` relayed); network loss → stale banner with last-fetch time, retry backoff.

##### UI page 4 — Work Request Board, route `/orchestration/work-requests`

Kanban by status (draft → submitted → triaged → approved → scheduled → completed; rejected/cancelled collapsed lane) with filters project/type/priority/requester. Card: objective, type, priority, service class (after triage), approval badges, linked run chip. "New work request" form:

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| type | single-select | `data_processing\|annotation\|simulation\|training\|evaluation\|packaging` | — | Select type | Required (FR-D2.1) |
| objective | textarea | ≤4,000 chars | empty | What outcome is needed and why | Required, ≥20 chars (FR-D2.2) |
| inputs | asset multi-picker | typed refs (`dss-`, `scc-`, `mdl-`, `evs-`, `cmp-`, `coh-`) | empty | Add input assets | ≥1 ref; existence + project scope checked |
| priority | single-select | `low\|normal\|high\|urgent` | normal | — | Required |
| expected_output | structured row(s) | `{asset_type, description}` | empty | e.g. dataset_snapshot | Required ≥1 (FR-D2.2) |
| due_date | date | RFC 3339 date | empty | — | Optional; ≥ today |

Triage drawer (operator): assign service class, bind template+version+param prefill, set/confirm required approvals (gate matrix preview), disposition approve/reject with note.
- **API bindings:** create/list `POST/GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/work-requests`; detail/edit `GET/PATCH /api/v1/work-requests/{request_id}`; `POST ...:submit`; `POST ...:triage` (M16-5).
- **Output rendering:** board polls list every **30 s**; card transitions animate on status change; approval badges link to M16-6 subjects.
- **Exception handling:** `E_STATE_INVALID` (edit after submit, triage before submit) → toast + card refresh; `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` on triage → drawer hidden; `E_VALIDATION` → inline field errors.

##### UI page 5 — Approval Inbox, route `/approvals`

Unified queue, default filter `assigned_to_me=true`. Row: subject type chip (six types per gate matrix + intake types), requested-by, project, summary line, age, SLA hint. Detail pane: full subject payload (e.g., quota snapshot for `quota_escalation`, restriction policy excerpt for `restricted_data_use`), prior decisions on related subjects, decision form:

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| decision | radio | `approved\|rejected` | — | — | Required |
| comment | textarea | ≤2,000 chars | empty | Rationale (required when rejecting) | Required if rejected; retained verbatim (FR-D2.6) |
| conditions | text rows | ≤500 chars each | empty | Optional conditions | Optional, stored with decision |

- **API bindings:** `GET /api/v1/approvals?assigned_to_me=true`, `GET /api/v1/approvals/{approval_id}`, `POST /api/v1/approvals/{approval_id}:decide` (M16-6).
- **Output rendering:** inbox polls every **60 s**; decided subjects move to a History tab showing decision, decider, comment, timestamp (immutable, FR-D2.6).
- **Exception handling:** `E_STATE_INVALID` (already decided) → banner "Decided by {user} at {ts}" with refresh; `E_WRQ_SELF_APPROVAL` → blocking message "Requesters cannot decide their own subjects."; `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → row visible only if user is in the decider set, direct URL access shows 403 page.

##### UI page 6 — Queue View, route `/orchestration/queues`

Per-queue table (queue = tenant × workload class × service class): depth, oldest-wait, p50 running duration, throughput/h; expandable row lists queued runs (position, project, owner, age). Filters: `project`, `type` (template category), `priority` (service class), `status` (FR-D2.4).
- **API binding:** `GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/queues?project&type&priority&status` (M16-7). **Polling:** every **30 s** (matches the materialized-view refresh; staleness ≤60 s noted in the footer). Empty state: "No queued work matches the filter." Errors: `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → page requires `orch.run:read` at tenant scope.

#### 2.16.3 Interface Design

Module error codes (HTTP per §1.4.3): `E_WFT_NAME_TAKEN` (409), `E_WFT_GRAPH_INVALID` (422), `E_WFT_VERSION_NOT_PUBLISHED` (409), `E_WFT_DEPRECATED` (409), `E_WFR_PARAM_INVALID` (422), `E_WFR_APPROVAL_PENDING` (409, verb requires decided approvals), `E_WRQ_NOT_LINKABLE` (409, work request not approved), `E_WRQ_SELF_APPROVAL` (403), plus shared codes incl. `E_QUOTA_EXCEEDED`, `E_RESTRICTED_DATA`, `E_STATE_INVALID`.

##### M16-1 Workflow templates (collection, detail, version ops)
- `POST /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/workflow-templates` — create template with version 1 (draft unless `publish=true`).
- Request:
```json
{"name": "training-sft-standard", "category": "training", "description": "string?",
 "definition": {"tasks": [{"task_key": "prepare_data", "task_type": "exec.data_prep", "depends_on": [],
   "expected_outputs": [{"output_key": "manifest", "asset_type": "internal", "target_module": null}],
   "retry_strategy": {"limit": 3, "retry_policy": "on_transient", "backoff": {"base_s": 30, "cap_s": 600, "jitter": "full"}},
   "timeout_s": 7200, "resource_profile": {"workload_class": "data_processing", "gpu_class": null, "gpu_count": 0, "cpu_cores": 8, "mem_gb": 32}},
  {"task_key": "train", "task_type": "exec.training", "depends_on": ["prepare_data"],
   "expected_outputs": [{"output_key": "training_run", "asset_type": "training_run", "target_module": "M11"}], "...": "..."}],
  "on_exit": {"task_key": "report_status", "task_type": "exec.exit_report"}},
 "param_schema": {"type": "object", "required": ["dataset_snapshot"], "properties": {"dataset_snapshot": {"$ref": "#/defs/asset_dss"}, "model_candidate": {"$ref": "#/defs/asset_mdl"}, "priority": {"enum": ["low","normal","high","urgent"]}}},
 "approval_overrides": {"high_cost_threshold_gpu_hours": null}, "publish": false}
```
- **Processing:** 1. authz `orch.template:create` (tenant). 2. Name uniqueness per tenant → `E_WFT_NAME_TAKEN`. 3. Validate definition: DAG acyclic, all `depends_on` resolve, every `task_type` registered in the executor registry, `expected_outputs.asset_type` ∈ asset-type catalog with valid `target_module`, retry strategy bounds (`limit ≤ 10`), param schema is a valid schema covering every `{{param}}` reference in the definition → `E_WFT_GRAPH_INVALID` / `E_VALIDATION` with task-anchored `details[]`. 4. Tx: insert `workflow_templates` + `workflow_template_versions(version=1, status=draft|published)`, outbox `orch.template.created`. 5. 201.
- `GET` same path — filters `category`, `status`, `q`; per §1.4.1 list envelope. `GET /api/v1/workflow-templates/{template_id}` — template + version list + per-version run counts.
- `POST /api/v1/workflow-templates/{template_id}:new-version` — body `{definition?, param_schema?, change_note, publish: bool, replace_draft: bool}`. Processing: at most one `draft` version exists per template; `replace_draft=true` overwrites it (same version number, still draft); `publish=true` validates then transitions `draft→published` (immutable from that instant; per §0.7.6 #1 the published row is content-addressed via `definition_checksum`); calling with `publish=true` and no body publishes the existing draft. New version number = max+1, assigned at draft creation.
- `POST /api/v1/workflow-templates/{template_id}:deprecate` — body `{version: int|null, reason}`; deprecates one published version (`published→deprecated`) or, with `version=null`, the template (blocks new launches of every version; running runs are unaffected — they hold their pinned version to completion, FR-D1.7).
- **Errors:** above + `E_STATE_INVALID` (publish a published version, deprecate a draft). **Idempotency:** `Idempotency-Key` on POSTs; version creation also guarded by unique `(template_id, version)`. **Concurrency:** template row carries `version`/ETag; `:new-version` requires `If-Match` on the template. **Permission keys:** `orch.template:create` / `orch.template:read` / `orch.template:publish` (publish+new-version) / `orch.template:deprecate`.

##### M16-2 Launch / list workflow runs
- `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/workflow-runs`
- Request:
```json
{"template_id": "wft-01J...", "template_version": 4, "params": {"dataset_snapshot": "dss-01J...", "model_candidate": "mdl-01J...", "priority": "high"},
 "service_class": "standard", "work_request_id": "wrq-01J...|null", "run_label": "string?", "request_escalation": false, "dry_run": false}
```
- **Processing (tx boundaries explicit):**
  1. Authz `orch.run:execute` at project scope (M03-10); project active, tenant not suspended.
  2. **Template version resolve:** load `(template_id, template_version)`; must be `published` and template not deprecated → `E_WFT_VERSION_NOT_PUBLISHED` / `E_WFT_DEPRECATED`. Omitted `template_version` resolves to latest published and is pinned in the response (never floats afterwards).
  3. **Param validation** against the pinned version's `param_schema` (types, required, enum, asset-ref shape) → `E_WFR_PARAM_INVALID` with per-param `details[]`. Asset refs resolved for existence + project/tenant scope.
  4. **M08-9 usage checks** for every dataset-snapshot/cohort param: `POST /internal/v1/data-governance/usage-checks` with the M08-9 canonical shape `{caller:"m16", acting_user_id, context:{workflow_kind: category, project_id}, assets:[{ref, requested_use}]}` — `requested_use` mapped from the template category (training→`training_input`, evaluation→`evaluation_input`, simulation→`simulation_source`, synthetic generation→`generation_source`). Verdict `deny` → 403 `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` (deny-wins, BR-05); verdict `needs_approval` → mark `restricted_data_use` gate.
  5. Evaluate remaining approval gates (matrix §2.16.2): high-cost (estimate = Σ task resource_profile × historical p90 duration for this template version), release-critical evaluation, packaging.
  6. **M17-9 admission:** `POST /internal/v1/admission/check` with `{action:"launch", tenant_id, project_id, requester_id, service_class, workload_class, resource_request, run_ref:null, request_escalation}`. `deny` → 429 `E_QUOTA_EXCEEDED`, body `details` = quota snapshot + `escalatable` flag (UI escalation CTA); `escalate` → add `quota_escalation` gate; `allow` → capture `reservation_id`. If `dry_run=true`, return the preflight verdicts now (200, nothing created).
  7. **Tx:** insert `workflow_runs` (`wfr-{ulid}`, status `pending`, pinned template version, params JSONB, service_class, owner=caller, `work_request_id?`, `reservation_id?`, `hold_reasons[]` = open gates); materialize `task_executions` rows (status `pending`) from the DAG; insert ApprovalSubject rows for each open gate (`apr-{ulid}`, status `open`); outbox events `orch.run.created` (+ `orch.approval.requested` per subject, routed to M20 for decider notification); if `work_request_id` set, validate it is `approved` (`E_WRQ_NOT_LINKABLE`) and transition it `approved→scheduled` in the same tx. Commit.
  8. Response **202**:
```json
{"run": {"run_id": "wfr-01J...", "status": "pending", "template": {"template_id": "wft-01J...", "version": 4},
         "service_class": "standard", "owner_id": "usr-...", "work_request_id": "wrq-...|null", "reservation_id": "rsv-...|null"},
 "hold": {"pending_approvals": [{"approval_id": "apr-01J...", "subject_type": "quota_escalation"}]}|null,
 "preflight": {"usage_checks": [{"snapshot_id": "dss-...", "verdict": "allow"}], "quota_warnings": ["project gpu_h100 at 83%"]}}
```
  With zero gates, SVC-10 picks the run up next tick (`pending→admitted` recorded with the admission verdict; reservation already held).
- `GET` same path — filters `status`, `service_class`, `template_id`, `owner_id`, `created_after/before`; list envelope.
- **Errors:** `E_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` (429), `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` (403), `E_WFR_PARAM_INVALID` (422), `E_WFT_VERSION_NOT_PUBLISHED`/`E_WFT_DEPRECATED` (409), `E_WRQ_NOT_LINKABLE` (409). **Idempotency:** `Idempotency-Key` required; replay returns original 202 (§1.4.4). **Permission key:** `orch.run:execute`.

##### M16-3 Run detail / tasks / outputs
- `GET /api/v1/workflow-runs/{run_id}` →
```json
{"run_id": "wfr-01J...", "project_id": "prj-...", "template": {"template_id": "wft-...", "name": "training-sft-standard", "version": 4, "definition_checksum": "sha256:..."},
 "status": "running", "service_class": "standard", "owner_id": "usr-...", "work_request_id": "wrq-...|null", "rerun_of": "wfr-...|null",
 "params": {"dataset_snapshot": "dss-...", "priority": "high"}, "hold_reasons": [], "approval_refs": ["apr-..."],
 "timeline": {"created_at": "ts", "admitted_at": "ts", "started_at": "ts", "paused_ms_total": 0, "ended_at": null},
 "failure": null, "progress": {"tasks_total": 6, "succeeded": 3, "running": 1, "failed": 0, "skipped": 0}, "version": 7}
```
- `GET .../tasks` → items `{task_key, task_type, status, depends_on[], attempt, retry_limit, attempts: [{attempt, started_at, ended_at, outcome, failure_stage?, failure_category?, affected_inputs?[], recommended_next_action?, backoff_applied_s?}], expected_outputs[], log_ref}` (FR-D1.6, FR-D4.2 fields). `GET .../outputs` → items `{output_key, asset_type, target_module, registration_status: pending|registered|rejected, asset_id?, registered_at?}`.
- **Processing:** read models; authz `orch.run:read` at the run's project (OE reads platform-wide metadata). **Permission key:** `orch.run:read`.

##### M16-4 Run control verbs
- `POST /api/v1/workflow-runs/{run_id}:pause | :resume | :cancel | :rerun | :reprioritize` — bodies: pause/resume `{reason?}`; cancel `{reason}` (required); rerun `{params_override?: {}, service_class?: "..."}`; reprioritize `{service_class: "incident_response", reason}`.
- **Processing:**
  1. Authz: `orch.run:control` (pause/resume/cancel; run owner with `orch.run:execute` may also control **own** runs); rerun: `orch.run:execute` at project or `orch.run:control`; reprioritize: `orch.run:reprioritize`.
  2. Validate transition against the run state machine → `E_STATE_INVALID` with `details.allowed_transitions` (e.g., pause is legal only from `running`; cancel from any non-terminal; resume only from `paused`; rerun only from terminal states; runs held on approvals reject pause/resume with `E_WFR_APPROVAL_PENDING`).
  3. *pause:* tx set status `paused` + outbox `orch.run.paused`; SVC-10 stops dispatching new tasks; running attempts finish naturally (Temporal-signal semantics per §0.7.6 #4). *resume:* `paused→running`, dispatch resumes.
  4. *cancel:* tx set `cancelled` (+actor, reason), mark all `pending` tasks `skipped`, outbox `orch.run.cancelled` → SVC-10 signals executors SIGTERM-drain (per §0.7.6 #12); running attempts report final task-events; reservation release event to M17. Audit `orch.run.cancelled`.
