  5. *rerun:* creates a **new run** via the full M16-2 pipeline (steps 3–8: param re-validation, fresh M08-9 usage check, fresh M17-9 admission — policies may have changed) under the **same pinned template version**, `rerun_of = {run_id}`; response 202 with the new `wfr-` id. The original run record is never mutated.
  6. *reprioritize:* call M17-9 with `action:"reprioritize", target_service_class` — M17 owns who may use which class (FR-D3.5); deny → 403 relaying `E_QTA_CLASS_NOT_PERMITTED`; allow → tx update `service_class` + queue weight, outbox `orch.run.reprioritized`, audit **`ops.priority.adjusted`** `{run_id, from, to, reason}`.
- **Errors:** `E_STATE_INVALID`, `E_WFR_APPROVAL_PENDING`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_QTA_CLASS_NOT_PERMITTED` (403, relayed). **Idempotency:** verbs idempotent on state (pause of paused run → `E_STATE_INVALID`; repeated cancel with same Idempotency-Key replays). **Concurrency:** `If-Match` on run ETag required for all verbs (428 otherwise). **Permission keys:** as step 1.

##### M16-5 Work requests
- `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/work-requests` — body `{type, objective, inputs: [{"ref": "dss-...", "role": "source"}], priority, expected_outputs: [{"asset_type": "dataset_snapshot", "description": "..."}], due_date?, notes?}` → 201 `wrq-{ulid}` status `draft`. Requester = caller (FR-D2.2).
- `GET` same (filters `type`, `status`, `priority`, `requester_id`); `GET/PATCH /api/v1/work-requests/{request_id}` — PATCH edits draft fields (draft only) or `{status:"cancelled", reason}` from any non-terminal state (requester or `orch.work_request:triage` holder); other status values via verbs only → `E_STATE_INVALID`.
- `POST ...:submit` — `draft→submitted`; validation completeness check (objective, ≥1 input, ≥1 expected output); outbox `orch.work_request.submitted` (→ M20 notifies triagers).
- `POST ...:triage` — body `{disposition: "approved"|"rejected"|"needs_approval", service_class, template_binding: {template_id, version, param_prefill}?, required_approvals: ["restricted_data_use", ...], note}`. Processing: 1. authz `orch.work_request:triage`. 2. `submitted→triaged` with triage fields. 3. `needs_approval` → create ApprovalSubjects (gate matrix), WR stays `triaged`; auto-transition `triaged→approved` when all subjects approve, `→rejected` on any rejection (consumer of `orch.approval.decided`). 4. `approved`/`rejected` directly when no subjects required. Tx + outbox per step.
- Lifecycle continuation: `approved→scheduled` set by M16-2 step 7 when a run links the WR; `scheduled→completed` set by the run-success handler when the linked run succeeds (or via triager PATCH `{status:"completed", completion_note}` for multi-run requests after at least one linked run succeeded).
- **Errors:** `E_STATE_INVALID`, `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. **Idempotency/concurrency:** Idempotency-Key on POSTs; `If-Match` on PATCH/verbs. **Permission keys:** `orch.work_request:create/read/update` (requester-scope), `orch.work_request:triage`.

##### M16-6 Unified approval inbox
- `GET /api/v1/approvals?assigned_to_me=true&subject_type&status&project` — items `{approval_id, subject_type, status, scope: {tenant_id, project_id}, requested_by, requested_at, subject_ref: {type, id}, summary, payload_excerpt, decider_rule: {permission_key, scope}}`. Assigned-to-me = caller satisfies `decider_rule` at scope (resolved via M03-10 batch-check) or is an explicitly named decider.
- `GET /api/v1/approvals/{approval_id}` — full payload (quota snapshot / restriction policy / cost estimate / plan summary per type) + decision history of related subjects.
- `POST /api/v1/approvals/{approval_id}:decide` — body `{decision: "approved"|"rejected", comment, conditions?: []}`.
- **Processing:** 1. authz `orch.approval:decide` **and** the subject's `decider_rule.permission_key` at subject scope (defense in depth, mirrors M11 §2.11.5). 2. Self-approval guard: decider ≠ requested_by → `E_WRQ_SELF_APPROVAL` (403). 3. Tx: `open→decided`, insert immutable `approval_decisions` row (decision, comment, conditions, decider, decided_at — FR-D2.6), outbox `orch.approval.decided`. 4. Consumers: M16 run-hold resolver (all gates approved → remove hold, request admission if `quota_escalation` approved with override token → run proceeds `pending→admitted`; any rejection → run `cancelled` with reason `approval_rejected`); WR auto-transition (M16-5); M11/M14/M17 react to their subject types via the same event; M20 notifies the requester. 5. Audit `orch.approval.decided` `{subject_type, decision, subject_ref}`.
- **Subject intake:** subjects are created (a) locally by M16-2/M16-5; (b) from `*.approval.requested` outbox events of M11 (training plans), M14 (release reviews where tenant policy routes them here), M17 (quota escalation, FR-D3.4) — intake consumer is idempotent by `event_id`.
- **Errors:** `E_STATE_INVALID` (already decided), `E_WRQ_SELF_APPROVAL`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_VALIDATION` (missing comment on reject). **Idempotency:** decide accepts Idempotency-Key; second decide → `E_STATE_INVALID`. **Permission keys:** `orch.approval:read` (own assignments implicit), `orch.approval:decide`.

##### M16-7 Queue visibility
- `GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/queues?project&type&priority&status` → items `{queue_key: {workload_class, service_class}, depth, oldest_wait_s, p50_run_duration_s, throughput_per_h, entries: [{run_id, position, project_id, template_name, owner_id, queued_at, status}]}` from the materialized queue view (refresh §2.16.4). Filters map: `type`=template category, `priority`=service class, `status`∈ run states. Authz `orch.run:read` at tenant scope (FR-D2.4; AC-13). Read-only; staleness ≤60 s documented in response header `X-View-Refreshed-At`.

##### M16-8 (i) Task-event ingestion
- `POST /internal/v1/workflow-runs/{run_id}/task-events` (mTLS; SVC-10 + registered executor service identities only).
- Request:
```json
{"events": [{"task_id": "prepare_data", "attempt": 2, "event_seq": 7, "event_type": "failure", "occurred_at": "ts",
  "payload": {"failure_stage": "data_load", "failure_category": "data_quality", "affected_inputs": ["dss-01J..."],
              "recommended_next_action": "Re-run intake validation on dss-01J...", "exit_code": 3, "message_ref": "log://..."}},
 {"task_id": "prepare_data", "attempt": 3, "event_seq": 8, "event_type": "start", "occurred_at": "ts", "payload": {}}]}
```
  `event_type ∈ start|heartbeat|progress|success|failure|retry_scheduled|output_declared`.
- **Processing:** 1. Service identity ↔ run tenant check. 2. **Idempotent ingestion keyed by `(run_id, task_id, attempt, event_seq)`** — duplicates ACKed and dropped; out-of-order events accepted, state reducer applies only forward transitions. 3. Per event, tx: append `task_events` row; reduce task state (`failure` → apply retry strategy: attempts < limit ∧ category retryable per `retry_policy` → status `retrying` + schedule next attempt with full-jitter backoff (per §0.7.6 #3/#10), else `failed`); update run aggregate (any unrecoverable task failure → run `failed` with `failure` = first-cause `{task_key, stage, category}`, downstream pending tasks `skipped`; all terminal-success → run `succeeded`); on terminal run state: outbox `orch.run.succeeded|failed`, dispatch `on_exit` task if defined (exit-handler pattern per §0.7.6 #3), emit reservation-release + final metering trigger to M17, WR completion hook. 4. ERROR log on `failure` events with stage+category (FR-D4.2). 5. 200 `{accepted: n, duplicates: m}`.
- **Errors:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_NOT_FOUND`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (identity/tenant mismatch). **Permission key:** service-only `orch.task_event:write`.

##### M16-9 (i) Output registration fan-out
- `POST /internal/v1/workflow-outputs:register` (mTLS; executors + SVC-10 exit handler).
- Request: `{"run_id": "wfr-...", "task_id": "train", "outputs": [{"output_key": "training_run", "asset_type": "training_run", "target_module": "M11", "registration_payload": {"run_ref": "...", "artifact_uris": ["..."], "checksum": "sha256:..."}}]}`
- **Processing:** 1. Validate each output against the pinned template version's `expected_outputs` (key, asset_type, target module) → `E_VALIDATION` on undeclared outputs (no untracked asset creation). 2. Tx: upsert `workflow_outputs` (status `pending`), outbox event per output `orch.output.registered` `{run_id, output_key, asset_type, target_module, registration_payload, tenant_id, project_id, trace_id}`. 3. Owning modules (M05/M06/M07/M08/M10/M11/M12/M13/M14/M18) consume, create/attach the governed asset through their own write paths (single-writer preserved), then emit `{module}.asset.registered` ack `{run_id, output_key, asset_id}`; M16's ack consumer marks the output `registered` + `asset_id` (visible in M16-3 outputs and the run UI). Rejection acks mark `rejected` with reason and raise a WARN + `workflow_failure`-class event (alert rule fodder, FR-D4.4). 4. 202 `{accepted: n}`. Idempotent by `(run_id, output_key)` unique constraint; consumers idempotent by event_id (outbox pattern per §0.7.6 #9). **Permission key:** service-only `orch.output:register`.

#### 2.16.4 Data Design

**Storage:** PostgreSQL schema `orch` (control-plane rows, strong consistency for state machines); Redis for scheduler dispatch locks and idempotency-key cache (§1.4.4); task stdout/stderr live in object storage under tenant log prefixes, referenced by `log_ref` (never in the DB). All ids ULID with prefixes `wft-`, `wfr-`, `wrq-`, `apr-`.

**Entities (ER-style; key attributes only).**

`orch.workflow_templates` — 1:N versions
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| template_id | text PK | `wft-{ulid}` |
| tenant_id | text FK | NOT NULL; unique `(tenant_id, name)` |
| name / category / description | text / enum(11 per FR-D1.1) / text | name per UI regex |
| status | enum(active, deprecated) | template-level deprecation |
| owner_id, created_at, version (etag), updated_at | — | §1.4.4 |

`orch.workflow_template_versions` — immutable once published (FR-D1.7)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| template_id + version | PK composite | version int ≥1, monotonic |
| status | enum(draft, published, deprecated) | exact state machine |
| definition | JSONB | task graph: tasks[], on_exit, per-task retry_strategy/resource_profile/expected_outputs (FR-D1.2) |
| param_schema | JSONB | typed business params (FR-D1.5) |
| definition_checksum | text | sha256; written at publish; UPDATE forbidden by trigger when status=published |
| change_note, published_by, published_at | — | audit surface |

`orch.workflow_runs` — immutable record after terminal state (Table 0.3 #20)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| run_id | text PK | `wfr-{ulid}` |
| tenant_id / project_id / owner_id | text | NOT NULL, indexed (FR-D1.6 owner) |
| template_id + template_version | FK composite | pinned at launch |
| status | enum(pending, admitted, running, paused, succeeded, failed, cancelled) | exact machine |
| service_class | enum(exploratory, standard, release_critical, incident_response) | FR-D2.5 exact |
| params | JSONB | business inputs; asset ids only |
| work_request_id / rerun_of / reservation_id | text FK/null | rerun lineage; M17 reservation |
| hold_reasons | text[] | open approval gates |
| failure | JSONB null | `{task_key, failure_stage, failure_category, summary}` (FR-D1.6) |
| created/admitted/started/ended_at, paused_ms_total | timestamptz/int | timeline |
| version (etag) | int | optimistic locking on verbs |

`orch.task_executions` — current state per DAG node
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| run_id + task_id | PK composite | task_id = task_key from the pinned definition |
| task_type / depends_on | text / text[] | copied from definition at materialization (run is self-contained) |
| status | enum(pending, running, succeeded, failed, retrying, skipped) | exact machine |
| attempt / retry_limit | smallint | attempt = current attempt number |
| retry_strategy / resource_profile / expected_outputs | JSONB | frozen copies from the template version |
| next_attempt_at | timestamptz null | set when status=retrying (backoff target) |
| started_at / ended_at | timestamptz | first attempt start / terminal time |

`orch.task_attempts` — retry history (FR-D1.6, FR-D4.2)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| run_id + task_id + attempt | PK composite | attempt ≥1 |
| outcome | enum(succeeded, failed, lost) | `lost` = heartbeat timeout |
| failure_stage / failure_category | text / enum(infra_transient, infra_permanent, data_quality, validation, permission, quota, executor_bug, timeout, cancelled) | NULL unless failed (FR-D4.2) |
| affected_inputs | text[] | asset ids only (§1.4.7) |
| recommended_next_action | text ≤500 | executor-supplied or rule-derived |
| exit_code / backoff_applied_s / executor_identity / log_ref | int / int / text / text | log_ref → tenant object-storage prefix |
| started_at / ended_at | timestamptz | NOT NULL / null while running |

`orch.task_events` — append-only ingestion ledger; **unique `(run_id, task_id, attempt, event_seq)`** (M16-8 idempotency); columns event_type, occurred_at, payload JSONB, received_at, source_identity.

`orch.work_requests` (FR-D2.2, §11)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| request_id | text PK | `wrq-{ulid}` |
| tenant_id / project_id / requester_id | text | NOT NULL, indexed |
| type | enum(data_processing, annotation, simulation, training, evaluation, packaging) | FR-D2.1 exact |
| objective | text ≤4000 | NOT NULL |
| inputs / expected_outputs | JSONB | typed asset refs / `{asset_type, description}[]` |
| priority | enum(low, normal, high, urgent) | NOT NULL |
| status | enum(draft, submitted, triaged, approved, rejected, scheduled, completed, cancelled) | exact machine |
| triage | JSONB null | `{service_class, template_binding, required_approvals[], note, triaged_by, triaged_at}` |
| approval_state | JSONB | `{required: n, approved: n, rejected: n, subject_ids[]}` (FR-D2.2 "required approval status") |
| linked_run_ids | text[] | runs launched against this WR |
| due_date / completion_note / version (etag) | date / text / int | — |

`orch.approval_subjects` (BR-12, FR-D2.3)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| approval_id | text PK | `apr-{ulid}` |
| subject_type | enum(restricted_data_use, high_cost_compute, release_critical_evaluation, release_packaging, quota_escalation, training_plan, release_candidate_review) | gate matrix + intake |
| status | enum(open, decided) | exact machine |
| tenant_id / project_id / requested_by | text | scope + self-approval guard |
| subject_ref / payload / decider_rule | JSONB | `{type,id}` / type-specific evidence / `{permission_key, scope, named_users[]}` |
| origin / origin_event_id | enum(local, m11, m14, m17) / text unique-nullable | intake idempotency by event_id |
| consumed_at | timestamptz null | single-use override consumption (quota_escalation) |

`orch.approval_decisions` — 1:1 with subject; decision enum(approved, rejected), comment text NOT NULL-if-rejected, conditions JSONB, decider_id, decided_at; **append-only — UPDATE/DELETE revoked at the DB role level** (FR-D2.6 retention, BR-14). `orch.workflow_outputs` — unique `(run_id, output_key)`; asset_type, target_module, registration_payload JSONB, registration_status enum(pending, registered, rejected), asset_id null, rejected_reason, registered_at.

`orch.queue_view` — **materialized view**: queued/admitted runs joined to template category and service class, with depth, position (ORDER BY effective priority weight then queued_at), oldest_wait, throughput aggregates. Refresh: `REFRESH ... CONCURRENTLY` every 30 s by SVC-10 + debounced refresh on run-state outbox events (≤60 s staleness, surfaced in M16-7). `orch.outbox` + `orch.idempotency_keys` per §1.4.4/§0.7.6 #8/#9.

**Processing logic.**
- **Scheduler tick (SVC-10, every 2 s, leader-elected):** (1) promote `pending` runs with empty `hold_reasons` to `admitted` (reservation already granted at launch; runs whose gates cleared via approval get admission re-confirmed through M17-9 with the approval override token); (2) for `admitted|running` runs not paused, compute dispatchable tasks (all `depends_on` succeeded), order by service-class weight then FIFO, dispatch to the executor registered for `task_type` with per-run scoped credentials and `traceparent` (§0.7.6 #13); first dispatch flips run `admitted→running`; (3) lease management — heartbeat timeout 180 s (3 missed 60 s heartbeats) → attempt failed `failure_category=infra_transient` → retry policy applies; (4) `retrying` tasks whose backoff elapsed re-enter dispatch.
- **Retry/backoff:** per-task `retry_strategy` (Argo pattern §0.7.6 #3): `retry_policy=on_transient` retries categories `infra_transient|timeout` only; `always` retries any failure; full-jitter backoff `sleep = random(0, min(cap_s, base_s·2^attempt))` (§0.7.6 #10); exhausted → task `failed`, run fail-fast.
- **Hold resolver (consumer of `orch.approval.decided`):** recomputes `hold_reasons` for the affected run/WR; all gates approved → remove holds and (for `quota_escalation`) re-call M17-9 with the `approval_override` token; any rejection → run `cancelled (approval_rejected)` / WR `rejected`, requester notified via M20. Idempotent by event_id; a crashed resolver replays safely because hold computation is derived state.
- **Outbox relay:** at-least-once to the event bus; consumers idempotent by event_id (§1.4.5). Poison events → `orch` DLQ with replay tooling + depth alert (§0.7.6 #10).
- **Idempotency-key store:** `(tenant_id, key)` → response snapshot, 24 h TTL (§1.4.4); launch replays return the original 202 with the same `wfr-` id (per §0.7.6 #8 — a retried launch can never create a duplicate run).
- **Retention (DA-11, FR-D5.7):** run/task rows `active` 24 months (tenant-configurable), then `archived` (task_events + attempts exported to object storage; row stubs keep status/owner/timeline/failure); `purged-metadata-retained` only via tenant retention policy; approval subjects/decisions retained ≥ audit horizon (BR-14, follows M03 audit retention). Idempotency keys 24 h; queue MV transient.

#### 2.16.5 Permission Design

| Permission key | PA | TA | OE | RD (default) | Persona templates |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `orch.template:read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (platform) | ✓ | all |
| `orch.template:create` / `orch.template:publish` / `orch.template:deprecate` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (platform — operational artifact) | — | Platform Operator (`workflow.ops:*` §0.2.4) |
| `orch.run:read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (platform, metadata only) | ✓ (member projects) | all |
| `orch.run:execute` (launch, rerun) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ (member projects, **within project quota** — admission still applies) | Robotics Data Eng., Simulation Eng., Model Eng., Evaluation Lead |
| `orch.run:control` (pause/resume/cancel; rerun for recovery) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (platform) | own runs only | Platform Operator |
| `orch.run:reprioritize` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — (grantable; class ceiling enforced by M17 policy regardless) | Platform Operator |
| `orch.work_request:create/read/update` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (read) | ✓ (own/project) | all |
| `orch.work_request:triage` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (platform) | — | Platform Operator |
| `orch.approval:read` / `orch.approval:decide` | ✓ | ✓ | decide only subjects whose `decider_rule` is ops-domain (e.g., quota_escalation) | grant-only | Governance Approver (`approval:decide` §0.2.4) |
| `orch.task_event:write` / `orch.output:register` | service identities only (SVC-10, executors) | | | | — |

**Granularity:** templates tenant-scoped; runs/work requests project-scoped; approvals decided at subject scope with the subject's own `decider_rule.permission_key` re-checked at decision time (double gate). **OE boundary (§0.5.3):** OE sees run/queue metadata platform-wide but holds **no business-domain keys** — params render as asset ids without dereference; opening a dataset/model from a run detail re-checks business permissions and returns `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` for OE. Asset-level deny-wins: `orch.run:execute` never overrides M08-9 restriction verdicts (BR-05, §0.5.3).

**Credential scoping:** SVC-10 and executor services authenticate by mTLS SAN; each executor identity is tenant-bound and may call M16-8/M16-9 only for runs of its tenant (run→tenant check vs SAN). At dispatch, SVC-10 mints **per-run, per-task short-lived credentials** (storage STS scoped to the run's artifact prefix per M02 bindings; M08 manifest URLs for granted snapshot versions only — same model as M11 §2.11.5). Executor identities report task events but hold no user-facing API permissions; M17-9 admission is callable by M16/SVC-10 service identity only (mTLS, never edge-exposed).

#### 2.16.6 Logging Design

| Level | Triggers |
|---|---|
| DEBUG | scheduler tick decisions, dispatch ordering, MV refresh timing (off in prod) |
| INFO | template published/deprecated; run created/admitted/started/paused/resumed/succeeded; verb invocations; WR transitions; approval decided; output registered `{output_key, asset_id}` |
| WARN | retry scheduled `{attempt, backoff_s, category}`; heartbeat missed; output registration rejected; approval pending > SLA; DLQ entry; queue MV staleness > 60 s |
| ERROR | **task failure with `failure_stage` + `failure_category` + `recommended_next_action` ref (FR-D4.2)**; run failed (first cause); admission call failure (launch fails closed `E_UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE`); outbox relay error |
| FATAL | scheduler leader cannot persist state transitions (dispatch halted) |

**JSON fields (per §1.4.6 shared schema +):** `run_id`, `task_id`, `attempt`, `event_seq`, `queue` (`{workload_class}/{service_class}`), `template_id`, `template_version`, `service_class`, `decision`, `work_request_id`, `approval_id`, `error_code`. **Masking (§1.4.7):** workflow `params` may reference restricted dataset ids — log **asset ids only, never payloads or manifest contents**; param values that are free text logged as `{param_count, params_hash}`; signed URLs and minted credentials never logged; approval comments logged by `approval_id` reference only (full text lives in the row + audit).

**Shipping:** stdout JSON → platform log pipeline (OTel collector, PII scrub per §0.7.6 #14); `traceparent` propagated API → outbox → SVC-10 → executor → M16-8 callback so one run is one trace (§0.7.6 #13). Task stdout/stderr ship to tenant object-storage log prefixes, referenced by `log_ref`.

**Audit events (M03-11):** `orch.template.published` / `orch.template.deprecated`, **`orch.run.launched`** `{run_id, template@version, params_asset_ids, service_class}`, **`orch.run.cancelled`** `{actor, reason}`, `orch.run.rerun` `{rerun_of}`, **`ops.priority.adjusted`** `{run_id, from, to}` (emitted here; ops-domain catalog per M17), **`orch.approval.decided`** `{subject_type, decision, subject_ref, comment_ref}` (FR-D2.6, FR-D5.4, AC-14), `orch.work_request.submitted/triaged/approved/rejected/scheduled/completed/cancelled` (lifecycle events mirrored by M15 §2.15.4), `orch.output.registered`.

---

### 2.17 M17 — Compute Operations & Quota Management

#### 2.17.1 Module Overview

**Module ID & name:** M17 — Compute Operations & Quota Management.

**Scope boundary.**
- *Owns:* quota policies at tenant level and project sub-allocations (FR-D3.1, DA-10); the admission decision point for every run launch and reprioritization (M17-9, BR-12); priority/escalation **policy** — who may use which service class, when escalation is allowed (FR-D3.4/D3.5 policy side); consumption metering, attribution, and cost computation (FR-D3.6, M17-10); utilization rollups and summaries by workload class (FR-D3.3); the cross-module job status board projection (FR-D3.2, FR-D4.1); incident records linked to runs/assets (FR-D4.5); alert **rule definitions and evaluation** (FR-D4.4 — delivery is M20); operational dashboards and reports (FR-D4.3, FR-D4.6, RPT-10).
- *Excludes:* workflow control verbs, DAG state, approval inbox mechanics (M16 — M17 only supplies verdicts and creates approval subjects via intake events, boundary §1.6.3); compute/storage resource **inventory and bindings** (M02 — M17 reads binding capacity, never registers machines); notification delivery, dedup, channels (M20); domain asset content (owning modules).

**User roles & personas.** Primary: **Platform Operator / Ops Engineer** (quota administration, utilization, incidents, alert rules — `quota:*, queue:*, incident:*` persona template §0.2.4). TA reads tenant utilization and sets project sub-allocations; RD reads own project quota/consumption and job statuses; Program Managers consume operational reports. All roles see quota warnings surfaced in M16 launch UX.

**Upstream/downstream.** Upstream: M02 (tenant resource bindings → capacity baselines per DA-20); M16 (admission requests via M17-9; run state events feeding the job board and releasing reservations); executors + SVC-10 (consumption samples via M17-10); M05/M13/M14/M16 events (alert-rule input stream: data-quality blocks, evaluation regressions, approval blockers, workflow failures); SVC-11 Quota Admission & Metering Collector. Downstream: M16 (verdicts allow|deny|escalate; quota-escalation approval subjects into M16-6); M20 (fired alerts via M20-4; quota warnings); leadership reporting (RPT-10); M03-11 (audit).

**FRD coverage:** FR-D3.1–D3.6 (DA-10 quota model), FR-D4.1–D4.6, RPT-10, BR-12 (with M16), AC-13 (utilization/jobs part), §11 Workflow Run "resource attribution". Research basis per §0.7.6: Kueue ClusterQueue/LocalQueue two-level quota with borrowing and usage-history-weighted admission (#5), Volcano queue priority (#6), OpenCost allocation spec with explicit idle line (#7), Alertmanager-style alerting split (#14 — rules here, grouping/delivery M20).

#### 2.17.2 Functional & UI/UX Design

**Primary functions.**
1. Allocate shared compute to tenants (hard ceiling) and projects (sub-allocation) per DA-10 — the Kueue ClusterQueue/LocalQueue analog (per §0.7.6): tenant ceiling = ClusterQueue nominal quota; project sub-allocation = LocalQueue share with `guaranteed` and `limit` (borrowing cap); idle guaranteed capacity is borrowable by sibling projects and reclaimed at next admission (FR-D3.1).
2. Admit, deny, or escalate every launch/reprioritize against quota, sub-allocation, and service-class policy (M17-9; BR-12); escalation creates an M16-6 approval subject (FR-D3.4).
3. Emit quota warnings at the configured threshold (default **80%**) to M20 and the launch preflight (FR-D3.4).
4. Own the priority policy: which roles/personas may use which service class (`incident_response` default OE/TA-only) and approve M16-4 `:reprioritize` requests (FR-D3.5).
5. Meter consumption from executors (M17-10), attribute it across **exactly** the dimensions `project, team, campaign, model, workflow, cost_center` (FR-D3.6), and compute cost OpenCost-style (quantity × rate, idle reported as an explicit platform-overhead line, per §0.7.6).
6. Utilization summaries for operators by workload class — exactly `data_processing | simulation | training | evaluation | packaging` (FR-D3.3) — via the fixed mapping table below.
7. Job status visibility by project across `requested | queued | running | completed | failed | cancelled` (FR-D3.2 exact; FR-D4.1), with failed jobs showing failure stage, category, affected inputs, recommended next action (FR-D4.2 — fields recorded by M16-8, surfaced here).
8. Incident annotations linked to affected runs/assets/alerts (FR-D4.5).
9. Alert rules for **workflow failures, quota breaches, data quality blocks, evaluation regressions, release approval blockers** (FR-D4.4 exact five types); the M17 evaluation engine fires matched alerts to M20-4 (delivery, dedup, routing = M20).
10. Operational dashboards: throughput, backlog, failures, utilization, asset production (FR-D4.3, RPT-10) and operational reports by project/team/capability/campaign/release plan (FR-D4.6 — `capability`=workload class; `release_plan`=runs whose `model_candidate` param links to an open M14 release candidate).

**Two-level quota model (DA-10; Kueue ClusterQueue/LocalQueue analog per §0.7.6).**

| Level | Object | Semantics | Kueue analog |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tenant | QuotaPolicy ceiling (`ceiling_concurrent`, `ceiling_period_hours` per resource class) | **Hard** — never exceeded, never escalatable (admission step 4) | ClusterQueue nominal quota |
| Project | Sub-allocation `guaranteed` | Always admittable up to here (admission step 5) | LocalQueue nominal share |
| Project | Sub-allocation `limit` (> guaranteed) | Borrowing cap: admit between guaranteed and limit only while sibling projects leave tenant headroom idle; borrowed reservations flagged `preemptible_for_reclaim` and requeued (M16 pause/requeue signal) when the lending project needs its guarantee back | borrowingLimit + reclaim |
| Service class | `service_class_caps` (% of ceiling per class) + class eligibility policy | Caps prevent exploratory work from saturating capacity; `incident_response` restricted to OE/TA grant; class weight orders the M16 dispatch queue (FR-D2.5/D3.5) | WorkloadPriorityClass |
| Request | Escalation (`escalatable`, `max_overage_pct`) | Above `limit` → approval subject in M16-6 (FR-D3.4) instead of silent denial | — |

**Template-category → workload-class mapping (fixed, used by admission, metering, rollups, dashboards).**

| Workload class (FR-D3.3 exact) | Template categories (FR-D1.1) |
|---|---|
| data_processing | ingestion, validation, alignment, annotation, dataset_publishing, reporting |
| simulation | simulation, synthetic_generation |
| training | training |
| evaluation | evaluation |
| packaging | packaging |

**State machines (exact).** QuotaPolicy version: `active → superseded` (PATCH creates a new version). Incident: `open → investigating → resolved`. AlertRule: `enabled ↔ disabled` (soft delete).

##### UI page 1 — Quota Policy Editor, route `/ops/quotas` (tenant) and `/admin/quotas` (platform)

Master-detail: left = tenants (platform view) or resource classes (tenant view); right = policy editor + project sub-allocation grid.

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| resource_class | single-select | enum(gpu_h100, gpu_a100, gpu_l40s, cpu_core, mem_gb, storage_gb) | — | Select resource | Required; must exist in tenant's M02 bindings |
| ceiling_concurrent | number | int ≥0 | — | e.g. 64 | Required; ≤ bound capacity from M02 (warning if over) |
| ceiling_period_hours | number | decimal ≥0, per calendar month | — | e.g. 20000 | Required for metered classes |
| service_class_caps | 4 sliders | % of ceiling per class | 100/100/100/100 | — | Each 0–100; `incident_response` cap cannot be set below 10 (policy floor) |
| warning_threshold_pct | number | 50–95 | **80** | — | Required (FR-D3.4) |
| escalation.escalatable | toggle + max_overage_pct | bool, 0–100 | true, 20 | — | If false, denials are final |
| high_cost_threshold_gpu_hours | number | ≥0 | 500 | — | Feeds M16 high-cost gate |
| project sub-allocations | grid rows | per project: guaranteed, limit, priority_weight, default cost_center | — | — | Σ guaranteed ≤ ceiling (`E_QTA_SUBALLOC_EXCEEDS_TENANT`); limit ≥ guaranteed |

- **Validation logic:** client-side sum bar visualizes Σ guaranteed vs ceiling; server authoritative. Saving a policy creates a new version (old → `superseded`); diff preview shown before save.
- **API bindings:** `POST/GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/quota-policies`, `GET/PATCH /api/v1/quota-policies/{policy_id}` (M17-1); `PUT/GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/quota` (M17-2).
- **Output rendering:** current usage bars (in_use/reserved/remaining) per row from the admission cache, refreshed every **30 s**.
- **Exception handling:** `E_QTA_SUBALLOC_EXCEEDS_TENANT` → grid error with the overflowing rows highlighted; `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → editor read-only (TA may edit only M17-2 sub-allocations; M17-1 ceilings are OE/PA); `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED` (stale ETag) → reload-merge dialog.

##### UI page 2 — Utilization Dashboards, route `/ops/utilization` (+ `/admin/utilization`)

Stacked-area utilization per workload class (exact five, FR-D3.3), capacity line from M02 bindings, **idle band** rendered explicitly (OpenCost pattern per §0.7.6); window selector (24 h/7 d/30 d/custom); tenant/project drill-down (platform view adds tenant dimension). API: M17-3. Polling: **60 s**. Empty/loading: skeleton chart; gaps annotated "no samples". Errors: `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → tenant users cannot open `/admin/utilization`.

##### UI page 3 — Consumption Explorer, route `/ops/consumption`

Pivot table + chart over consumption records.

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| group_by | multi-select (ordered) | ⊆ {project, team, campaign, model, workflow, cost_center} | project | Add dimension | 1–3 dimensions (FR-D3.6 exact set; else `E_QTA_UNKNOWN_DIMENSION`) |
| window | date-range | RFC 3339 | last 30 d | — | ≤ 12 months span |
| resource_class | multi-select | enum | all | — | Optional |
| include_cost | toggle | bool | true | — | Cost columns need `ops.utilization:read` |

- **API binding:** `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/consumption?group_by=` (M17-4); tenant/platform variants reuse the same renderer via M17-3 scope. **Output:** rows with gpu_hours, cpu_core_hours, mem_gb_hours, storage_gb_month, cost, plus a final **unattributed/idle** row at tenant scope; CSV export. No polling (on-demand query). **Errors:** `E_QTA_UNKNOWN_DIMENSION` → chip-level error; `E_VALIDATION` window too large.

##### UI page 4 — Job Status Board, route `/ops/jobs`

Cross-module board (FR-D3.2/D4.1): tabs per status `requested | queued | running | completed | failed | cancelled`; columns: ref (run `wfr-`/request `wrq-`), type/category, project, owner, service class, queue position (queued), started, duration, and for failed — failure stage, category, affected inputs (ids), recommended next action (FR-D4.2) with one-click "Open run in M16" and "Create incident" CTAs. Filters `status`, `project`. API: `GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/jobs?status&project` (M17-5). **Polling: every 15 s** while the tab is visible. Errors: `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → RD sees only member projects (server-side filter, not UI-only).

##### UI page 5 — Incident Timeline, route `/ops/incidents`

List + timeline detail per incident: status chips (`open → investigating → resolved`), severity, linked runs/assets/alerts rendered as chips with deep links, comment-style updates.

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| title | text | ≤200 chars | empty | Short incident title | Required |
| severity | single-select | `critical\|major\|minor` | major | — | Required |
| summary | textarea | ≤4,000 chars | empty | What happened | Required |
| started_at | datetime | RFC 3339 | now | — | ≤ now |
| links | reference rows | `{type: workflow_run\|asset\|alert, ref}` | empty | wfr-… / dss-… | Each ref existence-checked (`E_INC_LINK_INVALID`) |

- **API bindings:** `POST/GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/incidents`, `GET/PATCH /api/v1/incidents/{incident_id}`, `PUT .../links` (M17-6). **Output:** timeline polls detail every **30 s** while incident non-resolved. **Errors:** `E_INC_LINK_INVALID` → row error; `E_STATE_INVALID` (resolve an open incident without investigating? — allowed: open→investigating→resolved only; direct open→resolved rejected with allowed_transitions) → toast.

##### UI page 6 — Alert Rule Editor, route `/ops/alert-rules`

Rule list (type, severity, scope, enabled, last fired) + editor:

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| rule_type | single-select | `workflow_failure\|quota_breach\|data_quality_block\|evaluation_regression\|release_approval_blocker` | — | — | Required (FR-D4.4 exact five) |
| scope | pickers | project?/template category?/service class? | tenant-wide | — | Optional narrowing |
| condition | structured per type | e.g. workflow_failure: `{consecutive_failures: int≥1}` or `{failure_rate_pct: 1–100, window_min: 5–1440}`; quota_breach: `{threshold_pct: 50–100}` | type default (e.g. threshold_pct=100) | — | Required; schema per rule_type (`E_VALIDATION` details) |
| severity | single-select | `info\|warning\|critical` | warning | — | Required |
| targets | multi-select | roles at scope + named users | Platform Operator | — | ≥1 target |
| enabled | toggle | bool | true | — | — |

- **API bindings:** `POST/GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/alert-rules`, `GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/alert-rules/{rule_id}` (M17-7). **Output:** "last fired" + 7-day fire-count sparkline; test-fire button (sends a test alert via M20-4 marked `test=true`). **Errors:** `E_CONFLICT` duplicate rule name; `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` for non-OE/TA.

##### UI page 7 — Ops Dashboard (RPT-10), route `/ops/dashboard` (+ `/admin/ops-dashboard`)

Panel grid per FR-D4.3/RPT-10: **throughput** (runs succeeded/day by category), **backlog** (queue depth + oldest age, from M16-7 data), **failures** (failure-rate trend by stage/category; top recommended actions), **utilization** (five workload classes + idle), **asset production** (outputs registered/day by asset type, from `orch.output.registered` events), **quota** (per-project usage vs sub-allocation, warning markers at 80%), **cost attribution** (top cost centers/projects). Dimension switcher: project / team / capability(workload class) / campaign / release plan (FR-D4.6). API: M17-8. **Polling: 60 s**; each panel renders skeleton → data → error chip independently (`E_UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE` per panel, no page-wide failure).

#### 2.17.3 Interface Design

Module error codes: `E_QTA_SUBALLOC_EXCEEDS_TENANT` (422), `E_QTA_POLICY_OVERLAP` (409, second active policy for same tenant+resource_class), `E_QTA_UNKNOWN_DIMENSION` (422), `E_QTA_CLASS_NOT_PERMITTED` (403, service-class policy), `E_QTA_NOT_ESCALATABLE` (422, escalation requested but policy forbids), `E_INC_LINK_INVALID` (422); plus shared codes (`E_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` used by M16 relaying admission denials).

##### M17-1 Tenant quota policies
- `POST /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/quota-policies` — body per UI page 1 fields (resource_class, ceilings, service_class_caps, warning_threshold_pct, escalation, high_cost_threshold_gpu_hours, effective_from?).
- **Processing:** 1. authz `ops.quota:update` at **platform** scope (OE/PA; TA cannot raise own ceilings). 2. Validate against M02 binding capacity (over-commit allowed with `warnings[]` in response; hard error only if resource_class unbound). 3. One active policy per `(tenant, resource_class)` → `E_QTA_POLICY_OVERLAP`. 4. Tx: insert version 1 `active`; outbox `ops.quota.updated`; admission cache invalidate. 5. 201.
- `GET` same (TA/OE read); `GET/PATCH /api/v1/quota-policies/{policy_id}` — PATCH with `If-Match` creates version n+1 `active`, supersedes prior in the same tx (version history retained; Table 0.3 #24). Audit `ops.quota.updated` `{policy_id, version, changed_fields, before/after summary}`.
- **Errors:** above + `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED`. **Idempotency:** Idempotency-Key on POST; PATCH guarded by ETag. **Permission keys:** `ops.quota:update` (write), `ops.quota:read`.

##### M17-2 Project sub-allocations
- `PUT /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/quota` — body `{"allocations": [{"resource_class": "gpu_h100", "guaranteed": 16, "limit": 32}], "priority_weight": 1.0, "default_cost_center": "cc-robotics-01"}`; `GET` same.
- **Processing:** 1. authz `ops.quota:update` at tenant scope (TA allowed here — sub-allocation within own ceiling; OE/PA too). 2. Validate Σ guaranteed across the tenant's projects ≤ tenant ceiling per resource_class → `E_QTA_SUBALLOC_EXCEEDS_TENANT` (details list per-class totals); `limit ≥ guaranteed`. 3. Tx: upsert allocation rows (versioned), outbox `ops.quota.updated` (project scope), cache invalidate. 4. 200.
- `GET` response (also the RD-visible quota view, FR-D3.4):
```json
{"project_id": "prj-...", "priority_weight": 1.0, "default_cost_center": "cc-robotics-01",
 "allocations": [{"resource_class": "gpu_h100", "guaranteed": 16, "limit": 32,
   "usage": {"in_use": 14, "reserved": 2, "pct_of_guaranteed": 100, "warning": true}}],
 "tenant_policy_ref": {"policy_id": "qta-01J...", "warning_threshold_pct": 80}, "version": 3}
```
- **Idempotency:** PUT is full-replace idempotent; `If-Match` required. **Permission keys:** `ops.quota:update` / `ops.quota:read` (RD reads own project's quota+usage here — FR-D3.4 visibility).

##### M17-3 Utilization summaries
- `GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/utilization?window=7d&granularity=1h&workload_class&project_id` and `GET /api/v1/admin/utilization?tenant_id&...` (platform).
- **Processing:** 1. authz — tenant route `ops.utilization:read` at tenant; admin route platform scope (OE/PA). 2. Serve from `ops.utilization_rollups` (grain auto-selected: 1 h for ≤7 d, 1 d beyond; never raw-record scans). 3. Response (FR-D3.3, idle explicit per §0.7.6 #7):
```json
{"window": {"start": "ts", "end": "ts", "granularity": "1h"},
 "series": [{"workload_class": "training", "points": [{"ts": "ts", "used_gpu_hours": 412.5, "capacity_gpu_hours": 512.0, "utilization_pct": 80.6}]},
            {"workload_class": "simulation", "points": ["..."]}],
 "idle": {"points": [{"ts": "ts", "idle_gpu_hours": 38.0, "idle_pct": 7.4}]},
 "totals": {"by_class": {"data_processing": 120.0, "simulation": 233.0, "training": 412.5, "evaluation": 96.0, "packaging": 8.0}, "idle": 38.0}}
```
- **Errors:** `E_VALIDATION` (window > 12 months, granularity < 1 h, or 1 h grain requested for > 7 d windows). **Permission key:** `ops.utilization:read`.

##### M17-4 Consumption attribution
- `GET /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/consumption?group_by=campaign,cost_center&window_start&window_end&resource_class`
- **Processing:** 1. authz `ops.utilization:read` at project (RD member ok). 2. Validate `group_by` ⊆ {project, team, campaign, model, workflow, cost_center}, 1–3 dims → `E_QTA_UNKNOWN_DIMENSION`. 3. Aggregate monthly-partitioned `ops.consumption_records` via rollups when the grouping is pre-aggregated, else partition-pruned query. 4. Response items `{keys: {campaign: "cmp-...", cost_center: "cc-..."}, gpu_hours, cpu_core_hours, mem_gb_hours, storage_gb_month, cost: {amount, currency, rate_card_version}}` + `unattributed` row (FR-D3.6). Dimensions sourced: project/workflow from the run; campaign/model/evaluation refs from run params; team + cost_center from project settings (M02) with per-run override param. **Permission key:** `ops.utilization:read`.

##### M17-5 Job status board
- `GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/jobs?status=failed&project=prj-...&page_size`
- **Processing:** 1. authz `ops.utilization:read` at tenant **or** project membership (RD auto-filtered to member projects server-side). 2. Serve from `ops.job_status` projection (fed by M16 run/WR events — M17 never queries `orch.*` directly, boundary §1.6.3). Status mapping (FR-D3.2 exact): `requested`=WR submitted/triaged/approved without run; `queued`=run pending/admitted; `running`=run running/paused (`paused` flagged); `completed`=succeeded; `failed`; `cancelled`. 3. Response items (FR-D4.1/D4.2):
```json
{"items": [{"ref": "wfr-01J...", "ref_type": "workflow_run", "business_status": "failed", "category": "training",
  "workload_class": "training", "service_class": "standard", "project_id": "prj-...", "owner_id": "usr-...",
  "queue_position": null, "queued_at": "ts", "started_at": "ts", "duration_s": 5210,
  "failure": {"stage": "data_load", "category": "data_quality", "affected_inputs": ["dss-01J..."],
              "recommended_next_action": "Re-run intake validation on dss-01J..."},
  "freshness_s": 12}], "next_page_token": null, "total_size": 41}
```
- **Permission key:** as step 1.

##### M17-6 Incidents
- `POST /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/incidents` body `{title, severity, summary, started_at, links?: [{type, ref}]}` → 201 `inc-{ulid}` status `open`; audit `ops.incident.created`.
- `GET` same (filters `status`, `severity`, `created_after`); `GET/PATCH /api/v1/incidents/{incident_id}` — PATCH: status transitions per machine `open→investigating→resolved` (resolve requires `resolution_note`; illegal jump → `E_STATE_INVALID`), edits to summary/severity append timeline entries; `PUT .../links` full-replace link set, each ref validated for existence + tenant scope (run ids against `ops.job_status`; asset ids via M08-2 envelope; alert ids local) → `E_INC_LINK_INVALID` (FR-D4.5).
- **Processing tx:** incident row + timeline entry + outbox `ops.incident.created|updated|resolved` (→ M20 notifies targets). **Idempotency/concurrency:** Idempotency-Key on POST; `If-Match` on PATCH/PUT. **Permission keys:** `ops.incident:create/update` (OE/TA), `ops.incident:read` (all tenant roles).

##### M17-7 Alert rules
- `POST /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/alert-rules` body `{name, rule_type, scope: {project_id?, template_category?, service_class?}, condition: {...per-type schema}, severity, targets: [{kind: role|user, ref}], enabled}` → 201 `alr-{ulid}`.
- Per-type condition schemas: `workflow_failure` `{consecutive_failures?:int}|{failure_rate_pct, window_min}`; `quota_breach` `{threshold_pct}` (≥ warning threshold fires warning, 100 fires breach); `data_quality_block` `{recordings_blocked_min_count?, window_min}` (input: M05 quarantine/block events); `evaluation_regression` `{any_regression: true}|{metric_drop_pct}` (input: M13 report-vs-baseline events); `release_approval_blocker` `{pending_longer_than_h}` (input: M14/M16 approval-subject age).
- `GET` same; `GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/alert-rules/{rule_id}` (DELETE = disable + tombstone; rules referenced by open incidents are not hard-deleted).
- **Processing:** validate condition against per-type schema (`E_VALIDATION` with field paths); tx insert + outbox `ops.alert_rule.updated` (engine reload). **Permission keys:** `ops.alert_rule:create/update/delete` (OE/TA), `ops.alert_rule:read`.

##### M17-8 Operational dashboards (RPT-10)
- `GET /api/v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/ops-dashboard?window=7d&dimension=project|team|capability|campaign|release_plan` and `GET /api/v1/admin/ops-dashboard?...` (platform, adds tenant dimension).
- **Processing:** assemble panel payloads from rollups + projections; all panels respect the `dimension` parameter (FR-D4.6). Authz: tenant route `ops.utilization:read`; admin route platform OE/PA. Response (RPT-10 panel contract):
```json
{"generated_at": "ts", "window": "7d", "dimension": "project",
 "panels": {
  "throughput": {"freshness_s": 60, "series": [{"key": "prj-...", "runs_succeeded_per_day": [{"date": "2026-06-10", "count": 41}]}]},
  "backlog": {"freshness_s": 30, "series": [{"key": "prj-...", "queue_depth": 12, "oldest_wait_s": 5400, "trend": ["..."]}]},
  "failures": {"freshness_s": 60, "rate_by_category": {"data_quality": 0.04, "infra_transient": 0.02}, "by_stage": {"data_load": 9}, "top_next_actions": [{"action": "Re-run intake validation...", "count": 6}]},
  "utilization": {"freshness_s": 300, "by_workload_class": {"training": 0.81, "simulation": 0.55, "data_processing": 0.34, "evaluation": 0.4, "packaging": 0.1}, "idle_pct": 0.07},
  "asset_production": {"freshness_s": 60, "per_day_by_type": [{"date": "2026-06-10", "dataset_snapshot": 2, "model_candidate": 1, "evaluation_report": 3}]},
  "quota": {"freshness_s": 30, "rows": [{"key": "prj-...", "resource_class": "gpu_h100", "used_pct": 83, "warning": true}]},
  "cost": {"freshness_s": 3600, "top": [{"key": "cc-robotics-01", "cost": 18250.0, "currency": "USD"}]}},
 "errors": []}
```
  Panel sources: throughput/failures/asset_production from `ops.job_status` + `orch.output.registered` event rollups; backlog from queue snapshots (M16-7 data replicated on events); utilization/cost from `ops.utilization_rollups`; quota from the admission cache. **Errors:** per-panel degradation — a cold rollup yields `panels.{name}.error` + entry in `errors[]`, never a page-wide 500.

##### M17-9 (i) Admission check
- `POST /internal/v1/admission/check` (mTLS; **M16/SVC-10 service identity only** — single touchpoint per §1.6.3).
- Request:
```json
{"action": "launch|reprioritize", "tenant_id": "tnt-...", "project_id": "prj-...", "requester_id": "usr-...",
 "service_class": "standard", "target_service_class": "incident_response|null", "workload_class": "training",
 "resource_request": {"gpu_class": "gpu_h100", "gpu_count": 8, "est_gpu_hours": 96, "cpu_cores": 32, "mem_gb": 256},
 "run_ref": "wfr-...|null", "request_escalation": false, "approval_override": "apr-...|null", "dry_run": false}
```
- **Processing (numbered, decision order fixed):**
  1. Verify caller identity (mTLS SAN allowlist) → else 403.
  2. Resolve active QuotaPolicy for `(tenant, resource_class)` and the project sub-allocation; load cached counters `{in_use, reserved}` (Redis admission cache, reconciled §2.17.4). No policy → default-deny for GPU classes, allow for unmetered classes (logged WARN).
  3. **Service-class eligibility:** requested (or target) class checked against class policy (`incident_response` → requester must hold `ops.quota:update` or `orch.run:reprioritize` at tenant; `release_critical` → project must have an open release candidate or explicit grant). Fail → verdict `deny`, reason `class_not_permitted` (M16 relays `E_QTA_CLASS_NOT_PERMITTED`). For `action=reprioritize` this is the whole check (no new reservation) → verdict `allow|deny`, done.
  4. **Tenant ceiling (hard):** `in_use + reserved + requested > ceiling` → verdict **`deny`**, `escalatable: false` (DA-10 hard ceiling; never escalatable above tenant ceiling).
  5. **Project sub-allocation:** ≤ `guaranteed` → admit; ≤ `limit` ∧ tenant headroom available → admit with `borrowed: true` (Kueue borrowing per §0.7.6; borrowed reservations carry `preemptible_for_reclaim: true` — reclaim = M16 pause/requeue signal at next admission pressure); > `limit` → step 6.
  6. **Escalation:** policy `escalatable` ∧ overage ≤ `max_overage_pct` (or `request_escalation=true` within bounds) → verdict **`escalate`** + `escalation: {subject_type: "quota_escalation", decider_permission: "ops.quota:update", payload: quota_snapshot}` (M16 creates the `apr-` subject in its launch tx — FR-D3.4); else verdict **`deny`** with `escalatable: false` reason `suballocation_exceeded` (`E_QTA_NOT_ESCALATABLE` semantics carried in details).
  7. `approval_override` present (re-check after approved escalation): validate the subject is `decided(approved)` and unconsumed → admit bypassing step 5 up to the approved overage, consume the override (single-use).
  8. **Warning evaluation:** projected usage ≥ `warning_threshold_pct` (default 80) → append `warnings[]` + emit `ops.quota.warning`; projected usage ≥ 100% of the effective ceiling (hard-cap deny) additionally emits `ops.quota.breach` (both alert-engine inputs; WARN log with `quota_remaining`).
  9. **Tx (verdict allow):** insert `ops.admission_reservations` `{reservation_id, run_ref?, resource vector, expires_at = now()+15min if run not started}` + decision log row; update cache atomically. `dry_run=true` skips writes (M16-2 preflight).
  10. Respond ≤50 ms p99:
```json
{"verdict": "allow|deny|escalate", "reservation_id": "rsv-...|null", "borrowed": false,
 "reasons": ["..."], "warnings": ["project gpu_h100 at 83% of sub-allocation"],
 "quota_snapshot": {"resource_class": "gpu_h100", "ceiling": 64, "in_use": 41, "reserved": 12, "remaining": 11, "project_guaranteed": 16, "project_limit": 32, "project_in_use": 14},
 "escalation": {"subject_type": "quota_escalation", "decider_permission": "ops.quota:update"}|null}
```
- **Worked examples (tenant ceiling 64 gpu_h100 concurrent; project guaranteed 16 / limit 32; in_use+reserved: tenant 41, project 14):**

| Request | Step outcome | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| 8 GPUs, standard | project 14+8=22 > guaranteed 16, ≤ limit 32, tenant 41+8=49 ≤ 64 headroom | `allow`, `borrowed: true`, reclaim-preemptible |
| 24 GPUs, standard | project 38 > limit 32; policy escalatable, overage ≤ max_overage_pct | `escalate` → `apr-` subject (FR-D3.4) |
| 30 GPUs, incident_response (requester lacks class grant) | step 3 class eligibility fails | `deny`, reason `class_not_permitted` |
| 26 GPUs, standard (tenant 41+26=67 > 64) | step 4 tenant hard ceiling | `deny`, `escalatable: false` (DA-10) |

- **Reservation lifecycle:** consumed (replaced by metered usage) as M17-10 samples arrive; released on run terminal events from M16; expired reservations GC'd (15 min unstarted). Failure mode: M17 unreachable → M16 fails launches closed (`E_UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE`) — quota is never bypassed (BR-12). **Idempotency:** admission is read-modify-write guarded by per-`(tenant, resource_class)` Redis lock; duplicate check for the same launch Idempotency-Key returns the same reservation. **Permission:** service identity `ops.admission:execute` (internal mTLS only).

##### M17-10 (i) Consumption metering ingestion
- `POST /internal/v1/consumption-records` (mTLS; executor identities + SVC-10).
- Request: `{"records": [{"run_id": "wfr-...", "task_id": "train", "attempt": 2, "window_start": "ts", "window_end": "ts", "resource_class": "gpu_h100", "quantity": 8.0, "unit": "gpu_hours", "workload_class": "training", "attribution": {"project_id": "prj-...", "team": "manipulation", "campaign_id": "cmp-...|null", "model_id": "mdl-...|null", "workflow_template": "training-sft-standard@4", "cost_center": "cc-robotics-01"}}]}`
- **Processing:** 1. identity↔tenant check. 2. **Idempotent insert** into the monthly partition, unique `(run_id, task_id, attempt, resource_class, window_start)` — duplicates dropped, counted in response. 3. Update admission cache (decrement reservation, increment in_use). 4. Malformed records → per-record errors + DLQ (never block the batch). 5. 200 `{accepted, duplicates, rejected}`. **Permission:** service identity `ops.metering:write`.

#### 2.17.4 Data Design

**Storage:** PostgreSQL schema `ops`; **`ops.consumption_records` partitioned by month** (`window_start`); Redis admission cache (counters per tenant×project×resource_class; authority = PostgreSQL, cache rebuilt on restart). Rate cards (currency per gpu_hour/cpu_core_hour/mem_gb_hour/storage_gb_month per §0.7.6 #7) stored as versioned `ops.rate_cards`.

**Entities (ER-style).**

`ops.quota_policies` — versioned (Table 0.3 #24); state `active → superseded`
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| policy_id | text PK | `qta-{ulid}` (new ULID per version; `lineage_id` groups versions) |
| tenant_id / resource_class | text / enum(gpu_h100, gpu_a100, gpu_l40s, cpu_core, mem_gb, storage_gb) | unique partial `(tenant_id, resource_class) WHERE status='active'` |
| ceiling_concurrent / ceiling_period_hours | int / numeric | hard tenant ceiling (DA-10) |
| service_class_caps | JSONB | `{exploratory, standard, release_critical, incident_response}` % caps (FR-D2.5 exact keys) |
| warning_threshold_pct | smallint | default **80** (FR-D3.4) |
| escalation | JSONB | `{escalatable: bool, max_overage_pct}` |
| high_cost_threshold_gpu_hours | numeric | feeds the M16 high-cost gate |
| version / status / effective_from / created_by | int / enum(active, superseded) / timestamptz / text | PATCH = new version, old superseded in same tx |

`ops.project_quota_allocations` — project_id + resource_class PK; guaranteed, limit (CHECK `limit >= guaranteed`), priority_weight numeric, default_cost_center, version, status; trigger validates Σ guaranteed ≤ active tenant ceiling (`E_QTA_SUBALLOC_EXCEEDS_TENANT`).

`ops.consumption_records` — **partitioned by month on `window_start`** (FR-D3.6)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| record_id | text PK (per partition) | ULID |
| run_id + task_id + attempt + resource_class + window_start | unique | **M17-10 idempotency key** |
| tenant_id / window_end / workload_class | text / timestamptz / enum(5 exact) | NOT NULL |
| quantity / unit | numeric / enum(gpu_hours, cpu_core_hours, mem_gb_hours, storage_gb_month) | ≥0 |
| project_id / team / campaign_id / model_id / workflow_template / cost_center | text columns | **attribution dims as columns** for index-friendly group-bys (FR-D3.6 exact set) |
| cost_amount / rate_card_version | numeric / text | computed at rollup (OpenCost: quantity × rate, §0.7.6 #7) |
| source_identity / received_at | text / timestamptz | executor SAN; late-arrival detection |

`ops.admission_reservations` — `rsv-{ulid}`, run_ref nullable, tenant/project, resource vector JSONB, borrowed bool, `preemptible_for_reclaim` bool, status enum(held, consumed, released, expired), expires_at; index `(tenant_id, resource_class, status)`. `ops.admission_decisions` — append-only: request summary, verdict, reasons[], quota_snapshot JSONB, latency_ms (BR-12 evidence; 6-month retention then archive). `ops.utilization_rollups` — PK (grain enum(hour, day, month), ts, tenant_id, project_id, workload_class, resource_class): used_quantity, capacity_quantity, idle_quantity, cost. `ops.job_status` — projection (event-sourced from M16 outbox): ref, ref_type, business_status (six FR-D3.2 values), category, workload_class, service_class, project, owner, timestamps, failure JSONB (FR-D4.2 fields), last_event_id (idempotent upsert). `ops.rate_cards` — versioned currency rates per unit (per §0.7.6 #7; on-prem custom pricing supported).

`ops.incidents` — state `open → investigating → resolved` (FR-D4.5)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| incident_id | text PK | `inc-{ulid}` |
| tenant_id / title / severity | text / text ≤200 / enum(critical, major, minor) | NOT NULL |
| summary / root_cause / resolution_note | text | resolution_note required at `resolved` |
| status / started_at / resolved_at / created_by / version (etag) | enum + timestamptz + text + int | exact machine |
| → `ops.incident_links` | 1:N | (incident_id, link_type enum(workflow_run, asset, alert), ref, added_by, added_at) |
| → `ops.incident_timeline` | 1:N append-only | (entry_id, actor, entry_type enum(status_change, note, link_change), body, at) |

`ops.alert_rules` (FR-D4.4)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| rule_id | text PK | `alr-{ulid}` |
| tenant_id + name | unique | — |
| rule_type | enum(workflow_failure, quota_breach, data_quality_block, evaluation_regression, release_approval_blocker) | exact five |
| scope / condition / targets | JSONB | per-type condition schema (§2.17.3 M17-7) |
| severity / enabled / last_fired_at / fire_count_7d | enum(info, warning, critical) / bool / timestamptz / int | — |

`ops.alert_firings` — engine state: fingerprint PK (rule_id + label hash), status firing|resolved, first/last_seen, count (M17-side evaluation state; M20 keeps its own delivery-side state).

**Processing logic.**
- **Admission cache:** read-through counters `{in_use, reserved}`; written synchronously in M17-9/M17-10 tx-commit hooks; **reconciliation job every 5 min** recomputes from reservations + current-month consumption and repairs drift (drift > 2% → WARN).
- **Rollup jobs (SVC-11):** hourly rollup at :05 (idempotent re-runs by grain+ts upsert); daily at 00:15; monthly close on day 1 applies the month's rate-card version, computes idle = bound capacity − Σ attributed (explicit overhead line per §0.7.6 #7), freezes the month (later-arriving samples land in a correction bucket, flagged in reports).
- **Alert engine (SVC-11 evaluator loop):** subscribes to the event bus (`orch.run.failed`, `ops.quota.warning|breach`, M05 `data.quality_status.changed` demotions to `quarantined|rejected`, M13 `mlops.eval.regression_detected`, M16 `orch.approval.requested` age sweep) + a 60 s timer sweep for window/age conditions; matches enabled rules by scope; transitions `ops.alert_firings`; on fire/resolve calls **M20-4** with rule, severity, labels, summary (delivery/dedup/routing = M20). Engine restart-safe: firing state persisted.
- **Borrow-reclaim sweep (SVC-11, every 60 s):** when a project's guaranteed capacity is requested but unavailable because siblings borrowed it, select borrowed reservations flagged `preemptible_for_reclaim` ordered by service-class weight ascending then most-recent admission (Kueue fair-sharing candidate ordering per §0.7.6 #5), and emit `ops.quota.reclaim_requested` events; M16/SVC-10 responds by pausing/requeueing those runs at the next safe task boundary (checkpoint-aware, §0.7.6 #12). `incident_response` runs are never reclaim targets.
- **Release-plan dimension derivation (FR-D4.6):** a nightly job joins run params (`model_candidate`) against M14 release-candidate lineage events to tag consumption/rollup rows with `release_plan_ref`; runs without a linked open RC stay untagged (reported as "no release plan").
- **Job-status projection consumer:** idempotent by event_id; lag monitored (lag > 60 s → WARN; board shows freshness).
- **Retention (DA-11):** consumption partitions 24 months hot then archived to object storage (metadata-retained); rollups retained indefinitely (small); admission decisions 6 months; incidents retained per tenant audit policy (BR-14); job_status rows follow M16 run retention.

#### 2.17.5 Permission Design

| Permission key | PA | TA | OE | RD (default) | Persona templates |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `ops.quota:read` | ✓ | ✓ (tenant) | ✓ (platform) | ✓ (own project allocation/usage only) | Platform Operator; Program Manager (read) |
| `ops.quota:update` — M17-1 tenant ceilings | ✓ | — | ✓ (platform) | — | Platform Operator |
| `ops.quota:update` — M17-2 project sub-allocations | ✓ | ✓ (own tenant) | ✓ | — | Platform Operator |
| `ops.utilization:read` | ✓ | ✓ (tenant) | ✓ (platform) | ✓ (member-project consumption M17-4/M17-5 only) | Platform Operator; Program Manager |
| `ops.incident:create` / `ops.incident:update` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (platform) | — | Platform Operator |
| `ops.incident:read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (tenant) | all |
| `ops.alert_rule:create/update/delete` | ✓ | ✓ (tenant) | ✓ (platform) | — | Platform Operator |
| `ops.alert_rule:read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | all |
| `ops.admission:execute` / `ops.metering:write` | service identities only (M16/SVC-10, executors, SVC-11) | | | | — |

**Granularity & boundaries:** quota ceilings are platform-administered (a TA must not raise its own tenant's ceiling — separation of duty); sub-allocations tenant-administered; consumption visible to RD only for member projects (server-side filter). **OE persona rule (§0.5.3):** OE holds full `ops.*` platform-wide but **no business-resource content access** — job board and incidents show asset/run **ids and operational metadata only**; dereferencing an asset re-checks business-domain keys (deny for OE). **RD:** may execute runs within project quota (M16 `orch.run:execute` + M17 admission) but cannot see other projects' quotas or adjust priority. Quota-escalation approval requires `ops.quota:update` at the subject's scope (decided in M16-6, double-checked at decision time).

**Credential scoping:** **M17-9 admission and M17-10 metering are internal mTLS-only endpoints** — never exposed at the edge gateway (§1.4.1); executor service identities report consumption for their own tenant's runs only (SAN↔tenant check); SVC-11 holds `ops.metering:write` + event-bus consume; rate cards and cost data carry no credentials. Alert test-fires execute under the caller's user identity and are labeled `test=true` end-to-end.

#### 2.17.6 Logging Design

| Level | Triggers |
|---|---|
| DEBUG | admission cache hits, rollup batch sizes, rule-match evaluation detail (off in prod) |
| INFO | quota policy/sub-allocation versions created; admission verdicts (allow) with quota_remaining; reservation lifecycle; rollup job completion; incident transitions; alert fired/resolved `{rule_id, severity, fingerprint}` |
| WARN | **quota threshold ≥80% (warning_threshold) with `quota_remaining`**; admission deny/escalate (with reasons); borrowed-capacity reclaim triggered; cache↔DB drift >2%; projection lag >60 s; metering DLQ entry |
| ERROR | **task/job failure observed in projection with `failure_stage` + `failure_category` (FR-D4.2)**; rollup job failure; alert engine cannot deliver to M20-4 (after retries); reservation leak detected |
| FATAL | admission store unavailable (M16 launches failing closed platform-wide) |

**JSON fields (per §1.4.6 +):** `run_id`, `task_id`, `attempt`, `queue` (`{workload_class}/{service_class}`), `decision` (verdict), `quota_remaining`, `reservation_id`, `resource_class`, `policy_id`, `rule_id`, `incident_id`, `error_code`. **Masking (§1.4.7):** consumption attribution and admission logs reference campaigns/models/datasets **by id only — never names or payloads** (params may reference restricted datasets); cost amounts logged at INFO only in aggregate (per-record cost stays in the DB); incident free-text summaries logged by `incident_id` reference.

**Shipping:** stdout JSON → OTel collector pipeline (scrubbing per §0.7.6 #14); `traceparent` continuity M16-2 → M17-9 → reservation → M17-10 samples (one launch = one trace). Metering ingestion logs one INFO line per batch, never per record.

**Audit events (M03-11):** **`ops.quota.updated`** `{policy_id|project_id, version, before/after summary}` (FR-D5.4 governance surface), **`ops.priority.adjusted`** `{run_id, from, to, actor}` (catalog owner; emitted at M16-4, also logged here on policy evaluation), **`ops.incident.created`** (+ `ops.incident.resolved`), `ops.alert_rule.created/updated/deleted`, `ops.admission.denied` / `ops.admission.escalated` `{project, resource_class, requested, remaining}` (BR-12 evidence trail), `ops.quota.warning` / `ops.quota.breach` (threshold crossings and hard-cap denials, AC-13/RPT-10 traceability).

### 2.18 M18 — Analytics & Mining

#### 2.18.1 Module Overview

**Module ID & name:** M18 — Analytics & Mining (**mandated module**, fourth platform-management mandate item; cross-cutting group).

M18 is the platform's *computation layer over governed data* (DA-15, §1.6.2): it answers "how do I find it, what does it look like, and what is missing" across every asset family that the data factory, simulation factory, and ModelOps modules produce. It implements the three mandated capability pillars — **(1) hybrid scalar-vector retrieval**, **(2) data visualization aggregates**, **(3) data distribution computation & inspection (tag/attribute + high-dimensional clustering)** — plus the cohort, coverage, and clustering services the rest of the platform contracts against (M18-7…M18-11). Architecture follows §0.7.4: filterable-HNSW vector store with payload-indexed scalar filtering (#2), pre-filter/filtered-ANN strategy selection by selectivity (#1), RRF/weighted fusion (#3), tiered index policy by collection size (#6), FiftyOne-Brain-style embedding atlas with lasso-to-grid linking (#7), NeMo-Curator-style embed→cluster→dedup mining jobs (#8), and HDBSCAN noise points surfaced as edge-case candidates (#9).

**Owns:** Embedding Spaces and Embedding & Vector Index entries (Table 0.3 #30, `emb-{asset_id}:{model}:{version}`); embedding (re)index jobs; the hybrid retrieval engine (M18-1/M18-2) and its query planner; Distribution Jobs & Distribution Reports (Table 0.3 #31, `dst-`); render-ready visualization aggregates (trajectory overlays, sensor heatmaps, temporal sequences — M18-6); Data Cohorts (+ versions, member manifests — Table 0.3 #29, `coh-`, FR-A7.4); Coverage Summaries and gap analysis (FR-A7.6, RPT-03, M18-9/M18-10); the generic internal clustering service (M18-7, FR-C6.3 support for M15); index maintenance ingestion (M18-11, fed by SVC-04).

**Excludes:** the **governed catalog of record, governance flags, and usage gates** (M08 — DA-15/§1.6.2: M08 owns *what exists and who may use it*; M18 owns *how to find/understand it*; every M18 result list is re-checked against M08 governance before serving, deny-wins); **episode playback and synchronized 4D review** (M19 — §1.6.4 split: M18 *computes* render-ready aggregates, M19 *renders* synchronized playback); source-asset truth (M05/M06/M07/M08/M10/M13/M15 — M18 holds rebuildable derived projections only and never mutates a source row); annotation task creation (M07-3 — cohort materialization *calls* it), snapshot drafts (M08-4), generation requests (M10-4), regression sets (M13-10), scenario requests (M09-6); failure-record lifecycle (M15 — M18-7 is stateless computation for M15-5); embedding model training/hosting (embedding generators are registered workflow job types per DA-13 analogy; the platform pins identity+version, it does not bundle models); compute admission (M17) and workflow execution (M16; executors SVC-04/SVC-05).

**Roles & personas:** all engineering personas use search/atlas/cohorts within their projects — Data Collection Lead (coverage vs. campaign goals, `coverage:read`), Robotics Data Engineer (mining, dedup verdicts, re-index), Annotation QA Lead (cohorts → M07 task batches), Simulation Engineer (gap → scenario/generation requests), Model Engineer & Evaluation Lead (similarity mining, failure clusters, regression cohorts); **Program Manager reads coverage** (RPT-03; persona template `report:read, coverage:read` — read-mostly, §0.2.4). Built-in roles per §1.4.8; OE has **no** M18 grants (business-data domain, §0.5.3). Service identities: M08 (delegated hybrid queries with acting-user context), M15 (M18-7), SVC-04 Embedding & Index Maintainer (M18-11), SVC-05 Distribution & Clustering Computer (job executor).

**Upstream:** M05 recordings / M06 episodes, segments, tags / M07 annotation sets / M08 snapshots, governance-flag change events / M10 synthetic batches / M13 evaluation outcomes / M15 failure facts — all via outbox events consumed by SVC-04 into M18-11 (§0.5.2 edges M06→M18, M08→M18); M06-5 step store + M05-12 streams as read sources for M18-6 aggregate computation; M01/M02/M03 (session, scope, authz/audit); M16/M17 (job workflows + admission).
**Downstream:** M04-5 (campaign coverage actuals), M07-3 (cohort → annotation tasks), M08-1 (delegated hybrid ranking) and M08-4 (cohort → snapshot draft members), M09-6 (gap → scenario requests, §0.5.2 edge M18→M09), M10-4 (cohort → generation requests), M13-10 (cohort → regression sets, edge M18→M13), M15-5 (cluster results), M19 (atlas/aggregate embeds; receives saved-view cohort handoffs), M20 (job completion notices).

**FRD/mandate coverage:** mandate pillars 1–3 (this section); FR-A7.4 (cohorts; §1.7.1 "A7.4 with M18"); FR-A7.6 + RPT-03 (coverage, §1.7.3); FR-C6.3 (clustering support via M18-7/M15-5); contributes to FR-A7.3 mining and BP-02 step 1 (gap discovery); AC-05 (hybrid search round-trip with M08-1); research basis §0.7.4 #1–#9.

#### 2.18.2 Functional & UI/UX Design

**Primary functions**

| # | Function | Description | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Hybrid scalar-vector retrieval | One query = scalar metadata filters (campaign, task, robot, environment, object, modality, quality/annotation status, dataset membership, tags) ∧ vector similarity (text-to-embedding or exemplar-asset); strategy = pre-filter vs filtered-ANN vs iterative filtering chosen by selectivity estimate; scalar+vector ranking fused via RRF or weighted normalization | Mandate #1; §0.7.4 #1/#2/#3 |
| F2 | Similarity search | "More like this" by exemplar episode/segment/frame; vector-only ranking with optional scalar guardrail filters | Mandate #1 |
| F3 | Embedding space registry & index jobs | Registered embedding models per modality family (video-clip/text joint space, trajectory-state space, image space); (re)index jobs pinning model identity+version; tiered index policy (HNSW hot ≤ ~100M / DiskANN-class disk-resident cold / IVF-PQ heavily-filtered archival) selected per space by size/SLA | Mandate #1; §0.7.4 #6 |
| F4 | Tag/attribute distributions | Counts/histograms by labels, scenario tags, structured metadata with drill-down to asset lists | Mandate #3a |
| F5 | Embedding distributions | Projection (UMAP/t-SNE) + clustering (HDBSCAN/k-means) over embedding sets; cluster cards, density layers; **HDBSCAN noise points flagged as edge-case candidates** | Mandate #3b; §0.7.4 #7/#9 |
| F6 | Visualization aggregates | Render-ready trajectory overlays (end-effector/joint paths), sensor heatmaps (tactile/force grids, joint utilization), temporal multi-series charts; consumed by M18 pages and M19 panes (M19 renders playback — §1.6.4) | Mandate #2 |
| F7 | Cohorts | Reusable, versioned asset selections from search results / atlas lasso / upstream refs; materialized into annotation tasks, dataset members, simulation/generation requests, regression sets | FR-A7.4 |
| F8 | Coverage summaries | Dataset coverage by task, scenario, environment, object, robot, failure category, real/synthetic composition | FR-A7.6, RPT-03 |
| F9 | Gap analysis | Coverage actuals vs campaign goals (M04-4) and scenario plans (M09); sparse-cluster/noise findings; handoff to M09-6/M04-5 | FR-A7.6; BP-02 |
| F10 | Clustering service | Generic attribute/embedding clustering for internal callers (M15-5 failure clustering: task, object, action phase, scenario, model output) | FR-C6.3 |
| F11 | Index maintenance | Upsert/delete of vectors + scalar payloads from source-module events; payload fields registered before bulk ingest | §0.7.4 #2; SVC-04 |

**UI pages.** All pages are project-scoped (project switcher in header), follow §1.4 envelope/error conventions, and share these output/exception rules: success → rendered results + toast where a resource was created; **empty → "No assets match — relax filters"** (with one-click "clear vector query" / "clear filters" chips); loading → skeleton grid/scatter; network failure → retry banner preserving query state; 403 `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` → page-level read-only notice; 403 `E_RESTRICTED_DATA` never renders raw — **restricted results are excluded server-side and the grid shows a count notice "N restricted assets excluded from results"** (deny-wins, §0.5.3); 429 `E_RATE_LIMITED` → countdown honoring `Retry-After`; 422 `E_VALIDATION` → field-anchored messages; 503 `E_UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE` (ANN backend) → degraded banner "similarity ranking unavailable — scalar filtering still active".

**P1 — Hybrid Search** (binding `M18-1`, `M18-2`; route `/analytics/search`). Layout: left **filter rail** (facet groups with live counts: campaign, task, robot/embodiment, environment, object class, modality, quality status, annotation status, dataset membership, scenario tags, synthetic/real, custom tags), top **query box** with **similarity toggle** (`Scalar only | Text similarity | Exemplar`), embedding-space selector, **fusion weight slider** (alpha), result **grid with governance badges** (RESTRICTED-redaction never occurs here — excluded with count; DEPRECATED grey-strike, SUPERSEDED arrow, SYNTHETIC permanent badge per BR-03) plus per-item score popover (`scalar_score / vector_score / fused_score`, strategy chip). Row actions: open in M19 (`wbs-` session), open in M08 catalog detail, add to cohort draft. Bulk: "Save as cohort" → P4 dialog.

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| query_text | text | UTF-8 | — | "robot fumbles drawer handle in dim light" | **max 1024 chars**; required iff mode=text |
| mode | toggle | `scalar_only \| text \| exemplar` | scalar_only | — | — |
| exemplar | asset picker | `epi-/seg-/rec-@frame` | — | "Pick an exemplar episode/segment/frame" | required iff mode=exemplar; must be indexed in chosen space (`E_ANA_EXEMPLAR_NOT_INDEXED`) |
| embedding_space | select | registered spaces (M18-3) | project default space | — | required unless scalar_only; space must be `ready` (`E_ANA_SPACE_NOT_READY`) |
| fusion.method | select | `rrf \| weighted` | rrf | — | — |
| fusion.alpha | slider | float **0.0–1.0** step 0.05 | **0.5** | — | weighted mode only (0 = pure scalar, 1 = pure vector) |
| k | number | int **1–500** | **50** | — | integer in range |
| filters.* | facet pickers | closed FR-A7.2 facet set + tags | none | — | keys must be registered payload fields (`E_ANA_FILTER_UNINDEXED`) |

Validation logic: mode switch clears the inapplicable input; alpha slider disabled under RRF (tooltip "RRF is rank-based"); filter chips render removable with per-facet counts; the response's `strategy` block renders as an explainability chip ("pre-filtered 1,204 candidates → exact scan" / "filtered ANN, ef=256"); `index_freshness_at` older than 120 s renders a staleness marker.

Interaction flow & bindings:
1. Facet rail hydrates from cached facet statistics (same source as the selectivity estimator); counts refresh after each query.
2. Submit (debounced 400 ms on filter changes; explicit button for vector queries) → `M18-1`; exemplar picks route through `M18-2` when no scalar relevance is requested.
3. Result grid renders 50/page with `next_page_token` infinite scroll; per-item governance badges come from the response `governance` block — the UI never fetches M08 per row.
4. "Save as cohort" collects the current result id set (≤ 500k guard client-side) → P4 dialog → `M18-8 POST` with `definition.kind=search_spec` carrying the exact M18-1 request snapshot for reproducibility.
5. Score popover renders the fused/scalar/vector triple and the rank — `fused_score` is the sort key; ties broken by `vector_score` then recency.

**P2 — Embedding Atlas** (bindings `M18-4` analysis_type=embedding_distribution, `M18-5`; route `/analytics/atlas`). **Projection scatter** (WebGL, 50k points max per view; server-side sampling beyond) with **lasso selection** linked live to a result grid (lasso → grid filter, §0.7.4 #7); **cluster panel** (cluster cards: size, label suggestion from top tags, cohesion, exemplar thumbnails; **noise card** labeled "unclustered / edge-case candidates" per §0.7.4 #9); **density layer** toggle (hex-bin underlay); color-by selector (cluster, task, quality_status, synthetic, campaign, eval outcome). Lasso selections stay **client-side until saved as a cohort** (P4 dialog → M18-8 with the selected `asset_refs` + projection provenance).

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| scope | radio + picker | `filters \| cohort_ref (coh-) \| snapshot_ref (dss-)` | filters | "Choose what to project" | ref resolvable in project |
| embedding_space | select | registered spaces | project default | — | must be `ready` |
| projection.method | select | **enum `umap \| tsne`** | umap | — | — |
| projection.n_neighbors | number | int 2–200 | 15 | — | umap only |
| projection.min_dist | number | float 0.0–0.99 | 0.1 | — | umap only |
| projection.perplexity | number | int 5–50 | 30 | — | tsne only; < sample size/3 |
| clustering.algorithm | select | `hdbscan \| kmeans \| none` | hdbscan | — | — |
| clustering.min_cluster_size | number | int 5–10000 | 25 | — | hdbscan only |
| clustering.k | number | int 2–500 | — | "k" | kmeans only, required |
| sample_limit | number | int 1k–500k | 100k | — | downsampling note shown when scope exceeds limit |

Submit → `202` job chip with progress (queued/running); completed jobs hydrate from the **projection cache** instantly when `(scope-version, space, method, params)` hash matches a prior run (§2.18.4). Cluster card click → grid filter; "Send cluster to cohort" / "Send noise to cohort" actions.

Interaction flow & bindings: 1. submit → `M18-4 POST` (analysis_type `embedding_distribution`) → job chip polls `M18-4 GET` every 3 s (backoff to 15 s after 1 min); 2. `succeeded` → `M18-5 GET /visualization-data` → Arrow IPC payload streamed into the WebGL scatter; 3. lasso → client-side point-id set → grid filters locally (no server call); 4. "Save lasso as cohort" → P4 dialog → `M18-8 POST` with `definition.kind=lasso_selection` `{distribution_report_ref, point_count}` + the resolved `member_refs`; 5. payload URL expiry mid-session → silent re-fetch of `M18-5` (signed URL re-mint).
