Processing: 1. authz (transitive); asset readable; taxonomy path valid at pinned version (M06-9). 2. `existing_failure_id` null → **call M15-1 in user context** (idempotent by propagated key): `{source: "workbench", source_evidence: {asset_ref, anchor, session_ref}, category, severity, note}` → `fal-` id; else verify the existing record is in-project. 3. **Tx:** insert `workbench.failure_tag` link record (`ftg-{ulid}`: asset_ref, anchor, `fal-` ref, actor) — commit (M15 owns the failure record; M19 owns only the visual-anchor link, §1.6.4/§1.6.5). 4. Audit **`workbench.failure_tag.created`** `{asset_ref, failure_id}`; **201** `{"failure_tag_id": "ftg-01J...", "failure_record_ref": "fal-01J..."}` — timeline gains a failure tick; M15 triage proceeds in M15 (BP-05).

**M19-7 `POST /api/v1/projects/{project_id}/saved-views` · `GET` (same) · `GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/saved-views/{view_id}`** — saved views & cohort handoff (FR-X1.7).
Permissions: create `workbench.view:create`; read own + project-shared `workbench.view:read`; PATCH/DELETE owner (`workbench.view:update/delete`); share flag change requires `workbench.view:share`.
POST body: `{ "name": "drawer-failures-low-light", "source": { "kind": "session_snapshot", "session_ref": "wbs-01J..." }, "state": { "source_refs": ["epi-..."], "blueprint": "teleop_qa@2", "timeline_ns": 41200000000, "overlays": {...}, "selection_refs": ["epi-...", "epi-..."] }, "shared": false }`.
Processing: 1. authz; name unique per owner+project (`E_CONFLICT`); state validated (refs resolvable — stale refs accepted at save but flagged); 2. **Tx:** insert `workbench.saved_view` (`viw-{ulid}`) — commit; **201**. GET list (`?shared&owner&source_kind&page_*`) →

```json
{ "items": [ { "saved_view_id": "viw-01J...", "name": "drawer-failures-low-light",
    "source": { "kind": "session_snapshot", "source_kind": "episode", "refs": ["epi-01J..."] },
    "blueprint": "teleop_qa@2", "shared": true, "stale": false, "owner": "usr-...",
    "selection_count": 14, "updated_at": "..." } ], "next_page_token": null, "total_size": 6 }
```

Open flow re-validates refs (`E_WB_VIEW_REF_STALE` 409 with superseding-ref hint where M08 `superseded_by` exists). PATCH rename/state/shared (If-Match); setting `shared=true` audits **`workbench.view.shared`** `{view_id, scope: project}`. DELETE soft (tombstone, 30 d undo). **Cohort handoff:** the gallery's "Create cohort" action calls **M18-8 in the acting user's context** with `definition.upstream_ref = {type: "saved_view", ref: "viw-..."}` + `member_refs = state.selection_refs` — M18 owns the cohort from then on (dataset creation / simulation requests / regression sets proceed via M18-8 `:materialize`, X1.7); the view stores the resulting `coh-` ref for round-trip navigation.

**Domain events:** `workbench.session.opened|expired`, `workbench.review.decision_recorded|decision_delivered`, `workbench.failure_tag.created`, `workbench.comment.created`, `workbench.view.shared` — consumed by M20 (notifications), M07/M13/M15 reconciliation views, RPT-09 joins (decisions feeding the closed loop).

#### 2.19.4 Data Design

**Storage:** PostgreSQL schema `workbench.*`. M19 stores **no asset content** — only session descriptors, anchors, decisions, links, and view states; all heavy data is referenced by signed URLs minted by owning modules. **Sessions are ephemeral (TTL 72 h); decisions, comments, failure-tag links, and saved views are durable** (governance record, BR-14).

`workbench.session` — state `active → expired` (exact); **partitioned by week** (high-churn, GC'd)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| workbench_session_id | text PK | **`wbs-{ulid}`** (`ses-` is reserved for M01 login sessions, Table 0.3 #27 — never used here) |
| tenant_id / project_id | text | NOT NULL; idx (project_id, created_by, status) |
| source_kind | enum | exact FR-X1.2 6-value set |
| source_refs / overlays | jsonb / jsonb | 1–4 primary refs; overlay refs validated at open |
| blueprint | text | `template@version`, pinned at open |
| queue | jsonb? | `{ref, cursor, total}` for X1.6 queue mode |
| status | enum(active, expired) | exact machine; expired is terminal |
| status_context | jsonb | X1.3 field snapshot + `refreshed_at` (≤ 60 s cache) |
| created_by / created_at / last_activity_at / expires_at | text / timestamptz ×3 | TTL = last_activity_at + 72 h |

`workbench.review_decision` — **immutable rows** (no UPDATE except the routing columns); durable (BR-14)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| review_decision_id | text PK | `rvd-{ulid}` |
| session_ref / tenant_id / project_id | text | session may be expired/GC'd later — ids retained |
| decision_type / verdict | enum(annotation_qa, eval_failure_triage) / enum per type | `E_WB_DECISION_TYPE_INVALID` guard |
| target_ref | text | `ant-/qar-/ans-/evr-#failure/fal-`; idx |
| rationale / anchor | text ≤ 4 KB / jsonb | anchor snapshot (ts range, entity_path) |
| failure_category / severity / duplicate_of | jsonb? / enum? / text? | conditional per verdict (P3 rules) |
| **routed_status** | enum(pending, delivered) | exact machine; partial idx WHERE pending |
| delivery_attempts / delivery_rejected / target_result_ref | int / bool / text? | saga bookkeeping; rejected = target 4xx (decision stands as audit fact) |
| created_by / created_at / delivered_at | text / timestamptz ×2 | — |

| Entity | Key attributes | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| `workbench.comparison_session` | `cps-{ulid}` PK, target jsonb, candidates jsonb[2..4], delta_summary jsonb (M13-7 snapshot, recomputable), status, TTL fields as session | same weekly partitioning + state machine as session |
| `workbench.comment` | `cmt-{ulid}` PK, asset_ref, anchor jsonb (ts/range, entity_path, spatial), session_ref?, body text ≤ 8 KB, issue_links jsonb, mentions text[], edit_history jsonb, tombstoned_at?, created_by/at | idx (asset_ref, (anchor->>'ts_ns')); durable |
| `workbench.failure_tag` | `ftg-{ulid}` PK, asset_ref, anchor jsonb, failure_record_ref (`fal-`), session_ref?, created_by/at | unique (asset_ref, failure_record_ref, anchor-hash); durable link record — M15 owns the failure |
| `workbench.saved_view` | `viw-{ulid}` PK (Table 0.3 #31), name, source jsonb, state jsonb (refs, blueprint, timeline_ns, overlays, selection_refs), shared bool, stale bool, owner, version int (If-Match), tombstoned_at? | unique (project_id, owner, name); durable; **never stores URLs or content** |
| `workbench.blueprint_template` | `template@version` PK, source_kind, pane layout jsonb (panes → entity-path bindings) | seeded + release-managed (blueprint-as-code, §0.7.4 #10) |

**Processing logic.**
- **Session GC:** sweeper expires sessions where `last_activity_at + 72 h < now` (state `active → expired`, exact machine); expired partitions dropped after 30 days (descriptor metadata only — decisions/comments/tags reference sessions by id and survive via their own rows; DA-11 `purged-metadata-retained` applies to the session id trail in audit).
- **Routing relay:** outbox consumer delivers pending `review_decision` intents (M19-5 step 3) with full-jitter retry; idempotent by decision id; `delivered` flips exactly once; pending > 1 h raises an M17-visible alert.
- **Status-context refresh:** banner block re-resolved from M08-2/M12/M13 on manifest fetch with a 60 s cache — the banner is never staler than a minute (X1.3).
- **View staleness check:** weekly sweep marks saved views whose `source_refs` no longer resolve (purged/superseded) → `stale` flag (gallery chip; `E_WB_VIEW_REF_STALE` on open).
- **Retention (DA-11/BR-14):** decisions, failure-tag links, comments, shared saved views retained indefinitely (audit-relevant); private views archivable per tenant policy; comparison delta snapshots follow session retention (recomputable from M13-7).

#### 2.19.5 Permission Design

| Permission key | PA | TA | OE | RD | Personas / notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `workbench.session:create` (M19-1/2/3) | ✓ | ✓ (tenant) | — | ✓ (assigned projects) | all engineering personas; **always conjunct with per-ref asset read checks** |
| `workbench.comment:create` (M19-4) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | all engineering personas; edit/delete author-only (TA moderation with reason, audited) |
| `workbench.review:decide` (M19-5) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ + persona template (Annotation QA Lead: `qa:*`; Evaluation Lead: `failure:triage`) **+ transitive target key** | deny in M07/M13/M15 ⇒ deny here (BR-13; §1.6.4) |
| `workbench.failure_tag:create` (M19-6) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ **+ transitive `failure.record:create`** | AC-11 path |
| `workbench.view:create/read/update/delete` (M19-7) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | read covers own + project-shared |
| `workbench.view:share` (M19-7 PATCH shared) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | share audited (`workbench.view.shared`) |

**Granularity:** project-level; every session/manifest/comment/decision re-derives tenant/project from its refs and re-checks (§1.4.1). **Restricted assets are excluded from timeline manifests — deny-wins (§0.5.3):** per-stream restriction gates are enforced by the *owning* modules at URL-mint time (M05-12/M06-5 re-check) **and** by M19 at manifest assembly (excluded streams + count notice; primary refs the user cannot read block session creation outright). Grants are re-evaluated on every manifest fetch, not just at session open — revocation takes effect within one URL TTL. OE has no workbench access (business data, §0.5.3). Governance Approvers get read-only review (no decide/tag keys).

**Credential scoping:** **signed stream URLs are short-lived (TTL ≤ 600 s) and minted only by owning modules** — M19 never stores, re-signs, or extends them (manifest `expires_at` = min stream expiry; refresh = full re-authorization); manifests are per-user responses, never shared artifacts (sharing a session link triggers the recipient's own authz + URL minting); saved views store **refs and layout state only — never URLs or content**; comparison delta snapshots store metric numbers, not artifacts. Decision/tag routing always propagates the acting user (service identity never substitutes — FR-X1.8 attribution).

#### 2.19.6 Logging Design

| Level | Triggers |
|---|---|
| DEBUG | per-stream resolution timing, blueprint resolution, clock-mapping detail (off in prod) |
| INFO | session opened/expired `{source_kind, ref_count}`; **manifest served `{stream_count, excluded_restricted_count, sync_quality_summary, latency_ms}`**; comment created `{comment_id}`; decision recorded `{decision_type, verdict}` + delivered `{target_result_ref, attempts}`; failure tag created `{failure_record_ref}`; view saved/shared |
| WARN | partial manifest (unaligned streams excluded, `sync_quality=approximate` fraction); status-context refresh failure (stale banner served with `refreshed_at`); routing retry `{attempt, backoff_s}`; delivery_rejected by target (4xx, with target error code); signed-URL refresh storm (> 10/min/session); stale saved-view open |
| ERROR | `E_WB_STREAMS_UNALIGNED` (no common timeline); routing exhausted retries (decision `pending` > threshold — saga alarm to M17/M20); M05-12/M06-5/M13 upstream unavailable (`E_UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE`); session GC failure |
| FATAL | routing relay cannot persist delivery state (relay halted to avoid duplicate side effects) |

**JSON fields (per §1.4.6 shared schema +):** `session_id` (`wbs-`/`cps-`), `source_kind`, `stream_count`, `excluded_restricted_count`, `manifest_latency_ms`, `decision_id`, **`decision_type`**, `verdict`, `routed_status`, `target_ref`, `failure_record_ref`, `view_id`, `error_code`.

**Masking (§1.4.7):** **comment bodies and decision rationales are never logged — referenced by `cmt-`/`rvd-` id only** (PII-bearing free text rule); signed URLs never logged (URL-mint events log `{stream_key, expires_at}` only); status-banner contents logged as field-presence booleans, not values; mention user ids at DEBUG only; spatial anchors logged as anchor-hash.

**Shipping:** stdout JSON → OTel pipeline; `traceparent` spans UI → M19 → M05-12/M06-5 manifest resolution and M19 → M07/M13/M15 routing calls, so a review decision is traceable from scrubber to the owning module's audit record (one trace per FR-X1.8 governance fact). Operational metrics exported to the platform pipeline (M17-8 visibility): manifest p95 latency (SLO ≤ 2 s for ≤ 12 streams), routing delivery lag (SLO ≤ 60 s p95, alert > 1 h), session GC backlog, signed-URL refresh rate.

**Audit events (M03-11; FR-X1.8 — all status-affecting workbench activity):** **`workbench.session.opened`** `{source_kind, refs, restricted}` (content-access trail incl. restricted assets, FR-A8.8 alignment); **`workbench.review.decided`** `{decision_type, verdict, target_ref, routed_status}` and `workbench.review.delivered` `{target_result_ref}`; **`workbench.failure_tag.created`** `{asset_ref, failure_record_ref}`; `workbench.comment.linked` `{issue_link kinds}` (issue links create actionable work); **`workbench.view.shared`** `{view_id, scope}`; `workbench.comment.moderated` (TA deletion with reason). Downstream effects are *additionally* audited by the owning modules (M07 adjudication, M15 record creation) under their own actions — double-entry across the §1.6.4 boundary, with the `rvd-`/`ftg-` id linking both sides.

### 2.20 M20 — Notification & Alerting

#### 2.20.1 Module Overview

**Module ID & name:** M20 — Notification & Alerting.

**Scope boundary.**
- *Owns:* the in-app notification center and the email channel (DA-14); per-user notification preferences (event type × channel matrix, digests); notification fan-in from the platform event bus (M20-3) and from the M17 alert engine (M20-4); audience resolution, deduplication, grouping, severity routing, and inhibition (Alertmanager pattern per §0.7.6); delivery attempts, retries, and delivery-status records.
- *Excludes:* **alert rule definitions and evaluation** (M17 — M20 receives already-fired alerts and only decides who/how/when to deliver, FR-D4.4 split per §1.7.1); business event production (owning modules emit via their outboxes); OTP/login mail (M01/SVC-14 — separate pipeline, never mixed with notifications); audit logging of business decisions (M03); SMTP/ESP infrastructure operation (DA-17 — M20 records send status only).

**User roles & personas.** All roles consume the notification center; every authenticated user manages only their own preferences. Platform Operator/OE are the primary *audience* of critical operational alerts (quota breaches, workflow failures); Governance Approvers receive approval-assignment notifications (M16-6); requesters receive decision outcomes. No role administers other users' inboxes.

**Upstream/downstream.** Upstream: all modules via outbox events on the platform event bus (workflow failures, quality blocks, approval requests/decisions, evaluation regressions, release blockers — §0.5.2 last edge); M17 alert engine via M20-4 (FR-D4.4 delivery); M03 (audience resolution: role bindings at scope via M03-10 batch facilities; user status); M01 (session context for self-scoped reads); SVC-12 Notification & Alert Dispatcher (delivery worker). Downstream: users (in-app + email per DA-14); M03-11 (audit of preference changes); operational metrics back to M17 dashboards (delivery failure rates).

**FRD coverage:** FR-D4.4 (delivery half — alert channels and prompt surfacing; rules/evaluation in M17), DA-14 (in-app + email, per-user preferences), supporting role for FR-D2.3/D2.6 (approval assignment/decision notifications), FR-D3.4 (quota warning delivery), BP-06 step 4 (operator awareness). Research basis per §0.7.6: Alertmanager dedup/grouping/severity-routing/inhibition (#14), transactional outbox + idempotent consumers (#9), full-jitter retry with capped attempts and DLQ (#10), circuit breaker on the email relay (#11).

#### 2.20.2 Functional & UI/UX Design

**Primary functions.**
1. Maintain a per-user in-app notification inbox with unread tracking (DA-14).
2. Deliver email for event types/severities the user (or platform policy) has enabled, via the corporate SMTP/ESP relay (DA-17).
3. Fan in (a) domain events from module outboxes (M20-3) and (b) fired alerts from the M17 engine (M20-4); resolve audiences (explicit users, roles at scope); apply per-user preferences.
4. **Deduplicate** repeats within a window (default 15 min: same `dedup_key` increments a count instead of creating a new row), **group** related notifications for email batching (`group_key` = tenant × event family × severity; `group_wait` 30 s, `group_interval` 5 min), **route by severity** (critical → immediate email + in-app regardless of digest settings; warning → per preferences; info → in-app only by default), and **inhibit** lower-severity alerts on the same subject while a higher-severity alert is firing — the Alertmanager pattern (per §0.7.6).
5. Per-user preference matrix: event type × channel (in_app, email) + digest mode, with platform-locked rows that cannot be disabled (approval assignments, security/session notices).
6. Record every delivery attempt with outcome for operability (failure rates feed M17 dashboards).

**Notification event-type catalog (v1; grouped into preference categories).**

| Category | Event types (examples) | Default in_app / email | Locked |
|---|---|---|---|
| workflow | `orch.run.failed`, `orch.run.succeeded` (own runs), `orch.run.cancelled` | ✓ / failed-only | — |
| approvals | `orch.approval.requested` (assigned), `orch.approval.decided` (requester) | ✓ / ✓ | **requested = locked** |
| quota | `ops.quota.warning`, `ops.quota.breach`, `orch.approval.decided` (subject `quota_escalation`) | ✓ / breach-only | — |
| data_quality | `data.quality_status.changed` — demotions to `quarantined|rejected` (M05) | ✓ / — | — |
| evaluation | `mlops.eval.regression_detected` (M13) | ✓ / ✓ (leads) | — |
| release | approval-blocker alerts via M20-4 (M17 alert-rule type `approval_blocker`, fed by the SVC-11 age sweep over `orch.approval.requested`) | ✓ / ✓ | — |
| incident | `ops.incident.created/resolved` (targets) | ✓ / critical-only | — |
| work | `orch.work_request.submitted` (triagers), task assignments (M07) | ✓ / — | — |
| security | session/admin notices (M01/M03) | ✓ / ✓ | **locked** |

**Severity routing matrix (Alertmanager-style, per §0.7.6; applied after preference filtering).**

| Severity | In-app | Email | Digest eligibility | Dedup window | Grouping |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| critical | always (preference cannot mute platform-critical: quota breach affecting own project, incident assigned, locked categories) | immediate, bypasses digest and `min_email_severity` | never digested | 15 min, re-notify on `repeat_interval` 4 h | group_wait 30 s only (no long batching) |
| warning | per matrix | per matrix ∧ `min_email_severity ≤ warning` | hourly/daily allowed | 15 min | group_wait 30 s, group_interval 5 min |
| info | per matrix (default on) | per matrix (default off) | hourly/daily allowed | 15 min | digest-preferred |

**State machine (exact).** Notification: `created → delivered | failed → read` (`delivered` when all enabled channels have a terminal-success attempt — in-app insert succeeds synchronously; `failed` when every enabled channel exhausted retries; `read` set by user action from `delivered`).

##### UI element 1 — Notification bell dropdown (persistent app header)

Bell icon with unread badge; dropdown lists the latest 20 notifications: severity dot, title, subject deep link, relative time, group count ("+4 similar"), per-row mark-read, "Mark all read", "View all" → full page.
- **API bindings:** `GET /api/v1/users/me/notifications?page_size=20` (M20-1) on open; badge from the same response's `unread_count`; `POST /api/v1/notifications/{notification_id}:read`; `POST /api/v1/users/me/notifications:read-all`.
- **Output rendering & polling:** the badge **polls M20-1 (`page_size=1`) every 30 s** while the app is focused; the dropdown fetches fresh on open; rows render `severity ∈ info|warning|critical` as neutral/amber/red dots; deep links route to the subject (run, approval, incident, report). Empty state: "You're all caught up."
- **Exception handling:** `E_UNAUTHENTICATED` → silent redirect per M01; network failure → badge keeps last value with stale indicator; `E_STATE_INVALID` on double-read → ignored (idempotent UI).

##### UI page 2 — Notification center, route `/notifications`

Full list with filters and infinite scroll.

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| status | segmented | `all\|unread\|read` | unread | — | — |
| category | multi-select | catalog categories | all | Filter by category | values from catalog |
| severity | multi-select | `info\|warning\|critical` | all | — | — |
| date range | date-range | RFC 3339 | last 30 d | — | ≤ 90 d (retention window) |

- **API binding:** M20-1 with query params `status`, `category`, `severity`, `created_after/before`, `page_token`. Row click marks read (`:read`) and navigates. **Polling: none** (manual refresh button + bell keeps live count). Grouped rows expand to show collapsed members with counts and first/last timestamps.
- **Exception handling:** `E_VALIDATION` (range > 90 d) → range picker error "Notifications are retained for 90 days."; `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` cannot occur (self-scope only).

##### UI page 3 — Preference matrix, route `/settings/notifications`

Matrix: rows = event categories (catalog), columns = **In-app**, **Email**, **Digest** (`immediate | hourly | daily`); plus a global field `min_email_severity`.

| Name | Type | Format | Default | Placeholder | Validation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| matrix[category].in_app | toggle | bool | per catalog | — | Locked rows render disabled-on (`E_NTF_PREF_LOCKED` server guard) |
| matrix[category].email | toggle | bool | per catalog | — | Same lock rule |
| matrix[category].digest | single-select | `immediate\|hourly\|daily` | immediate | — | Digest applies to non-critical email only (critical always immediate — severity routing) |
| min_email_severity | single-select | `info\|warning\|critical` | warning | — | — |

- **Validation logic:** locked rows (approvals-assigned, security) show a lock icon with tooltip "Required by platform policy"; client blocks edits, server re-validates. Save is a full-matrix PUT with `If-Match`.
- **API bindings:** `GET /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences`, `PUT` same (M20-2).
- **Output rendering:** save → toast "Preferences updated"; a banner notes "Critical alerts are always delivered immediately."
- **Exception handling:** `E_NTF_PREF_LOCKED` (422) → row reverts with explanatory toast; `E_NTF_UNKNOWN_EVENT_TYPE` (422) → matrix refetches catalog (stale client); `E_PRECONDITION_FAILED` → reload-merge prompt.

#### 2.20.3 Interface Design

Module error codes: `E_NTF_PREF_LOCKED` (422), `E_NTF_UNKNOWN_EVENT_TYPE` (422), `E_NTF_CHANNEL_UNSUPPORTED` (422), `E_NTF_AUDIENCE_UNRESOLVED` (422, internal callers only); plus shared §1.4.3 codes.

##### M20-1 Notification center reads & read-marking
- `GET /api/v1/users/me/notifications?status&category&severity&created_after&created_before&page_size&page_token`
- **Processing:** 1. self-scope: `user_id` from session headers only (no user param — IDOR-proof by construction). 2. Query `notify.notifications` partition-pruned by user + created_at; grouped rows return `group_count` and collapsed member ids. 3. Response:
```json
{"items": [{"notification_id": "ntf-01J...", "category": "quota", "event_type": "ops.quota.breach", "severity": "critical",
  "title": "GPU quota breached: prj-manipulation", "body_excerpt": "gpu_h100 at 100% of sub-allocation...",
  "subject_ref": {"type": "quota_policy", "id": "qta-01J..."}, "deep_link": "/ops/quotas?policy=qta-01J...",
  "group_count": 3, "status": "delivered", "read_at": null, "created_at": "ts"}],
 "next_page_token": null, "total_size": 124, "unread_count": 7}
```
- `POST /api/v1/notifications/{notification_id}:read` — ownership check (404 `E_NOT_FOUND` for others' rows — no existence leak); `delivered→read` (`read_at`); already-read → 200 no-op (idempotent). `POST /api/v1/users/me/notifications:read-all` — optional body `{category?, before?}`; single UPDATE; returns `{"marked": n}`.
- **Errors:** `E_VALIDATION` (range > retention). **Permission key:** authenticated session (self-scope); no admin read of foreign inboxes exists.

##### M20-2 Preferences
- `GET /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences` → current matrix + catalog (categories, event types, lock flags, defaults) so the UI is always schema-driven.
- `PUT /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences` — body:
```json
{"min_email_severity": "warning",
 "matrix": {"workflow": {"in_app": true, "email": false, "digest": "daily"},
            "approvals": {"in_app": true, "email": true, "digest": "immediate"},
            "quota": {"in_app": true, "email": true, "digest": "hourly"}}}
```
- **Processing:** 1. Validate categories/types against the catalog → `E_NTF_UNKNOWN_EVENT_TYPE`; channels ∈ {in_app, email} → `E_NTF_CHANNEL_UNSUPPORTED`. 2. Locked categories must remain enabled → `E_NTF_PREF_LOCKED` (422, details list offending rows). 3. Tx: upsert `notify.preferences` (one row per user × category), bump version; audit **`notify.preference.updated`** `{changed_categories, before/after summary}`. 4. 200 with effective matrix.
- **Idempotency/concurrency:** PUT full-replace idempotent; `If-Match` required (428 without). **Permission key:** `notify.preferences:update` (granted to every authenticated role for self only — PA cannot edit another user's matrix).

##### M20-3 (i) Event fan-in dispatch
- `POST /internal/v1/notifications:dispatch` (mTLS; outbox relays/bus consumers + module service identities).
- Request:
```json
{"event": {"event_id": "01J...", "event_type": "orch.approval.requested", "occurred_at": "ts", "tenant_id": "tnt-...",
           "project_id": "prj-...", "subject": {"type": "approval", "id": "apr-01J..."}, "trace_id": "..."},
 "audience": {"mode": "role_at_scope", "role_or_permission": "orch.approval:decide", "scope": {"type": "tenant", "id": "tnt-..."}, "user_ids": null},
 "rendering": {"template_key": "approval.requested", "title_params": {"subject_type": "quota_escalation"}, "body_params": {"project": "prj-...", "requested_by": "usr-..."}},
 "severity": "warning", "category": "approvals",
 "dedup": {"dedup_key": "approval/apr-01J...", "window_s": 900}, "group": {"group_key": null}}
```
- **Processing (numbered):**
  1. mTLS identity check; schema validation (`E_VALIDATION`); template_key must exist in the rendering catalog.
  2. **Idempotency:** unique `(event_id, audience_hash)` — duplicate dispatch of the same event returns the original result (`Idempotency-Replayed` semantics; outbox is at-least-once per §0.7.6 #9).
  3. **Audience resolution:** `mode=users` → verify active users; `mode=role_at_scope` → resolve via M03 role-binding query (batch), capped at 500 recipients (`E_NTF_AUDIENCE_UNRESOLVED` if empty and `require_audience=true`).
  4. Per recipient: load preferences; compute effective channels = (category matrix ∩ event channels-hint) ∪ severity override (critical forces in_app+email immediate); skip users with all channels off (recorded as `suppressed_pref`).
  5. **Dedup:** active row with same `(user_id, dedup_key)` inside `window_s` → increment `group_count`, refresh `last_seen_at`, **no new row, no new email** (re-notify only if severity escalated).
  6. Tx per batch: insert `notify.notifications` (status `created`; in-app visibility is the row itself — flips `delivered` immediately if in-app is the only channel); enqueue `notify.delivery_attempts` for email (grouped per `group_key` with `group_wait` 30 s / `group_interval` 5 min before SVC-12 renders one batched email); commit.
  7. SVC-12 delivers email (template render, SMTP relay per DA-17, circuit breaker per §0.7.6 #11); records attempt outcomes; notification → `delivered` on first channel-complete success, `failed` when all enabled channels exhaust retries.
  8. Response 202 `{"accepted": true, "recipients": 14, "created": 11, "deduplicated": 2, "suppressed_pref": 1}`.
- **Errors:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_NTF_UNKNOWN_EVENT_TYPE`, `E_NTF_AUDIENCE_UNRESOLVED`. **Permission key:** service-only `notify.dispatch:execute`.

##### M20-4 (i) Alert fan-in (from M17 rules)
- `POST /internal/v1/alerts:fire` (mTLS; SVC-11 alert engine only).
- Request:
```json
{"rule_id": "alr-01J...", "rule_type": "quota_breach", "status": "firing|resolved", "severity": "critical",
 "fingerprint": "sha256:...", "labels": {"tenant_id": "tnt-...", "project_id": "prj-...", "resource_class": "gpu_h100", "run_id": null},
 "summary": "gpu_h100 at 100% of sub-allocation for prj-manipulation", "description_ref": "alr-...#snapshot",
 "targets": [{"kind": "role", "ref": "persona.platform_operator", "scope": {"type": "tenant", "id": "tnt-..."}}],
 "fired_at": "ts", "test": false}
```
- **Processing:** 1. Identity check (SVC-11 SAN only). 2. **Alert dedup by `fingerprint`**: an active firing with the same fingerprint refreshes `last_seen` and increments count — no new notifications until `repeat_interval` (4 h default) elapses (Alertmanager semantics per §0.7.6). 3. **Inhibition:** while a `critical` firing exists for the same `{tenant, project, subject label-set}`, incoming `warning|info` alerts on that subject are recorded but **not delivered** (released if the critical resolves and they still fire). 4. **Grouping:** group_key = `(tenant_id, rule_type, severity)`; within `group_wait`/`group_interval`, members batch into one notification per recipient ("3 quota alerts in prj-…"). 5. Resolve targets → reuse the M20-3 pipeline (step 3 onward) with category mapped from rule_type and severity routing (critical → immediate email regardless of digest prefs). 6. `status=resolved` → mark firing resolved; for critical alerts send a follow-up "resolved" notification to the same audience. 7. `test=true` → deliver only to the invoking user, labeled TEST, skip dedup/inhibition state. 8. 202 `{"accepted": true, "deduplicated": bool, "inhibited": bool}`.
- **Errors:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_NOT_FOUND` (unknown rule_id — logged, alert still delivered to fallback OE audience to fail safe). **Permission key:** service-only `notify.alert_fire:execute`.

#### 2.20.4 Data Design

**Storage:** PostgreSQL schema `notify`; notifications are the highest-volume control-plane rows → `notify.notifications` **partitioned by month** (`created_at`) with `(user_id, status)` index per partition; Redis for dedup-window lookups and unread-count cache (TTL 60 s, invalidated on write).

**Entities (ER-style).**

`notify.notifications` — state `created → delivered | failed → read`
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| notification_id | text PK | `ntf-{ulid}` |
| user_id / tenant_id / project_id | text | user_id NOT NULL, indexed |
| category / event_type / severity | text / text / enum(info, warning, critical) | catalog-validated |
| title / body | text / text | rendered at creation; body ≤ 4 KB; asset references stored as ids + display names |
| subject_ref / deep_link | JSONB / text | `{type, id}` |
| dedup_key / group_key / group_count | text / text / int | unique partial `(user_id, dedup_key) WHERE status IN ('created','delivered') AND last_seen_at > now()-window` enforced in logic |
| source_event_id / alert_fingerprint | text / text | idempotent fan-in keys |
| status / read_at / created_at / last_seen_at | enum + timestamptz | exact state machine |

`notify.preferences` — per-user matrix (DA-14)
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| user_id + category | PK composite | category from `notify.catalog` |
| in_app / email | bool / bool | locked categories force `in_app=true` (server trigger) |
| digest | enum(immediate, hourly, daily) | applies to non-critical email only |
| min_email_severity | enum on the user-level row (`category='_global'`) | default `warning` |
| version / updated_at | int / timestamptz | optimistic locking for PUT |

`notify.delivery_attempts` — per-channel delivery ledger
| Attribute | Type | Constraints |
|---|---|---|
| notification_id + channel + attempt | PK composite | channel enum(email) — in-app has no attempts (row visibility = delivery) |
| status | enum(pending, sent, failed) | terminal failure after attempt 3 → notification `failed` |
| provider_message_id / error_code / attempted_at / next_retry_at | text / text / timestamptz / timestamptz | full-jitter retry: 30 s base, 10 min cap, ≤3 attempts (§0.7.6 #10), then DLQ |

`notify.catalog` — reference data: category, event_type, default channels, locked flag, template_key (seeded migration; extended only by platform releases). `notify.alert_firings_inbox` — fingerprint PK, rule_id, status firing|resolved, first/last_seen, count, inhibited_by (M20-side delivery state, distinct from M17's evaluation state). `notify.dispatch_idempotency` — `(event_id, audience_hash)` unique, response snapshot, 24 h TTL sweep.

**Processing logic.**
- **Dedup window:** Redis key `ntf:dedup:{user}:{dedup_key}` (TTL = window_s, default 900); hit → UPDATE group_count/last_seen on the existing row (no insert). DB unique-key fallback when Redis is cold (correctness never depends on cache).
- **Digest scheduler (SVC-12):** hourly/daily sweeps collect undelivered-by-email non-critical notifications per user with digest prefs, render one digest email, mark members' email attempts `sent(digest)`. Critical severity bypasses digests by routing rule.
- **Email worker (SVC-12):** consumes the delivery queue, applies group_wait/group_interval batching, circuit breaker on SMTP relay (open circuit → attempts deferred, WARN + M17 metric per §0.7.6 #11), SIGTERM drain requeues in-flight (per §0.7.6 #12).
- **Unread cache:** per-user counter maintained on insert/read; rebuilt lazily on miss.
- **Retention (DA-11):** notifications 90 days hot, then partition drop with monthly archival of aggregate counts only (no body archive — bodies are derivable from audit/source events); delivery attempts 30 days; dispatch idempotency 24 h; preference history retained via audit trail (BR-14).

#### 2.20.5 Permission Design

| Permission key | PA | TA | OE | RD | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| notification read / `:read` / `:read-all` (M20-1) | ✓ self | ✓ self | ✓ self | ✓ self | strictly self-scoped via session identity; no cross-user or admin inbox read exists |
| `notify.preferences:update` (M20-2) | ✓ self | ✓ self | ✓ self | ✓ self | locked categories enforced server-side for every role incl. PA |
| `notify.dispatch:execute` (M20-3) | service identities only | | | | outbox relays / bus consumers / module services (mTLS SAN allowlist) |
| `notify.alert_fire:execute` (M20-4) | service identity only | | | | SVC-11 alert engine exclusively |

**Granularity:** all user-facing surfaces are self-scoped — tenant isolation holds because notifications are created only from events already scoped to the recipient's tenants/projects (audience resolution via M03 happens at dispatch, not at read). **Content minimization for cross-boundary recipients:** OE recipients of operational alerts receive titles/bodies built from operational metadata and asset **ids** — alert templates never embed restricted dataset content or model payloads, so delivery cannot widen business-resource access (§0.5.3 OE rule). Deep links re-authorize on click (target module's own permission checks decide what renders).

**Credential scoping:** M20-3/M20-4 are internal mTLS endpoints, never edge-exposed (§1.4.1); SVC-12 holds SMTP relay credentials in the platform secret manager (never in `notify.*` rows or logs, §1.4.7); provider message ids are stored, provider API keys are not. Email rendering runs in SVC-12 with read access only to `notify.*` — it cannot dereference business assets, guaranteeing templates stay metadata-only.

#### 2.20.6 Logging Design

| Level | Triggers |
|---|---|
| DEBUG | audience resolution detail, template render timing, dedup cache hits (off in prod) |
| INFO | dispatch accepted `{event_type, recipients, created, deduplicated, suppressed_pref}`; alert fired/resolved/inhibited `{rule_id, fingerprint}`; digest batch sent `{user_count, notification_count}`; preference updated |
| WARN | email delivery retry `{attempt, backoff_s}`; SMTP circuit breaker open/half-open; dedup overflow (group_count > 100 — noisy source flagged toward M17); audience resolution empty; unread-cache rebuild |
| ERROR | delivery terminally failed (notification → `failed`, channel, error_code); template render failure (fallback plain-text used); dispatch idempotency conflict with differing payload; DLQ entry |
| FATAL | dispatch consumer cannot persist notifications (event-bus consumption halted to avoid loss) |

**JSON fields (per §1.4.6 shared schema +):** `notification_id`, `event_type`, `category`, `severity`, `rule_id`, `fingerprint`, `recipient_count` (aggregate — recipient user ids logged only at DEBUG), `channel`, `attempt`, `decision` (delivered|deduplicated|inhibited|suppressed_pref), `error_code`. **Masking (§1.4.7):** notification titles/bodies are **never logged** (they may name assets and people) — logs carry `notification_id` + `template_key` only; recipient emails masked `s***@domain` in delivery logs; alert label values logged as ids only (labels may reference restricted dataset/model ids — ids only, never payloads); SMTP credentials and provider responses logged as status codes only.

**Shipping:** stdout JSON → OTel collector pipeline (PII scrub per §0.7.6 #14); `traceparent` carried from the source event through dispatch to the delivery attempt, so one business event (e.g., `orch.run.failed`) is traceable from M16 outbox → bus → M20-3 → SVC-12 email send (§0.7.6 #13). Delivery metrics (success rate, latency, breaker state) exported to the platform metrics pipeline and surfaced on the M17-8 ops dashboard.

**Audit events (M03-11):** **`notify.preference.updated`** `{user_id, changed_categories, before/after summary}` (the only user-governance action M20 owns — inbox reads and read-marks are not audit-relevant; alert and dispatch activity is reconstructed from M17 audit events plus M20 operational logs).


---

## 3. Backend Service Designs

This chapter specifies the fifteen independent backend services (SVC-01…SVC-15) that execute the asynchronous, compute-heavy, and cross-cutting behaviors of the modules designed in Chapter 2. Scope discipline: services **implement module behaviors already defined in Chapter 2** — they add no business features, own no governed business entities (Table 0.3 single-writer rule holds), and cross module boundaries only through the §1.5 interface contract. Every service is bound by the global conventions of §1.4 (type-prefixed ULIDs, snake_case JSON, event envelope per §1.4.5, logging per §1.4.6, sensitive-data rules per §1.4.7) and by the reliability pattern pack of §0.7.6. Each service realizes one or more system actors of §0.4.1.

### 3.0 Common Service Framework

**3.0.1 Service roster and intake styles.**

| SVC | Name | Ch.1 alias | Serves | Primary work intake |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SVC-01 | Ingestion & Intake Validation Worker | SVC-INGEST | M05 | workflow-task executor + timer sweeps |
| SVC-02 | Alignment & Episode Builder | SVC-ALIGN | M06 | workflow-task executor |
| SVC-03 | Pre-annotation & Segmentation Suggestion Runner | SVC-PREANN | M07/M06 | workflow-task executor |
| SVC-04 | Embedding & Index Maintainer | SVC-INDEX | M18 | bus consumer + reindex-job executor |
| SVC-05 | Distribution & Clustering Computer | SVC-ANALYTICS | M18/M15 | job executor + internal-API compute backend |
| SVC-06 | Simulation & Synthetic Generation Bridge | SVC-SIM | M10 | workflow-task executor (external bridge) |
| SVC-07 | Training Executor Bridge | SVC-TRAIN | M11 | workflow-task executor (external bridge) |
| SVC-08 | Evaluation Executor | SVC-EVAL | M13 | workflow-task executor + bus adapter |
| SVC-09 | Packaging Worker | — | M14 | workflow-task executor |
| SVC-10 | Workflow Scheduler & Task Dispatcher | SVC-SCHED | M16 | scheduler loop + outbox-event consumer |
| SVC-11 | Quota Admission & Metering Collector | SVC-METER | M17 | internal API host + bus consumer + timers |
| SVC-12 | Notification & Alert Dispatcher | SVC-NOTIFY | M20/M17 | bus consumer + delivery-queue worker |
| SVC-13 | Audit Event Shipper | — | M03 + all producer modules | outbox relay |
| SVC-14 | OTP Mailer | SVC-MAIL | M01 | outbox-event consumer |
| SVC-15 | Retention, Archival & Lineage Maintainer | — | all modules | timer sweeps + bus consumer |

System-actor mapping (§0.4.1): Ingestion Worker + Intake Validator → SVC-01; Alignment Engine → SVC-02; Pre-annotation Generator → SVC-03; Embedding Indexer → SVC-04; Distribution Computer → SVC-05; Simulation Executor + Synthetic Generator → SVC-06; Training Executor → SVC-07; Evaluation Executor → SVC-08; Packaging Worker → SVC-09; Workflow Scheduler → SVC-10; Quota Enforcer → SVC-11; Notification Dispatcher → SVC-12; Audit Shipper → SVC-13; OTP Mailer → SVC-14. SVC-15 implements DA-11 / FR-D5.7 retention (§1.7.1). All fifteen are **independently scalable worker deployments**: separate images, separate replica counts, separate autoscaling policies; none shares a process with a module API server (SVC-10/SVC-11 share their module's *schema*, never its API pods).

**3.0.2 Messaging contract.** Two message classes flow over the durable platform bus/queues:

- **Domain events** — topics named `evt.{module_domain}.{entity}.{verb}` (e.g., `evt.data.ingest_session.completed`, `evt.orch.run.cancelled`). Topic name = `evt.` + the envelope's `event_type`. Payload is exactly the §1.4.5 envelope:
```json
{"event_id": "01J… (ulid)", "event_type": "data.ingest_session.completed", "occurred_at": "RFC3339",
 "tenant_id": "tnt-…", "project_id": "prj-…|null", "actor": {"type": "user|service", "id": "…"},
 "subject": {"type": "ingest_session", "id": "ing-…"}, "payload": {"…": "…"}, "trace_id": "…"}
```
  Events are produced **only** via module transactional outboxes relayed by SVC-13 (per §0.7.6 #9) — never by direct broker publish inside request handlers (§1.4.4).
- **Commands / task dispatches** — queues named `cmd.{svc}.{action}` (e.g., `cmd.svc-02.build_episodes`). Workflow-task dispatches are produced exclusively by SVC-10 and carry the task-dispatch envelope:
```json
{"task_token": "ttk-01J…", "run_id": "wfr-…", "task_id": "align_streams", "attempt": 1,
 "template_ref": "wft-…@4", "task_type": "exec.alignment", "tenant_id": "tnt-…", "project_id": "prj-…",
 "params": {"…": "…"}, "resource_profile": {"workload_class": "…", "gpu_class": "…|null", "gpu_count": 0, "cpu_cores": 8, "mem_gb": 32},
 "lease_s": 7200, "heartbeat_interval_s": 30, "upload_credentials": {"url_pattern": "PUT {signed}", "ttl_s": 7200}, "traceparent": "00-…"}
```
- A control queue `cmd.{svc}.control` carries `{action: "cancel|drain", task_token, reason}` for SVC-10-initiated cancellation and drain signals (per §0.7.6 #12).

**3.0.3 Delivery semantics, idempotency, emissions.**

- All queues/topics are durable, partitioned, **at-least-once**. Every consumer is idempotent:
  - domain-event handlers dedupe on `event_id` (Redis seen-set, 48 h TTL, plus a database unique-key fallback so correctness never depends on cache);
  - workflow-task handlers are idempotent by `(run_id, task_id, attempt)`;
  - all module-API writes performed by services carry `Idempotency-Key` or land on natural unique keys (per §1.4.4 and the Stripe pattern per §0.7.6 #8).
- Services never publish events directly. Every service-side state change that must be platform-visible is written through an owning-module internal endpoint (that module's outbox emits), or — where a service owns local durable state (SVC-10/11/12/15 ledgers) — through the service's own outbox table relayed by SVC-13 (per §0.7.6 #9).
- No distributed transactions anywhere; cross-service consistency is event-carried + idempotent-consumer, exactly as §1.4.4 mandates for modules.

**3.0.4 Retry, DLQ, replay.** Retry ownership lives at exactly one layer per flow (per §0.7.6 #10):

1. For `cmd.*` workflow tasks, business-failure retries are owned by **SVC-10**, applying the pinned template `retry_strategy` (per §0.7.6 #3). The queue itself redelivers only on consumer crash/lease loss, with the **same** `attempt` number (idempotent resume, not a new retry).
2. For `evt.*` consumers and standalone queues, the consuming service owns retries with the platform default policy: **full-jitter exponential backoff — base 30 s, multiplier 2, cap 600 s, max 5 attempts** (`sleep = random(0, min(cap, base·2^attempt))`, per §0.7.6 #10). Service-specific overrides are stated per service.
3. In-process micro-retries against flaky endpoints are allowed (≤2, sub-second) but never extend past the message lease — single-layer ownership is preserved.
4. Messages exhausting attempts, or failing non-retryable classification (schema-invalid, `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, terminal business-state conflicts), route to the per-queue dead-letter queue **`dlq.{queue_name}`** with headers `{first_failure_at, attempts, last_error_code, trace_id}`.
5. **Replay procedure (uniform):** `dlq_depth > 0` fires a `workflow_failure`-class alert via M17-7 rules → M20. The Operations Engineer opens or links an M17-6 incident, inspects messages in the ops queue console, fixes the root cause, and replays selected messages to the origin queue under the OE identity with the attempt counter reset and header `replayed_from: dlq`. Every replay batch is audited via M03-11 (`ops.dlq.replayed` `{queue, count, incident_ref}`). Poison messages that must never be replayed (e.g., expired OTP mails) are purged with the same audit trail.

**3.0.5 Circuit breakers.** Every call to an external dependency (object-storage endpoints, simulator/generator endpoints, Ray clusters and GPU node agents, SMTP/ESP relay, vector index) and every cross-service internal API call is wrapped in a circuit breaker: Closed → Open after the failure threshold (default ≥50% failures over a 20-call window within 30 s), fail-fast while Open, Half-Open trial after 60 s; success closes, failure re-opens (per §0.7.6 #11). Breaker state transitions export as metrics and feed M17-7 alert rules so an open circuit pages before users notice. **Fail-closed rule:** a breaker on a *gating* dependency (M03-10 authz, M17-9 admission, M08-9 usage checks) rejects the work item (`E_UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE`, message requeued) — authorization and quota gates are never bypassed (BR-12, §1.4.2).

**3.0.6 Health & lifecycle.**

- Endpoints: `GET /healthz` (liveness: process up, event loop responsive, no deadlocked pool) and `GET /readyz` (readiness: broker connected, primary datastore reachable, required secrets loaded; readiness intentionally FAILS during drain).
- Shutdown sequence on SIGTERM, preceded by a `preStop` delay (default 10 s) so brokers/load balancers observe the readiness flip first: **stop consuming → finish or checkpoint in-flight work within the grace period → ack or deterministically requeue → exit** (per §0.7.6 #12).
- `terminationGracePeriodSeconds` is sized per service to its maximum checkpoint time (stated per service; default 60 s).
- Bridges to long-running external work (SVC-06/07/08) never kill that work on restart: external job handles are persisted and re-attached (Temporal activity semantics per §0.7.6 #4).

**3.0.7 Backpressure & concurrency.**

- **Bounded prefetch:** default 2 × worker-pool size, hard caps per service. Unacked-message count is the backpressure signal: a saturated pool stops fetching, queue depth grows, and depth/lag-based autoscaling (KEDA-style HPA) adds replicas.
- **Partition-aligned worker pools:** each service declares a partitioning key (e.g., `recording_id`) used both for queue partition assignment and in-process serialization — per-asset ordering without global locks; distinct keys parallelize freely.
- **Connection pooling:** bounded DB pools (default 10 conns/replica), HTTP keep-alive pools per host, object-store client pools per binding. Pool exhaustion is treated as backpressure (wait/yield), never as an error storm.
- **Fairness:** per-tenant in-flight caps on multi-tenant work queues prevent one tenant's burst from starving others (stated per service where relevant).

**3.0.8 Observability.**

- Structured JSON logs per §1.4.6: `{ts, level, service, module, trace_id, span_id, tenant_id?, action, message, payload_summary?, result, latency_ms?, error_code?}`; DEBUG off in production by default.
- W3C `traceparent` propagated API → outbox → queue message headers → service → downstream calls and callbacks, so one business request is one trace across every retry and hop (per §0.7.6 #13).
- RED metrics per service (message/request rate, error rate, duration histograms) plus `queue_depth`, `consumer_lag`, `dlq_depth`, `breaker_state`, `inflight_tasks`, and per-service domain gauges (e.g., `index_staleness_age_p95`).
- Sensitive data per §1.4.7: no payload bodies, signed URLs, login codes, or credentials in logs; assets logged as `{asset_id, byte_size, checksum}`; emails masked to `s***@domain`; the OTel collector scrubs residual PII before export (per §0.7.6 #14).

**3.0.9 Security.**

- Service-to-service calls authenticate by **mTLS service identity** (SAN allowlists per internal endpoint, §1.4.2); each identity holds only its **service-scoped permission keys** registered in M03 §2.3.5 (e.g., `sim.run.events:write` for SVC-06) plus `iam.authz:execute`.
- Object-storage access uses task-scoped, write-only signed URLs minted by the dispatching side (TTL = task lease), or per-service credentials confined to exact prefixes (e.g., SVC-02 → `canonical/{project}/…` only; raw prefixes are deny-overwrite per BR-01).
- Secrets (SMTP credentials, KMS keys, logical-binding credentials) live in the platform secret manager, fetched at startup, never serialized into queues, ledgers, or logs.
- Tenant isolation at every hop: a message's `tenant_id` must match the scope of every downstream call; mismatches are rejected, logged ERROR, and counted (isolation-violation metric alarms at >0).

---

### 3.1 SVC-01 — Ingestion & Intake Validation Worker

**Purpose & owning module(s).** Executes the asynchronous half of M05's session-completion and intake-validation pipeline — M05 §2.5.3 (M05-5 step 5): multipart finalization, structural validation (mcap-doctor-equivalent checks + recover salvage, per §0.7.2), normalization to indexed MCAP, modality-stream extraction and registration (FR-A2.3/A2.4), quality-rule evaluation (FR-A3.1–A3.4), Quality Report writing (M05-7), quality-status setting, and quarantine routing (FR-A2.7, FR-A3.3). Side duties from M05 §2.5.3: multipart abort + orphan-part GC for aborted sessions (M05-3) and the stale-session expiry sweep. Realizes system actors *Ingestion Worker* and *Intake Validator*.

**Business processing logic.** Per dispatched validation task (M16 template `intake_validation`, executor binding per M05-5 step 4; one task per recording group):

1. Dedupe on `(run_id, task_id, attempt)`; emit `start` to M16-8; load context from task params: recording ids, manifest ref, storage prefix, campaign governance tags.
2. **Finalize objects:** complete object-store multipart uploads per file; verify the composed full-object SHA-256 against the part-digest composition recorded at M05-5 step 2; mismatch → finding `checksum_mismatch` (severity error), file marked unusable.
3. **Structural validation:** mcap-doctor-equivalent checks (summary section present, chunk/message indexes intact, readable schema records); truncated files (on-robot crash) go through automated recover salvage with the salvage delta logged as finding `salvaged_truncation` (per §0.7.2 #11).
4. **Normalization:** legacy containers (`ros1_bag`, `ros2_db3`) transcoded to indexed MCAP under the recording's `derived/` prefix; recorded as a derivation — original bytes are never touched (BR-01, FR-A2.1, per §0.7.2 #1).
5. **Modality extraction:** enumerate topics; map to the exact FR-A2.3 modality registry; write per-stream rows (topic, message_count, measured hz, gap count, clock domain, timestamp source — per §0.7.2 #9) through M05's internal stream-registration write path.
6. **Rule evaluation:** resolve the quality-rule set (resolution order campaign > sensor_suite > embodiment > dataset_purpose > tenant default, M05-9); evaluate checks (`required_modalities`, `topic_frequency`, `timestamp_gap`, `metadata_completeness`, plus implicit `file_integrity` and `duplicate_check`); produce findings with measured-vs-threshold evidence refs.
7. **Report + status (per recording, one M05 transaction):** write the Quality Report (rule-set versions + `workflow_run_ref` pinned, FR-A3.4 auditability); compute the target status from rule outcomes (`on_error_status` / `on_warn_status`) → `accepted | accepted_with_warnings | quarantined | rejected | requires_manual_review`; M05's outbox emits `data.recording.accepted` / `data.recording.quarantined` / etc. Quarantined and rejected recordings become invisible to downstream selection (FR-A2.7).
8. Set the session terminal state (`completed`, or `failed` with per-file causes); emit `success|failure` to M16-8 (failure events carry FR-D4.2 fields: failure_stage, failure_category, affected_inputs, recommended_next_action); register recordings as workflow outputs via M16-9; post storage/CPU consumption records to M17-10.

State machine (per-recording pipeline; checkpointed as M16-8 `progress` markers + a staging manifest under `derived/_staging/{run_id}/`):

```text
received → objects_finalized → structure_validated → normalized
        → streams_registered → rules_evaluated → report_written → status_set (terminal)
```

**Failure recovery.**
- Every step is individually idempotent: multipart completion replays as a no-op when already complete; normalization writes to a staging key then atomic-renames; stream/report writes are upserts keyed `(recording_id, report_version)`.
- Crash mid-step → queue redelivery (same attempt) resumes from the last checkpointed step in the staging manifest; nothing is visible to consumers until step 7 publishes atomically (WAP staging per §0.7.2 #12).
- Business failure after SVC-10's retry budget → task `failed`; the recording stays `pending_validation` and surfaces on the RPT-01 validation backlog; the operator reruns via M16-4 `:rerun` after remediation.

**Service interface.**
- Input queues: `cmd.svc-01.validate_intake` (SVC-10 dispatch); `cmd.svc-01.control`; subscription `evt.data.ingest_session.aborted` (multipart abort + orphan-part GC per §0.7.2 #3); timer: stale-session sweep every 10 min (aborts `open/uploading` sessions past expiry through M05's internal abort path, audited — M05 §2.5.3 concurrency summary).
- Consumed event schema (abort GC):
```json
{"event_id": "01J…", "event_type": "data.ingest_session.aborted", "occurred_at": "…", "tenant_id": "tnt-…",
 "project_id": "prj-…", "actor": {"type": "user", "id": "usr-…"}, "subject": {"type": "ingest_session", "id": "ing-…"},
 "payload": {"storage_prefix": "raw/…", "open_multipart_uploads": ["upload-id…"]}, "trace_id": "…"}
```
- Outputs: M05 internal writes (stream rows, quality reports, quality status — SVC-01 is the sole writer of `pending_validation → *` per the M05 §2.5.3 state machine); M16-8 task events; M16-9 output registration (`raw_recording → M05`); M17-10 consumption records; object-storage writes confined to `derived/` and `_staging/` prefixes.

**High concurrency.**
- Pool model: per-replica worker pool (default 8), one worker streams one recording end-to-end; file IO uses 64 MiB streaming windows — large recordings are never fully buffered.
- **Partitioning key: `recording_id`** (queue partition = hash(recording_id)) — serializes all work per recording; a session's recordings parallelize across workers and replicas.
- Prefetch bound: 2 × pool (16). Per-tenant in-flight cap: 16 recordings (fairness under bulk imports).
- Backpressure: saturated pool halts fetch; queue depth drives autoscaling; storage-binding throughput caps bound per-binding parallelism.

**High availability.**
- ≥2 replicas across zones; partition rebalancing on replica loss (unacked work redelivers — nothing lost).
- Health: readiness = broker + M05 internal API + tenant storage-binding reachability probe; grace period 120 s (finish current step + flush staging manifest).
- Circuit breakers: object-storage endpoint per binding; M05 internal API; M03-10.
- Retry: crash redelivery = same attempt; business retries SVC-10-owned per the `intake_validation` template (`limit 3, base 30 s, multiplier 2, cap 600 s, full jitter, retry_policy on_transient`); GC/sweep consumers use the §3.0.4 default.
- DLQ: `dlq.cmd.svc-01.validate_intake`, `dlq.evt.data.ingest_session.aborted`; replay per §3.0.4 (resumes from checkpoint).
- Consistency: at-least-once + idempotent step writes ⇒ exactly-once effect on recording status; a recording never regresses from a terminal quality status (M05 state-machine guards); duplicate-detection verdicts (`E_ING_DUPLICATE_RECORDING`) are deterministic on composed digests.

**Scaling signals & capacity notes.**
- IO/CPU-bound (checksumming, MCAP parsing, container transcode); no GPU.
- Scale on `cmd.svc-01.validate_intake` depth and bytes-in-flight; capacity unit ≈ 1 worker per 150–250 MB/s sustained object-store throughput, ~2 cores/worker for parse+checksum.
- Burst profile follows campaign ingest waves; `evt.data.ingest_session.opened` rate is the leading pre-scale indicator.

### 3.2 SVC-02 — Alignment & Episode Builder

**Purpose & owning module(s).** Executes M06's alignment + normalization pipeline — M06 §2.6.3 (M06-2 step 6): tolerance-policy approximate-time alignment over accepted recordings (DA-05), canonical episode materialization as chunked Parquet + encoded video (RLDS-style steps, LeRobot-v3-style layout, AV1 with H.264 fallback — DA-04, per §0.7.2 #4/#6/#7), alignment summaries (FR-A4.1), append-only lineage edges (BR-02, FR-A4.7), and `alignment_failed` flagging with snapshot-eligibility exclusion (FR-A4.3). Sole writer of episode `building → ready | alignment_failed` (M06 §2.6.5 credential scoping). Realizes system actor *Alignment Engine*.

**Business processing logic.** Per dispatched task (template `alignment_normalization`; one task per recording covering the placeholder episodes created at M06-2 step 4):

1. Dedupe `(run_id, task_id, attempt)`; emit `start`; load pinned context from params: episode placeholder ids + versions, `policy_id@version` (pair tolerances, reference clock, resample strategy, `max_flagged_frame_ratio`), `calibration_set_id` (FR-A4.4), embodiment schema ref, boundary ranges.
2. **Stream load:** read source MCAP streams via M05 signed reads; prefer hardware timestamps per the recorded clock-domain/timestamp-source metadata (per §0.7.2 #9); index messages per topic for the requested ranges.
3. **Approximate-time matching:** per modality pair, match messages within the policy slop (default `half_slowest_period`, per §0.7.2 #8) on the reference clock; resample per strategy; frames whose constituents exceed tolerance are marked `flagged=true`.
4. **Step assembly:** build RLDS-style step rows — `observation.*` per the embodiment schema, `action.*`, `language_instruction?`, boundary flags `is_first/is_last/is_terminal`, `source_ts_ns`, `aligned_ts_ns`, `flagged` (DA-04). No cross-embodiment normalization at rest (OXE lesson, per §0.7.2 #5).
5. **Materialization:** append step rows into chunked Parquet under `canonical/{project_id}/data/chunk-…` staging; transcode camera topics to AV1 (H.264 fallback) with random-access keyframe intervals; write per-frame decode timestamps into Parquet (per §0.7.2 #7); update the `meta/episodes.parquet` slice index (episode → chunk/file/row+frame ranges).
6. **Verdict:** compute the flagged-frame ratio; ratio ≤ policy max → `ready`; otherwise `alignment_failed` — episode stays readable for diagnosis but is excluded from snapshot eligibility (FR-A4.3; M08 enforces via the eligibility flag).
7. **Publish (per-episode M06 transaction):** alignment summary (slop per pair, flagged ratio, reference clock, resample strategy — FR-A4.1) → storage slice refs → status → **lineage edge** `recording → transform align@svc-02:vX → episode@version` (append-only, BR-02) → M06 outbox `data.episode.created` / `data.episode.alignment_failed` (M06 §2.6.4 processing logic #2). M18 indexing rides this outbox (→ SVC-04).
8. Emit `success` to M16-8 (payload summary: step_count, flagged_ratio); register episodes via M16-9 (`episode → M06`); post CPU/storage (and encoder-GPU, if used) consumption to M17-10.

State machine (per episode; checkpointed in the staging manifest `canonical/{project}/_staging/{run_id}/{episode_id}.json`):

```text
pending → streams_loaded → matched → steps_built → parquet_staged → video_staged
        → published(ready | alignment_failed)
```

**Failure recovery.**
- Staging-then-publish: all Parquet/video writes land under `_staging/` keyed `(episode_id, version, attempt)`; the M06 publish transaction is the only visibility flip (WAP per §0.7.2 #12).
- Crash mid-step → redelivery resumes from the checkpoint; re-running a published episode version is a no-op (terminal status guard — idempotent).
- Deterministic alignment failures (tolerance violations) are *successful task outcomes* with verdict `alignment_failed` — never retried. Only infrastructure failures retry per template policy.
- Orphaned staging older than 48 h is GC'd by a sweep; GC is safe because publish is atomic.

**Service interface.**
- Input queues: `cmd.svc-02.build_episodes` (SVC-10), `cmd.svc-02.control`.
- Dispatch payload (compact):
```json
{"task_token": "ttk-…", "run_id": "wfr-…", "task_id": "align_streams", "attempt": 1, "task_type": "exec.alignment",
 "tenant_id": "tnt-…", "project_id": "prj-…",
 "params": {"recording_id": "rec-…", "episode_ids": ["epi-…"], "policy_ref": "alp-…@3", "calibration_set_id": "cal-…",
            "boundaries": {"source": "manual_ranges", "ranges": [{"start_ts": "…", "end_ts": "…"}]}},
 "resource_profile": {"workload_class": "data_processing", "cpu_cores": 16, "mem_gb": 64, "gpu_count": 0},
 "lease_s": 7200, "heartbeat_interval_s": 30, "traceparent": "…"}
```
- Outputs: M06 internal episode/lineage/summary write path (service identity scoped to episode writes only); object storage `canonical/{project_id}/…` under write-scoped credentials (M06 §2.6.5); M16-8 task events; M16-9 registration; M17-10 metering.

**High concurrency.**
- Pool model: per-replica pipeline pool (default 4 episode pipelines); each pipeline internally parallelizes per-stream decode and per-camera encode up to `cpu_cores`.
- **Partitioning key: `recording_id`** — serializes chunk-file appends per recording's project chunk and guarantees episode-version ordering; distinct recordings parallelize freely.
- Prefetch: 1 × pool (tasks are heavy; over-prefetch wastes lease time). Lease-aware fetch: never accept work whose p50 duration exceeds the remaining drain budget during scale-in.
- Backpressure: pool saturation halts fetch; per-binding write throughput caps prevent canonical-store saturation.

**High availability.**
- ≥2 replicas; partition reassignment on loss; `terminationGracePeriodSeconds` 600 (checkpoint current chunk write + flush staging manifest).
- Health: readiness = broker + canonical-store credentials valid + M06 internal API (M06 §2.6.6 FATAL conditions map to unready).
- Circuit breakers: object storage (M05 reads / canonical writes per binding), M06 internal API, M03-10.
- Retry: SVC-10-owned per `alignment_normalization` template (`limit 3, base 30 s, multiplier 2, cap 600 s, full jitter, on_transient`); redelivery resumes the same attempt from checkpoint.
- DLQ: `dlq.cmd.svc-02.build_episodes`; replay per §3.0.4 (replay restarts from the staging checkpoint).
- Consistency: per-episode atomic publish; lineage edges append-only; episode versions immutable once terminal (M06 §2.6.3); slice indexes updated only inside the publish transaction.

**Scaling signals & capacity notes.**
- CPU-bound; video encode dominates (~70% of wall time; AV1 software encode ≈ 0.3–1× realtime per 8 cores; optional encoder-accelerator nodes cut 5–10×).
- Scale on queue depth × p50 task duration (SLO: backlog drains < 2 h); memory profile sized to the longest episode step buffer (cap 64 GB).
- Storage ingress/egress is the secondary ceiling — co-locate replicas with the tenant storage-binding region.

### 3.3 SVC-03 — Pre-annotation & Segmentation Suggestion Runner

**Purpose & owning module(s).** Runs registered machine-suggestion generators (DA-13 registry: `vlm_captioner`, `detector`, `segmenter`, `action_segmenter`) as jobs for M07 §2.7.3 (M07-6 pre-annotation jobs) and M06 §2.6.3 (M06-7 segmentation suggestions). Persists generator identity, version, and confidence on every output (FR-A6.3). Outputs are suggestions only — never auto-applied; acceptance flows through M07-7 review and M06-6 `suggestion_ref` (BR-06 stance, M06-7). Realizes system actor *Pre-annotation Generator*.

**Business processing logic.** Per dispatched task (templates of category `annotation`, e.g., `preannotate-vlm-caption`, or the M06-7 suggestion workflow):

1. Dedupe `(run_id, task_id, attempt)`; emit `start`; resolve the job spec: generator `{name, version}` pinned at job creation (FR-A6.3; DA-13), target set (annotation tasks/episodes from a cohort, or recording/episode for segmentation), generator params, label-schema/taxonomy version pins.
2. **Input staging:** resolve episode content manifests via M06-5-equivalent internal reads (signed URLs); assemble frames/clips/step windows per the generator's input contract; batch into micro-batches.
3. **Inference:** invoke the generator runtime — platform-hosted inference deployment or tenant logical compute binding (M02, DA-20) — per micro-batch; collect raw outputs with per-item confidence.
4. **Output mapping:** map raw outputs to platform shapes — captions/labels/boxes/masks → pre-annotation records (M07); boundary/action proposals → suggestion items `{level, start_ts, end_ts, proposed_label_path?, confidence}` (M06); label paths validated against the pinned taxonomy version (`E_EPI_TAXONOMY_VERSION` class failures are non-retryable).
5. **Persist with provenance:** write through owning-module service paths — M07 pre-annotation ingestion via M16-9 output registration (service permission `label.preannotation:ingest`, M07 §2.7.5); M06 suggestion items via the suggestion-set write path (suggestion write only — SVC-03 cannot touch episodes or segments, M06 §2.6.5). Every record carries `{generator_identity, generator_version, confidence, job_ref}` (FR-A6.3).
6. Flip the suggestion set / pre-annotation job `running → completed | failed` through the owning module; emit `success|failure` to M16-8; meter GPU seconds to M17-10.

State machine (per job):

```text
received → inputs_staged → inferring(batch i/n, checkpointed per batch_seq)
         → outputs_mapped → persisted → completed | failed
```

**Failure recovery.**
- Crash mid-inference → redelivery resumes from the last flushed `batch_seq`; persisted batches dedupe on upsert keys `(job_ref, batch_seq)` — no duplicate suggestions.
- Generator endpoint failures: timeout/5xx → transient (retry); contract/shape errors → fail fast (non-retryable).
- A `failed` job leaves flushed suggestions visible but the set marked `failed` with cause — reviewers see partial coverage explicitly (FR-A5.2 / A6 review trail intact).
- Generator-version retirement mid-job: the job completes on the pinned version (pinned-ref immutability, mirroring §0.7.6 #1).

**Service interface.**
- Input queues: `cmd.svc-03.run_generator` (SVC-10), `cmd.svc-03.control`.
- Dispatch payload (compact):
```json
{"task_token": "ttk-…", "run_id": "wfr-…", "task_id": "run_generator", "attempt": 1, "task_type": "exec.preannotation",
 "tenant_id": "tnt-…", "project_id": "prj-…",
 "params": {"job_kind": "preannotation|segmentation_suggestion", "job_ref": "paj-…|sgs-…",
            "generator": {"name": "action-segmenter", "version": "2.1"},
            "targets": {"cohort_id": "coh-…", "episode_ids": null, "recording_id": null},
            "taxonomy_pin": "tax-…@4", "generator_params": {}},
 "resource_profile": {"workload_class": "data_processing", "gpu_class": "gpu_l40s", "gpu_count": 1},
 "lease_s": 14400, "heartbeat_interval_s": 30, "traceparent": "…"}
```
- Outputs: M07 pre-annotation records (via M16-9 → M07 registration consumer); M06 suggestion items + set status; M16-8 events; M17-10 metering; transient batch scratch in an auto-expiring object prefix (no durable payload writes).

**High concurrency.**
- Pool model: GPU worker pool — one job pipeline per GPU with internal micro-batching; CPU pre/post-processing pool at 2 × GPU workers feeds it.
- **Partitioning key: `job_ref`** — a job is sequential over its batches; jobs parallelize across workers/replicas. Per-`generator_endpoint` concurrency caps protect shared inference deployments.
- Prefetch: 1 per idle GPU worker. Per-project concurrent-job cap (default 4) keeps interactive annotation traffic responsive.
- Backpressure: GPU saturation halts fetch; endpoint caps queue work visibly rather than overloading generators.

**High availability.**
- ≥2 replicas per GPU class; jobs rebalance on replica loss via redelivery; grace period 300 s (flush current batch).
- Health: readiness = broker + generator-registry read + ≥1 healthy generator endpoint (degraded endpoints reported per-generator).
- Circuit breakers: per generator endpoint (open → job pauses and retries, WARN + M17 metric), M06/M07 internal APIs, object storage.
- Retry: SVC-10-owned (`limit 3, base 60 s, multiplier 2, cap 900 s, full jitter`); in-process endpoint micro-retries ≤2 within the lease.
- DLQ: `dlq.cmd.svc-03.run_generator`; replay per §3.0.4 (resumes from `batch_seq`).
- Consistency: batch-level idempotent upserts ⇒ no duplicate pre-annotations/suggestions; provenance fields immutable once written; never writes to episodes/segments (enforced by permission scope).

**Scaling signals & capacity notes.**
- GPU-bound (VLM captioning heaviest: ~0.5–2 s per frame-set on L40S-class hardware).
- Scale on queue depth per `gpu_class` and per-generator p50 latency; throughput unit ≈ 1 GPU = 1 concurrent job.
- Suggestion jobs burst after ingest/alignment waves — SVC-01/SVC-02 completion event rates are the leading autoscale indicator.

### 3.4 SVC-04 — Embedding & Index Maintainer

**Purpose & owning module(s).** Maintains M18's vector indexes: embeds new/changed episodes, segments, and frames per registered embedding space (M18-3), upserts/deletes index entries via M18-11 (i) (`POST /internal/v1/index/upsert` / `…/delete`), tracks per-entry staleness, and executes full/partial reindex jobs (new space versions). Entry identity per Table 0.3 #30: `emb-{asset_id}:{model}:{version}`. Fed by the M06 §2.6.4 "M18 index feed" and the M08 §2.8.3 publish fan-out. Realizes system actor *Embedding Indexer*.

**Business processing logic.**

1. **Change intake:** consume asset-change events — `evt.data.episode.created`, `evt.data.episode.tags_changed`, `evt.data.segment.changed`, `evt.data.snapshot.published` (M08 §2.8.3 step 8), `evt.label.annotation_set.approved` (M07), `evt.sim.batch.validated` (M10) — dedupe by `event_id`; upsert the **staleness ledger** row `(asset_ref, embedding_space) → {content_digest, state, last_indexed_at}`; state → `stale`.
2. **Coalescing:** a debounce window (default 60 s) per `(asset_ref, space)` collapses bursts (tag-edit storms) into one embed unit. Deletion/archival events (module archival emissions; SVC-15 transitions) mark `delete_pending` and skip embedding.
3. **Batch embed:** workers claim `stale` entries in batches per embedding space (`FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`); fetch content via owning-module signed reads per the space's input contract (text fields, keyframes, trajectory windows); run the space's embedding model on GPU; produce vectors.
4. **Index write:** call M18-11 upsert — batch ≤256 entries:
```json
{"entries": [{"entry_id": "emb-epi-…:ep-clip:2", "asset_ref": "epi-…@2", "embedding_space_id": "esp-…",
  "vector_ref": "inline|obj://…", "payload": {"tenant_id": "tnt-…", "project_id": "prj-…", "modality": "video", "tags_digest": "sha256:…"},
  "content_digest": "sha256:…"}]}
```
  `delete_pending` entries call M18-11 delete. M18-11 is idempotent by `(entry_id, source_event_id)` (M18 §2.18.3); `content_digest` rides in the payload for staleness comparison only.
5. Ledger state → `indexed` (or `deleted`); embedding-job progress (M18-3) updated through M18's job write path.
6. **Reindex jobs:** `cmd.svc-04.reindex` (created by M18-3 POST, e.g., a new space version) iterates the asset population with a cursor checkpoint (per project × asset type), embedding under the new `{model, version}` while the old space keeps serving; M18 flips the space alias on completion; old entries delete lazily.

State machine (per ledger entry / reindex job):

```text
entry:   stale → embedding → indexed | failed(retry) ;  delete_pending → deleted
reindex: pending → running(cursor=…) → completed | failed   (cursor checkpoint per batch)
```

**Failure recovery.**
- Crash mid-batch → claim lock expires; another worker re-claims; M18-11 idempotency makes double-upserts harmless.
- A `failed` entry retries per the §3.0.4 default; after max attempts it remains `failed(stale)` and is surfaced by the staleness metric — search degrades gracefully (M18-1 responses carry index freshness).
- The ledger is rebuildable: a nightly reconciliation sweep compares owning-module change cursors against `last_indexed_at` and re-marks drift as `stale` (self-healing against missed events).

**Service interface.**
- Inputs: subscriptions listed in step 1 (consumer group `svc-04`); `cmd.svc-04.reindex`; nightly reconciliation timer.
- Consumed event example:
```json
{"event_id": "01J…", "event_type": "data.episode.created", "occurred_at": "…", "tenant_id": "tnt-…", "project_id": "prj-…",
 "actor": {"type": "service", "id": "svc-align"}, "subject": {"type": "episode", "id": "epi-…"},
 "payload": {"version": 1, "status": "ready", "step_count": 8421, "modalities": ["video", "joint_state"]}, "trace_id": "…"}
```
- Outputs: M18-11 upsert/delete (mTLS, service permission `analytics.index:write`); M18 embedding-job status writes; staleness/lag metrics; no end-user object-storage writes (model weights cached read-only).

**High concurrency.**
- Pool model: GPU embed pool (one batch stream per GPU) + CPU fetch/preprocess pool at 4 × GPU workers.
- **Partitioning key: `asset_ref`** (hash) — per-asset ordering so an upsert never races its own delete; embedding spaces parallelize independently.
- Prefetch: event-consumer prefetch 500 (ledger upserts are cheap); embed batches sized to GPU memory (e.g., 256 frame-entries).
- Backpressure: the ledger *is* the buffer — event intake never blocks on GPU availability; `stale` depth drives GPU autoscale.

**High availability.**
- ≥2 replicas per GPU class; ledger in PostgreSQL (service schema `idx`); replicas coordinate via `SKIP LOCKED` claims; grace period 120 s.
- Health: readiness = broker + ledger DB + M18-11 reachability.
- Circuit breakers: M18-11/vector store (open → embedding pauses; ledger keeps absorbing events), owning-module content reads, model runtime.
- Retry: §3.0.4 default (base 30 s ×2, cap 600 s, max 5) per entry; reindex batches retry in place against the cursor.
- DLQ: `dlq.evt.svc-04.intake` (malformed events), `dlq.cmd.svc-04.reindex`; replay per §3.0.4.
- Consistency: eventual — staleness SLO p95 ≤ 10 min from asset event to searchable vector; deletes win over concurrent upserts (content-digest compare); reindex is shadow-build + alias flip (readers never see a half-built space).

**Scaling signals & capacity notes.**
- GPU-bound (embedding forward passes). Capacity unit ≈ 1 GPU = 200–800 entries/min depending on space (text vs multi-frame video).
- Scale on `stale` ledger depth and staleness-age p95 per space.
- Reindex jobs are planned bulk loads — run on borrowed/preemptible capacity (M17 `exploratory` class) and pause under reclaim (per §0.7.6 #5).

### 3.5 SVC-05 — Distribution & Clustering Computer

**Purpose & owning module(s).** Computes M18's analytical aggregates: tag/attribute distributions and embedding-distribution analyses (UMAP/t-SNE projections; HDBSCAN/k-means clustering) for M18-4/M18-5; coverage-summary and coverage-gap analysis for M18-9/M18-10 (feeding M04-5 coverage reports and M09-6 scenario requests); visualization payload computation for M18-6; and the synchronous clustering backend behind M18-7 (i) used by M15-5 failure clustering. All results cached by params-hash. Realizes system actor *Distribution Computer*.

**Business processing logic.**

1. **Job intake:** consume `cmd.svc-05.compute` (one message per M18-4/M18-6/M18-10 job row) carrying `{job_id (dst-…), analysis_type, scope, params, requested_by}`.
2. **Params-hash gate:** compute `params_hash = sha256(canonical_json(analysis_type, params, input_selector) ‖ input_digest)`, where `input_digest` = the population digest (cohort version, snapshot manifest hash, or max change-cursor of the matched asset set) ‖ embedding-space version where applicable. Cache hit in the report store → link the existing distribution report to the job, mark `completed(cached)`, stop.
3. **Population resolution:** materialize the asset population (episodes/segments/failure records/…) via M18's query engine; scope and governance verdicts (restricted assets excluded, deny-wins §0.5.3) were resolved at job creation by M18 and are honored from the job context.
4. **Compute by analysis_type:**
   - `tag_distribution` → grouped counts/histograms over tags/labels/structured metadata (taxonomy-version-aware);
   - `embedding_distribution` → fetch vectors from the index; projection (UMAP or t-SNE per params, seeded) → clustering (HDBSCAN or k-means) → cluster summaries (size, medoid exemplar refs, label purity);
   - `coverage_gap` → join distributions against campaign coverage goals / scenario plans; emit gap rows with severity and supporting counts (M18-10);
   - visualization payloads → render-ready point/cluster/density arrays in the M18-5 `visualization-data` shape.
5. **Persist:** write the distribution report + payloads to the M18 report store (large point arrays → object storage `analytics/{project}/reports/{report_id}/…`; the report row keeps refs); job → `completed`; M18's outbox emits `analytics.report.completed` (consumed by M04 coverage cache, M09 gap intake, M15).
6. **Synchronous clustering (M18-7):** SVC-05 also hosts the compute pool behind `POST /internal/v1/analytics/cluster` — bounded inputs (≤50 k items, p95 ≤ 30 s) on a dedicated low-latency pool; larger inputs are rejected by M18-7 toward the async job path.

State machine (per job):

```text
queued → resolving → computing(stage: project | cluster | aggregate, checkpointed)
       → persisting → completed | completed(cached) | failed
```

**Failure recovery.**
- Stage checkpoints: the resolved population manifest and intermediate matrices are staged under `analytics/_staging/{job_id}/`; crash → redelivery resumes from the last completed stage.
- Determinism: seeded algorithms (UMAP/t-SNE/k-means) persist their seed in params — a resumed or replayed job reproduces the same layout.
- Failed jobs report cause to M18 (job row `failed` + reason); users resubmit cheaply.
- Cache staleness is impossible by construction: population changes change `input_digest`, hence the params_hash — cached reports are immutable and content-addressed.

**Service interface.**
